Chapter 1 Transmigration! A Side Relationship of the Red Chamber, Activating the System in a Desperate Situation

A cold night, a chilling rain.

Lin Chen was forcibly dragged into this unfamiliar world by a piercing pain and a cacophony of insults.

His forehead burned, his damp, cold coarse linen clothes clung to his skin, every bone felt as if it had been shattered and reassembled. Memories that weren't his own flooded his mind like a burst dam—a collateral descendant of the Jia family, orphaned at a young age, living under the roof of others, the semblance of kinship quickly eroded after his parents' deaths, leaving only the daily exploitation, humiliation, and beatings from the wicked servants under the steward, Lai Da.

"You dog, you dare to bear the surname Lin? You dare to live under the eaves of the Jia family?"

"Bah! You really think you're some kind of master? Your parents' reputation has long been ruined by you, you sickly wretched!"

"Get out! Master Lai said, if you don't gather this month's tribute money today, he'll break your legs and throw you out to feed the wild dogs!"

The shrill voice of the wicked servant Zhang Ergou echoed in his ears, accompanied by several muffled kicks to his ribs. Lin Chen tried to open his eyes, but his eyelids felt as heavy as a thousand pounds. Fragments of memories belonging to the original "Lin Chen" were burning: yesterday, when he asked for his already meager monthly allowance, Master Lai, on the grounds of "offending the steward and harboring resentment," ordered him to be dragged to the woodshed in the backyard, brutally beaten, and left to die in this leaky side courtyard.

The original owner of this body, that cowardly, sickly, and bewildered youth, had already succumbed to a high fever and despair last night.

In his place stands Lin Chen, a martial arts grandmaster from the 21st century. He remembers triggering a tribulation of lightning atop Kunlun Mountain in his quest to break through to the pinnacle of martial arts, only to fail at the last moment and be swallowed by the raging thunder. When he opened his eyes again, he was greeted by this hellish scene.

"Still got some tough bones?" Zhang Ergou spat, then kicked Lin Chen hard in the stomach.

The excruciating pain instantly cleared Lin Chen's mind a little. The unwavering will of a martial arts master forcefully suppressed his physical weakness and confused memories. He barely opened his eyes a crack; rain mixed with blood blurred his vision. He could only see two vicious servants dressed in gray short-sleeved shirts, their faces contorted with rage, punching and kicking him. All around him was a dilapidated courtyard, overgrown with weeds, tiles broken, rain dripping from holes in the eaves, splashing onto the waterlogged flagstones, and hitting his face.

This was no era he was familiar with. The air was thick with the smell of decaying wood and damp earth, and... a faint flow of "qi" completely different from that of Earth. As soon as this thought arose, more memories flooded in: this was the Great Chu Dynasty, Jinling City, the Rongguo Mansion built by imperial decree. A world that seemed similar to the "Dream of the Red Chamber," yet utterly different—martial arts reaching the divine level, ninth-rank Foundation Establishment, seventh-rank capable of fighting a hundred men, fifth-rank Qi Control, third-rank River Overturning, first-rank Mountain Shattering, and the legendary pinnacle of martial arts transcending the mortal realm.

And he was the most insignificant, the most vulnerable among the countless collateral branches of the Jia family. Orphaned, destitute, even his monthly allowance was withheld, and now he was driven to the brink of despair.

Anger, like a frozen volcano, exploded in Lin Chen's heart. In his life of triumph, he had never suffered such humiliation. Even now, with his body weak and devoid of internal energy, the pride of a grandmaster and his fighting instincts would not allow him to be kicked and beaten to death like a dead dog!

"Ding!"

Just as he mustered his last ounce of strength, preparing to deliver a brutal blow to the wicked servant before him, even at the cost of broken bones, a clear, emotionless mechanical voice suddenly resounded deep within his mind.

“Host qualified… Soul strength meets requirements… World anchoring complete… Binding the [Dream of the Red Chamber Sign-in System]…”

“Binding successful! Newbie gift pack being distributed…”

“Congratulations, host, you have obtained: Ninth-Rank Early Stage Cultivation Infusion, Basic Fist Technique: Stone-Splitting Fist (Infusion), Body Tempering Powder x1.”

As soon as the cold mechanical voice faded, a warm yet powerful current of heat surged forth from the depths of Lin Chen's dantian without warning, instantly engulfing his limbs and bones! Wherever the heat flowed, frozen blood began to surge, broken bones emitted a subtle, teeth-grinding friction sound, healing and strengthening at an astonishing speed, and the stagnant blood from hidden injuries beneath the skin was rapidly dissolved and expelled. His previously weak and feeble body, like a parched tree receiving rain after a long drought, burst forth with vibrant life!

At the same time, the key points, energy flow, and power generation techniques of a fist technique called "Stone-Splitting Fist" were deeply imprinted in his mind, as if he had practiced it a thousand times, making it an instinct of his body. Information about a medicinal powder called "Body Tempering Powder" also clearly surfaced.

All of this happened extremely quickly, in the blink of an eye. Outside, Zhang Ergou and another wicked henchman named Wang San only saw the "sickly" man on the ground, who had previously only breathed his last, seem to twitch violently, as if countless tiny mice were scurrying under his skin, making a slight crackling sound.

"Huh? Not quite dead yet?" Zhang Ergou paused for a moment, then revealed an even more cruel smile. "Still pretty tough! Wang San, come on, give him a quick death, break both his legs, and throw him into the back alley!"

Wang San responded, grinning maliciously as he raised his foot, aimed at Lin Chen's knee, and stomped down hard!

Just as the muddy soles of his shoes were about to touch the bone—

the "corpse" on the ground suddenly opened its eyes!

What kind of eyes were those? Deep, cold, with a trace of lingering confusion, but more than anything, a sharp, piercing light that seemed to ignite from someone who had weathered life's storms and seen through death! Absolutely not the look of a fifteen or sixteen-year-old, bullied orphan from a collateral branch of the family!

Wang San was stung by that gaze, his movements involuntarily slowing by a fraction of a second.

That fraction of a second determined the outcome.

"Hah—!"

A low, short exhale, not particularly loud, yet carrying a strange penetrating force. Lin Chen, lying on the ground, thrust his right arm upwards at an unbelievable angle and with a speed defying human logic! His fingers weren't clenched into a fist, but rather held together like a chisel, imbued with the first wisp of internal energy of a ninth-grade early-stage martial artist, striking Wang San's shinbone with perfect precision!

"Crack!"

The crisp sound of bone breaking was particularly jarring amidst the rain.

"Ah—!" Wang San let out a piercing scream, clutching his shinbone and staggering backward before collapsing with a thud into the muddy water, writhing in agony.

Zhang Ergou was stunned, staring in disbelief at Lin Chen, who had instantly regained his strength and even crippled Wang San with a single move. The young man before him, though still soaked and covered in blood, stood there with his back straight, his face devoid of any trace of illness or cowardice, only an indifferent coldness. Especially his eyes, which sent chills down Zhang Ergou's spine.

"You...you..." Zhang Ergou stammered, instinctively taking a step back. Then he remembered that he was the "stronger one," a trusted lackey of Master Lai, while the other was just a cripple who had just been on the verge of death! "Fine! Lin Chen, you dare to resist? You even dare to injure Wang San? I think you're tired of living!"

Shouting with feigned bravado, Zhang Ergou mustered his courage and charged at Lin Chen with his fist raised. Although he hadn't formally practiced martial arts, relying on his strength and having bullied Master Lai, he had learned a few rudimentary farming techniques and felt confident he could easily handle a sickly man who had just experienced a "last gasp of life."

Lin Chen remained expressionless, not even glancing at Zhang Ergou's flawed attack. His martial arts master's combat experience and the newly infused essence of the Stone-Splitting Fist instantly merged in his mind. Without moving his feet, he merely shifted his body slightly, narrowly avoiding Zhang Ergou's fist. Simultaneously, he clenched his right fist, lowered his waist and hips, and a newly formed, slightly raw but sufficiently concentrated internal energy surged into his fist, carrying the most basic "clanging" force of fundamental fist techniques, striking Zhang Ergou's unprotected ribs from below.

"Bang!"

A muffled thud. Zhang Ergou's forward momentum abruptly halted. His eyes bulged, his mouth gaped open, but no sound came out. A sharp pain instantly seized his breath, and his entire body curled up like a boiled shrimp, vomiting bile, collapsing to the ground, only convulsing in agony.

From Lin Chen's outburst to the two falling to the ground, only a few breaths had passed.

The rain continued to fall, washing away the mud and the faint smell of blood. Lin Chen stood in the rain, slowly withdrawing his fist, looking down at his still thin hands, now imbued with power. The weakness and despair that belonged to "Lin Chen" had completely faded, replaced by a sense of control over power and an initial, cold scrutiny of this unfamiliar world.

Early stage of the Ninth Rank… In this high-martial-arts world of the Red Chamber, he had only just stepped into the threshold of martial arts, capable of strengthening his body and splitting bricks and stones. But for him, who had just been on the verge of death, it was the only light in his desperate situation.

"System?" Lin Chen murmured in his mind.

"Host, I am here," a cold, mechanical voice responded.

"How does the daily check-in work?"

"This system is a check-in system exclusive to the Dream of the Red Chamber. The host can perform a basic check-in once a day at any location, receiving random rewards (including but not limited to cultivation points, martial arts skills, elixirs, weapons, and special items). First-time visits to important locations in the Dream of the Red Chamber world (such as the main hall of the Rongguo Mansion, Xiaoxiang Pavilion, Hengwu Garden, etc.) will trigger a location-based first-time check-in, with rewards usually being more generous. Specific characters, events, and time points may also trigger special check-ins. More features will be gradually unlocked as the host progresses."

Simple and clear. Lin Chen's gaze swept over the two groaning figures on the ground, then over the dilapidated, leaky courtyard. In his memory, this was the dwelling Lai Da had "given" to him after his parents' deaths; it was separated from the stables by only a wall, freezing in winter and scorching in summer, infested with mosquitoes.

"Basic check-in."

"Ding! Check-in successful! Obtained: Low-grade Qi and Blood Pill x1 (Can slightly restore Qi and blood, strengthen the physique)."

A pill the size of a longan, dark red in color, appeared out of thin air in Lin Chen's hand, carrying a faint medicinal fragrance.

Lin Chen swallowed the Qi and Blood Pill without hesitation. The pill dissolved instantly in his stomach, transforming into a warm current that further nourished his body, which had just been strengthened by his cultivation, dispelling the lingering chill and weakness. Even the scratches on his skin scabbed over at a visible speed.

Excellent. Although it was only the lowest grade pill, it was a godsend for him at this moment.

He walked up to Zhang Ergou, who was still groaning, and knelt down. Zhang Ergou's face instantly filled with terror: "Master Lin...Master Lin, spare me! I was blind and didn't recognize your greatness! I offended you! It was Master Lai, Master Lai ordered it! It has nothing to do with me!"

"My monthly allowance, and the one that was previously withheld, will be returned in full by noon tomorrow. Also, find me a clean courtyard, at least the kind of place for a respectable guest." Lin Chen's voice was calm and even, yet carried an undeniable chill. "If you're short even a copper coin, or if I don't see a new place by tomorrow..." His gaze fell on Zhang Ergou's ribs, "Next time, it won't just be ribs that are broken."

"Yes, yes, yes! Definitely! Definitely! I'll get the new courtyard right away! Please, Master Lin, spare me!" Zhang Ergou endured the excruciating pain, kowtowing repeatedly.

Lin Chen stopped looking at him, stood up, stepped over Wang San, and walked towards the dilapidated, drafty hut. The hut was bare, containing only a creaking, broken bed and a table with a missing leg. He picked up the blurry bronze mirror on the table.

The person in the mirror appeared to be fourteen or fifteen years old. His face was pale from long-term malnutrition and illness, but his features already possessed the handsome features of the original "Lin Chen." Furthermore, due to the replacement of his soul and the initial nourishment of his cultivation, he had gained an indescribable sharpness and depth. His once dry, yellow hair, now damp with rain, appeared somewhat darker and shinier. Most strangely, his eyes were deep and dark, occasionally flashing with a faint, captivating light that even he himself didn't notice.

"Succubus Physique... Successfully bound..." The system notification sounded again. "Physique Effects: Slightly increases the user's Qi and Blood recovery speed, passively emits a faint alluring aura, slightly increasing the favorability of others, especially the opposite sex. By establishing emotional bonds with others, one can absorb pure energy to nourish the foundation of martial arts; the deeper the bond, the purer the energy, and the stronger the amplification. Specific effects increase as the physique grows."

Emotional bonds? Absorbing energy? Lin Chen raised an eyebrow slightly. This physique was different from his original pure martial arts path; it seemed to lead him down the path of "love"? However, since he was already here, he might as well make the best of it. In this unfamiliar world of high-level martial arts and the Red Chamber, having more support was never a bad thing.

He sat cross-legged on the tattered bed, ignoring the rain outside and the occasional groans of pain, and began to circulate the faint yet undeniably real internal energy within his body, following the mental techniques of the Stone-Splitting Fist, familiarizing himself with this newborn body and its power.

Tomorrow, he would go to the main hall of the Rongguo Mansion to sign in.

Then, he would take a good look at this familiar yet strange… high-martial-arts world of the Red Chamber.


Chapter 2: The Awakening of the Succubus Physique, the Unintentional Power of Seduction

The next morning, as dawn broke.

Lin Chen slowly opened his eyes from his meditation. A night of breathing exercises had allowed him to consolidate his early-stage ninth-rank cultivation. The faint but resilient internal energy within him flowed gently through his meridians like a stream, dispelling the last trace of chill and weakness. He moved his limbs, feeling light and strong, a stark contrast to his near-death state yesterday.

He stood up and picked up the blurry bronze mirror. The face reflected in the mirror caused even Lin Chen, who had weathered countless storms and whose will was as firm as a rock, to pause slightly in surprise.

The changes are more obvious than the fleeting glimpse of yesterday.

His skin, once excessively pale from prolonged illness, now possessed a warm, jade-like luster, like the finest mutton-fat jade shimmering with a faint glow in the morning light. His thin cheeks had become slightly fuller, the lines still clear, but the gaunt, sickly look had faded, giving him a refined and upright appearance. His eyebrows and eyes were still the same, but the arches seemed more defined, the eye sockets deeper, and his eyes were jet black, like ink jade immersed in a cold pool, clear yet unfathomable. Most remarkably, when he concentrated, a faint, elusive, almost eerie light seemed to flicker deep within his eyes, not intentional, yet effortlessly drawing the eye and making one's heart skip a beat.

The refinement of his features was elevated to a new level, creating a unique aura that straddled the line between youthful innocence and a certain gender-transcending, highly aggressive charm. He possessed a cool and aloof demeanor, yet subtly exuded an allure.

This is… the external manifestation of the Succubus Physique?

Lin Chen touched his face; it felt smooth, and the blood and energy beneath his skin were abundant and vigorous. He tried to focus his inner vision and could “see” that the wisp of internal energy in his dantian seemed to be tinged with a very faint, ambiguous pink halo, rippling slightly with his breath. On the system interface, under the entry for [Succubus Physique], it was simply described: “Early Awakening Stage, Appearance and Temperament Optimized, Passively Radiates a Weak Aura of Allure, Slightly Improves Blood and Energy Recovery. Energy Absorption Function Not Activated (Requires Establishing an Emotional Bond).”

“Can it be freely controlled?” Lin Chen's mind raced, and he tried to control it.

At first, there was some hesitation; the invisible aura emanating from him was like uncontrollable smoke. But he was, after all, a martial arts master, with control over his own qi and spirit far exceeding that of ordinary people. After several attempts, he found a certain "frequency," slowly restraining the overflowing, captivating allure inward. The person in the mirror's temperament changed accordingly, gradually settling from a dazzling, almost eerie beauty into a more restrained, cool, and composed youthful handsomeness. Although still outstanding, it no longer possessed such direct impact.

"Being able to control it is not bad," Lin Chen muttered to himself. In the deep confines of the Jia family mansion, being overly conspicuous was not a good thing, especially when one's strength was insufficient. This ability to restrain one's aura was the first practical skill brought about by his physique.

A feeling of hunger rose in his stomach. With his cultivation just beginning, and his body undergoing tempering and purification, he urgently needed replenishment. In his memory, the original owner's "food" was always the lowest among the servants, often being skimped on. He had intimidated Zhang Ergou yesterday, but the food had not yet arrived.

He picked up a worn-out wooden bucket from the corner of the house, pushed open the creaking, dilapidated door, and walked towards the well outside the courtyard. In the early morning, the back alleys of the Jia mansion were already bustling with servants and maids, sweeping the courtyard and carrying odds and ends.

When Lin Chen appeared on the path leading to the well with the empty bucket, the previously hushed sounds of conversation and work fell eerily silent for a moment.

Several pairs of eyes, some open, some hidden, fell upon him, filled with surprise, inquiry, and even a hint of barely perceptible admiration.

"Is that... that sickly Lin Chen from the west?"

"He looks like him... but why does he seem... different?"

"His complexion is much better, and his appearance is... strangely handsome?"

"Shh, keep your voice down! I heard that Zhang Ergou and Wang San went to cause him trouble yesterday, and they both had to be carried out in bed! Wang San broke his leg, and Zhang Ergou broke several ribs!"

"Really? Isn't he always sickly?"

"Who knows... Maybe he was hiding his abilities before? Or maybe he had some kind of stroke of luck? Anyway, he looks different; it's best not to provoke him."

Fragments of whispers drifted on the wind. Lin Chen seemed not to hear them, walking straight to the well, setting down the wooden bucket, and turning the turner. His movements were fluid, showing no trace of the weakness he usually displayed when carrying water.

Just then, a soft tinkling of jade pendants and light footsteps came from another path leading to the back room of Rongxi Hall. A maidservant, dressed in a light green vest and pale blue skirt, with beautiful features and a composed demeanor, carrying a food box, was walking quickly with her head down, as if in a hurry.

The two met unexpectedly on the path by the well.

The maidservant looked up, her gaze meeting Lin Chen's as he straightened up after drawing water.

In an instant, Xiren—the girl Lin Chen recognized from his memories, the one Jia Mu had given to Baoyu and who later became the head maid of the Grand View Garden—froze completely.

Her eyes widened slightly, and she nearly dropped the food box. The soft morning light illuminated the boy's profile, glistening with a few water droplets. His face, which had just lost its allure, appearing cool and clean, still possessed an indescribable attraction. Especially his eyes, dark and deep, calmly looking at her, yet they made Xiren's heart skip a beat, her cheeks flush slightly, and her breath catch in her throat for a moment.

She wasn't unfamiliar with handsome men; Baoyu was a sculpted, porcelain-like figure. But this boy before her… was different. It was a more serene, more… feeling, carrying an indescribable yet deeply moving power. Like a glimpse of moonlight in the cold night, cool yet alluring.

"Ding! Significant emotional fluctuations detected in the opposite sex 'Xiren' (Jia Baoyu's chief maid). Succubus physique passive effect triggered, initial connection established. Gained Xiren's favorability +10 (initial acquaintance). Energy absorption function slightly activated, obtaining a wisp of pure energy (weak), which has automatically nourished the martial arts foundation, and the internal energy circulation speed has slightly increased (lasting for one hour)."

The system notification sounded in Lin Chen's mind. At the same time, he clearly felt the wisp of internal energy in his dantian tremble slightly, as if it had been injected with an extremely subtle yet pure and gentle warm current, its circulation speed noticeably increased, and its nourishing effect on his body strengthened.

So that's how it is. This is "drawing energy through emotional bonds"? Just a chance encounter, a slight attraction from the other person, and it directly benefits one's cultivation? And this energy… seems exceptionally pure, far more efficient than slowly absorbing the sparse "qi" of heaven and earth.

Lin Chen understood, but his face remained impassive. He gave Xi Ren a slight nod as a greeting, then picked up the bucket and stepped aside to make way.

"Ah…" Xi Ren suddenly realized her lapse in composure, her face flushing even more. She quickly lowered her head, curtsied hastily, and her voice was slightly flustered: "Young Master Lin…" She didn't know how to address this almost unnoticed young master from a collateral branch, only instinctively using a respectful title. Then, carrying the food box, she practically fled, her heart still pounding like a drum.

Only after walking quite a distance did Xixi clutch her chest and stealthily glance back. The well was empty, only the soft creak of the pulley remained. She recalled the rumors she'd vaguely heard yesterday, that Lin Chen had suddenly become incredibly powerful, injuring Lai Da's wicked servants… and then she remembered the fleeting glimpse of his stunning face and bearing… A ripple of emotion stirred within her. This Young Master Lin seemed truly completely different from the sickly, easily bullied orphan of the rumors.

Lin Chen carried the water back to the dilapidated house. He poured the water into a vat, stood in the courtyard, and closed his eyes to sense it. The wisp of "pure energy" provided by Xi Ren hadn't been exhausted yet, still slowly nourishing his inner energy and body, its effect roughly equivalent to half an hour of meditation. Although weak, its advantage lay in its "automatic" and "pure" nature, requiring almost no refining.

"Succubus physique... the more favored, the more significant the increase in combat power." Lin Chen pondered the system's initial description. This energy generated by "favoritism" seemed to be the key. The world of *Dream of the Red Chamber* was never short of outstanding women and complex emotional entanglements. This physique unexpectedly suited this world.

However, relying on the emotions of others for cultivation is ultimately an unorthodox path. Lin Chen remained clear-headed about this. His own martial arts cultivation was fundamental. The energy provided by his physique was icing on the cake, but it shouldn't be the root of the problem.

He walked to a relatively flat open space in the courtyard and assumed a fighting stance. The key points of the Stone-Splitting Fist clearly appeared in his mind. This was the most basic fist technique, emphasizing a solid foundation and powerful, forceful strikes. It consisted of nine forms, corresponding to the needs of a ninth-rank martial artist to hone their strength and temper their muscles and bones.

Lin Chen didn't immediately execute it. Instead, he first simulated it with his mind, combining it with countless fist techniques from his previous life, breaking down the power generation techniques, breathing coordination, and internal energy circulation routes of each form of the Stone-Splitting Fist. Soon, he found several subtle points that could be optimized to better suit his current physical condition and power generation habits.

Then, he began to move.

He began with a stance, lowering his waist and adopting a horse posture, his breath sinking. A fist was slowly thrust out, seemingly deliberate, yet carrying a steady, powerful force. His inner energy surged with the force of the punch, concentrating at the tip of his fist.

Whoosh! The force of the punch stirred up a few specks of dust on the ground.

He began practicing, from the first move, "Stone Collapse," to the ninth, "Earth Shattering," his movements gradually becoming fluid and natural. He didn't pursue speed, but rather carefully felt the power transmission of each punch, the contraction and relaxation of his muscles, and the surging and flowing of his internal energy. His experience as a grandmaster from his previous life allowed him to precisely control every ounce of power in his body, maximizing the power of this basic fist technique to its current level.

The wind from his punches grew increasingly fierce, the sound of air being torn apart echoing in the quiet courtyard. He was enveloped in heat, his blood surging, his skin slightly flushed—a sign of vigorous blood and energy circulation. After practicing several times, he felt a sense of well-being throughout his body, his strength had increased, and his control over the initial stage of the ninth rank had become much more proficient.

"Stone Shattering Fist, emphasizing intent over form, its foundation lies in power flowing to the extremities, and strength penetrating outwards..." Lin Chen withdrew his fist and stood, his breath even and long, a thoughtful glint in his eyes. This fist technique, though rudimentary, was like a towering building rising from the ground, perfectly suited to refining the martial arts foundation of this body.

His ears twitched slightly; he heard deliberately soft footsteps and suppressed breathing outside the courtyard wall. More than one person.

A barely perceptible chill crept onto Lin Chen's lips. It seemed yesterday's events and his changes this morning had attracted the attention of some "interested individuals." Was it Lai Da? Or someone else?

He ignored them and turned to go back inside. His immediate priority was to improve his strength as quickly as possible. This afternoon, he should go to the main hall of the Rongguo Mansion to "sign in."

As for those spying in the shadows… as long as they didn't come looking for trouble, Lin Chen wouldn't bother with them for now. But if anyone was obstinate, he wouldn't mind using his newly acquired Stone-Splitting Fist to give them a proper "lecture."


Chapter 3: First Sign-in at the Rongguo Mansion Main Hall, Another Opportunity Arrives

Inside the dilapidated house, Lin Chen slowly finished his practice, concluding yet another round of the Stone-Splitting Fist. He was shirtless, a thin layer of sweat glistening in the morning light. The muscles beneath his skin, though not exaggerated, were already showing definition, brimming with restrained resilience. Overnight, his body had changed significantly; his blood and energy were abundant, making him a completely different person from yesterday.

He wiped away the sweat and changed into his only old, faded robe. The robe was still loose, hanging somewhat loosely on his body, but on his now upright figure, it exuded a refined, unconventional elegance. Looking at himself in the blurry bronze mirror, he once again suppressed the alluring aura that unconsciously emanated from him, making himself appear more like an ordinary, somewhat quiet, and aloof member of a collateral branch of the family.

"Time to sign in," Lin Chen murmured to himself.

Based on his memory and the system's prompts, the main hall of the Rongguo Mansion, "Rongxi Hall," was one of the core centers of power and fortune within the entire Jia family, so the first sign-in reward should be quite good. Moreover, he needed a plausible reason to "naturally" reveal some changes in front of everyone, paving the way for future actions.

Leaving the secluded west courtyard and passing through several corridors, the scenery became increasingly refined as he walked towards the center of the mansion, and the servants and maids became increasingly well-dressed. Occasionally, a servant would hurry by, and upon seeing Lin Chen, most would pause for a moment, a flicker of surprise in their eyes, but quickly lower their heads and hurried away. However, their scrutinizing gazes and the whispers behind them couldn't be concealed.

"Is that Lin Chen from the west courtyard?"

"He looks like... but why does he seem so energetic? And his appearance..."

"Shh, I heard Zhang Ergou and his gang suffered a huge loss yesterday, even Grandpa Lai's face turned blue!"

"Really? He's not a..."

"Say less, let's go, let's go."

Lin Chen kept his eyes straight ahead, his steps steady. His senses were keen; the whispers naturally didn't escape his ears, but he paid them no heed. The servants' attitude was merely a harbinger of a shifting tide. In this grand mansion, strength and demonstrated value were the true passports.

Rongxi Hall, located on the central road of the Rongguo Mansion, was the main hall for receiving distinguished guests, holding important family ceremonies and meetings, and possessed extraordinary grandeur. From afar, the five-bay hall, its roof covered with blue glazed tiles, gleamed in the sunlight. The vermilion pillars were so thick that two people needed to encircle them, adorned with carved beams and painted rafters, showcasing the magnificence of the Duke's mansion. A high platform stood before the hall, flanked by imposing stone lions, and beneath the eaves hung a large plaque inscribed with the late emperor's calligraphy—a solemn and dignified inscription of "Rongxi Hall" with nine dragons in red gold.

It was not a time for a grand court assembly or a banquet, and the main gate of Rongxi Hall was tightly closed, with only two well-dressed servants in blue robes standing guard in the corridor. Upon seeing Lin Chen approach, the two exchanged a glance, both seeing surprise in each other's eyes. One of them stepped forward, his tone hesitant yet more polite than he would to an ordinary servant: "Are you... Young Master Lin from the West Courtyard?"

"Indeed." Lin Chen stopped, his expression calm. "I've heard that the manor's library contains several volumes of notes by senior martial artists, which I greatly admire and wish to borrow for study. Would it be convenient for me today to enter and pay my respects to Master Zheng, or perhaps consult with the steward?"

This was a reason he had already devised. Although the original owner's parents were from a collateral branch of the family, his father was said to have been obsessed with martial arts, though his talent was mediocre, leaving behind only a few rudimentary notebooks. Using this as an excuse was perfectly reasonable. It could both test Jia Zheng's attitude towards his "martial arts" and pave the way for him to demonstrate his abilities later. More importantly, it provided a plausible pretext for him to approach Rongxi Hall and sign in.

The two servants exchanged glances. In the past, someone from a fallen collateral branch of the family, let alone wanting to enter the library, would likely have faced difficulties even trying to see the steward beside Master Jia. But yesterday's events had already begun to spread, and coupled with the young man's composed demeanor and clear gaze, completely unlike his usual timid self, they began to have doubts.

“Well… Master Zheng was summoned to the Prince of Bei Jing’s residence early this morning and isn’t here right now,” a servant said cautiously. “If Young Master Lin wishes to borrow books from the collection, he needs a token from the storeroom, and the stewardesses must keep a record. Perhaps you could wait a moment, and I’ll go and inform Steward Lai?”

Mentioning Steward Lai, the servant carefully observed Lin Chen’s expression. They had heard about yesterday’s events.

Lin Chen’s expression remained unchanged; he simply nodded slightly. “Thank you.”

His true intention wasn’t to borrow books immediately; he just wanted to be able to sign in near Rongxi Hall. His gaze swept over the tightly closed vermilion gate and the imperial plaque above it. This was it.

“System, sign in at Rongxi Hall.”

"Ding! Detected that the host has entered an important location within the Red Mansion for the first time [Rongguo Mansion Main Hall (Rongxi Hall)], triggering the first location check-in!"

"Check-in successful! Congratulations to the host for obtaining: Body Tempering Pill x1, Inner Breath Circulation Technique (Yellow-grade)."

Unlike the basic check-in, this time the system's notification sounded more solemn. At the same time, a stream of information flooded into the mind: a Yellow-grade low-grade inner breath cultivation technique called "Small Circulation Inner Breath Method," and a detailed description of the Body Tempering Pill's effects.

Body Tempering Pill: A high-grade mortal-grade pill, refined with several gentle and nourishing medicinal herbs. It can temper the body, nourish the meridians, remove hidden injuries, and strengthen the foundation. It significantly helps ninth-grade martial artists break through minor realms.

Small Circulation Inner Breathing Technique: A low-grade Yellow Rank cultivation method that guides inner breath to initially establish a simple "Small Circulation" within the body, accelerating the accumulation and circulation of inner breath and stabilizing the foundation. It is an introductory cultivation method one level higher than basic breathing exercises.

The rewards are good! Especially the inner breath cultivation method, which is exactly what he needs right now. The initial ninth-grade cultivation and the Stone-Splitting Fist infused by the system are more like a "gift," while this cultivation method is the "method," allowing him to cultivate and utilize this power more efficiently. The Body Tempering Pill is also a timely rain for breaking through realms.

Having obtained the items, Lin Chen lingered. Just then, the servant returned quickly, a perfectly timed smile on his face: "Young Master Lin, Steward Lai said that borrowing books must follow the rules of the manor. You may return first; once the tokens and records are completed, someone will inform you."

"Thank you," Lin Chen replied noncommittally, turning to leave. Lai Da's reaction was within his expectations. Having lost his subordinate yesterday, and now seeing him "enter the manor," the old servant likely harbored both hatred and resentment, and would probably delay and make things difficult for Lin Chen regarding the book borrowing. However, Lin Chen wasn't in a hurry.

Back in the dilapidated west courtyard, Lin Chen paused slightly before even entering. Beside the broken water vat in the courtyard sat a slightly worn food box. Faint footprints, not his, marked the ground beside it.

Someone had been here.

He opened the food box. Inside were two plates of fairly delicate pastries (osmanthus cake and jujube and yam cake), a bowl of warm rice porridge, and two refreshing side dishes. It was far better than the leftovers and often nonexistent meals the original owner used to receive. At the bottom of the food box, there was even a small paper packet containing several clean cotton cloths, presumably for wiping him down.

No name was left, but Lin Chen could almost guess who it was.

To deliver such a kind meal so quietly in the inner courtyard of the Jia mansion, knowing of his changed living conditions and potential lack of supplies, combined with their chance encounter that morning, it was most likely Xiren. This head maidservant of Baoyu was meticulous and thoughtful. Her kindness might stem from a fondness for his changed appearance and demeanor, curiosity about his sudden "toughness," or even a touch of pity for his "lonely" situation; whatever the reason, in this time and place, this sentiment was especially precious.

Lin Chen didn't stand on ceremony and ate the food cleanly on the stone bench in the courtyard. Martial artists naturally have large appetites, especially since he was currently training his body and needed a lot of nourishment. The food transformed into a warm current, subtly resonating with his internal energy.

After finishing his meal, he placed the food box in a corner of the room. Returning to the courtyard, he didn't immediately take the Body Tempering Pill. Instead, he sat cross-legged and carefully studied the *Small Circulation Internal Breathing Method* in his mind.

The method, though only a little over a thousand words long, clearly explained how to guide internal energy along the Ren and Du meridians and other major meridians to construct a simple yet stable cycle. Compared to basic breathing techniques that only vaguely accumulate "qi," this method already touched upon the principle of "qi circulation," and its efficiency was incomparable.

Lin Chen calmed his mind and, following the method's instructions, slowly mobilized the wisp of internal energy in his dantian. He stopped letting it wander aimlessly and instead tried to guide it, from the dantian down, past the perineum, up along the Du meridian, past the coccyx, the spine, and the jade pillow, to the Baihui point on the top of the head, then down along the Ren meridian, past the Tanzhong point, and back to the dantian.

Initially, it was quite sluggish, the internal energy weak, and the meridians slightly narrow and congested due to the original owner's weak physique and lack of true cultivation. However, Lin Chen's will was firm, his mental strength powerful, and his self-control meticulous. He was patient and calm, guiding, nurturing, and cleansing the energy again and again with his mind.

An hour later, the wisp of internal energy finally completed a complete, albeit small, but truly real, cycle!

"Buzz—"

A slight humming sound seemed to emanate from within his body. The instant the Small Heavenly Circuit was successfully established, Lin Chen clearly felt that his control over his internal energy had increased significantly! The "activity" of his internal energy had also noticeably increased, its self-circulation speed more than 30% faster than before! Moreover, with each cycle, wisps of sparse primordial energy drifting between heaven and earth were slowly drawn into his meridians, refined, and transformed for his own use. Although the speed was still slow, it was a qualitative leap from nothing to something!

"This is the benefit of the system's cultivation method." Lin Chen opened his eyes, a flash of brilliance in them. He felt his early ninth-grade cultivation level had completely stabilized, and even his internal energy had become slightly more profound.

It was time.

He took out the Body Tempering Pill. The pill was the size of a longan, pale gold in color, with fine cloud-like patterns on its surface, and emitted a faint herbal fragrance. Without hesitation, Lin Chen tilted his head back and swallowed it.

The pill didn't immediately dissolve upon entering his stomach, but instead sank into his dantian. Then, a gentle yet abundant medicinal power, like a stream thawing in early spring, slowly spread from his dantian to his limbs and bones. Wherever it passed, his meridians felt slightly warm, as if being soaked and widened by a gentle current. Some extremely subtle hidden injuries and blockages that even Lin Chen hadn't noticed before were gently washed away and smoothed by this medicinal power. His muscles and bones also felt a tingling sensation, a deeper level of nourishment and strengthening.

Lin Chen immediately activated the newly mastered *Small Circulation Inner Breathing Technique*, guiding his internal energy to accelerate circulation in conjunction with the diffusion of the medicinal power. This allowed him to more efficiently deliver the medicinal power to all parts of his body, while simultaneously purifying and strengthening his own internal energy within the circulation.

Time passed slowly. He was enveloped in heat, with even faint white steam rising from the top of his head. Beneath his skin, blood and energy surged like a tidal wave, making a soft, gurgling sound. His previously somewhat thin body seemed to have been infused with new vitality; his muscle lines became clearer and smoother, and his skin shone with an inner radiance.

After an unknown amount of time, the warm medicinal power within his body was gradually absorbed completely. Just as the last trace of medicinal power merged into his meridians—

"Pop!"

A very faint sound, like a bubble bursting, rang out from within his body. The internal energy in his dantian suddenly strengthened, its circulation speed increasing dramatically! The circulation path seemed to widen slightly, able to accommodate and circulate more internal energy. His bones and muscles resonated, his blood surged, and a power nearly twice as strong as before filled his limbs!

Mid-stage Ninth Rank, achieved!

Lin Chen stood up and casually threw a punch. The fist wind tore through the air, emitting a clear "swoosh," far more powerful than before. He felt his internal energy swell, his spirit invigorated, and his senses seemed sharper. Most importantly, the problems that had plagued his original body—weakness, illness, and a weak foundation—had been fundamentally improved under the combined effects of the Body Tempering Pill and the Small Heavenly Circuit Heart Sutra. Now, he could truly say he had taken his first step firmly in this world of high martial arts.

"Ding! Detected continued positive affinity with the opposite sex 'Xi Ren,' energy absorption function continues to be slightly active. Gained Xi Ren affinity +5 (curiosity/goodwill). Pure energy continues to nourish."

The system notification sounded at the opportune moment. Lin Chen could feel that the faint but continuous energy from Xi Ren was still slowly merging into his internal energy circulation. Although the effect was far less than that of the Body Tempering Pill and his own cultivation, it was superior in that it was long-lasting and "automatic."

He glanced at the sky; it was almost noon. Footsteps came from outside the courtyard, this time not deliberately quiet.

Zhang Ergou appeared at the courtyard gate, limping, his face still bruised, carrying a coarse cloth bundle in his hands. His eyes darted away, avoiding Lin Chen's gaze. Behind him followed an unfamiliar old woman.

“Master Lin…” Zhang Ergou’s voice trembled as he placed the bundle on the stone bench in the courtyard. “This is your… your monthly allowance, and the money that was… accidentally withheld from the past two months. It’s all here. Please check it.” A corner of the bundle opened, revealing several strings of copper coins and a few pieces of silver.

“This is Granny Liu, who manages the ‘Listening Bamboo Pavilion’ on the west side of the back building,” Zhang Ergou pointed to the woman behind him. “Manager Lai instructed that the courtyard will be yours, Master Lin’s, residence from now on. Granny Liu will take you there; everything has been mostly taken care of.”

The old woman, surnamed Liu, was about forty years old, dressed in clean blue cloth clothes. With a cautious yet respectful smile, she curtsied to Lin Chen: "This old servant, Liu, greets Young Master Lin. Tingzhu Pavilion has been prepared. Although it cannot compare to your proper courtyard, it is clean and tidy, and has everything you need. You may move in at any time."

Tingzhu Pavilion? Lin Chen searched his memory. It was a small, independent courtyard in the back courtyard area, originally intended for a guest or distant relative. It was indeed a hundred times better than this dilapidated hut next to the stables. Lai Da was rather "sensible" this time, or rather, he couldn't yet ascertain Lin Chen's background and chose to back down and observe.

"Thank you for your trouble." Lin Chen nodded slightly to Granny Liu, not even glancing at Zhang Ergou, picked up the bundle, weighed it in his hand—the weight was roughly right. "Lead the way."

"Yes, young master, this way please." Granny Liu quickly stepped aside to lead the way.

Lin Chen took one last look at the dilapidated house that held countless painful memories of the original owner, then turned and left without any further hesitation.

A new residence, a new beginning. He had officially entered the Jia family's world. And his path of martial arts, with this first important check-in at this location, had officially set sail.

Next, he should familiarize himself with Tingzhu Pavilion, and then plan his next check-in location. Xiaoxiang Pavilion… seemed like a good choice.


Chapter 4: First Encounter at Xiaoxiang Pavilion, Sign-in to Obtain Movement Technique

Tingzhu Pavilion was indeed far superior to the dilapidated hut next to the stables.

The independent courtyard, paved with blue bricks, had a clump of green bamboo standing tall in the corner even in winter, adding a touch of tranquility and elegance to the small space. The three main rooms, though not spacious, were bright and clean, complete with tables, chairs, and beds. The bedding was also new cotton, carrying the fresh scent of soap. There was also a small side room that could be used as a kitchen, with some newly delivered firewood piled in the corner.

Granny Liu led Lin Chen in, said a few polite words like "Just let me know if you need anything," and then bowed and withdrew, her attitude respectful and impeccable.

Lin Chen casually placed the bundle containing his monthly allowance on the table and looked around his new living quarters. The improved environment meant he could cultivate more quietly, and it also meant a subtle change in his "status" in the eyes of the servants of the Jia family. But this was not enough, far from enough.

He sat cross-legged on the newly made bed, once again circulating the "Small Heavenly Circulation Breathing Technique" to consolidate his mid-stage ninth-rank cultivation. His inner energy flowed joyfully through his slightly expanded meridians, the Small Heavenly Circulation stable and efficient, constantly absorbing the sparse primordial energy in the air and transforming it into his own power. The intent of the Rock-Splitting Fist flowed within his heart, resonating with his inner energy, awaiting only the right opportunity to unleash even greater power.

In the afternoon, just as he was preparing to further his training in the Stone-Splitting Fist, footsteps sounded outside the courtyard gate. It wasn't Granny Liu; the footsteps were slightly unsteady, carrying a deliberately affected tone.

"Is Young Master Lin here?" A middle-aged man, dressed in a respectable silk jacket, with a fair complexion and no beard, stood at the courtyard gate, a forced smile on his face. Lin Chen recognized him; this was another trusted confidant of Lai Da, often running errands and relaying messages for him, known as "Lai Wang'er."

"What is it?" Lin Chen walked into the courtyard, his tone indifferent.

Lai Wang'er sized up Lin Chen, a barely perceptible hint of surprise and jealousy flashing in his eyes. The young man before him was tall and upright, with a calm demeanor; there was no trace of his former timid and sickly appearance. No wonder he could injure Zhang Ergou and Wang San. He forced a smile and said, "Steward Lai instructed that there are many things to do in the manor right now, and everyone is short-handed. I heard that the young master's health has improved considerably, and it just so happens that the charcoal allowance for Xiaoxiang Pavilion needs to be delivered. The old woman who is usually in charge is on leave today, and she can't handle it for the time being. I thought that since the young master is free, why don't you go and deliver the charcoal to Miss Lin? It would also show our manor's consideration for relatives."

Sending him to deliver charcoal to Daiyu? Lin Chen sneered inwardly. This Lai Da, having suffered a loss yesterday, was now resorting to a softer approach. Delivering charcoal was originally the job of a lowly maid or servant, but to send a nominal "young master" to do it was ostensibly "helping," but in reality, it was a warning and humiliation, reminding him that even though he had moved into Tingzhu Pavilion, he was still essentially a dependent, a member of the collateral branch of the family, at the beck and call of others, no different from a servant. Incidentally, he probably also wanted to see if Lai Da would resist, or use this opportunity to embarrass himself in front of Daiyu.

"Xiaoxiang Pavilion..." Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. According to the system prompt, that was an important location in the Red Mansion, where the first sign-in could be triggered. He was just thinking about how to "naturally" approach those important locations when Lai Da "thoughtfully" delivered the opportunity. As for humiliation? What kind of temperament did Lin Chen possess? Why would he care about such petty tricks? Before absolute power, these lowly schemes were nothing more than the performance of a clown.

"Where is the charcoal?" Lin Chen asked directly, his face revealing neither joy nor anger.

Lai Wang'er was taken aback by his quick response. More sarcastic remarks he had prepared stuck in his throat, and he could only manage a dry laugh: "It's in the storeroom in the back courtyard. It's already been packaged, two baskets of silver frost charcoal. Young master, please follow me."

Following Lai Wang'er to the storeroom in the tenement, they found two bamboo baskets of high-quality silver frost charcoal, covered with oilcloth, and quite heavy. Lai Wang'er feigned offer to help, but Lin Chen waved him off. With his current mid-ninth-grade cultivation, the two baskets of charcoal were as light as a feather.

Watching Lin Chen easily lift the baskets and walk steadily towards the Grand View Garden, the fake smile on Lai Wang'er's face slowly vanished, replaced by a chilling expression. He spat and muttered, "What are you showing off for! Let's see how long you can be so smug!" He then hurriedly turned and went back to report to Lai Da.

West of the Jia Mansion, in the Grand View Garden.

Though it is winter, the garden still possesses a unique charm. Pavilions and towers are nestled behind sparse trees, the pond water glistens coolly, and a thin layer of frost covers the artificial rocks. Passing through the hanging flower gate, along the winding cobblestone path, and crossing the Qin Fang Pavilion Bridge, a tranquil bamboo grove comes into view. Hidden among the bamboo are several exquisite houses with white walls and blue tiles—this is the Xiao Xiang Pavilion.

The place was secluded and quiet, rarely frequented by strangers. A faint, cool fragrance permeated the air, resembling neither plum nor bamboo, pure and aloof. Lin Chen walked to the courtyard gate and saw a small plaque hanging above the lintel, bearing the three characters "Xiaoxiang Pavilion." The calligraphy was elegant, yet subtly exuded an unyielding sharpness.

Just as he was about to raise his hand to knock, the system notification sounded in his mind:

"Ding! Detected that the host has set foot in the important location of the Red Chamber, [Xiaoxiang Pavilion], triggering the first location check-in!"

"Check-in successful! Congratulations to the host for obtaining: Light and Agile Steps (Yellow Rank, Body Technique Manual)."

A stream of information flowed in; it was a basic body technique for lightness, agility, and nimble movement. Although only Yellow Rank, it perfectly compensated for Lin Chen's current weakness of only having fist techniques and lacking agility and variation. Light and Agile Steps emphasized the coordination of breath and footwork, with light and swift movements, especially suitable for use in complex terrain or close-quarters combat.

"An unexpected delight." Lin Chen was pleased. This sign-in system truly provided the necessary assistance based on his current needs. He composed himself, temporarily setting aside the movement technique he had just acquired, and lightly knocked twice on the door knocker.

A short while later, the door creaked open halfway. A bright-eyed maidservant in a light green jacket peeked out; it was Xueyan, Daiyu's personal maidservant from Suzhou. Seeing a handsome, unfamiliar young man standing outside, carrying two large baskets of charcoal, she paused, "Who are you?"

"Lin Chen from the West Courtyard, here on the orders of the steward to deliver this month's charcoal to Miss Lin," Lin Chen replied.

“Lin…Lin Chen?” Xueyan blinked, seemingly finding the name familiar but unable to place it. The Jia family had many collateral branches, and as a maidservant in the inner quarters, her memory of the almost invisible, sickly orphan in the west courtyard was vague. However, seeing the young man's composed demeanor, unlike an ordinary servant, she said, “Thank you for your trouble, please come in. The young lady is inside.”

Lin Chen carried a charcoal basket and entered the courtyard. The courtyard wasn't large, filled with lush bamboo. Despite the winter chill, it remained verdant, rustling softly in the wind, adding to its tranquility. A few slender plum trees in the corner were just beginning to unfurl their tiny buds. The curtains of the main room hung low, and faint coughs and the rustling of pages turning could be heard from within.

Xueyan led Lin Chen to place the charcoal basket in a sheltered spot under the eaves. Just as she was about to go in to announce their arrival, the curtain moved, and a young woman with exquisite features and a delicate face emerged. She wore a moon-white cotton-padded jacket embroidered with plum blossoms and a light blue vest over it. She carried a book in her hand, her face was somewhat pale, and her figure was slender, like a willow swaying in the breeze, yet her back was ramrod straight, exuding an air of aloofness and coldness.

It was Lin Daiyu.

She had been reading inside when she heard the commotion outside, which seemed to be with strangers, and came out to investigate. When her gaze fell upon the young man in the courtyard, Daiyu's breath hitched almost imperceptibly.

The young man before her was tall and slender, standing amidst the green bamboo and white snow, carrying a charcoal basket, yet he did not appear crude; instead, there was a strange sense of harmony about him. His face was handsome, his skin pale from long periods without sunlight, yet radiant and lustrous. His eyes were particularly deep and unfathomable, looking calmly at her like two bottomless, icy pools. Most strangely, Daiyu clearly sensed a very faint, indescribable aura surrounding the young man. That aura was untouched by worldly dust, pure and clean, yet deep within it lay a subtle, almost instinctive attraction, causing her mind, tense from the winter chill and years of pent-up emotions, to involuntarily relax slightly. The lingering, persistent unease and sorrow in her chest seemed to lessen somewhat.

This feeling was extremely subtle, fleeting yet undeniably real. Daiyu, with her sensitive and perceptive nature from a young age, sensed this atmosphere far more keenly than most. She couldn't help but glance at Lin Chen a few more times. This young man… seemed different from all the other men in the manor. Baoyu was lively and clingy, like a ball of fire; the other men were either pedantic, vulgar, or gave her an unsettling look. But this young man was cold and quiet, like the bamboo outside the Xiaoxiang Pavilion, yet possessing something more inexplicable and intriguing than bamboo itself.

“Miss, this is Young Master Lin Chen from the West Courtyard, here to deliver charcoal,” Xueyan said from the side.

“Thank you for your trouble.” Daiyu withdrew her gaze, nodded slightly, her voice clear and melodious, like jade striking stone, yet lacking her usual aloofness. She noticed that the young man's hands, carrying the charcoal, were as steady as a rock, his breathing even and long, clearly not as weak as he appeared. “Xueyan, prepare tea.”

“Yes.” Xueyan was somewhat surprised that her mistress would offer tea, and quickly agreed, turning to prepare it.

“No need for that,” Lin Chen said, his voice calm and gentle. “The charcoal has been delivered, so I won’t disturb your peace any longer.” He had already achieved his main purpose (signing in) and had no intention of lingering and attracting attention. Moreover, he keenly sensed a very faint flow of “qi” on Daiyu, different from that of ordinary young ladies. Though subtle and unclear, considering the background of this high-martial-arts world, this Miss Lin was probably not as simple as she appeared. This place was not suitable for prolonged exploration.

Just as he was about to take his leave—

“Ding! Detected that the important plot character 'Lin Daiyu' has developed significant curiosity and slight favorability towards the host. The passive effect of the Succubus Physique has been triggered. Gained Lin Daiyu's favorability +15 (curiosity). Gained a wisp of pure energy (purer than Xiren's), which has automatically nourished the martial arts foundation, slightly increased the speed of internal energy circulation, and increased the initial comprehension speed of 'Light and Agile Steps'.”

As the system notification sounded, an energy that was noticeably cooler, purer, and even carried a hint of ethereal beauty than the one he had previously obtained from Xiren quietly flowed into Lin Chen's dantian, blending seamlessly with his own internal energy. It not only accelerated the circulation of his internal energy but also instantly gave him a deeper understanding of the "Light and Agile Steps" manual he had just acquired, making many key points suddenly clear.

The energy provided by Daiyu, in both quality and quantity, far surpassed that of Xiren! And it seemed to also come with a bonus to the comprehension of specific martial arts? Was it because of her special status, or due to her inherent characteristics?

Lin Chen's mind raced, but he showed no emotion on his face. He simply nodded to Daiyu again and turned to leave.

"Wait a minute." Daiyu suddenly spoke, her voice still clear and cold, but less chilling. "You... also have the surname Lin?"

“Yes. Our ancestors are from the same clan as you, so we're distant relatives,” Lin Chen stopped and replied. This wasn't a lie. In the original owner's memories, his father and Daiyu's father, Lin Ruhai, were indeed related by a very distant clan. This was one of the reasons why the original owner was able to live in the Jia family mansion after her parents' deaths, although this relationship was so thin it was almost negligible.

“I see.” A hint of realization flashed in Daiyu's eyes, followed by a deeper complexity. Same surname, same dependence on others, same loneliness… This young man must have many unavoidable circumstances, right? Seeing him sent to deliver charcoal today, how could someone as intelligent as her not understand the implications? Besides the initial goodwill she felt due to his peculiar aura, a slight ripple of shared suffering also stirred within her. “He's cold today, so I'm sorry to trouble you to make this trip. Xueyan, fetch one of the ‘Clear and Moistening Pills’ I prepared the other day, and give it to this… Young Master Lin to moisten his throat and dispel the chill.”

“Miss, that medicine…” Xueyan hesitated. The Qingrun Pill was for the young lady herself, and the ingredients were rare.

“It’s alright, just fetch it,” Daiyu said calmly, her tone leaving no room for argument.

Xueyan had no choice but to go inside. She quickly retrieved a delicate little porcelain bottle, poured out a single, translucent, and refreshingly fragrant pill, placed it on a clean handkerchief, and handed it to Lin Chen.

Lin Chen pondered for a moment, then did not refuse. “Thank you for the medicine, Miss.” He took the pill; it was slightly cool to the touch, and the fragrance was intoxicating. Although he didn’t know its exact grade, it was definitely not an ordinary one. This Miss Lin is indeed extraordinary.

He didn't take it immediately, but carefully put it away and took his leave again.

This time, Daiyu didn't say anything more, only watching his tall figure disappear at the end of the bamboo path before slowly withdrawing her gaze. The book in her hand remained unturned for a long time, her mind still savoring the strange feeling she had just experienced, and the calm eyes of the young man.

"Lin Chen..." she murmured the name, a faint trace of confusion and inquiry, imperceptible even to herself, appearing on her pale face.

After leaving the Xiaoxiang Pavilion for a distance, Lin Chen found a secluded spot behind a rockery. With a thought, he began to try out the "Light and Agile Steps" he had just acquired.

He recalled the key points in his mind, circulating his inner energy along a specific route to the meridians of his legs, and his feet followed. At first, it was a little awkward, and his body swayed. But with the foundation of a grandmaster from his previous life, he had meticulous control over his body, and with the added insight from the pure energy provided by Daiyu, his steps gradually became smoother after a few tries.

He moved nimbly among the artificial rocks and grotesque stones, his toes lightly touching the protruding edges, and he floated several feet away like a falling leaf, landing silently. His movements were light and swift, a stark contrast to his previous steady steps. While far from the level of "walking on snow without leaving a trace," it was sufficient for daily travel, dodging, and even for use in conjunction with the Rock-Splitting Fist in mobile combat.

"Not bad," Lin Chen stopped, his breathing steady. This light and agile movement, combined with the Rock-Splitting Fist—one nimble, the other powerful—created a preliminary complementary relationship. Coupled with the recently acquired internal energy cultivation method, his martial arts foundation was rapidly solidifying.

He glanced back towards the Xiaoxiang Pavilion, where verdant bamboo groves created a tranquil, picturesque scene. That Miss Lin had not only given him a decent sign-in reward but also provided a rather special kind of energy. Moreover, her act of giving him medicine, though perhaps out of kindness stemming from shared suffering, had also drawn Lin Chen's attention to her.

"The Twelve Beauties of the Red Chamber... This world is indeed interesting." A profound light flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. Signing in, physique, high martial arts skills, and these "beauties" whose fates seemed altered yet remained shrouded in mystery...

He didn't linger. Utilizing his Lightness Steps, he swiftly traversed the garden paths, several times faster than he had arrived, returning to Tingzhu Pavilion.

Next, he needed to properly digest what he had gained today and prepare for any potential follow-up actions from Lai Da. However, with his Lightness Steps, his methods of response would be even more flexible.


Chapter 5 Punishing the Evil Servants, First Display of Martial Arts Power

The few days at Tingzhu Pavilion were a rare period of tranquility for Lin Chen since his transmigration.

With no one to disturb him, he devoted all his energy to martial arts cultivation. During the day, he practiced the Stone-Splitting Fist beside the clump of green bamboo in the courtyard, the wind from his punches whistling through the bamboo leaves. At night, he sat cross-legged on his couch, circulating his inner energy using the "Small Circulation Inner Breathing Method," using the pure energy from Daiyu as a guide to accelerate his inner breath circulation, consolidating his mid-stage ninth-rank cultivation and slowly progressing towards the late stage. In his spare time, he studied the "Light and Agile Steps," his figure flashing and leaping within the small courtyard, gradually becoming more proficient.

The effects of the Body Tempering Pill were completely absorbed, eradicating the long-standing ailments and hidden injuries within the original body. His bones and muscles were strong, and his blood and qi were abundant. Now, although he still wore that slightly oversized old robe, his posture was as upright as a pine or bamboo, his movements were steady and agile, and his eyes were bright and piercing. Occasionally, when he glanced around, a hint of his deliberately restrained allure would still inadvertently slip out, enough to make the maids who occasionally passed by the courtyard blush and whisper among themselves.

The changes were obvious. The old woman who delivered meals initially did so perfunctorily, but later the food gradually became more refined and punctual; the maids responsible for sweeping and cleaning would also make a point of keeping the courtyard exceptionally tidy. The servants, being the most well-informed, had already quietly spread the news in the back courtyard that the sickly young man in the west courtyard had suddenly become "tougher," had beaten up one of Steward Lai's men, had acquired a new courtyard, and had even received medicine from Miss Lin. Although Lai's faction still harbored resentment towards him, most of the servants no longer dared to easily neglect this "Young Master Lin."

This tranquility was broken on the morning of the fifth day.

Lin Chen had just finished practicing his Stone-Splitting Fist, his body steaming with heat, and was wiping the fine sweat from his brow with a cloth. The courtyard gate was pushed open rudely, and three figures barged in.

The leader was a burly man, dressed in a blue robe and small hat, with a menacing face. He was Zhao Hang, another trusted henchman of Lai Da, rumored to possess immense strength and known for his tyranny among the servants. Behind him were Zhang Ergou, whose ribs Lin Chen had broken a few days earlier, and a thin man with a shifty, weasel-like face, Qian Si, who was in charge of purchasing supplies for the kitchen.

Zhang Ergou, still aching from his ribs, looked at Lin Chen with fear and resentment, hiding behind Zhao Hang. Zhao Hang, however, stood boldly in the courtyard, his gaze sweeping over Lin Chen's sweat-drenched forehead and muscular physique. A flicker of fear crossed his eyes, but remembering Lai Da's orders and the promised benefits, his courage returned.

"Young Master Lin, what a fine mood you're in, practicing your martial arts so early in the morning." Zhao Hang grinned, revealing yellow teeth, but his tone wasn't exactly respectful. "We've come today to discuss something with you."

Lin Chen put down his cloth and looked at them calmly. "Speak."

Zhao Hang felt a little uncomfortable under his indifferent gaze and cleared his throat. "According to the rules of the manor, each household's monthly allowance, besides daily expenses, requires an additional 'tribute' at the end of the year to appease everyone and ensure a smooth year ahead. In previous years, you weren't well, and Steward Lai, being kind, waived it for you. Now that you're much better and can practice martial arts again, this rule naturally needs to be reinstated. It's not much; this month's allowance will cover half of what you owed last year, plus an advance for three months' 'tribute' next year, totaling twenty taels of silver. Deliver it to Steward Lai before noon today."

He held up two fingers and waved them, his tone leaving no room for argument.

Twenty taels of silver? Lin Chen sneered inwardly. The meager savings left by the original owner's parents had long been drained. Now, all he had was the few months' worth of allowance Zhang Ergou had returned a few days ago, after deductions—no more than five or six taels of loose silver and copper coins. Clearly, seeing that he seemed to have some "value" and had moved into a slightly better courtyard, they were using a different method to completely squeeze him dry, force him to submit, and perhaps even provoke him so they could have an excuse to suppress him again.

Seeing Lin Chen remain silent, Zhang Ergou assumed he was afraid. He poked his head out from behind Zhao Hang and chimed in with a shrill voice, "That's right! Lin Chen, don't think you're so great just because you can fight! This manor is a place where rules apply! Steward Lai is only letting you make up for the lack of filial piety by giving you this money; don't be ungrateful!"

Qian Si chimed in sarcastically, "Oh dear, Young Master Lin looks much better now, so he probably doesn't need this little bit of money. Perhaps it's because his parents are protecting him from the afterlife, accumulating good karma, that he's recovered. This filial piety money is just repaying the manor's kindness on behalf of his parents; it's only right."

Mentioning the original owner's parents! Lin Chen's previously calm eyes instantly turned cold. In the original owner's memories, that deceased, also unpopular collateral relative couple in the Jia family were the only people in this cold manor who had given him warmth, and also the deepest pain and obsession in his heart. Qian Si's words were extremely malicious; they weren't just after money, but also trying to destroy his spirit!

Zhao Hang, oblivious to the subtle change in Lin Chen's aura, became even more smug upon seeing that Qian Si's words seemed to have "hit the nail on the head." He stepped forward, closing in on Lin Chen, and threatened in a low voice, "Lin Chen, be smart. Hand over the money, and everyone's appearance will look good. If you don't... hmph, don't blame us for not showing any consideration for our shared household. Your parents were useless; they couldn't survive in this household and died young. Don't be like them, ignorant of the rules, and end up with nowhere to even settle down!"

"Considerations? Rules?" Lin Chen slowly raised his head, a cold smile curving his lips, but his eyes held no smile, only a chilling glint. "You dare mention my parents?"

Before he could finish speaking, Zhao Hang felt a blur before his eyes, and a gust of wind rushed towards him! He had no time to react; a hand with distinct knuckles, yet containing terrifying power, had already gripped his outstretched wrist like an iron clamp!

"Crack!"

A teeth-grinding sound of bones dislocating rang out! Zhao Hang let out a pig-like scream as his wrist twisted at an unnatural angle; the excruciating pain instantly shattered his will.

Lin Chen didn't stop moving. With a slight pull, Zhao Hang's massive body was involuntarily dragged forward, staggering. Lin Chen's other hand, fingers like a knife, moved with lightning speed, precisely striking an acupoint under Zhao Hang's ribs.

"Ugh!" Zhao Hang's scream abruptly stopped. Like a rooster being choked, his face instantly turned a deep liver color. All the strength seemed to have been drained from his body, and he was about to collapse limply.

But Lin Chen didn't give him a chance to fall. He released his grip, shifted his feet, and used the Light and Agile Step, his figure moving like a ghost to Zhao Hang's side. His right fist clenched, his inner energy surged, and the third move of the Rock-Splitting Fist, "Mountain-Splitting Force," slammed into Zhao Hang's side without any fancy moves!

"Bang!"

A heavy thud. Zhao Hang's massive body, weighing over two hundred pounds, was sent flying by this punch, crashing straight into the stone pillar in the yard where clothes were dried, like a tattered sack!

"Boom!"

The stone pillar shifted with the impact. Zhao Hang lay slumped beside it, blood gushing from his mouth and nose, curled up in a ball, barely breathing. He clearly had several broken ribs and his internal organs had been shaken.

It all happened too fast; from Lin Chen's attack to Zhao Hang's collapse, it was only a breath's time.

Zhang Ergou and Qian Si were completely stunned. Their smug grins froze on their faces, replaced by boundless terror. They only saw Lin Chen's figure blur, and then Zhao Hang screamed and flew away, his fate unknown.

"A ghost...a ghost!" Qian Si cried out, turning to run.

Lin Chen's eyes were icy cold. With a flick of his toe, a fist-sized pebble flew up from the ground, which he caught and casually tossed.

"Whoosh—thud!"

The pebble struck Qian Si precisely in the back of the knee. Qian Si cried out "Ouch!" and collapsed to the ground, clutching his leg and screaming in agony, unable to stand.

Zhang Ergou's legs went weak, and a sudden heat rose in his crotch—he had lost control of his bladder in terror. Tears streamed down his face as he watched Lin Chen approach, as if he were facing a demon. He knelt on the ground with a thud, kowtowing repeatedly: "Master Lin! Grandpa Lin, spare my life! Spare my life! It was Lai Da! Lai Da ordered us to come! It wasn't my fault! Qian Si's words were just his own nonsense! I'll never dare to do it again! Please, Master Lin, spare my dog's life!"

His forehead bled from the kowtowing; he was terrified beyond measure.

Lin Chen stopped in front of him, looking down at him. His voice was calm, yet carried a chilling coldness: "Go back and tell Lai Da that my monthly allowance, Lin Chen's, is exactly what it is. Not a copper coin more, not a penny less. As for filial piety? Let him come and talk to me personally."

He paused, his gaze sweeping over the slumped Zhao Hang and the wailing Qian Si: "And also, if I hear anyone mention my parents even half a word again, no matter who it is..."

His gaze shifted to the small, square stone table in the courtyard, used for holding tea sets. It was an original item from Tingzhu Pavilion, made of hard stone.

Lin Chen took a deep breath, his mid-stage ninth-rank internal energy surging forth, all concentrated in his right fist. A faint, almost invisible, earthen-yellow halo appeared on his fist—the outward manifestation of the power of the Stone-Splitting Fist reaching a certain level!

He exhaled and threw a punch! No unnecessary flourishes, only the purest, most ferocious power!

"Stone Shattering!"

"Boom!!!"

A deafening roar, ten times louder than when Zhao Hang slammed into the stone pillar, exploded! Stone chips flew everywhere, and dust filled the air.

When the dust settled, Zhang Ergou and the several rough-working women and servants who had rushed over and gathered outside the courtyard gate to peek all stared wide-eyed, mouths agape, frozen in place as if under a spell.

The heavy bluestone table, centered on the spot where Lin Chen's fist had struck, was riddled with cracks that spread out like a spiderweb, before finally shattering completely with a "crash," scattering into a pile of pebbles of varying sizes!

One punch shatters rock!

This was solid bluestone! Not bricks! An ordinary early-stage ninth-rank martial artist might be able to break a stone tablet or crack a brick, but to shatter such a thick stone table with such a clean and decisive punch would require a mid-stage ninth-rank martial artist, with their fist technique honed to a certain level and their power extremely concentrated!

This Young Master Lin… he had not only recovered his health, but he had truly possessed genuine and terrifying martial arts strength!

The courtyard was deathly silent. Only Qian Si's suppressed groans and Zhang Ergou's chattering teeth could be heard.

Lin Chen slowly withdrew his fist, his breathing slightly rapid, but quickly calmed down. He glanced at his fist; the skin was slightly red, but there was no damage. This punch, delivered in anger and with the intention of establishing his authority, had been quite effective.

He no longer looked at the three people sprawled on the ground, turned around, and calmly swept his gaze over the terrified servants outside the courtyard gate.

Meeting his gaze, the servants lowered their heads in panic, as if pricked by needles, not daring to breathe. Some of the more timid ones even collapsed to the ground, their legs giving way.

"Clean this up," Lin Chen said, then ignored them and walked straight back into the house, closing the door behind him.

Only after the door closed did the servants outside seem to regain their breath, but their eyes, still filled with horror, looked at the rubble and the three collapsed figures.

"Quick...quickly, go report to Sister Yuanyang!" a more quick-witted old woman said in a trembling voice.

"Then they..."

"First...first, move them aside! Wait for orders from above!"

The servants scrambled about, but dared not make a sound, carefully beginning to clean up the mess. Looking at the closed door, their eyes were filled with awe and fear.

Following this incident, news of Lin Chen "shattering a stone table with a single punch and severely injuring three of Lai Da's most wicked servants" spread like wildfire, ten times faster than before, throughout every corner of the Jia family's backyard.

Meanwhile, in Rongqing Hall, Grandmother Jia was reclining on a couch, her legs being gently massaged by Yuanyang with a small hammer. Another head maid, Amber, hurried in and whispered a few words in Yuanyang's ear.

Yuanyang paused slightly, a hint of surprise flashing across her beautiful face, before quickly regaining her composure. She said softly to Grandmother Jia, "Grandmother, there was a commotion just now at Tingzhu Pavilion in the back."

"Oh? Who's causing trouble again?" Grandmother Jia asked slowly, her eyes still closed.

“It was that young master Lin Chen from the West Courtyard who got into a fight with some of Steward Lai’s men,” Yuanyang said, choosing her words carefully. “It was quite a commotion.
