I heard… young master Lin smashed a stone table with one punch, and Zhao Hang, Zhang Ergou, Qian Si, and a few others were injured.”

Grandmother Jia suddenly opened her eyes, a glint of light flashing in them: “Lin Chen? The one Yu’er mentioned a few days ago… that distant relative from the same clan?”

“Yes. I heard he recovered from his illness a few days ago and even delivered charcoal to Xiaoxiang Pavilion for Miss.” Yuanyang nodded.

“Small enough to smash a stone table with one punch?” Grandmother Jia sat up straighter, an interest appearing on her face. “How old is he? Has he practiced martial arts?”

“Reporting to Grandmother, young master Lin should be fifteen this year. He used to be… weak and sickly, and I’ve never heard of him practicing martial arts. His father, however, seemed to have a fondness for it, but… his talent was ordinary.” Yuanyang recounted the information she had gathered.

"Fifteen...frail and sickly...suddenly recovered, and can even shatter stone with a single punch..." Grandmother Jia murmured, her fingers lightly tapping the edge of the couch. "That's interesting. Those under Lai Da's command are becoming increasingly unruly. However, this Lin boy has quite a temper, and he's strong too."

Yuanyang lowered her head, remaining silent. She knew the old lady had her own decision.

"Go and see what's going on. Give Lai Da a good talking-to, tell him to keep his men in line and stop them from causing trouble. As for Lin Chen..." Grandmother Jia's eyes gleamed, "Since he's a relative of Yu'er and has some skills, it's not right for him to always live in that remote place. See what kind of character he has. If he knows his place and understands the rules, we might keep an eye on him. In our household, we've had fewer and fewer capable people in martial arts these past few years."

"Yes, Grandmother, I understand," Yuanyang replied respectfully, but a chill ran through her. Grandmother's words clearly indicated she had designs on Lin Chen. It seemed this long-dormant orphan of the collateral branch in the West Courtyard was about to come into someone's sights.

Inside Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen sat cross-legged, listening to the gradually fading sounds of chaos outside the courtyard, and slowly exhaled a breath of stale air.

The goal of establishing his authority had been achieved. After this incident, at least on the surface, Lai Da's side wouldn't dare resort to such underhanded tactics again in the short term. Grandmother Jia's attention was also expected; whether it would be a blessing or a curse remained to be seen.

But more importantly, he clearly felt that in the instant he unleashed that stone-shattering punch, shocking everyone, his inner energy seemed to have become more vibrant and refined, and his understanding of the Stone-Shattering Fist had deepened. Martial arts cultivation indeed requires honing through actual combat and imposing presence.

He opened his palm, palm facing upwards, a faint but resilient wisp of inner energy pulsating from his fingertips.

"Strength is fundamental," he murmured to himself. "The Jia family… Jinling… this world of high-martial arts in the Red Chamber, I've only just begun."

Outside the window, the thin winter sunlight filtered through the paper windowpane, casting soft shadows on his handsome, serene profile.


Chapter 6 Baochai's Visit, Cold Fragrance Meets Allure

Three days had passed since the incident of establishing authority, and the inner quarters of the Jia family seemed to have returned to their former tranquility. However, beneath the surface, the undercurrents had subtly shifted.

Lai Da's faction had completely fallen silent, and no one dared to cause trouble at Tingzhu Pavilion anymore. Even the other distant relatives in the west courtyard, who were similarly neglected, found their lives somewhat easier. When servants saw Lin Chen, they would bow their heads and give way from afar, respectfully addressing him as "Young Master Lin," their eyes filled with not only fear but also a primal awe of a "martial artist." The statement made by Grandmother Jia that day, subtly hinted at by Yuanyang, further confirmed that this orphaned son of a distant relative was no longer the same as before.

Lin Chen was happy to have some peace and quiet, immersing himself entirely in cultivation. He had mastered the nine forms of the Rock-Splitting Fist, his power generation techniques becoming second nature. Combined with the "Small Circulation Inner Breathing Method," his inner energy was growing increasingly profound, and he was only a hair's breadth away from reaching the late stage of the ninth rank. His "Light and Agile Steps" were also progressing rapidly. With the aid of the special energy given to him by Daiyu, he could now walk silently, turning and twisting like the wind. His figure flickered in the small courtyard, leaving only afterimages.

Early that morning, after the snow had cleared, sunlight filtered through the thin clouds, casting a soft golden glow on the glazed tiles still covered in snow. In the Jia family garden, red plum blossoms bloomed, pines and cypresses stood evergreen, and the snow on the artificial rocks remained unmelted, resembling a jade sculpture.

Lin Chen, dressed in a thin blue robe, arrived alone at a relatively secluded clearing in the northeast corner of the garden, nestled among pine trees. The rugged rocks and undulating terrain here were perfect for further honing his light-footed movement skills in complex terrain. He took a deep breath of the crisp, cool air, circulating his inner energy to dispel the chill, and with a swift movement, he rose into the air like a wisp of blue cloud.

Stepping on protruding rocks, he used the momentum to turn and leap, brushing past snow-covered branches, startling down a flurry of snowflakes. He darted and weaved among jagged rocks and gnarled ancient trees, his movements agile and graceful, strangely blending into the tranquil snowscape around him, yet carrying a sense of power poised to be unleashed. Each step was as light as a feather, each exertion precise and refined, perfectly demonstrating the essence of the "light, skillful, swift, and stable" light footwork.

Just as he reached the pinnacle of his practice, he spun down from the top of a towering artificial rock, his clothes fluttering. Just as he was about to land, his ears twitched, keenly catching the faint footsteps coming from the other end of the pine forest path, and… a very delicate, cool fragrance.

The fragrance was neither perfumed nor powdery, like the first bloom of a cold plum blossom, or a secluded orchid in a valley, clear and refreshing, as if it could cleanse the dust of the world and clear the mind. Unlike the cool fragrance of bamboo and plum blossoms in Xiaoxiang Pavilion, this fragrance was more dignified and aloof, as if carrying an invisible, warm yet insurmountable barrier.

Lin Chen's mind raced. His body subtly twisted in mid-air, his light steps deftly changing direction, dissipating the momentum of his descent. Like a falling leaf, he silently drifted down onto a smooth, snow-covered rock beside the path, landing steadily. His breathing was even and long, as if he had been standing there admiring the snow all along.

Almost at the same moment he landed, two people emerged from around the bend in the path.

Leading the group was a young girl, about thirteen or fourteen years old, dressed in a honey-colored cotton-padded jacket, a rose-purple two-tone gold and silver fur-lined vest, and a pale yellow silk cotton skirt. All her attire was neither new nor old, appearing neither extravagant nor lacking in refinement, yet exuding a perfect balance of elegance and poise. Her hair was styled in a glossy black bun, adorned only with a simple pearl hairpin. Her skin was full and smooth, her face like a silver basin, her eyes like water apricots, her lips naturally red, her eyebrows naturally dark. Her features were beautiful, and her demeanor graceful. Her entire being possessed an impeccable, dignified composure, cultivated through careful upbringing, like fine jade, warm and lustrous, yet also revealing an inherent coolness and self-restraint.

This was none other than Xue Baochai, the young lady of the Xue family, who was staying at the Jia mansion with her mother and brother.

Beside her was a clever-looking maidservant in a silver-red jacket, Ying'er, her personal attendant.

Baochai had accompanied her mother, Aunt Xue, to Rongqing Hall to pay respects to Grandmother Jia. However, Aunt Xue and the Wang sisters engaged in much conversation, so she excused herself after a short while, intending to visit Tanchun, who had recently caught a cold. Passing through the garden, she smelled the refreshing fragrance of plum blossoms and, with Ying'er by her side, detoured to pick a few sprigs of red plum to bring to Tanchun for enjoyment and relaxation.

Unexpectedly, just as they rounded the pine grove, they saw a figure descend from the artificial hill and stand atop a snow-covered rock.

Baochai paused slightly, her gaze falling on the young man ahead.

The young man, dressed in a slightly worn blue robe, stood tall and straight like a bamboo shoot amidst pristine white snow and verdant pines. He seemed to have just been practicing some kind of movement technique; his aura lingered, and a subtle, almost imperceptible, "field" surrounded him. Sunlight filtered through the pine needles, casting dappled patterns that clearly defined his profile. His skin, a pale, almost unseen white, was radiant and healthy-looking, without any sign of illness. Most striking were his eyes, calmly gazing at her, deep and still as an ancient, undisturbed pool.

However, in that instant of eye contact, Baochai's usually tranquil heart was stirred as if a tiny pebble had been thrown into it, creating barely perceptible ripples.

A strange feeling swept through her.

From a young age, she was calm and composed, meticulous and adaptable, rarely showing her emotions. Furthermore, due to an innate "heat toxin" within her, she constantly needed to take "Cold Fragrance Pills" to suppress it, making her more composed and self-controlled than most. Ordinary men rarely caught her eye. Even Baoyu, though good, was, in her eyes, nothing more than a "stubborn rock," requiring constant guidance. But this young man before her…

He seemed to emanate an extremely faint aura, completely different from the icy landscape. That aura was understated, even somewhat restrained, yet possessed an indescribable, almost instinctive attraction. Cool, yet harboring a deep heat; serene, yet containing a captivating power. It subtly resonated with the "heat" within her, which she constantly suppressed with the Cold Fragrance Pills, creating a connection that even she herself was unaware of…

Especially the young man's gaze, clear and calm, lacked the usual astonishment or scrutiny a man might have upon first seeing her. He simply looked at her with a calm expression, yet it seemed to penetrate her layers of dignified exterior, reaching a corner of her heart that she herself had never explored. This caused Baochai, who always prided herself on "concealing her ignorance and humility," to momentarily lose her composure, her breath skipping a barely perceptible beat.

She immediately composed herself, focusing her gaze and suppressing the strange feeling within. Her face remained as gentle and dignified as ever, and she gave a slight nod in greeting. However, several thoughts raced through her mind: Who is this young man? Judging from his attire, he doesn't seem to be the master, but his bearing is extraordinary, and that movement just now… it seems to be martial arts? When did such a person appear in the Jia family?

"Ding! Detected that the important plot character 'Xue Baochai' has developed a subtle curiosity and emotional connection with the host. The passive effect of the Succubus Physique has been triggered. Gained Xue Baochai's favorability +10 (a mixture of aloofness and curiosity). Gained a wisp of pure energy (mild and mellow in nature, balanced and peaceful), which has automatically nourished the martial arts foundation, slightly improving the refinement and stability of internal energy."

A system notification sounded in Lin Chen's mind. Simultaneously, an energy completely different from Daiyu's cool elegance and Xiren's gentle refinement slowly flowed into his dantian. This energy was more mellow, peaceful, and balanced, like fine aged wine, nourishing silently and rapidly merging into his internal energy. It not only increased the total amount but also made the flow of his internal energy more stable and solid, as if his foundation had been invisibly strengthened.

The energy provided by Baochai actually leaned towards "stabilizing the foundation"? Was it because of her calm temperament, or related to her cultivation technique (if any) or physique? Lin Chen pondered inwardly, but his expression remained unchanged. He nodded slightly in return to Baochai, without taking the initiative to speak. At this moment, he had largely suppressed his alluring aura, his demeanor leaning more towards cold silence.

"Who is this…" Ying'er, standing behind Baochai, looked at Lin Chen curiously and asked in a low voice.

Lin Chen was about to speak when the system notification sounded in his mind again:

"Host detected as being in the Jia Mansion Garden (important scenic area). Perform today's basic check-in?"

"Check-in."

"Ding! Successfully checked in at the Jia Mansion Garden! Obtained: Defensive Jade Pendant (High-grade Mortal Rank, automatically activates, can withstand one full-force attack from a ninth-rank martial artist, or weaken part of an eighth-rank martial artist's attack. It requires three days to recharge after its energy is depleted before it can be used again)."

A warm, lustrous cyan jade pendant, carved with simple cloud patterns, quietly appeared in Lin Chen's sleeve. He instantly knew how to use it and carefully stored it close to his body. Although the pendant's grade wasn't high, it might come in handy at a crucial moment.

“My name is Lin Chen, a member of the collateral branch of the West Courtyard. I was practicing my footwork here just now, and I apologize for disturbing you, young lady.” Lin Chen finally spoke, his voice calm and gentle.

“Greetings, Young Master Lin.” Baochai’s eyes flickered slightly. So this was Lin Chen, the one who had been the subject of much gossip in the manor lately. A single punch shattering a stone, alarming the old lady… it wasn’t just hearsay. Judging from his calm demeanor and clear eyes, he didn’t seem like a reckless or fierce person. “It was my maid and I who were passing by and disturbed your peaceful practice, young master. Your movements just now were quite exquisite; were you practicing martial arts?”

Her tone was gentle, carrying just the right amount of curiosity and distance—neither impolite nor overly familiar.

"A smattering of knowledge, just for health and fitness," Lin Chen replied simply, not wanting to elaborate.

Seeing his lack of interest in deeper conversation, Baochai stopped there, smiling slightly, "Martial arts training can certainly improve health; that's a good thing. Please, sir, make yourself at home." With that, she gestured to Ying'er and prepared to detour to pick a red plum blossom not far away.

The two passed each other.

The cool, ethereal fragrance emanating from Baochai (the scent of the Cold Fragrance Pill) grew increasingly clear, briefly mingling with the faint, yet still lingering, alluring aura emanating from Lin Chen. The cool fragrance seemed to isolate everything, yet it was ultimately subtly permeated by that almost imperceptible attraction, leaving a faint trace in Baochai's heart, a trace perhaps even imperceptible to her.

Lin Chen could sense that, in that fleeting moment, Baochai's heartbeat seemed to quicken, though her expression remained unchanged. The balanced and peaceful energy emanating from Baochai also seemed to fluctuate slightly.

Only when the figures of mistress and servant disappeared behind the plum trees and the cool fragrance gradually faded did Lin Chen withdraw his gaze.

Xue Baochai… the refined lady of the Red Chamber, renowned for her "virtue of stillness," seemed to possess a unique depth in this world of high martial arts. The qualities of the energy she provided gave him a new understanding of the operation of the *Small Circulation Inner Breathing Method*, making his inner breath seem longer and more profound.

"An interesting girl," Lin Chen murmured to himself, a thoughtful glint in his eyes. However, his primary concern right now was his own cultivation. This chance encounter with Baochai was an unexpected bonus, but improving his strength remained his top priority.

He didn't linger. His figure flashed again, and with a light, agile step, he swiftly left the pine forest like a wisp of smoke, returning to Tingzhu Pavilion.

Under the plum tree, Baochai held a freshly broken, vibrant red plum blossom, but didn't leave immediately. She gazed in the direction Lin Chen had disappeared, her eyes thoughtful.

“Miss, that Young Master Lin… he seems to be the one who beat up Steward Lai’s men a few days ago, and even alarmed the Old Madam,” Ying’er whispered. “He’s handsome, and has a good bearing; he’s not as fierce as the rumors say.”

“Seeing is believing, hearing is deceiving,” Baochai said calmly, handing the plum blossoms to Ying’er. “Take only three-tenths of what the servants say. However…” She paused, the image of the young man standing calmly and aloof on the snow-covered stone flashing before her eyes again, along with the strange aura that stirred her heart.

“This Young Master Lin is indeed… somewhat different from the others in the manor.” She finally said this, her tone calm and even, as if stating an objective fact. But her fingertips unconsciously traced the cold plum blossom branch.

“Let’s go see Third Sister.” She composed herself, regaining her usual dignified composure, as if the fleeting flutter of emotion from just moments before had never occurred.

However, amidst the cool fragrance of the red plum blossoms, there seemed to be a faint, indescribable scent emanating from the pine forest and snow-covered rocks, lingering persistently.

In the Listening Bamboo Pavilion, Lin Chen sat cross-legged, holding the newly acquired defensive jade pendant in his palm. The pendant was warm to the touch, with a very faint flow of energy within.

“System, bring up the current panel.”

A semi-transparent screen, visible only to him, unfolded before his eyes:

[Host: Lin Chen]

[Cultivation: Mid-Ninth Rank (Stable, Approaching Late Stage)]

[Cultivation Technique: Small Circulation Inner Breath Technique (Low-Grade Yellow Rank, Beginner)]

[Martial Skills: Stone-Splitting Fist (High-Grade Mortal Rank, Proficient), Light and Agile Step (Low-Grade Yellow Rank, Proficient)]

[Physique: Succubus Physique (Early Awakening, Progress 5%)]

[Items: Body Tempering Pill (used), Low-grade Qi and Blood Pill (remaining 0), Defense Jade Pendant (Mortal Rank High-grade, fully charged), Cleansing Pill (gift from Lin Daiyu)...]

[Energy Absorption: Xiren (Slight, continuous), Lin Daiyu (Slight, continuous, with Spiritual Enlightenment bonus), Xue Baochai (Slight, continuous, with Stabilization bonus)]

[Special: Romantic Bond (Not yet activated)]

"With Baochai's energy absorption, the effects are different." Lin Chen examined the panel. "His physique progress seems to have slightly improved due to the interaction. It seems that interacting with these important figures is itself a way to promote physique awakening and gain benefits."

He put away the panel and gripped the jade pendant tightly. Baochai's appearance reminded him that the Jia family was full of hidden talents, and the water was deeper than he had imagined. The Xue family were imperial merchants, incredibly wealthy, and in this high-martial-arts world, they probably controlled extraordinary resources. This brief encounter with Baochai might bring unexpected consequences.

But regardless, personal strength is the fundamental way to cope with all changes.

"Continue cultivating." Lin Chen cleared his mind of distractions and once again entered a state of complete absorption in his cultivation. With his inner energy becoming increasingly stable, he continued to steadily advance towards the late stage of the ninth rank.

Outside the window, the sun shone brightly, and the accumulated snow gradually melted. Beneath the tranquility of the Jia mansion that day, new ripples had begun to spread.


Chapter 7 System Mission: Protect Daiyu and Overcome Difficulties

The peaceful cultivation in Tingzhu Pavilion was shattered in the afternoon by a sudden, serious voice in his mind, quite different from the usual notification sounds.

"Ding! Detected that the host has begun to integrate into the world of *Dream of the Red Chamber*, with steady improvement in strength. First main quest issued: Protection."

"Quest Content: Protect the important plot character 'Lin Daiyu' once, successfully repelling the malicious harassment from the wicked servants around Aunt Zhao, ensuring Daiyu's physical and mental well-being."

"Quest Time Limit: Within one hour."

"Quest Reward: Qi and Blood Pill x2, Inner Breath Amplification Card (1 hour)."

"Failure Penalty: Randomly deduct the proficiency of one mastered martial art, or lose some cultivation."

"Quest Hint: The wicked servants' actions have begun, target: Xiaoxiang Pavilion."

Main quest? Lin Chen's eyes snapped open, a glint of light flashing within them. The system had finally issued a clearly targeted quest, directly linked to Daiyu. Aunt Zhao? That petty, despicable, and troublesome concubine of Jia Zheng in the original novel? In this high-martial-arts world, her wicked servant was probably more than just an ordinary old woman.

A guardian quest… with a tight deadline. Lin Chen didn't hesitate, rising to his feet. He already had a good impression of Daiyu, and the pure energy she provided was quite beneficial to his cultivation; both morally and logically, he couldn't stand idly by. Besides, there were quest rewards.

He flashed into the courtyard, unleashing his full power with his Lightness Step, his entire body like a wisp of green smoke, speeding towards the Grand View Garden. After breaking through to the mid-ninth rank, his inner energy was even more profound, easily supporting the Lightness Step for long-distance travel. He deliberately chose secluded paths, avoiding crowded and inconspicuous places, moving with incredible speed.

In his mind, the system simultaneously displayed a simplified map of the Jia family's inner quarters. A red dot (representing a malicious target) was moving from the southeast courtyard where Aunt Zhao resided towards Xiaoxiang Pavilion at a considerable speed. Another faint green dot remained inside Xiaoxiang Pavilion.

"I'll make it." Lin Chen estimated the distance, then further increased his speed with his inner energy.

Xiaoxiang Pavilion, surrounded by verdant bamboo, remained serene. But at this moment, this tranquility was shattered by a loud commotion and the sound of shattering porcelain.

Inside the courtyard, Xueyan stood with her arms outstretched, blocking the main doorway. Her face was flushed with anger, tears welling in her eyes. She glared at the three fierce-looking maids before her and shouted, "You...you have no nerve! How dare you trespass into the young lady's room and break her medicine and poetry collection! I'm going to tell the old lady and Second Mistress Lian!"

The three maids were all tall and burly. The leader was a middle-aged woman with slanted eyebrows and triangular eyes, surnamed He. She was a confidante of Aunt Zhao, brought from her maiden home. Relying on Aunt Zhao's occasional favor and the fact that she had given birth to Tan Chun and Jia Huan, she was quite arrogant in the household. Behind her were two old women with menacing faces and unfriendly eyes.

Granny He, holding a feather duster, tapped her palm listlessly, a forced smile on her face. "Miss Xueyan, you've completely wronged us. We were sent by Auntie to deliver the newly acquired 'top-grade bird's nest' to Miss Lin, to nourish her body. But this threshold is too high, and my eyesight isn't good. I tripped and accidentally knocked the things off Miss Lin's table. Oh dear, what a sin, what a sin!" She spoke of sin, but her face showed no remorse. Instead, she kicked the broken medicine bowl and the stained manuscript on the ground.

The bowl contained Daiyu's daily medicine, now spilled everywhere, the strong smell of medicine filling the air. The manuscript, recently written by Daiyu, was still wet with ink, stained with medicine and filth, a complete mess.

"You...you all did this on purpose!" Xueyan trembled with anger. This old woman He had barged in like a madwoman; it was no accident!

"Xueyan, that's enough." A clear, slightly trembling voice came from inside the room. Daiyu lifted the curtain and stepped out. Her face was paler than usual, her lips tightly pressed together. Her slender body, wrapped in a silver-fur coat, still looked heartbreakingly thin. She didn't even glance at the mess on the ground, only coldly staring at Granny He and the other two, her eyes like ice, carrying an unassailable aloofness and suppressed anger. "Now that the things have been delivered, you can all go back. Please 'thank' Auntie for her kindness on my behalf."

The calmer and more composed she appeared, the more disrespected Granny He felt, and the resentment she harbored due to Aunt Zhao's jealousy of Daiyu's favor with Grandmother Jia intensified. She rolled her triangular eyes and feigned a step forward: "Oh dear, Miss Lin, you don't look well. It must be because the room isn't warm enough, or the servants aren't attending to you properly. Our mistress dotes on you, and specifically instructed me to check carefully to see if any ignorant servants in Xiaoxiang Pavilion have neglected you. I'll report this to our mistress, and she'll punish them severely!" As she spoke, she reached out to grab Daiyu's arm, actually intending to shove her away to further humiliate her, or perhaps to "inspect" the room to find something else to blame.

"Insolence!" Xueyan shrieked, trying to stop them, but was rudely shoved aside by another old woman, nearly falling.

Daiyu's eyes flashed coldly, and she instinctively took a half-step back, avoiding the old woman's hand. She was weak and untrained in martial arts; facing these three clearly prepared and strong servants, she had no chance in a physical confrontation. A surge of immense humiliation and powerlessness welled up within her, along with a deep-seated sorrow. Living under someone else's roof, even these lowly servants dared to bully her like this?

Just as the old woman's hand was about to touch Daiyu's sleeve, and a smug, malicious smile appeared on her face—

"Whoosh!"

A faint sound of something cutting through the air rang out! Immediately afterward, the old woman let out a piercing scream, as if stung by a scorpion, jerking back her arm. A deep, red, swollen finger mark, bleeding profusely, was visible on the back of her hand—she had been struck by a small pebble that had shot from nowhere!

"Who? Who's there!" Granny He cried out in pain and anger, clutching her hand. She and the other two old women looked in surprise and uncertainty toward the direction from which the pebbles had come—the courtyard gate.

A figure in blue had silently appeared inside the doorway. Sunlight streamed in from behind, casting long shadows on the snow. The newcomer had handsome features and a calm expression, but his eyes were deep and cold. His gaze swept over the mess in the courtyard and the pale-faced Daiyu, finally settling on Granny He and the other two, as if they were three buzzing flies.

“Lin…Young Master Lin?” Xueyan exclaimed in surprise.

Daiyu looked at the suddenly appearing Lin Chen, and her icy, tense heartstrings inexplicably relaxed slightly. The helplessness and fear she had felt just moments before seemed to have been somewhat dispelled by this sudden yet solid figure. It was him…he had appeared once again when she needed him.

"Who are you? How dare you interfere in Aunt Zhao's affairs!" Granny He shouted, her voice trembling with fear, but the sharp pain in her hand and the sudden, silent movement of the other man made her heart pound. She recognized the young man; Lin Chen, the rising star of the West Courtyard, was rumored to possess some martial arts skills. But relying on her status as Aunt Zhao's person and her "legitimate" reason, she still tried to put on a brave face.

"Get out," Lin Chen said, his voice low but carrying an undeniable chill. He didn't even offer an explanation or reprimand; those three simple words struck Granny He like a hammer blow to the heart.

"You…you're just a distant relative, yet you dare to yell at us? We're acting on Aunt Zhao's orders…" another woman shouted, mustering her courage.

Before she finished speaking, everyone saw a blur, and Lin Chen's figure vanished from the doorway. The next instant, he appeared like a ghost before the shouting woman, his speed far beyond human comprehension!

The old woman hadn't even seen the movement clearly when she felt a supple yet overwhelming force surge through her chest. She was sent flying backward uncontrollably, crashing into the courtyard wall with a thud. She slumped to the ground, unable to get up for a moment. Lin Chen had used a skillful technique, not intending to kill, but enough to temporarily incapacitate her.

The old woman and the remaining old woman were terrified, finally truly feeling fear. This young man's actions were so decisive and ruthless!

"You...you dare to hit someone! This is outrageous! This is outrageous!" the old woman screamed, retreating hastily.

"Hit someone?" Lin Chen approached step by step, his pace slow but carrying a heavy sense of oppression. "Break into the young lady's boudoir, smash property, intending to disrespect your mistress. According to the rules of the manor, death is the only punishment. I am only giving her a small punishment today, which is already merciful."

With each word he spoke, Granny He's face paled further. She then remembered that although she relied on Aunt Zhao, this Miss Lin was ultimately the old lady's darling and, more importantly, her rightful mistress. If things escalated today, even Aunt Zhao might not be able to completely protect them.

"Get out of here!" Lin Chen stopped, his gaze piercing Granny He like an icy spike.

Granny He trembled, her last shred of courage vanishing. She dared not linger any longer, ignoring the dazed woman on the ground, and scrambled towards the courtyard gate. The remaining able-bodied woman hurriedly helped her companion up and chased after her in a disheveled state, leaving behind a mess and a panicked silhouette.

Silence returned to the courtyard, broken only by the rustling of bamboo leaves in the wind and Xueyan's suppressed sobs.

Lin Chen turned to look at Daiyu. She was still standing there, her back straight, but her slender shoulders trembled slightly, her tightly pressed lips were bloodless, and her eyes were red-rimmed. Her previous strong exterior seemed to have cracked after the crisis had passed, revealing her inner vulnerability and grievance.

"Miss Lin, you've been frightened." Lin Chen softened his tone, walking up to her and maintaining a proper distance. He could sense the intense fluctuations in Daiyu's cool and refined aura; she was clearly troubled.

"Ding! Main quest 'Guardian' completed! Successfully repelled the evil servants, protecting Lin Daiyu from any substantial harm. Quest rating: Perfect."

"Mission Rewards Received: Qi and Blood Pill x2, Inner Strength Amplification Card (1 hour). Rewards have been sent to System Space."

"Detected that the host has successfully protected the important plot character 'Lin Daiyu,' causing a significant emotional fluctuation in her, and the Succubus Physique effect has been significantly triggered. Gained Lin Daiyu's Favorability +20 (Favorability → Dependence). Received a large infusion of pure energy, significantly nourishing the martial arts foundation, increasing total inner strength, and deepening comprehension of 'Lightness Step' and 'Stone-Splitting Fist.' Special Effect: Cultivation speed increased by 50% for a short period."

System notifications rang out in succession, and the influx of generous rewards and energy invigorated Lin Chen. However, his attention was currently focused more on Daiyu.

"Thank you...thank you very much, Young Master Lin." Daiyu took a deep breath, forcibly calming her turbulent emotions, and curtsied to Lin Chen, her voice tinged with a barely perceptible sob. She looked up into Lin Chen's calm eyes, which held no pity, no contempt, only a serene and reassuring power. The figure that had stood in front of her just moments before, and this calm gaze now, strangely soothed the fear and humiliation in her heart.

“It was nothing.” Lin Chen shook his head, his gaze sweeping over the medicine stains and stained manuscript on the ground. “The young lady’s medicine and manuscript…”

“It’s alright.” Daiyu lowered her eyes, her long eyelashes casting shadows on her pale cheeks. “The medicine can be brewed again. The manuscript… it’s better it’s ruined; it wasn’t anything special to begin with.” Her tone was flat, but she couldn’t hide her disappointment. Those poems were her life’s work, her only solace in her loneliness.

Lin Chen paused for a moment, then suddenly said, “I’ve heard it said that literature is born of nature, and a skillful hand may occasionally create something beautiful. Today’s loss may be the catalyst for a better piece in the future. Your talent, young lady, is not confined to a single piece of poetry.”

Daiyu was slightly taken aback by his words, looking up at Lin Chen. The young man’s expression was serious; his words of comfort were not empty. He could actually say such things… The disappointment in her heart seemed to have been somewhat lessened. She softly hummed in agreement, saying nothing more, but her previously tense body visibly relaxed.

Xueyan, having wiped away her tears, began cleaning up the mess on the floor. Lin Chen also stepped forward to help, clearing away the large pieces of broken porcelain.

“Young Master Lin,” Daiyu, watching his movements, suddenly asked softly, “were you… using martial arts just now? I noticed your movements were very fast.”

“Yes. Martial arts can strengthen the body, and also provide self-defense, protecting oneself and others,” Lin Chen replied, placing the last piece of broken porcelain into the winnowing basket.

"Protect yourself and others..." Daiyu repeated softly, a thoughtful glint in her eyes. She had been frail since childhood, often feeling like a drifting duckweed, her fate beyond her control. Martial arts... could this feeling of wielding power truly change anything? The thought flashed through her mind, but she quickly suppressed it. How could she, with her frail body, learn martial arts?

"If you encounter similar situations again, young lady, don't suffer in silence. You can quickly send someone to find the old lady, or... you can come to Tingzhu Pavilion to find me," Lin Chen said earnestly, looking at Daiyu. This wasn't just polite talk. Having accepted the mission of protection and formed this "bond" with Daiyu, he naturally cared about her safety.

Daiyu felt a warmth in her heart. Looking at Lin Chen's handsome and sincere face, a strange feeling quietly blossomed within her. In this vast Jia mansion, besides her grandmother, it seemed only this young man would so directly and without any ulterior motive, say he would protect her. She nodded gently, a faint, almost imperceptible blush appearing on her pale face.

"Xueyan, go and fetch the jar of 'Longjing' tea I kept the other day, to make tea for Young Master Lin," Daiyu instructed, her tone regaining its usual coolness, though less chilly.

"Yes, Miss!" Xueyan replied, giving Lin Chen a grateful look before hurrying off.

Lin Chen didn't linger. He drank half a cup of tea, offered Daiyu a few words of comfort, and seeing that her emotions had largely stabilized, he rose to take his leave. Aunt Zhao wouldn't let today's events rest easily; he needed to make some preparations. Moreover, the newly acquired Qi and Blood Pill and Inner Breath Amplification Card would be perfect for his cultivation, aiming to break through to the late stage of the ninth rank.

Leaving Xiaoxiang Pavilion and walking along the snow-covered path, Lin Chen could clearly feel the surging internal energy within him and the cool, nourishing energy he had gained from completing the mission. Daiyu's increased favorability not only brought a large amount of energy, but it also seemed related to her inherent "spiritual and refined" qualities, making his control over his internal energy and martial arts even more delicate and subtle.

"Aunt Zhao..." A cold glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. Today was just a small punishment; if she continued to be unruly, he wouldn't mind letting this woman know what true "martial arts" rules were.

Back at Tingzhu Pavilion, he immediately took out a Qi and Blood Pill and swallowed it, simultaneously using the "Internal Energy Amplification Card."

"Buzz—"

A gentle yet powerful medicinal force spread within his body, combining with the amplification card's burning-like acceleration effect on his internal energy. Lin Chen sat cross-legged, circulating the "Small Circulation Internal Energy Method," guiding this surging power to attack the barrier of the late-stage Ninth Rank.

This time, he was determined to succeed.


Chapter 8: Sign in at Rikain House and unlock the music buff.

The night passed without incident. 

Lin Chen sat cross-legged in the quiet room of Tingzhu Pavilion, his aura deep and concentrated. The effect of the Internal Breath Amplification Card had long since worn off, but the medicinal power of that Qi and Blood Pill, combined with the pure energy nourishment from Daiyu's increased favorability and the system's hidden rewards after completing the task, allowed him to break through that invisible barrier as if it were the natural course of his life. 

Late Ninth Rank! 

The total amount and condensation of internal energy increased by nearly fifty percent compared to the mid-stage! The wisp of energy in his dantian became thicker and more active, flowing endlessly through the broadened and toughened meridians. When circulating the 'Small Zhou Tian Internal Breath Method,' the resulting small circulation became more stable and powerful, and the efficiency of absorbing and transforming the thin external vital energy improved significantly. It was as if all his bones and muscles had undergone a deep tempering, with strength, speed, endurance, and sensory sensitivity all comprehensively enhanced. Every gesture contained even greater explosive power than before. 

He slowly withdrew his power, opened his eyes, and a divine light shone in them, a faint gleam flickering before he withdrew his energy. With a gentle fist clenched, the air seemed to burst in his palm, producing faint crackling sounds. 

"Late ninth rank, among the Jia family's collateral or even direct descendants of this age, it should be considered quite good." Lin Chen evaluated in his mind. In the original owner's memory, the younger generation of the Jia family, like Jia Lian and Jia Zhen, might have practiced martial arts in their early years, but were too indulgent in pleasure and had long since neglected it. Jia Huan, Jia Cong, and others were even less capable. Only Jia Rong, son of Jia Zhen of the Eastern Residence, seemed to still maintain some martial habits, though his exact strength was unknown. It was said that Jia Zheng also aspired to martial arts in his youth, but his talent limited his achievements. 

"But that's far from enough." Lin Chen knew very well that the Jia residence was just a mansion in Jinling City; outside was a much broader world of martial arts and divine powers. A seventh-rank could defeat a hundred people, a fifth-rank could fly by riding qi...... That was the realm he truly longed for. 

He stood up and walked into the courtyard. Dawn was faintly visible, and the air was crisp and cold. He didn't practice the Stone Splitting Fist, but instead focused his mind into the system space. Besides the newly obtained Blood Pill and the Internal Breath Amplification Card (already used), when completing previous tasks, the system seemed to have mentioned deepening his understanding of the 'Light Spirit Step' and 'Stone Splitting Fist.' 

With a thought, he tried to perform the Light Spirit Step. As soon as his body moved, he felt something different. His footsteps became lighter and more agile, his turns smooth and effortless, and his control over power was exquisite, as if he could predict the most suitable way to land at every point. Moving through the bamboo and stones in the courtyard, he left behind trailing afterimages, moving nearly twice as fast as before his breakthrough! Moreover, his breath was long and effortless. 

"Alright!" A look of satisfaction appeared in Lin Chen's eyes. The importance of movement techniques in actual combat is self-evident, especially when strength is close or when the weak face a strong opponent. Masterful footwork can often determine life or death. 

He practiced the Stone-Splitting Fist once more. The punch wind howled, fierce and domineering, yet now carried a sense of smoothness as if it were light. The force was more concentrated, and the penetration was stronger. One punch landed on another spare training stone in the courtyard (which he had specially had brought over). With a muffled "bang," stone chips flew everywhere, leaving a clear fist mark on the stone, about an inch deep! This power was enough to easily split steles and crack stones. If it hit a vital part of the body, the consequences would be unimaginable. 

"The power of the Stone-Splitting Fist is considered top-tier among ninth-grade martial arts. Combined with the Light Spirit Step, it combines offense and defense, significantly boosting combat power." Lin Chen withdrew his fist and stood firm, his breath steady. 

Next, they explored a new check-in location. Following the detailed guidelines, the next place was the "Pear Fragrance Courtyard." It was originally the residence and rehearsal place for the Duke of Rongguo in his later years, later becoming the residence and rehearsal space for the palace's opera troupe's "Twelve Officials," making it a place quite popular and full of artistic charm. 

"System, showing the location of Pear Fragrance Courtyard." 

A map appeared in his mind. The Pear Fragrance Courtyard was located at the southeast corner of the Grand View Garden, near the Jia She Courtyard. It was an independent courtyard with a tranquil environment. 

Lin Chen changed into a clean blue robe, composed his aura, and left the Tingzhu Pavilion. At dawn, in the Jia residence's backyard, maids had already begun cleaning. Upon seeing him, everyone bowed respectfully, their eyes filled with awe. Although yesterday's incident at Xiaoxiang Pavilion was deliberately suppressed, news of Old Man He and the other two fleeing in disgrace still spread in small circles. Lin Chen's story of "punishing the wicked servant for Miss Lin" added a touch of legend. 

He walked calmly toward Lixiang Courtyard. On the way, he checked the system panel. The progress bar for the [Succubus Constitution] had quietly risen to 8% under the dual effects of last night's breakthrough and a significant increase in Daiyu's favorability. It seemed that every time he deeply interacted with important people, especially during intense emotional fluctuations, the awakening of his constitution would accelerate. 

The Pear Fragrance Courtyard was rather secluded, with tall walls and two old pear trees standing in front of the gate, their branches and leaves now withered. Before approaching, faint sounds of sobs and chatters could be heard from inside the courtyard, accompanied by faint sounds of strings and bamboo instruments, mixed with the clear laughter and chatter of young girls, creating a lively atmosphere. 

The courtyard gate was slightly ajar. Lin Chen didn't barge in directly, but first circled to a quiet corner on the side. There was a large tree here, its branches stretching close to the courtyard wall. He performed the Light Grace Step, tapping his toes on the trunk a few times, his body as light as a monkey flipping onto the wall, leaning on the wall to look inside. 

The courtyard was quite spacious, with a two-story building facing it with carved beams and painted rafters. Below was a spacious hall, with doors and windows wide open, revealing crowds of people inside. More than a dozen beautiful young women dressed in various costumes were rehearsing. Some were practicing their movements, their sleeves fluttering; Some were playing the pipa and moon zither; In the center stood a girl dressed in lotus-colored robes, her appearance the most outstanding, holding a musical score, softly chanting, her voice melodious and moving, like a yellow oriole emerging from the valley. This must be the "Twelve Officials" of Fangguan, Ouguan, and Ruiguan. 

Several instructor matrons were giving guidance nearby, and a few little maids were running in and out, bringing tea and water. 

Lin Chen's gaze swept over the rehearsing crowd and settled on a corner of the courtyard. There was a small octagonal pavilion there, with an ancient zither inside. Its body was jet black, its strings reflecting the morning light. Around the pavilion, several plum trees were about to bloom, elegant and distinctive. 

"This place is very nice." Lin Chen silently recited in his heart, "System, sign in at Pear Fragrance Courtyard." ” 

"Ding! Detected host first time entering the important location of the Red Mansion [Lixiang Courtyard], triggering the first sign-in at the location! ” 

"Check-in successful!" Congratulations, host, you have received: Music Mastery (Special Skill, Passive). ” 

A cool and gentle flow of information flooded his mind—not a specific sheet music or instrument fingering, but a profound understanding and instinctive mastery of 'sound.' The five tones—Gong, Shang, Jue, Zhi, Yu—transformed; the eight tones of silk, bamboo, metal, stone, gourd, earth, leather, and wood—harmonized together; the rhythmic tension and tempo, the melodic ups and downs, and the infuse and transmission of emotion...... All the profound meanings of music were etched into his consciousness like imprints. It was as if, in an instant, he transformed from an ordinary person with only a superficial understanding of music into a master who had been immersed in the art for years. 

Moreover, at the moment he acquired the "Musical Mastery" skill, he clearly felt that the succubus constitution aura he had concealed within his body resonated with this newly acquired musical insight in a strange way! It was as if this "musical rhythm" had become the perfect vessel to carry and amplify his unique enchanting aura! 

"Succubus Constitution Hidden Effect Unlocks: Musical Resonance. The host can infuse their enchanting aura into the sound waves through playing, singing, and other musical forms, greatly enhancing the influence on the listener. The strength of the effect depends on the host's musical mastery, emotional investment, cultivation level, and the listener's temperament. ” 

Just as expected! A hint of understanding flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. In the world of Dream of the Red Chamber, music is always an elegant pursuit, closely tied to emotional expression. This succubus constitution combined with music is like adding wings to a tiger. Not only can it serve as a means to cultivate one's character and relax the mind, but it can also produce unexpected effects in specific situations. 

He was immersed in the insight of acquiring a new skill when suddenly, a crisp and cheerful burst of laughter came from outside the Pear Fragrance Courtyard, approaching from afar. 

"Hahaha, I told you it's lively here—they must be reviving a new play again! Let's go, let's go take a look! ” 

As she spoke, a young girl wearing a bright red orangutan felt cloak, a Guanyin pouch, and deerskin boots rushed in like a gust of wind. Behind her followed a maid wearing a red silk jacket and a blue satin vest—Cuilü. 

The girl ran in a hurry, a few strands of hair on her forehead tousled by the wind, her cheeks flushed, her eyes wide and bright, her eyes lively and lively, smiling before speaking, radiating a natural, free-spirited, heroic, and generous aura. She entered the courtyard, ignoring the way, only gazing toward the rehearsal hall, when she tripped over a protruding stone slab. She cried out in alarm and lunged forward! 

The direction she pounced was exactly right in the corner where Lin Chen was hiding! 

Lin Chen raised his eyebrows slightly. If he didn't act now, the girl would probably fall hard. He didn't have time to think. His figure floated down from the wall, his light and agile steps reaching their peak, appearing beside the girl like a ghost. His right hand reached out and gently supported her arm, sending out a gentle force that steadied her forward posture. 

"Be careful, miss." After steadying her, Lin Chen immediately let go and stepped back half a pace, his movements natural and without any hint of intimacy. 

"Phew—that was close!" The girl steadied herself, patted her chest, let out a long breath, and finally looked up at the person supporting her. At that glance, she was instantly stunned. 

The young man before her stood in a simple blue robe, standing in the morning light and plum shadows, tall and upright, with a handsome face almost dazzling. Especially those eyes, calm and deep, as if they could absorb all the light around him. When he supported her earlier, his movements were unbelievably fast, the force in his arm gentle yet firm, instantly putting her at ease. At this moment, he looked at her calmly, his expression serene, showing none of the usual awe or awkwardness typical of men when they saw her, only a comforting coolness and ...... An indescribable, strange charm that made her heart skip a beat for no reason. 

"You...... Who are you? The girl blinked her big eyes, curiously sizing up Lin Chen, her face showing no sign of shyness. She was none other than Shi Xiangyun, the young lady of the Shi family and grandniece of Grandmother Jia. 

"I am Lin Chen, a disciple of the Western Courtyard. Passing by here, I saw the young lady nearly fall, so I reached out my hand." Lin Chen replied, already guessing the person's identity. With such a cheerful and cheerful personality, besides Shi Xiangyun, there was no one else in Dream of the Red Chamber. 

"Lin Chen?" Xiangyun tilted her head, then her eyes lit up, "Oh! I know you! You're the Brother Lin from the West Courtyard who beat up Lai Da's men, and just a few days ago helped Sister Lin, right?" Her tone was excited, filled with undisguised curiosity and admiration, "The whole household is buzzing with praise that you're amazing! One punch can shatter stone! I've wanted to meet you for a long time!" ” 

She was naturally familiar and admired heroes (although Lin Chen wasn't yet a true hero, being able to fight evil slaves and protect her sisters was already excellent in her eyes). Seeing the real person—such a handsome figure—her fondness for him instantly increased. 

"Ding!" Detected that an important plot character, 'Shi Xiangyun,' has developed strong curiosity and appreciation toward the host. Succubus constitution passive effect triggered. Gained Shi Xiangyun's affection +10 (Appreciation). Obtained a strand of pure energy (lively, passionate, and straightforward), which has automatically nourished the martial foundation and slightly increased internal energy activity and explosive power. ” 

The system notification sounded, and a surge of energy completely different from Daiyu's elegance, Baochai's richness, and Xiren's gentleness was injected. This energy was full of vigorous vitality and open enthusiasm, like the warm winter sun, making Lin Chen's internal energy seem more lively and full of tension, and even his mood became somewhat clearer. 

"It's just a minor skill for self-preservation. You flatter me, miss." Lin Chen shook his head slightly. 

"How is this a mere skill!" Xiangyun waved her hand disapprovingly, full of enthusiasm, "Being able to protect the people you want to protect and driving away bullies—that's Dingding's best skill!" I really admire people like that! Brother Lin, that move was so fast just now. Was it lightness skill? How long have you been practicing martial arts? Are you strong? Can you teach me? "She asked rapidly, her eyes shining with eager anticipation. 

Lin Chen was amused by her frank and straightforward demeanor, making him chuckle. This Miss Shi truly lived up to her reputation. "She knows a bit of martial arts and foot techniques, just to strengthen her body. Miss, you are of noble lineage—why bother learning these things?" ” 

"So what if you're a noble man?" Xiangyun lifted her chin, "Our ancestors said that our Shi family's ancestors also fought on the battlefield and earned military merits! What's wrong with girls learning some self-defense skills? It's better than staying cooped up all day, chatting around flowers and flowers!" Her tone was straightforward, without any shyness or pretentiousness. 

At this moment, the rehearsing performers and the teaching matron in the courtyard were also startled and all turned to look. Seeing that it was Shi Xiangyun and a handsome young man who was unfamiliar, they were all a bit surprised. 

Lin Chen didn't want to linger longer and avoid unnecessary attention, so he said to Xiangyun, "I have other matters to attend to, so I'll go ahead." Since you enjoy watching operas, please go ahead. ” 

"Hey, Brother Lin, don't be in such a hurry to leave!" Xiangyun quickly said, "I was just getting bored watching a play alone! Did you hear the music just now and came over? Do you know how to play the zither? I see there's a zither in the pavilion. How about we play a piece together? I may not play well, but I love listening to someone play the zither!" She pointed at the guqin in the octagonal pavilion in the courtyard, her face full of anticipation. 

Orchestra? Lin Chen's heart stirred. He had just acquired "Musical Mastery" and unlocked the physical effect of "Musical Resonance," so he could take this opportunity to try it out firsthand. Moreover, interacting with someone as straightforward and energetic as Xiangyun might also benefit his constitution awakening. 

"I know a little." Lin Chen didn't refuse and walked toward the octagonal pavilion. 

Xiangyun was overjoyed and happily followed behind him, saying to Cuilü, "Go talk to Ouguan and the others, let them practice on their own first, and I'll talk with Brother Lin for a while." ” 

The two arrived at the pavilion. The qin was a fine seven-stringed guqin, with a paulownia wood body and ice strings. Though not a famous piece, it was still usable. Lin Chen sat cross-legged in front of the qin, his fingers caressing the strings, and a natural sense of familiarity surged in his heart. He tuned the tune and tried a few notes; the sound was clear and melodious. 

Xiangyun sat across from him, resting her chin in her hands, staring at him without blinking, full of anticipation. 

Lin Chen pondered briefly, and a melody already formed in his mind. He slowly closed his eyes, calming his mind. In his mind, scenes of watching the sea of clouds and sunrise atop Kunlun and feeling the vastness of heaven and earth flashed through his previous life. What he had seen and felt since transmigrating—the depth of the Jia household, Daiyu's solitary purity, Baochai's composure, Xiangyun's openness...... All kinds of emotions swirled through his heart. 

He opened his eyes, his fingertips gently twitching. 

"Buzz—" 

The first sound was clear and ethereal, as if descending from beyond the heavens, instantly capturing Xiangyun's mind. 

Immediately after, the music flows out. At first, it is gentle, like a morning mist spreading as a prelude, carrying a hint of a strange and searching for the first time in another world. As the music rises, it becomes cold and solitary, like the green bamboo outside the Xiaoxiang Pavilion, standing in the wind—this is Daiyu. As the melody turns, it becomes broad and steady, upright and calm, as if the earth bears weight—this is Baochai. Suddenly, the music becomes lively and lively, passionate and bright, full of vitality and energy, like the warm winter sun melting away ice and snow—this is Xiangyun. 

Lin Chen did not deliberately activate his succubus physique, but when he fully immersed himself in the performance, infusing his emotions and insights into the music, the skill of "musical mastery" and the resonance effect of the "succubus constitution" naturally activated. 

The intangible, alluring aura that fused his emotions at that moment spread with every note. It was no longer simply about appearance or temperament, but rather, through music, conveying his spiritual world and emotional experiences in a more direct and moving way. 

Xiangyun was completely stunned. She didn't understand profound music theory, but the sound of the zither seemed alive, striking straight into her heart. She heard solitude, warmth, openness, and a calm yet powerful force that defied fate. The young man playing the zither before her seemed to shine in her eyes. His handsome face and calm gaze merged with the soul-stirring music, creating a scene that made her heart race and her breath slightly. 

She had never heard such music before, nor had she ever felt like this. It was as if a place in her heart that had always been empty was quietly filled by this music, by the person who played it. 

It wasn't just Xiangyun. The performers who had been rehearsing in the courtyard had stopped at some point and quietly looked toward the pavilion. The instructor nanny forgot to urge them, her eyes showing surprise. Even the birds perched in the trees stopped singing, tilting their heads as if listening. 

When the song ended, the lingering notes lingered endlessly. 

The pavilion was silent, with only the gentle sound of the wind blowing through plum branches. 

After a long while, Xiangyun finally let out a long sigh of relief and clapped her hands, praising, "Great! Wonderful! Brother Lin, you play so well! I...... I've never heard such beautiful piano music before! What kind of piece is this? ” 

"An improvisational piece, but no title yet." Lin Chen withdrew his hand, calming the slight stirrings stirred by his immersion in the performance. He could feel that during the earlier performance, his spirit seemed to be tempered in some way, and he had gained a preliminary understanding of the fusion of melody and allure. The lively and open energy from Xiangyun seemed to be transmitted much faster. 

"Improvised?" Xiangyun's eyes widened in admiration, "Brother Lin, you're amazing! You know martial arts, and you play the zither so well! And you're ...... And so beautiful! "She was straightforward and spoke quickly, saying whatever came to mind. Only after saying did she realize the last half of her sentence was a bit abrupt, her cheeks slightly flushed, but her eyes remained bright and open, never avoiding it." 

"Ding!" Detected a significant increase in affection for the host of important plot character 'Shi Xiangyun', deepening emotional resonance. Gained Shi Xiangyun's affection +10 (Appreciation → Admiration). Received a large amount of lively and straightforward energy infusion, significantly increased internal breath activity, and gained insight into the fierce explosive power of the 'Stone Cracking Fist'. Special effect: Slightly increased strength and explosive power for a short time. ” 

Lin Chen was slightly moved; Xiangyun's favor came quickly and directly, and the energy she provided was quite distinctive. He smiled slightly and said, "Miss, you flatter me. It's just a leisure pastime, not as approachable and straightforward as you can see." ” 

Hearing him praise her, Xiangyun was even happier and smiled, "Brother Lin, stop calling me 'girl' anymore, it's so distant. My surname is Shi, my given name is Xiangyun. You can call me Yun'er or Xiangyun either way! From now on, we're friends, okay?" ” 

"Better to obey than to be respectful, Sister Xiangyun." Lin Chen readily agreed. 

"That's more like it!" Xiangyun smiled with curved eyes, suddenly remembering something and asked, "Brother Lin, will you come to Lixiang Courtyard often in the future? I still want to hear you play the zither! Oh, right, your martial arts are so good, could you teach me some too? You don't have to be very strong, just strengthen your body, and occasionally beat up bad guys is enough!" ” 

Looking into her bright, expectant eyes, Lin Chen thought for a moment and nodded: "If there's a chance, it's not a bad idea to spar and exchange some skills." Strengthening the body is always good. ” 

"That's great!" Xiangyun cheered, almost jumping up again, but Cuilu gently tugged her sleeve beside her, and she remembered to be more reserved, sticking out her tongue in embarrassment. 

After a few more words, Lin Chen saw the sun rising higher and got up to take his leave. Although Xiangyun was a bit reluctant, she knew he must have something to do, so she didn't press further, only promising to visit each other often in the future. 

Leaving Lixiang Courtyard and walking back, Lin Chen was in a good mood. This check-in not only gave him the practical "Music Mastery" skill and unlocking new constitution effects, but also unexpectedly met Shi Xiangyun, gaining a new source of energy. This Miss Shi's candid enthusiasm truly made people feel refreshed. 

"Next, it's time for Jia Huan to provoke you......" Lin Chen recalled the detailed outline. The tree wishes to be still, but the wind won't stop; as strength increases, it naturally attracts more attention, even jealousy. However, he didn't care. 

Perfect, you can try the late-stage Ninth-Rank Stone-Splitting Fist, combined with the burst power of your new insights—see how powerful it is. 

He walked steadily toward Ting Zhuxuan. Sunlight shone on him, casting a firm and slender shadow on the snow.


Chapter 9 Martial Arts Sparring: Crushing the Jia Family Disciples

Two days have passed since Lin Chen returned from Pear Fragrance Courtyard.

He has been keeping a low profile, focusing all his energy on consolidating his late-stage Ninth Rank cultivation and digesting the insights gained from the lively and open energy provided by Xiangyun. This energy has made his control over his inner energy more agile, allowing him to unleash a more explosive, inch-force within the ferocity of his Stone-Splitting Fist, adding three times its power.

Inside Listening Bamboo Pavilion, the sound of Stone-Splitting Fist cutting through the air grows stronger each day. Lin Chen's figure darts and leaps across the courtyard, his punches like cannonballs, his kicks like whips, each strike carrying immense force, the air crackling and popping. He deliberately controls his strength, avoiding damage to the objects in the courtyard, but the fierce wind from his punches still causes the bamboo branches in the corners to tremble and the snow on the ground to scatter in all directions.

It was a sunny afternoon. Lin Chen had just finished a round of drills and was wiping the sweat from his brow with a cloth when a flurry of footsteps and shouts came from outside the courtyard gate. Judging from the sounds, there were quite a few people, and their intentions were hostile.

"This is it! That brat surnamed Lin lives here!"

"A bastard from a collateral branch dares to act so arrogantly in the manor. Not only did he beat up Steward Lai's men, but he also goes around flaunting himself. Does he really think he's someone important?"

"Third Master Huan, look, it's this boy!"

The courtyard gate was violently pushed open with a bang, and seven or eight figures rushed in, crowding the small courtyard. Leading the group was a boy of about twelve or thirteen, dressed in a brocade cotton robe and wearing a silver crown to tie his hair. His face was as white as powder, and his lips as red as cinnabar, but a sinister and unruly air between his brows ruined his otherwise good appearance. This was Jia Huan, the son of Aunt Zhao, Jia Zheng's illegitimate son.

Behind Jia Huan were five or six other young men of similar age, dressed smartly but with a frivolous air, mostly from collateral branches of the Jia family or servants. Most were those who usually hung out with Jia Huan and were good-for-nothings. Two burly men, dressed like guards, stood at the back, arms crossed, observing coldly with scrutinizing eyes.

Lin Chen draped a cloth over his shoulder, his gaze calmly sweeping over the group of uninvited guests. Jia Huan? Perfect timing. The "martial arts sparring, crushing the Jia family's youths" mentioned in the outline—it seemed this was the place. He felt no unease, even somewhat curious to see just how capable this spoiled brat of the Jia family in this high-martial-arts world truly was.

"You're Lin Chen?" Jia Huan sized Lin Chen up and down, his eyes flashing with jealousy and disdain. He had long heard of this orphaned son of a collateral branch in the West Courtyard—breaking a stone with a single punch, gaining the old lady's attention, and even helping Lin Daiyu a couple of days ago. It was said that even that wild girl Shi Xiangyun treated him differently. Why? A poor, orphaned, dependent member of a collateral branch—dared to show off? Dared to be even glanced at by those haughty sisters?

"Exactly," Lin Chen replied indifferently, not even bothering to ask the other party's purpose. It was nothing more than seeking trouble, avenging Aunt Zhao's mistreatment a few days ago, or simply being jealous of his recent "prominence."

"Heh, quite the airs," Jia Huan sneered, seeing his calm demeanor and complete disregard for him. "I heard you've been quite the fighter lately? A few rudimentary moves, and you dare disregard the rules of the manor, even daring to lay a hand on the elders? You've really gone too far!"

"Third Master Huan, why waste time arguing with this arrogant brat? Just teach him a lesson and show him what's what!" a weasel-faced member of the collateral branch fanned the flames.

"That's right! Make him kneel down and kowtow to Third Master Huan to admit his mistake!"

Several other spoiled brats chimed in, their gazes towards Lin Chen filled with malice and a twisted excitement. They were used to bullying the weak and fearing the strong, and seeing that Lin Chen was alone, and looked handsome and frail (in their eyes), they were even more emboldened by their numbers and the presence of two guards.

The two guards exchanged a glance, remaining silent. They had been temporarily summoned by Jia Huan from the outer courtyard, only knowing that they were going to teach a member of the collateral branch a lesson. Seeing the young man's steady demeanor and solid stance, he seemed to have some martial arts skills, but how strong could he be at such a young age? They'd just have to see how he fared.

"Did you hear me?" Jia Huan raised his chin arrogantly. "Lin Chen, if you know what's good for you, kneel down right now, kowtow three times, and compensate me tenfold for the bird's nest and poems you 'stole' from Aunt Zhao a few days ago... oh no, I mean, 'accidentally' damaged." Perhaps I'd be in a good mood and spare you this time. Otherwise… He clenched his fists, his knuckles cracking softly. Though useless, he was, after all, Jia Zheng's son, and had dabbled in some rudimentary martial arts, confident he could easily handle a collateral descendant's orphan.

"Steal? Compensate?" Lin Chen seemed to have heard a joke, a faint sneer playing on his lips. "Aunt Zhao sent people to trespass into Xiaoxiang Pavilion, smashing things, with malicious intent. I only expelled her wicked servant; that was already showing leniency. Today, you gathered a crowd to storm my courtyard, uttering vile words. Do you think I, Lin Chen, am easy to bully, or do you think the rules of this manor are for you spoiled brats to trample upon at will?"

His voice wasn't loud, but every word was clear, carrying a chilling penetrating force that made the jeering young scions freeze in their tracks.

Jia Huan's face flushed red and then paled, enraged by Lin Chen's words: "You sharp-tongued brat! You don't know what's good for you! Looks like I won't let you off easy until I teach you a lesson!" He turned to the two guards and shouted, "Liu San, Wang Wu! Seize him! Beat him until he begs for mercy!"

The two guards hesitated slightly, but being Jia Huan's men, they dared not disobey. They parted the crowd and stepped forward. They were burly, with bulging temples, clearly trained fighters, their cultivation level between the early and mid-stages of the ninth rank—far stronger than Zhang Ergou and Zhao Hang.

"Young Master Lin, apologies," the guard named Liu San said in a deep voice, flanking Lin Chen with Wang Wu. Their steps were steady, their arm muscles bulging, clearly skilled in combined grappling techniques.

Lin Chen remained unmoved, not even assuming a fighting stance, simply standing still as the two approached.

Just as Liu San's large, fan-like hand was about to grab his shoulder—

Lin Chen moved!

Without warning, without gathering power, he seemed to transform into a blurry green shadow! Using his full power of the Lightness Step, in the nick of time, he shifted half a step to the side like a ghost in a small space, and Liu San's seemingly certain grab missed its mark.

At the same time, Lin Chen's right fist, like a long-dormant venomous dragon, silently shot out from his ribs! The fist was as fast as lightning, carrying a piercing shriek as it tore through the air, its target the vulnerable ribs exposed when Liu San's grab missed! This punch didn't use any of the techniques of the Stone-Splitting Fist; it relied solely on the powerful internal energy and physical strength of a late-stage ninth-rank cultivator, combined with the explosive short-range force he had gleaned from Xiangyun's energy—simple, direct, and ruthless!

"Bang!"

A solid, muffled thud! Liu San felt an irresistible force slam into his ribs. His protective internal energy was torn apart like paper. A sharp pain accompanied the cracking sound of bones breaking. His nearly 200-pound frame was sent flying, his feet leaving the ground, crashing straight into the courtyard wall!

"Bang!" Liu San slammed heavily against the wall, letting out a muffled groan, sliding limply to the ground. He struggled for a moment, but couldn't immediately get up. A trickle of blood escaped from the corner of his mouth, his eyes filled with horror.

It all happened so fast! Wang Wu, standing nearby, hadn't even seen how Lin Chen made his move before his companion was already severely injured and lying on the ground. He was terrified, but reacted quickly, roaring as he unleashed a powerful punch, the size of a sand bowl, aimed straight for Lin Chen's face!

Lin Chen didn't retreat but advanced, his body lowering as he slipped under Wang Wu's armpit. Simultaneously, his left hand, fingers like a halberd, flashed towards Wang Wu's vital point on his lower back! His fingertips condensed internal energy, sharp as needles!

Wang Wu felt a numbness in his lower back, half his body instantly going limp and powerless. His forward momentum became unstoppable, and he staggered forward. Lin Chen had already moved behind him, his right leg whipping down silently yet powerfully into the back of Wang Wu's shin.

"Crack!"

"Ah—!" Wang Wu screamed, collapsing to the ground, clutching his twisted and deformed leg, drenched in cold sweat from the pain.

From the moment the two attacked to their fall, only three to five breaths had passed! Two guards at the early to mid-stage of the ninth rank were utterly helpless against Lin Chen, who had broken through to the late stage of the ninth rank!

The courtyard was deathly silent.

Jia Huan and the other spoiled brats were all dumbfounded, their arrogance and smugness completely frozen, replaced by boundless fear. They hadn't even seen Lin Chen's movements clearly; they only saw a flash of green, and the two guards, whom they considered quite powerful, were sent flying with screams—one with broken bones, the other with a broken leg!

This…how is this possible?! Is this even human?!

Lin Chen slowly withdrew his attack, his breath even and long, as if he had merely swatted away two flies. His gaze turned to Jia Huan and the others, calm and expressionless, yet sending chills down Jia Huan's spine, making his hair stand on end.

"You...you stay away!" Jia Huan's voice trembled as he retreated, his bravado masking his fear, hiding behind one of the纨绔 (wan ku, a spoiled, dissolute young man). "I...I'm Master's son! If you dare touch me, Master will not forgive you!"

"Yes! Yes! Third Master Huan is Master Zheng's son! Lin Chen, how dare you defy your superiors?!" Several other纨绔 also reacted, shouting, but their voices trembled, lacking any real conviction.

"Defy your superiors?" Lin Chen approached step by step, his pace slow, yet seemingly pounding on their hearts. "Gathering a crowd to trespass into the courtyard of your own clan, provoking you without cause, uttering vile words, and letting your servants commit violence. When did the rules of the Jia family become your shield to bully your own clan? If Master Zheng finds out, will he punish me for self-defense, or will he punish you for your disrespect, treachery, and bullying?"

With each word he spoke, Jia Huan and the others paled further. They had been acting recklessly because of their status, never considering such matters. Now, exposed by Lin Chen, and remembering Jia Zheng's upright and rigid nature, his strong emphasis on rules and propriety, they knew they would be at a disadvantage if things escalated.

"I...I..." Jia Huan was sweating profusely, watching Lin Chen approach, his calm gaze sending chills down his spine. He suddenly remembered the fates of Zhang Ergou, Zhao Hang, and Granny He, a coldness shooting from the soles of his feet to the top of his head.

"I was wrong! Brother Lin...I was wrong! I was blind and offended you!" Jia Huan suddenly knelt down with a thud, kowtowing without warning. "Please forgive me this time! I'll never dare to do it again! I'll never dare to do it again!"

Seeing Jia Huan kneel, the other spoiled brats dared not resist any longer, and they too knelt down, kowtowing repeatedly, begging for mercy.

Lin Chen stopped, looking at the group of cowardly bullies before him, their arrogance turning into servility, and felt only disgust. He hadn't intended to punish Jia Huan severely; after all, he was Jia Zheng's illegitimate son, and even when beating a dog, one should consider its owner. A slight punishment would suffice to make him back down.

"Get out," Lin Chen uttered coldly.

Jia Huan felt as if he'd been granted a pardon. Forgetting all decorum, he scrambled to his feet, beckoning to the other纨绔 (sons of wealthy families), helping the groaning Liu San and Wang Wu to their feet, and fled Tingzhu Pavilion in a disheveled state, as if chased by a vengeful ghost.

The courtyard returned to quiet, save for a faint stench of blood and the messy footprints left from the fierce fight.

Lin Chen walked to the wall, looked at the crack he'd punched, then at the bloodstains Wang Wu had left on the ground, his expression calm. His attack had been perfectly measured; Liu San had broken two ribs and suffered a minor internal injury, requiring over a month of rest; Wang Wu had a broken leg and would also be bedridden for two or three months. It was both punishment and a warning.

He believed that after this incident, at least among the younger generation, no one would dare to provoke him so easily again. Jia Huan had suffered a great loss this time; given his temperament, he would likely hate him intensely, but in the short term, he would definitely not dare to openly provoke him again.

As for the forces behind those two guards, or Jia Zheng's possible reaction… Lin Chen wasn't particularly worried. He was in the right, and had demonstrated sufficient strength and value. In this world of high martial arts, a collateral descendant who possessed late-stage ninth-rank strength and considerable combat power at the age of fifteen was not without value to the increasingly declining martial arts of the Jia family.

He walked back to the front of the house, picked up a cloth, and continued to wipe his hands. When he fought earlier, he had deliberately suppressed his alluring aura; now, calmed down, the slight fluctuations in his mental energy caused by the battle gradually subsided, and his unique physical attractiveness also diminished, making him appear once again as an ordinary young man with a slightly aloof demeanor.

“Ding! Detected that the host overwhelmingly defeated the opponent in the conflict, intimidating everyone and initially establishing a reputation among the younger generation of the Jia family. Martial arts beliefs have slightly improved. Gained a small amount of martial arts insight; proficiency in the Stone-Splitting Fist has increased.”

The system notification sounded, as expected. Battle, especially an overwhelming victory, is itself a form of training and verification.

Just as he was about to return to his room to continue his cultivation, a well-dressed, middle-aged steward with a short beard, dressed in a respectable manner, hurried over to the courtyard gate, his expression complex. Lin Chen recognized him; this was Li Gui, a close attendant of Jia Zheng.

"Young Master Lin," Li Gui stopped at the courtyard gate, bowing respectfully, his attitude polite yet cautious, "Master heard there was some... commotion here and specifically ordered me to come and investigate. I wonder what just happened..."

"It's nothing," Lin Chen interrupted him, his tone flat, "It was just that Third Master Huan brought a few friends and two guards to my courtyard to 'spar' martial arts. In a moment of excitement, they lost their temper and disturbed Master. Please, Steward Li, convey my apologies to Master Zheng."

Li Gui's lips twitched almost imperceptibly. He had seen Jia Huan and the others fleeing in disarray from afar, and heard the screams of the guards inside. How could this be explained away as a mere "sparring"? But Lin Chen's words were watertight, characterizing the conflict as a "sparring" and even offering an apology, leaving no room for criticism. Li Gui looked at the signs of a fight in the courtyard and the cracks in the walls, and was secretly alarmed. This young master Lin's strength was probably truly extraordinary.

"Young Master is too kind," Li Gui chuckled dryly. "Since it's a friendly spar, mistakes are inevitable. Master instructed me to convey a message: those who practice martial arts should prioritize physical fitness, manners, and propriety. They must not bully the weak or be prone to violence.
