Since you are interested in this path, I hope you will diligently practice and not neglect it. If you have any questions, you may come to Master for discussion."

These words sounded like a warning, but they actually contained implicit approval and a hint of…recruitment? Lin Chen understood, and cupped his hands, saying, "Thank you for your guidance, Master Zheng. Lin Chen will remember it."

Li Gui exchanged a few more polite words, then took his leave to report back to Jia Zheng.

Lin Chen stood in the courtyard, gazing intently in the direction Li Gui had gone.

Jia Zheng's attitude was intriguing. It seemed that the strength he had displayed had already attracted the genuine attention of this man, one of the de facto rulers of the Rongguo Mansion and also someone the original owner's father had tried to cultivate a relationship with.

Whether it was a blessing or a curse remained to be seen. But regardless, Lin Chen's path would only follow his own will.

He turned and went back into his room, closing the door. Outside the courtyard, news of "Young Master Lin Chen beating Master Huan to his knees in a few punches and kicks, breaking the guards' legs and ribs" had once again swept through every corner of the Jia family's inner quarters like wildfire.

From that moment on, Tingzhu Pavilion was no longer just a quiet, secluded courtyard in everyone's minds.


Chapter 10: Advancing the Succubus Physique, and the Wonderful Use of Favorability.

The matter of Jia Huan, like a boulder thrown into a deep pool, stirred up a turbulent undercurrent far greater than before beneath the surface of the Jia family's outward calm.

"Have you heard? That young master Lin Chen from the west courtyard beat up both of the guards that Third Master Huan brought! Liu San broke two ribs, and Wang Wu's leg was broken!"

"More than that! Third Master Huan knelt down on the spot, kowtowing and begging for mercy!"

"Really? Those are guards from Master Zheng's side, they're all skilled in martial arts!"

"Absolutely true! Steward Li Gui went to see for himself, and came back pale-faced!"

"Tsk tsk, this young master Lin is truly a master of disguise. He looks aloof and cold, but he's ruthless..."

"Ruthless? That's Third Master Huan's own doing! Bringing people to bully you, could you tolerate it?"

"I heard that when Master Zheng found out, he not only didn't reprimand him, but also sent Li Gui to tell Young Master Lin to study diligently and to seek his guidance if he had any questions!"

"It seems this Young Master Lin has truly caught Master Zheng's eye..."

Rumors spread at an alarming rate, constantly evolving and becoming increasingly exaggerated in detail. But the core remained unchanged: Lin Chen, this once unknown, sickly, and easily bullied orphan from a collateral branch, possessed formidable strength enough to crush ordinary guards, and acted decisively, unafraid of powerful figures (specifically referring to Aunt Zhao's faction). More importantly, he seemed to have gained some tacit approval, or even attention, from Jia Zheng, the de facto ruler of the Rongguo Mansion.

The entrance to Tingzhu Pavilion fell completely deserted. No one dared to approach casually anymore. Even the old woman delivering meals only dared to place the food box outside the gate, gently knock to signal, and then hurriedly leave, her attitude respectful to the point of fear. The rough maids who usually swept and cleaned now did so with extraordinary diligence and care, as if afraid that leaving even a speck of dust would anger the "evil star" within the courtyard.

Lin Chen was happy to have some peace and quiet. He completely shut out the outside noise, immersing his mind in his own cultivation and enlightenment.

The events surrounding Jia Huan, besides deterring villains, also brought him personal gains. His brief encounters with Liu San and Wang Wu, though not at his full strength, provided him with more direct evidence of his control over late-stage ninth-rank power and his adaptability in combat. The essence of his Stone-Splitting Fist, catalyzed by actual combat, became increasingly refined, its power concealing subtle variations.

More importantly, he vaguely sensed that since his transmigration, through his interactions with the four "golden beauties"—Daiyu, Baochai, Xiangyun, and Xiren—especially Daiyu's dependence, Xiangyun's admiration, Baochai's curiosity, and Xiren's care, the intangible bonds and energy stemming from his "succubus constitution" seemed to have reached a delicate critical point.

This feeling was profound, as if some dormant "acupoint" or "meridian" within his body, related to emotional resonance, was being quietly illuminated and opened. The wisp of internal energy in his dantian, besides its inherent solidity, seemed to be imbued with a very faint, ever-changing hazy radiance—sometimes cool as the moon, sometimes warm as the earth, sometimes lively as fire, sometimes gentle as water—corresponding to the different energy characteristics of the four women, yet harmoniously blending together, nourishing his martial arts foundation, making his mind exceptionally clear during cultivation, and significantly accelerating the growth of his internal energy compared to simple arduous training.

That evening, Lin Chen finished a round of the *Small Circulation Inner Breathing Technique* and slowly opened his eyes. The setting sun cast a warm glow through the window paper, illuminating the room. He didn't immediately get up, but instead focused inward, carefully observing the increasingly clear changes originating from his constitution.

Just as his mind calmed—

"Buzz..."

A soft tremor seemed to resonate in his mind, originating from the depths of his soul. At the same time, the inner breath within his dantian, infused with multicolored energy, suddenly shone brightly. Although it was fleeting, Lin Chen clearly felt that some invisible shackles within his body had been broken, and a feeling of greater clarity, more fluidity, and greater control surged throughout his body.

"Ding! Detected that the host's accumulated favorability with important plot characters 'Lin Daiyu,' 'Xue Baochai,' 'Shi Xiangyun,' and 'Xiren' has exceeded 50 points. [Succubus Physique] meets the initial advancement requirements!"

"Succubus Physique advancing..."

"Advancement complete! Current stage: Mid-Awakening (Progress 15%)."

"Unlock New Abilities:

Active Skill [Charm Convergence] (Beginner): The host can more precisely and freely control the emission and convergence of their charm aura. In the converged state, the body's natural attraction to the opposite sex is greatly reduced, and the temperament leans towards calmness and restraint; when actively released, it can slightly enhance the charm effect on specific targets, requiring a small amount of mental energy.

Passive Effect [Affinity Nourishment]: Through the invisible connection with targets with whom a favorable bond has been established (favorability ≥ 10), the host can continuously absorb a small amount of pure 'affinity energy,' automatically nourishing their martial arts foundation. The deeper the bond (the higher the favorability), the purer the energy, the faster the absorption speed, and the more significant the auxiliary effect on cultivation. Currently effective bonds: Lin Daiyu (favorability 65), Shi Xiangyun (favorability 40), Xue Baochai (favorability 25), Xiren (favorability 25)."

"Prompt: The Charm Concealment skill can help the host better integrate into the environment and avoid unnecessary attention and trouble. The Affinity Nourishment effect is a passive, permanent effect that strengthens as the bond deepens. Please maintain your bond relationships with each target carefully."

A series of system prompts rang in his mind, containing a considerable amount of information. Lin Chen's mind stirred slightly, and he brought up the semi-transparent system panel that only he could see. Sure enough, in the [Constitution] section, the status had changed to "Succubus Constitution (Mid-Awakening, 15%)", with descriptions of the two newly unlocked abilities listed below.

"Finally advanced." A knowing glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. His interactions with the women recently, especially Daiyu's high favorability rating of 65 points (dependence → admiration?) and Xiangyun's rapid increase of 40 points (appreciation → admiration), were undoubtedly key to his physique's advancement. While Baochai and Xiren had lower favorability ratings, they still provided a stable source of energy.

He first focused his attention on the new skill, [Charm Concealment]. This skill came at just the right time. As his strength increased and he moved around the mansion more, the aura he unconsciously emitted, easily attracting attention (especially from the opposite sex), could indeed cause unnecessary trouble. Being able to control it freely was ideal.

He closed his eyes, focused his mind, and tried to mobilize the mysterious and elusive spiritual power related to his physique. At first, it was somewhat sluggish, as if he were manipulating an unfamiliar, intricate musical instrument. However, his spiritual power was already far beyond that of ordinary people due to his transmigration and martial arts training, and after a few attempts, he grasped the basics.

With a thought, it was as if an invisible, soft veil enveloped his entire body from the inside out, slowly concealing and internalizing the naturally emanating, alluring aura. He walked to the half-length bronze mirror in the room.

The boy in the mirror still possessed handsome features and a tall, upright posture, but his demeanor had subtly changed. The dazzling brilliance that previously shone through, even when deliberately concealed, and instantly made him stand out, had dimmed considerably. He had become more serene, unassuming, and even possessed a scholarly air of refinement. If before he was a pearl hidden in a box, occasionally letting out a sliver of light, now he had returned to his true nature, his brilliance completely concealed within. He appeared simply as a clean-cut, slightly silent, handsome young man. Though still outstanding, he no longer possessed that intense, captivating, and aggressive charm.

"Very good." Lin Chen nodded in satisfaction. In this state, the probability of being noticed in a crowd would be greatly reduced, which better suited his current need for a low-key development. He made another thought and tried to slowly release a trace of his aura.

The person in the mirror's temperament instantly changed. Those deep eyes seemed to ripple, shimmering with light, and an indescribable, captivating charm subtly appeared between his calm brows. The lines of his lips also seemed to soften slightly, and his entire being exuded a strange attraction that made people's hearts race and unconsciously wanted to get closer. However, this state was slightly mentally taxing, and Lin Chen could feel that if he fully released it, the effect would be stronger, but it would also attract more attention.

"Mastering control is the true path." Lin Chen once again calmed and restrained his aura. This skill was undoubtedly one of the most practical benefits of his physical advancement.

Next, he focused his mind on his body, carefully experiencing the passive effect of [Nourishing Affection].

Without consciously circulating his inner energy, he could clearly perceive four strands of energy, each different in nature yet equally pure and gentle, flowing slowly into his dantian through some invisible yet real "thread," connected to the four women in the darkness, merging into his internal energy.

Daiyu's energy was the most refreshing and ethereal, like moonlight and dew, clearing his mind and enhancing his subtle control over his internal energy and martial arts; Xiangyun's energy was lively and open, like the warm winter sun, enhancing the activity and explosive power of his internal energy; Baochai's energy was mellow and balanced, like the earth's reserves, making his internal energy foundation more stable and solid; Xiren's energy was gentle and delicate, like a spring breeze and rain, helping to calm his blood and nourish his meridians.

Although the four energies were weak, they were continuous and perfectly matched his own internal energy, allowing him to absorb them almost without refining. Under this continuous nourishment, he could feel his internal energy steadily and solidly increasing at a rate 20-30% faster than simple cultivation, and his martial arts foundation was becoming increasingly solid.

"So this is the manifestation of 'the more favored you are, the more significant the increase in combat power'..." Lin Chen realized to himself. This "affinity nourishment" effect was practically comparable to carrying a gentle, continuously effective buff array. Moreover, the effect would strengthen as his bonds with them deepened.

This gave him a deeper understanding of the "succubus constitution." This constitution wasn't simply about "seduction" or "sexual exploitation," but rather a two-way energy resonance and nourishment based on genuine emotional bonds. He gained energy boosts, and while the other party developed feelings for him and reciprocated, they might also receive some positive feedback from him (such as Daiyu's soothing mood and Xiangyun's cheerful disposition), only this feedback was more subtle.

"Bonds..." Lin Chen murmured, his gaze piercing through the window paper, as if he could see the verdant bamboo of Xiaoxiang Pavilion, the zither music of Lixiang Courtyard, the cool fragrance of Hengwu Garden, and the lights of Yihong Courtyard. The karmic connections of this Red Chamber world seemed to be tightly intertwining with him in a way he had never anticipated.

He stopped thinking, calmed his mind, and sat down cross-legged again. With the enhanced perception brought by his newly advanced physique and the continuous nourishment of his affinity energy, he circulated the *Small Circulation Inner Breathing Technique*. His inner energy flowed joyfully through his meridians, steadily advancing towards the peak of the late ninth rank.

As night deepened, the Listening Bamboo Pavilion was utterly silent, save for the young man's even, long breaths, harmonizing with the invisible, faint energy flow that connected him to the four directions and nourished him.

In the Xiaoxiang Pavilion, Daiyu sat alone by the lamp, a scroll of Zhuangzi in her hand untouched for a long time. The image of him shielding her from the cold that day, and the warmth and flutter in her heart when she said, "May this sword protect Brother Lin," kept replaying in her mind. A faint blush rose on her pale face.

In the Hengwu Garden, Baochai was preparing rouge with snow water dissolved from the Cold Fragrance Pill. Her movements were elegant, but her thoughts drifted away. During the day, her mother and aunt had vaguely mentioned the young master Lin in the West Courtyard, and it seemed even her uncle (Wang Ziteng) had heard of him… The boy's calm eyes, and the strange aura she had glimpsed amidst the pine forest and snow-covered rocks that day, flashed through her mind again. She shook her head slightly, suppressing the strange feeling, and resumed her composed demeanor.

At Qiushuangzhai, where Shi Xiangyun was temporarily staying, Xiangyun was pulling Cuilu along, gesturing excitedly: "Cuilu, how fast was Brother Lin's move? I only saw a blur of shadow, and that big guy flew out! He's amazing! I'll definitely ask him to teach me a few moves tomorrow!" Her eyes were bright and full of anticipation.

In the Grand View Garden, Xiren was gently making Baoyu's bed. Her mind, however, was filled with the whispers of the maids during the day about how Young Master Lin had displayed such prowess again… The image of the young man's handsome, serene profile floated before her eyes, along with the racing heart she felt when they first met by the well. Had he been injured today? Tomorrow… should she find an excuse to visit Tingzhu Pavilion? Perhaps bring him some refreshments, or…

The four young women, each with their own thoughts, were unknowingly developing an increasingly close, invisible connection with the young man diligently cultivating in Tingzhu Pavilion. And this connection, like a gentle stream, was quietly converging, becoming an undeniable force propelling the young man on his path of martial arts.

The night is still long. But some changes have already taken root and sprouted.


Chapter 11: Sign in at Qinfang Gate and receive a high-level martial arts manual.

Night faded, and the first light of dawn broke.

Lin Chen awoke from his meditation, his eyes gleaming with inner light, his aura growing increasingly profound. A night of cultivation, aided by the continuous support of [Affinity Nourishment], had further solidified his inner energy, bringing him one step closer to the peak of the late-stage ninth rank. The [Charm Concealment] skill, granted by his physique advancement, allowed him better control over his aura; his demeanor was more composed in all his movements, almost unnoticed unless deliberately sought.

He rose, stretched his limbs, feeling his blood and energy coursing through his veins. Today, he was scheduled to go to Qinfang Gate to sign in. The detailed guide indicated that he could obtain the "Vajra Fist" manual there. Although he had mastered the Stone-Splitting Fist, it was ultimately a high-grade mortal-level technique, with its power having its limits. Only by acquiring a higher-grade fist technique, combined with his late-stage ninth-rank cultivation and even higher levels in the future, could he unleash its full combat potential.

After a quick wash and a breakfast left outside the courtyard gate by the old woman, Lin Chen changed into a neat dark blue cloth outfit, calmed his aura to the most ordinary and composed state, and left Tingzhu Pavilion.

Qinfang Sluice Gate, located in the northwest corner of Grand View Garden, is a crucial gate for drawing fresh water into the garden, connecting the outer river with the streams within. The scenery here is expansive, with the gentle murmur of water; usually, apart from the servants maintaining the waterways, few people visit, making it quite tranquil.

It was deep winter, and all the trees were bare. However, near Qinfang Sluice Gate, the flowing water slightly lessened the chill, and a few hardy old willows still clung to their branches with a few remnants of green. The ancient and sturdy gate, built of bluestone, had its massive sluice gate half-open, and clear river water rushed out from beneath, splashing up white spray and creating a loud, booming sound, especially resounding in the quiet winter morning. The mist, illuminated by the rising sun, transformed into tiny rainbows. Beside the gate stands a small octagonal pavilion, named "Qinfang Pavilion," a place to rest and admire the water.

Lin Chen walked to the sluice gate, leaning against the cold stone railing, gazing at the rushing river. The current was quite swift, carrying an overwhelming, unstoppable force that subtly resonated with the surging inner energy within him. He took a deep breath of the crisp, moist air, his mind stirring slightly:

"System, check-in at Qinfang Sluice Gate."

"Ding! Detected that the host has set foot for the first time at the important location of the Red Mansion, [Qinfang Sluice Gate], triggering first-time location check-in!"

"Sign-in successful! Congratulations, host, you have obtained: Vajra Fist (Mid-grade Yellow Rank, Fist Technique Manual)."

A massive and profound flow of information, far greater than when he obtained "Lightness Step" or "Mastery of Music," surged into Lin Chen's mind!

First was a complete fist technique outline and mental formula, approximately three thousand words long. The language was archaic, yet clearly structured, explaining a fist intent called "Vajra"—not the Buddhist concept of Vajra's indestructibility, but rather drawing upon the unparalleled strength and sharpness of "gold" and the supreme power of "hardness." It emphasized using oneself as a furnace, internal energy as the fuel, to forge an indestructible and unbreakable fist. Upon mastery, the fist strikes like the wrath of Vajra, possessing the power to split mountains and shatter the earth, far surpassing even the Stone-Splitting Fist.

Following this were a series of lifelike, dynamic diagrams, seemingly imprinted on one's consciousness. On these diagrams, a blurry figure demonstrated the moves of the Vajra Fist. The fist technique consisted of seven forms, each with several to dozens of variations, corresponding to different power generation techniques, internal energy circulation routes, footwork coordination, and offensive and defensive transitions. Beside the diagrams were densely packed annotations in tiny, neat characters, detailing the essence, key points, and common mistakes of each form, as well as how to coordinate it with internal energy cultivation methods, how to accumulate power, how to unleash it, how to deal with opponents of different body types, and how to execute it in complex environments… It was incredibly detailed.

This was not merely a set of fist techniques, but a systematic textbook on the art of fist, from beginner to expert! Its value far surpassed that of a simple mid-grade Yellow Rank martial art!

"Excellent fist technique!" Lin Chen thought to himself. The Vajra Fist's concept, moves, and accompanying power generation techniques far surpassed the Stone-Splitting Fist. Especially its internal energy circulation path was far more refined and complex, capable of mobilizing more and deeper layers of bodily power, unleashing a power far beyond the reach of the Stone-Splitting Fist. Moreover, the diagram's annotations were extremely detailed, greatly reducing the difficulty and time required for him to figure it out on his own.

He closed his eyes, repeatedly visualizing and contemplating the movements of the figure in the diagram in his mind, experiencing the "Vajra" fist intent. His internal energy unconsciously flowed and simulated the path shown in the diagram, and his muscles also produced a faint, corresponding rhythmic sensation.

After an unknown amount of time, he suddenly opened his eyes, a sharp glint in them, indicating a degree of understanding. He didn't immediately practice by the gate; although the area was sparsely populated, it was still spacious, and making too much noise would inevitably alert others. He suppressed the urge to try it out immediately, memorizing the contents of the manual. He then sat for a while in the Qinfang Pavilion, allowing the surging boxing intent and insights in his mind to settle before rising and retracing his steps.

On the return journey, his steps appeared ordinary, but with each step, he subtly adjusted his breathing, attempting to use the unique method recorded in the Vajra Fist manual to sink his internal energy into the soles of his feet, then release it through the ground, rebounding back to himself to temper his lower body and accumulate power. This is one of the basic stances for beginners in Vajra Fist, called "Vajra Standing Firm." At first, it felt a little awkward, but after a few steps, it gradually became smoother, as if his feet were rooted to the ground, each step firm and steady, and his internal energy flowing freely.

Back at Tingzhu Pavilion, he immediately closed the courtyard gate. He first sat cross-legged for a moment, adjusting his state to its optimal condition. Then, he walked to the specially cleared open space in the courtyard.

Without immediately practicing complex moves, he began from the top, practicing the most basic "Vajra Standing on the Ground" stance. His feet were neither too far apart nor too far apart, his waist and hips were relaxed, his spine straight like a dragon, and his head felt as if suspended by a rope. His internal energy, following a specific path, slowly sank into his dantian, then distributed to the soles of his feet, as if connecting with the earth. A steady, profound, and solid sense of power rose from the soles of his feet, permeating his entire body.

After standing for about the time it takes for an incense stick to burn, he felt his legs become sore and numb, his internal energy significantly depleted, but his spirit grew increasingly excited. He could clearly perceive the faint "earth energy" emanating from his feet interacting with his own internal energy. This stance truly had the remarkable effect of strengthening the foundation, stabilizing the lower body, and increasing strength.

After a short rest, he began practicing the first form of the Vajra Fist—the Vajra Starting Hand.

This form appears simple, merely a forward thrusting motion, but it contains exquisite techniques for unifying the waist and stance, connecting the shoulders, elbows, wrists, and fingers, and unleashing internal energy at a single point. Following the diagram, Lin Chen slowly assumed the posture, guiding his internal energy from his dantian, up the Du meridian, past the spine, through the occipital bone, through his arms, and finally sinking into his fists.

"Hah!"

He exhaled, then slowly pushed forward with his fist. The movement was slow, as if pushing a thousand-pound weight. The air was compressed by his fist, producing a muffled "whoosh." As his fist reached its full extension, he focused his mind, and his accumulated inner energy suddenly exploded!

"Bang!"

A faint, visible, transparent ripple appeared in the air in front of his fist! Although fleeting, the instantaneous burst of power startled even Lin Chen himself. The pure power of this punch was probably close to, or even surpassed, the full force of the Stone-Splitting Fist, while the amount of inner energy consumed was almost the same! More importantly, the method of force generation was more concentrated, and the penetrating power was stronger!

"Good!" Lin Chen withdrew his fist, his eyes filled with even greater joy. This was only the first move, not yet fully mastered, yet it already possessed such power. If he were to master all seven moves and integrate them, its power would be unimaginable.

He didn't rush things, patiently practicing the first move again and again, meticulously experiencing every subtle point of force application, adjusting the rhythm of his internal energy circulation until the movement became standard and the power flowed smoothly.

Next, he began trying the second move, "Vajra Subduing Demons," a powerful side-striking strike specifically designed to break through defenses; the third move, "Vajra Probing Claw," a swift and ruthless grappling technique; the fourth move, "Vajra Backing Mountain," a close-quarters combat technique using the shoulder, elbow, and knee as weapons, fierce and violent…

He was immersed in this new world of martial arts, completely absorbed. His past life as a martial arts master allowed him to understand the moves extremely quickly, and his coordination and control far surpassed that of ordinary people. Coupled with the continuous energy replenishment and clear mind brought by [Affinity Nourishment], as well as the incredibly detailed diagrams and annotations he had just acquired, his cultivation progress was astonishingly fast.

However, mid-grade Yellow Rank martial arts were ultimately extraordinary, with complex moves and an even more intricate and sophisticated internal energy circulation path. When he reached the fifth move, "Vajra Wrathful Gaze" (a move that intimidates opponents with its imposing aura, combined with special sound waves and eye-catching techniques), he encountered a bottleneck. This move not only required an extremely fast internal energy circulation but also demanded the integration of mental will into the fist's intent, projecting it outward through gaze and roar to intimidate the enemy. It placed extremely high demands on mental strength.

Lin Chen tried several times, but either his internal energy circulation couldn't keep up, or he couldn't concentrate his mind, unable to find the right method. Instead, his blood and qi churned, and his head felt slightly throbbing.

He stopped practicing, his brow furrowed slightly. Knowing he couldn't rush it, he stopped forcing himself to push forward and instead repeatedly practiced the first four moves to solidify his foundation, while carefully studying the diagrams and mental techniques of the fifth move.

Time flew by in focused practice. Before he knew it, the sun was setting.

Lin Chen was radiating heat, his inner shirt soaked with sweat. He swallowed a "Qi and Blood Pill," a reward from a previous mission, which he hadn't yet used. He then circulated his inner energy using the *Small Circulation Internal Breathing Technique* for a moment to replenish his energy. The pill transformed into a warm current, nourishing his body and meridians, which were slightly fatigued from the intense fist technique training.

Just as the Qi and Blood Pill's medicinal power fully dissolved, completely merging with his original internal energy and the four energies provided by the [Affinity Nourishment] to form a perfect cycle—

"Buzz!"

In his dantian, the wisp of internal energy, now as thick as a thumb, with a rich, subtle golden sheen, suddenly trembled violently! It was as if a reservoir overflowing with water had finally burst its final sluice gate!

A power even more powerful and refined than when he broke through to the mid-stage of the ninth rank erupted from the depths of his dantian, instantly surging through his limbs and bones, flowing into every meridian and every inch of his flesh! His bones crackled and popped like popping beans, his muscles felt as if they were being repeatedly squeezed and hammered by an invisible hand, becoming denser and more resilient. His blood and qi flowed like a mighty river, making a rushing sound. His five senses instantly reached a new level; even the faint sounds of insects crawling in the bamboo forest outside the courtyard could be clearly heard!

Reaching the late stage of the Ninth Rank, it was a natural progression, and he broke through in one fell swoop!

Not only that, at the moment of breakthrough, the internal energy within his body, which had become exceptionally active and powerful due to his practice of the Vajra Fist, seemed to resonate with the "Vajra" intent of the Vajra Fist on a deeper level. An indescribable, overwhelming sense of power, as if capable of shaking mountains, filled his heart. His understanding of the first four forms of the Vajra Fist instantly reached a new level, and the previously obscure fifth form, "Vajra's Wrathful Gaze," was now faintly approaching its threshold!

"Hoo—" Lin Chen exhaled a long, lingering breath, which condensed into a foot-long white arrow of energy in front of him, lingering for a long time. He slowly finished his practice, opened his eyes, and his gaze was bright and clear, with a fleeting golden glint before receding, making him appear even more profound and serene.

He felt the surging power within his body, far exceeding his previous strength, and casually threw a punch at a foot-thick bluestone block used for practicing his fist techniques, about three zhang away!

He didn't use any internal energy; it was solely the power of his physical body that generated the force of his punch.

"Boom!"

The air exploded where the fist struck! The blue stone pillar actually swayed slightly, and stone chips fell from the spot where the fist had swept across!

"Good!" Lin Chen felt a surge of joy. At the late stage of the ninth rank, combined with his nascent understanding of the Vajra Fist, his strength was now far greater than before his breakthrough! If he were to face guards like Liu San and Wang Wu, who were at the early to mid-stage of the ninth rank, a single punch would likely render them completely incapacitated. Even against ordinary late-stage ninth-rank martial artists, he was confident he could defeat them with the exquisite and powerful Vajra Fist!

He walked to the water vat in the courtyard, scooped up some cold water, washed his face, and calmed himself. The improvement in strength was certainly gratifying, but the road ahead was long. He had only practiced four forms of the Vajra Fist; the final two forms, "Vajra Pushing the Mountain," and the ultimate killing move, "Vajra Nirvana," were still untouched. Moreover, mastering a fist technique was not a matter of a day's work; it required countless hours of practice to internalize the moves and unleash their maximum power.

“Next, besides continuing my arduous training in the Vajra Fist to solidify my realm, I should also consider the next sign-in location.” Lin Chen wiped the water droplets from his face, gazing at the setting sun in the west. “Longcui Nunnery…Miaoyu…”

The detailed outline indicated that this would be the next important location. What kind of sign-in reward would that aloof and self-assured Master Miaoyu offer? And what kind of energy would she provide?

He felt a faint sense of anticipation.

Night fell, and candlelight flickered in Tingzhu Pavilion. The young man's cross-legged figure was reflected in the window, serene as an abyss, yet containing within him a power like Vajra, constantly being forged and growing ever more dazzling.


Chapter 12 Miaoyu's First Appearance, Qingxin Encounters Enchantment

Having broken through to the late ninth rank and glimpsed the essence of the Vajra Fist, Lin Chen could clearly feel a qualitative leap in his strength. His internal breath became even more powerful and refined, flowing through his meridians, faintly carrying the sound of wind and thunder. With every gesture, a sense of power surged, as if a gentle grip could crush metal and iron. More importantly, the Vajra Fist's completely different, more exquisite and complex internal energy circulation was subtly expanding and strengthening his meridians, making his martial foundation increasingly solid and solid. 

However, the rapid increase in strength, especially after the succubus physique advanced to the "mid-awakening stage," seemed to bring some new changes. Even though he was now skilled in using the [Enchantment Containment] skill to restrain most of the enchanting aura, when fully focused on cultivation or when his mind fluctuated, a faint yet purer inner attraction would still unconsciously emanate from him. This aura was not intentional, but seemed to carry a strange "spirit," reaching deep into the heart. 

"It still needs to be controlled more precisely." Lin Chen noticed this and thought to himself. In this Jia residence's inner courtyard, excessive, even extremely reserved "attraction" could attract unnecessary attention or even trouble. Especially the place they were about to visit...... 

He changed into a simpler moon-white fine cloth robe, concealing his aura to make himself look more like an ordinary youth with a clean temperament and a touch of scholarly air. Today's goal was the next check-in spot mentioned in the detailed outlet—Longcui Nunnery. 

Longcui Nunnery is located on the east side of the Grand View Garden, near the border of Ningguo Prefecture. It is an independent and tranquil nunnery. The nun master, Miaoyu, was originally a young lady from an official family in Suzhou. She was often ill from a young age, entered the monastic life, and practiced with her hair tied up. Later, after her master passed away, she was invited by the Jia family to practice here. She was exceptionally beautiful and talented, yet naturally aloof, obsessed with cleanliness, and disliked socializing, making her a very special presence in the Jia household. 

Lin Chen's understanding of Miaoyu mostly comes from the original novel. In this high-martial world, this extraordinary woman cultivating with her hair down must be no ordinary person. The Longcui Nunnery where she resides is both a quiet Buddhist sanctuary; perhaps she can sign in to obtain some items that help calm the mind and suppress allure—exactly what he needs right now. 

He left Tingzhu Pavilion and began the Lightness Step, swiftly moving through the somewhat bleak winter garden. After breaking through to the late ninth rank, the Agile Step became even more skillful to perform, faster, less consumable, leaving no trace on snow and landing silently. Before long, he passed through several pavilions and towers, approaching a plum grove to the east. 

Even before approaching, a cool plum fragrance wafted over, refreshing and refreshing. This plum grove was quite large, with red, white, and green plum blossoms blooming in competition, standing out even more gracefully against the backdrop of ice and snow. Deep within the plum grove, a corner of a blue-gray nunnery wall peeked out. Inside the wall, ancient trees towered overhead, and the faint sound of bells and chimes could be heard. The chanting of Buddhist chants drifted on the wind, refreshing the mind. 

Lin Chen slowed his pace, concealed his aura to the extreme, and walked along the plum grove path toward Longcui Nunnery. The air was filled not only with the scent of plum blossoms, but also with an extremely pure sandalwood scent that seemed to wash away the dust. It was completely different from the cold fragrance of Xiaoxiang Pavilion, the cold fragrance pills of Hengwu Courtyard, and even the playful makeup charm of Pear Fragrance Courtyard, making it appear even more ethereal and solitary. 

The nunnery gate was ajar, with a small wooden plaque hanging above it, inscribed with the three characters 'Longcui Nunnery.' The handwriting was slender and sharp, exuding a strong character. Beside the door was a couplet: 'Even with a thousand-year-old iron threshold, one must still have a rustic steamed bun.' The brushwork is desolate, carrying a sense of coldness as if seeing through the mundane world. 

Lin Chen stopped outside the nunnery gate but did not knock. He had come mainly to sign in, with no intention of disturbing the quiet cultivation inside. His gaze swept over the nunnery gate, the plum grove, and the ancient trees, and his thoughts stirred: 

"System, sign in at Longcui Nunnery." 

"Ding! Detected host first stepping into the important location of the Red Mansion [Longcui Nunnery], triggering the location for first sign-in! ” 

"Check-in successful!" Congratulations, host, you have received: Pure Heart Tea x1 (Mortal Grade top-grade, special item. Stir-fried from the tender leaves of several spiritual herbs that calm the mind. After drinking, it effectively suppresses restless qi and blood, calms emotional turmoil, provides excellent resistance to enchanting auras and mental disturbances, and can enhance temperament during assisted cultivation. Long-term consumption has a slight effect on refining the spirit). Appendix: A tea recipe (can be replicated by collecting materials yourself, but the effect is halved). ” 

A cool aura accompanied the information into his mind—it was the detailed description of the "Calming Heart Tea" and the tea recipe. Lin Chen was delighted; this was exactly what he urgently needed right now! Not only could it help him better control and restrain the spillover caused by his succubus constitution, but it also assisted cultivation, enhanced his temperament, and fought against external evil—a win-win situation. Moreover, there was a tea recipe, meaning he could continuously obtain it. 

He immersed his thoughts into the system space, where a small plain white porcelain jar appeared. The jar was cold and inscribed with the words "Clear Heart." Inside the jar were about three taels of tea leaves, delicate and emerald-green, faintly radiating spiritual light. Even before opening, a faint, elegant fragrance that calmed the mind emanated. 

Check-in complete—the goal had been achieved. Lin Chen didn't want to stay any longer and was about to turn and leave when he suddenly heard faint footsteps and the gentle tapping of a wooden bucket against the well rim from inside the hermitage. 

Did someone come out to fetch water? 

Lin Chen paused slightly and instinctively looked toward the source of the sound. Not far to the right of the hermitage gate stood an ancient well built of bluestone, its edge covered in a thin frost. A slender figure was carrying a small wooden bucket, walking out through the inner side door of the hermitage toward the ancient well. 

It was a young girl dressed in a moon-white monk's robe, covered with a blue crepe silk cloak. The monk's robe was loose, yet it could not hide her graceful figure. The hood of the cloak was not worn, revealing a head of long, cloud-black hair. It wasn't shaved but loosely styled with a simple ebony hairpin, a few strands falling along her cheeks, adding a touch of casualness and ethereal elegance. Her skin was extremely fair, almost translucent, with eyebrows and eyes like a painting, a graceful nose bridge, pale lips, and a face that was elegant and elegant, without any trace of worldly affection. Yet between her brows hung a faint, almost innate aloofness, and her gaze was calm and unruffled. When she looked around, she carried a detachment and indifference as if overlooking the mortal world. 

It was none other than Miaoyu, the master of Longcui Hermitage. 

She carried the wooden bucket, walked lightly to the well, set it down, tied the well rope, and moved with a unique rhythm, as if she were not drawing water, but performing a ritual of calming the mind. Winter sunlight filtered through the plum branches, casting a faint glow over her, making her appear as icy and jade-like as if she were an ordinary person. 

Lin Chen withdrew all his presence and stood quietly behind the plum tree, unwilling to disturb anyone. But just as Miaoyu bent down to throw the bucket into the well, her movements paused almost imperceptibly. 

She seemed to sense something, tilted her head slightly, and her cold gaze drifted toward the plum tree where Lin Chen was hiding. 

Their gazes silently met across the sparse plum branches and the chilly air. 

Lin Chen's heart tightened slightly. He knew his aura had been suppressed to the utmost, and his steps were extremely light, yet Miaoyu could sense something? This woman truly is extraordinary—either born with keen spiritual sense, or...... She possesses cultivation unknown to others. 

Miaoyu's gaze fell on Lin Chen, and in those cold, still, still eyes, a barely noticeable ripple flashed across her eyes. 

The young man before him stood among red plum blossoms and white snow, dressed in a plain robe, his posture upright and upright. His features were exceptionally handsome, but what attracted Miaoyu was not just his looks. It was the ...... peculiar aura radiating from him. 

It was an extremely reserved, calm temperament like a deep pool, as if able to absorb all the surrounding disturbances and dust, leaving only a piece of clear tranquility. But deep within this clear tranquility, Miaoyu's spiritual sense, far beyond ordinary people and especially sharp from years of secluded cultivation, caught a trace of a very subtle "quality" completely different from anyone she usually interacted with. 

It was not the filth of the turbulent world, nor the desire of the mundane, but a kind of ...... A warm, vibrant "attraction" closer to the primal life force, like earthfire surging deep within the frozen earth, or like the first ray of dawn shining on a solitary pine on a sheer cliff. This aura was completely at odds with the Buddhist Zen philosophy she cultivated, aimed at purity and extinction, the sandalwood and plum fragrance she had permeated for years, and the solitary and cold she deliberately maintained, even forming a silent yet indispensable "collision." 

Even stranger, in this "collision," her usually calm and indifferent heart lake uncontrollably stirred with a faint, unfamiliar ripple even to herself. It was a kind of ...... mixed with curiosity, inquiry, and a near-instinctive feeling of "attraction" that even she herself refused to admit. 

She had been frail since childhood, cultivated with her hair tied, read scriptures and worshipped Buddhism, and prided herself on seeing through appearances and understanding of her character. In the Jia household, even someone like Baoyu was nothing more than a "fool" in her eyes. But this young man before her...... He had clearly restrained everything, so why did his heart, which should have been calm and unruffled, soften for a moment? 

Miaoyu immediately focused, forcibly suppressing that trace of strangeness, her gaze returning to a cold clarity, as if the ripples from earlier had never appeared. She withdrew her gaze, no longer looking at Lin Chen, as if he were nothing more than a plum tree, a rock. Her fingers gripping the well rope tightened almost imperceptibly. 

"Ding!" Detected that an important plot character, 'Miaoyu', has shown an extremely faint but special mental touch to the host, triggering a slight passive effect of the succubus constitution. Gained Miaoyu's favorability +5 (curiosity mixed with alienation). Obtained a wisp of pure energy (characterized by clarity, solitude, and purity, implying Zen meaning), which has automatically nourished the martial foundation and slightly improved internal breath purity and mental stability, showing a faint resonance and amplification of the 'Clear Heart Tea' effect. ” 

The system's notification sounded in Lin Chen's mind. At the same time, an energy completely different from the previous four golden beauties quietly merged into his dantian. This energy was extremely clear and cold, like mountain snow and water, carrying a solitary and pure chill, yet also containing a faint, soothing Zen aura. As soon as it entered his body, it quickly merged into his inner energy, making the previously fierce and restless energy from Practicing Vajra Fist purer and calmer, and his focus and stability improved. More importantly, this energy seemed to resonate faintly with the "Clear Heart Tea" he had just obtained, instantly deepening his understanding of the effects of "Clearing the Heart." 

The energy provided by Miaoyu actually leaned toward "purification" and "calming the mind"? It truly matched her identity and temperament. Lin Chen thought to himself that this energy would be quite beneficial for stabilizing his current realm, tempering his temperament, and even controlling his enchanting aura. Although his favorability was only a mere 5 points, the quality was extremely high. 

Since it had already been discovered, hiding it would only make him appear more sneaky. Lin Chen stepped out from behind the plum tree, pressed his palms together toward Miaoyu, and bowed slightly, his demeanor respectful but neither arrogant nor servile: "I am Lin Chen, passing by a precious temple. I did not mean to disturb Master's quiet cultivation. Please forgive me." ” 

Miaoyu had already lifted the wooden bucket, its clear water shimmering, reflecting the daylight. She did not turn around, but in a cool, calm voice said, "This is a place of pure cultivation. Since you do not worship Buddha, please do as you wish." Her voice was as gentle as a jade chime, pleasant to the ear but carrying a chilling aura that kept people at arm's length, as if drawing an invisible boundary between them. 

"I was presumptuous." Lin Chen said no more, bowed once more, then turned and left along the path he had come, walking calmly without a trace of lingering attachment. 

It wasn't until Lin Chen's figure disappeared at the end of the plum grove path that Miaoyu slowly straightened up, picked up her bucket, and did not immediately return to the hermitage. She stood where she was, gazing in the direction the young man had left, a faint trace of confusion flashing in her cold eyes. 

"Lin Chen......" she softly repeated the name. Though she lived a reclusive life, she was not completely ignorant of the world. Recently, she had heard some rumors in the mansion about this collateral son from the Western Courtyard. She had thought he was just a rough martial artist with a fierce temperament, but seeing him today...... It seemed that was not the case. 

His calm and reserved aura, yet beneath it a strange gravity, and the calm and unmoved composure he showed when he left earlier...... Were completely different from the rumors. 

More importantly, in that instant, her mind stirred...... Was it because her cultivation was insufficient, and her inner demons had just arisen? Or was it this person...... Was she really something special? 

She shook her head, dispelling these chaotic thoughts. She had entered the monastic path (though her hair was still alive); the worldly realms were nothing but dreams and illusions. No matter how special that person was, it had nothing to do with her. 

However, on the way back to the hermitage with the bucket, the faint, cool, and warm scent of the young man still seemed to linger at the tip of her nose, mingling with the scent of plum and sandalwood, stirring a faint, subtle sensation in her usually cold and lonely heart, one even she herself hadn't noticed...... Ripples. 

Back at Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen closed the courtyard gate and immediately took out the jar of "Calming Heart Tea." 

The moment the jar was opened, an indescribable fragrance filled the air. The scent wasn't strong, but it lingered unusually long. It cleared the mind, and the slight restlessness in the heart and the subtle allure brought by the succubus's constitution seemed to be gently smoothed by an invisible hand, becoming calm and reserved. 

He took the tea set and, following the brief brewing method recorded in the recipe, took a small amount of tea leaves and brewed them with boiling water. The tea leaves unfolded in the snow-white porcelain cup, the liquor bright bright and emerald green, like a pool of spring water. Curling tea smoke rose, carrying the cool fragrance of plants. 

Lin Chen picked up his teacup and took a light sip. The tea was slightly bitter at the mouth, then transformed into a cool, sweet warm flow that slid down his throat and quickly spread throughout his limbs and bones. This coolness did not bring cold; instead, it was like a clear spring cleansing. Wherever it passed, the restlessness of qi and blood was calmed, the flow of internal breath became smoother and gentler, and his spirit was greatly revitalized, becoming exceptionally clear and focused. Most remarkably, he felt his control over his entire aura had improved slightly; the natural influence of his enchanting constitution was cleverly balanced and restrained by the tea's power, making him more "obedient." 

"Excellent tea!" A look of satisfaction appeared in Lin Chen's eyes. The effect of this Calming Heart Tea was even better than he had expected. It not only aided cultivation and improved temperament, but also effectively helped him control his constitution and aura—truly a timely help. 

He slowly finished a cup of tea, feeling the refreshing and calming power continuously working within his body. Then, he sat cross-legged, and with the dual aid of Qingxin Tea and the pure energy provided by Miaoyu, began to circulate the 'Minor Zhou Tian Internal Breath Technique' to consolidate his late-stage ninth-rank realm while carefully comprehending the intent of the Vajra Fist. 

This time, entering meditation was especially profound. His mind was as clear as a mirror, his inner breath flowing like pearls, and his understanding of martial arts became ever clearer. The fierce and unparalleled intent of the Vajra Fist, blended with the Qingxin tea power, was less agitated and gained a more steady and weighty aura. 

When Lin Chen opened his eyes again, the sky was filled with stars outside the window. He felt his mind and energy replenished, his whole body relaxed, and his cultivation had improved further. He also had some new ideas about the spiritual techniques involved in the fifth form of the Vajra Fist, "Vajra Wrath Eyes." 

"Miaoyu...... Longcui Nunnery ......" Lin Chen looked out the window toward the east, which was the direction of Longcui Nunnery. Though only a few words were exchanged today, this aloof and extraordinary young woman had already left a deep impression on him and provided him with extremely useful energy and items. 

The beauties of Dream of the Red Mansion truly each have their own unique charms. Their bonds are not only pathways for physical advancement and energy absorption, but also seem to align with a deeper thread of destiny in this high-martial world. 

He carefully stored the Qingxin Tea; it needed to be used sparingly, so it could be used at its most critical moment. Now, with his strength steadily improving and his physical condition improved, it was time to consider his next steps. 

The next incident mentioned in the detailed outline seemed to be related to Jia She's troublemaking. Was that greedy and foolish Master She finally about to lose his patience? 

A cold smile curled at the corner of Lin Chen's mouth. Very well, it's a good chance to use this newly acquired Vajra Fist to test the "strength" of the Jia family's main branch. 

He blew out the candle and lay down clothed. In the darkness, his eyes shone brightly, like cold stars.


Chapter 13: Dealing with Jia She's Difficulties and Establishing Authority Through Martial Arts

The effects of the calming tea lasted all night, and by dawn, Lin Chen still felt refreshed and clear-headed. His internal energy, slightly agitated due to his breakthrough and practice of the Vajra Fist, became increasingly refined and peaceful, circulating smoothly under the combined nourishment of the tea's power and the refreshing energy provided by Miaoyu. His control over his alluring aura also reached a new level, flowing seamlessly and effortlessly.

He practiced the Vajra Fist in the courtyard as usual. Having broken through to the late stage of the ninth rank, his internal energy was abundant, and he felt the power of this mid-grade Yellow Rank fist technique increased even more. The first four forms, "Vajra Starting Hand," "Subduing Demons," "Probing Claw," and "Backing Mountain," were already quite proficient. His punches were like thunder and lightning, causing the air to vibrate. The heavy training stones he had specially found in the courtyard had been dented and cracked by his hammering. The fifth form, "Vajra Wrathful Gaze," involved the use of mental energy, which he was still exploring, but he could already barely generate a trace of fist intent. Coupled with his gaze, it could instantly intimidate the opponent's mind, proving quite effective in actual combat.

Just as he was immersed in improving his boxing skills, completely focused, a series of slightly hurried footsteps came from outside the courtyard gate, followed by a deliberately raised, somewhat arrogant announcement:

"Is Young Master Lin here? Master She requests your presence!"

Master She? Jia She? Lin Chen withdrew his fist, his breathing calmed, and a knowing glint flashed in his eyes. What was bound to happen had finally arrived.

Since he revealed his strength, alarming Grandmother Jia and Jia Zheng, and subsequently thwarted the provocations of Aunt Zhao and Jia Huan, attitudes towards him within the Jia family had begun to diverge. Jia Zheng's faction (including Lady Wang and Tan Chun) had begun to accept him and even intentionally court him due to his strength and "rules," coupled with their possible dislike for Jia Huan. Aunt Zhao and Jia Huan's faction, however, harbored deep hatred for him. As for Jia She, the eldest son of the Rongguo Mansion, who inherited the title of First-Class General but was mediocre and greedy, his attitude was the most nuanced.

Jia She was an ambitious but incompetent man, greedy and lustful. He harbored a deep resentment towards the second branch of the family (Jia Zheng's lineage), especially because Grandmother Jia favored them and he himself inherited the title but had no real power. The appearance of Lin Chen, a collateral descendant with considerable martial arts skills who seemed to have the attention of Grandmother Jia and Jia Zheng, was likely seen by Jia She as a potential threat rather than an asset—if he were to be won over by the second branch, wouldn't that further strengthen their influence? Moreover, Lin Chen's conflicts with Aunt Zhao and Jia Huan, to some extent, also damaged the reputation of his "eldest branch" (Aunt Zhao was, after all, his half-brother's concubine).

Lin Chen pondered for a moment, then made up his mind. He brushed off non-existent dust from his robes and calmly walked towards the courtyard gate.

Two men stood outside. The one in the lead was a middle-aged steward in a silk robe, with a fair complexion and a mustache, a trusted retainer of Jia She, named Zhan Guang (meaning "to benefit from someone's influence"). The other was a tall, thin man in gray robes, with a cold, hard face, slightly bulging temples, and sharp, eagle-like eyes. A sword was at his waist, and his aura was calm and restrained—he was actually a late-stage ninth-rank martial artist! He was likely one of the guards recruited by Jia She.

When Zhan Guang saw Lin Chen emerge, his gaze swept over him, a barely perceptible hint of surprise flashing in his eyes. The young man before him stood tall and imposing, his demeanor composed and his eyes calm and unwavering, completely unlike the "violent" or "arrogant" figure of the rumors. Instead, he exuded an air of unfathomable depth. However, emboldened by his status as Jia She's confidant and the presence of a skilled fighter beside him, Zhan Guang cleared his throat and drawled:

"Young Master Lin, Master She has important matters to discuss with you. Please come to the study in the East Courtyard immediately."

His tone, seemingly polite, carried an undeniable air of command.

"What brings you here, Master She?" Lin Chen asked calmly, not immediately setting off.

Zhan Guang frowned slightly, seemingly displeased by Lin Chen's "negligence," but remembering his master's instructions to "appease first, then resort to force," he patiently explained, "Master She understands your plight, knowing that your parents died early and you live alone in this household. Although you possess meager assets, they are unlikely to last. Now that you are of age and have mastered martial arts, it is the perfect time for you to establish yourself. Master She owns several estates outside Jinling City, rich in resources, but in recent years they have been neglected, the farmers are lazy, and the harvests are poor. Out of consideration for our shared kinship, Master She wishes to entrust you with an important task: to manage the estates at 'Black Mountain Manor' outside the city. This will provide you with a secure and prosperous place to live, and it will also help the household increase its income and reduce expenses—a win-win situation!"

Black Mountain Manor? Lin Chen vaguely remembered it. It was Jia She's most remote and impoverished estate, located near the mountains. The land was barren, the farmers unruly, and the managers had complained endlessly for years, resulting in consecutive years of losses. Jia She's move, ostensibly to "entrust him with an important task," was actually an attempt to drive this "unstable element" out of the heart of Jinling City and exile him to a remote place. This would cut off his contact with Grandmother Jia and Jia Zheng, and also use the trivialities of estate affairs and the harsh environment to wear him down. If he refused, he would be seen as "ungrateful" and "disapproving of his elders' good intentions," giving Jia She an excuse to exert further pressure, even resorting to family discipline.

"What a clever scheme." Lin Chen sneered inwardly, but remained outwardly calm. He simply looked at Zhan Guang and slowly said, "I appreciate Master She's kindness. However, I am young and inexperienced, and know nothing about estate affairs. I am afraid I am not up to the task. Moreover, I have been focusing on martial arts recently and am currently making progress. If I were to leave the manor rashly, I fear I would neglect my cultivation. I ask Mr. Zhan to inform Master She that I am willing to serve in the manor and share the burden of my elders, but managing the estate is not my forte, and I dare not neglect the important matters of the manor."

He refused decisively, offering sufficient reasons that both indicated his lack of intention to leave and pointed out that he was "currently making progress in his martial arts," implying that he would rely on his strength.

Zhan Guang's face darkened, his mustache twitched, and his voice turned cold: "Young Master Lin, you should carefully consider Master She's kind offer. There are established rules for employing people in this household. Since you are neither a direct descendant nor hold any official position, staying here will ultimately make you a guest. Black Mountain Manor, though far away, is a solid foundation. If you manage it well, it will be the basis for establishing your own household in the future. Perhaps… you look down on Master She's estate, or do you feel that staying in the household offers… a better future?"

His words already carried a threatening and provocative tone.

The gray-clad man stepped forward, a faint aura of a late-stage ninth-rank martial artist emanating from him. His gaze, sharp as a knife, locked onto Lin Chen, and he said in a deep voice, "Young Master Lin, the master's orders cannot be disobeyed. It would be best if you made a quick decision and accompanied Mr. Zhan to the study to report."

He was directly using his power to intimidate him, resorting to force.

Lin Chen looked at the two men before him and suddenly smiled. The smile was faint, yet it inexplicably made Zhan Guang's heart tighten. Lin Chen shook his head, his tone still calm, but carrying an undeniable firmness: "My decision to stay or leave is my own. If Master She cares about this junior, Lin Chen is grateful. But such 'kindness' is beyond my ability to accept. As for my future..." He glanced at the man in gray, a sharp glint in his eyes, "My future is none of Master She's concern, nor does it rely on any estate or fortune. It relies on the fists in my hands and the spirit in my heart."

"You!" Zhan Guang's expression changed drastically as he pointed at Lin Chen. "What an ungrateful scoundrel! How dare you disrespect Master She! Just for those words, you are guilty of disobeying your elders!"

"Disobey?" Lin Chen scoffed, taking a step forward. His step, though slow, was accompanied by an indescribable, heavy aura! Like a sleeping tiger suddenly opening its eyes! His previously restrained aura, as if released from some kind of constraint, burst forth with a thunderous roar!

The powerful inner energy of a late-stage ninth-rank cultivator, combined with the indestructible spirit of the Vajra Fist forged in the crucible of steel, and the restrained yet profound allure of the succubus physique, merged to form an invisible yet mountain-like aura that pressed down upon Zhan Guang and the gray-clad man!

"Uh!" Zhan Guang was merely a scholar with some literary knowledge; he had never witnessed such a martial artist's aura. He felt his breath catch in his throat, his chest tighten as if weighed down by an invisible boulder, his legs went weak, and he staggered back several steps, his face turning deathly pale. The remaining words stuck in his throat as he stared at Lin Chen in terror, as if he had seen a ghost.

The gray-clad man's expression changed drastically! He had initially thought Lin Chen was just a greenhorn who had just entered the late stage of the ninth rank, and that he, having immersed himself in this realm for many years and possessing a weapon, would be able to suppress him. But now, with Lin Chen unleashing his full power, he was horrified to discover that the opponent's inner energy was even more profound and refined than his own! Especially that fist intent, fierce and unparalleled, carrying an indomitable and unyielding will, even made this seasoned head of the guards feel his mind shaken, giving him a sense of being unable to defeat him! What was even stranger was that this young man possessed an inexplicable attraction, causing his originally concentrated fighting spirit to waver slightly.

"A master!" Alarm bells rang in the gray-clad man's mind. His right hand instinctively gripped the hilt of his knife, his inner energy surging to its fullest extent to resist the oppressive aura. Fine beads of sweat appeared on his forehead; he dared not underestimate the situation any longer.

Lin Chen did not attack, but merely used his imposing presence to intimidate. His gaze was like lightning as he stared directly at the gray-clad man: "Brother, are you going to make a move?"

The gray-clad man's Adam's apple bobbed, his lips dry. To attack? He wasn't entirely confident! The young man before him possessed a deep, unwavering aura, seemingly standing casually, yet his entire being was flawless. His eyes were terrifyingly calm, as if he had everything under control. He had no doubt that if he truly drew his sword, he would be met with a devastating blow.

Just as the tension reached its peak, and the gray-clad man found himself in a predicament, hurried footsteps echoed from the path outside the courtyard.

"Stop!"

A low shout rang out. Li Gui, Jia Zheng's personal attendant, arrived hastily with two equally imposing guards. Li Gui's face was serious. He first bowed to Zhan Guang and the man in gray, saying, "Mr. Zhan, Instructor Zhou, what brings you here? Master Zheng heard that Master She has something to discuss with Young Master Lin, so he specially sent me to invite Young Master Lin to Rongxi Hall for a chat. He has important matters to discuss with him."

His words were cleverly worded, both subtly indicating that Jia Zheng was aware of the matter and "inviting" Lin Chen away, thus providing a way out for both sides. At the same time, the two guards he brought, both possessing auras above the mid-ninth rank, subtly created a standoff with the gray-clad man.

Zhan Guang, feeling as if granted a pardon, quickly took a few deep breaths and wiped the cold sweat from his brow. The gray-clad man, Instructor Zhou, also secretly breathed a sigh of relief, slowly releasing his grip on his knife. He clasped his hands in a fist salute to Li Gui, saying, "Manager Li, we are here on Master She's orders to invite Young Master Lin. Since Master Zheng also has business to attend to, then… then we will report back to Master She first." Having said this, he glared fiercely at Lin Chen, then glanced warily at the guards Li Gui had brought, beckoned to Zhan Guang, and the two hurriedly departed, their backs looking rather disheveled.

Lin Chen slowly withdrew his aura, reverting to his calm and reserved demeanor, as if he weren't the one who had just forced a late-stage ninth-rank martial artist to hesitate to draw his sword. He cupped his hands to Li Gui, saying, "Thank you for your help, Steward Li."

Li Gui quickly returned the greeting, his attitude even more polite than before, even showing some respect: "Young Master Lin, you flatter me. Master is indeed waiting for you in Rongxi Hall. Please follow me."

On the way, Li Gui whispered, "Young Master, Master She... he's probably not given up yet. Master instructed me to convey a message: Young Master, please stay in the manor and focus on your martial arts. Master will handle the affairs of the manor. However, Young Master, you must be more cautious in your future actions, lest you give others a handle against you."

Lin Chen nodded: "Thank you for your concern, Master Zheng. I understand." Jia Zheng's actions undoubtedly showed his stance—to protect him—and also his dissatisfaction with Jia She's overstepping boundaries. It seemed that the value he had demonstrated was enough to make this de facto ruler of the Rongguo Mansion take a gamble.

Upon entering the study in the east wing of Rongxi Hall, Lin Chen found Jia Zheng seated behind his desk, a book in his hand, his expression serious. Seeing Lin Chen enter, he put down his book, his gaze sharp as he scrutinized Lin Chen from head to toe, a barely perceptible hint of admiration flashing in his eyes.

"Junior Lin Chen greets Master Zheng," Lin Chen bowed respectfully, his manner neither servile nor arrogant.

"Hmm, sit." Jia Zheng gestured to the chair below him. After Lin Chen sat down, he slowly said, "I am aware of what happened earlier. Master She meant well, but his consideration was inadequate. Since you are determined to pursue martial arts, you should diligently practice and not waste this talent. In the manor's library, there are some martial arts texts and insights from predecessors that I collected in my early years. You can use this token to read and study them at any time." He then took out an ebony token from a drawer and handed it to Li Gui, who then passed it on to Lin Chen.

This was undoubtedly a formal recognition of Lin Chen's special status in the manor and a substantial form of support. The martial arts texts in the library were exactly what Lin Chen needed at the moment, allowing him to broaden his horizons and gain insights into other areas.

"Thank you for your kindness, Master Zheng. Lin Chen will certainly work diligently and live up to your expectations," Lin Chen said solemnly as he accepted the token.

Jia Zheng stroked his short beard, pondered for a moment, and then said, “Your strength has improved significantly, and your talent is becoming increasingly apparent, which is a good thing, but it also easily attracts jealousy. If you encounter similar situations again in the future, you can speak frankly to me, or seek help from Lian’er (Jia Lian) or Zhu’er’s wife (Li Wan). There’s no need to stubbornly resist. Our Jia family rose to prominence through military achievements, and we will naturally support capable members of the clan. However, the clan rules and family laws cannot be lightly disregarded. You must remember this.”

These words were both a show of care and a warning. They provided Lin Chen with protection, but also reminded him to abide by the rules and not cause trouble beyond repair.

“This junior will remember your teachings,” Lin Chen replied. Jia Zheng’s attitude was clearer and more proactive than he had expected. This was certainly a good thing, but it also meant that he had officially been drawn into the subtle and complex power struggle between the eldest and second branches of the Rongguo Mansion.

Emerging from Rongxi Hall, Lin Chen clutched the ebony token, feeling little joy, but rather a heightened sense of calm. Although Jia Zheng had temporarily blocked Jia She's obstruction, given Jia She's temperament, he wouldn't easily give up. And Jia Zheng's support wasn't entirely without ulterior motives.

"Ultimately, it still comes down to one's own strength," Lin Chen thought firmly. The books in the library would be perfect for enriching himself, especially regarding more systematic martial arts knowledge of this high-level martial arts world, and information on potentially higher-grade techniques and combat skills.

He returned to Tingzhu Pavilion, took out some Qingxin tea, and brewed another cup. Amidst the refreshing aroma of the tea, he once again immersed himself in practicing the Vajra Fist, while simultaneously planning his journey to the library.

Outside the courtyard walls, the news that "Young Master Lin Chen's imposing aura forced back a late-stage ninth-rank instructor under Jia She, and he received a library token personally bestowed by Jia Zheng" had once again shaken the entire Rongguo Mansion like a whirlwind.

Lin Chen, an orphan from a collateral branch of the Western Courtyard, has finally established himself firmly within this wealthy and powerful family, becoming a rising force that cannot be ignored, thanks to his formidable martial arts skills and unwavering composure.

As night deepened, the lights of Tingzhu Pavilion shone exceptionally brightly and serenely in the cold night.


Chapter 14 Xiangyun is drunk and frankly expresses her feelings.

Jia She's troublemaking was temporarily quelled thanks to Jia Zheng's strong intervention. But behind the scenes, the subtle struggle between the main and second branches, triggered by Lin Chen's sudden rise as a collateral descendant, surged like an undercurrent deep within the Jia household. The servants were silent as cicadas, acting with increasing caution, fearing to get caught up in it. 

Lin Chen seemed unaware, immersing himself entirely in the cultivation of martial arts. With the ebony tiles in hand in hand, he became a regular visitor there. The Rongguo Mansion Library was quite large. Although it wasn't dedicated to collecting martial arts classics, it still had two rows of bookshelves filled with martial arts manuals, martial arts miscellaneous notes, notes from predecessors, and even some incomplete military manuals and battle strategies accumulated over the ages. Most were of low grade, mainly Mortal and Yellow-grade low-grade, with a few mid-grade Yellow-grade books that were already treasured items. But for Lin Chen, who urgently needed a systematic understanding of the martial arts system of this world and broadening his horizons, it was nothing short of a treasure trove. 

During the day, he spent his days in the library, voraciously reading and pondering. At night, he returned to Tingzhu Pavilion, where with the help of Qingxin tea, he practiced the 'Lesser Zhou Tian Internal Breathing Method' and diligently practiced Vajra Fist. Although the library's texts lacked direct advanced techniques, they greatly enriched his martial arts knowledge, offering many valuable ideas for power techniques, internal breath application, and adaptability in battle. Especially several notes from senior martial artists traveling through Great Chu and neighboring countries gave him a clearer understanding of the vastness and dangers of this high-martial world. 

The cultivation of the Vajra Fist was steadily advancing. The fifth form, "Vajra Wrath Eyes," had already begun to take shape. Although it could not "witness like a hammer, shattering the soul gallbladder" as recorded, when fully unleashed, it could clearly intimidate close opponents, and combined with fist techniques, its power increased. With continuous [Emotional Nourishment] and arduous cultivation, the internal energy grew increasingly profound, now firmly at the late ninth rank, moving toward perfection. 

On this day, Grandmother Jia happened to be in high spirits and held a small banquet in Rongqing Hall, inviting the women of the household, along with younger generations like Baoyu and Jia Huan, to join the festivities. Since it was a family banquet and it was winter, it was set up in a warm pavilion, slightly partitioned with a screen, making it quite cozy. 

At the banquet, golden plates and jade chopsticks were served, with a variety of water and land dishes. Lady Wang, Aunt Xue, Li Wan, Lady Feng, and the other sisters sat around the table. Baoyu naturally sat next to Grandmother Jia, and Jia Huan, Jia Lan, and others were also present. Since it was a family gathering, the rules were somewhat relaxed, and with Lady Feng's banter and Aunt Xue's jokes, the atmosphere was quite lively. 

Shi Xiangyun also came to the residence with her aunt. She had always loved lively gatherings and was cheerful by nature. She chatted and laughed endlessly with Daiyu, Baochai, Tanchun, and others, and with Fengjie teasing her from time to time, she was in high spirits. Midway through the banquet, everyone started drinking as ordered. Xiangyun, bold and unyielding, accepted everyone. With Baoyu, Daiyu, and the others egging her on, she unknowingly drank a few more cups. She was never very good at drinking and was straightforward. The alcohol surge in, her cheeks flushed, her eyes dreamy, adding a touch of charm and charm, and she started talking more and more. 

"Hahaha, this is a good order! I'll take another!" Xiangyun raised her wine cup, staggered to her feet, and was about to recite a poem when she suddenly felt the world spin and her legs went weak. 

"Yun girl is drunk!" Wang Xifeng, sharp-eyed and quickly signaling to the maid beside her, "Quick, help Miss Shi to rest and have a bowl of hangover soup." ” 

But Xiangyun didn't agree, waving her hand: "No...... Not drunk! I can still drink! Sister Lin, Sister Bao, let's ...... again. Have another drink! "Saying that, she went to get the wine pot again. 

Seeing her like this, Daiyu was both amused and worried. She quickly had Zijuan and Cuilu join in, persuading her to help her out of the warm chamber. They rested briefly in the back side room, then ordered someone to quickly prepare sobering soup. 

Lying on the couch in the side room, Xiangyun felt a heat in her heart and a tightness in her chest. The hangover soup hadn't arrived yet, and after lying there for a while, she felt increasingly stifled. She was naturally an active person; how could she possibly lie still? While Cuilü was rushing out to urge the soup, she struggled to sit up on her own, grabbed a felt orangutan cloak to put on, and staggered out of the side room, wanting to get some fresh air. 

The night wind blew, and a chill crept in. She shivered, but the alcohol seemed to fade a little, though her mind remained foggy. She strolled aimlessly, unable to find direction, just wanting to find a quiet place to stay for a while. Unconsciously, she passed through several moon gates and arrived at the Grand View Garden. 

Night had deepened, the garden was silent and empty, only the wind lanterns under the corridor flickered, casting dim yellow shadows. The lingering snow hadn't melted, reflecting the cold moonlight, adding to the chill. Xiangyun wrapped herself tightly in her cloak, walking with uneven steps. The cold wind stirred her, and the drunkenness returned, making her feel dizzy and unsteady, everything seemed to sway. 

"Where is this place?" How...... Why is it so dark?" She murmured to herself, leaning on a plum tree, breathing softly. Just as she was lost in thought, she suddenly heard a faint, rhythmic sound of something breaking through the air not far away, as if someone was practicing martial arts. 

"Who...... Who's there?" Xiangyun's curiosity was piqued, and she forgot her fear. Following the sound, she stumbled toward it. Passing through a bamboo grove, the view suddenly opened up—a spacious training ground, right next to the back wall of Xiaoxiang Hall. This place was remote, rarely visited on regular days. 

Under the moonlight, on the snowy ground, a blue figure was dodging and dodging, his fist wind howling, his momentum astonishing. Every punch he threw produced a dull explosion, as if it could shatter rocks; His footsteps on the snow left only faint marks, his figure drifting like a ghost—sometimes only afterimages, sometimes slow, as heavy as a mountain. It was Lin Chen cultivating the Vajra Fist at night. 

He had just mastered the fifth form, "Vajra Wrathful Eyes," and was trying to infuse his spirit and will into his fist technique. With a single punch accompanied by a deep shout, his gaze shot like lightning into the void ahead, shaking a withered branch as thick as an arm several feet away, causing it to tremble and snow to fall everywhere. 

Xiangyun was stunned. Although she didn't know martial arts, she could tell that the man before her was extraordinary—his fierce punches and sharp movements far surpassed any instructor she had ever seen.
