Sunlight slanted in, illuminating his handsome face and calm expression. His eyes, deep as an ancient well, swept over the crowd without a trace of emotion, finally settling on the group of noisy old women at the head of the group.

It was Lin Chen.

"Young Master Lin?" Tan Chun was taken aback when she saw Lin Chen, then a complex emotion flashed in her eyes—surprise, doubt, and a barely perceptible...hope. She didn't know Lin Chen well; she had only witnessed his prowess at the banquet and had occasionally heard of his abilities. But what was his purpose in appearing so suddenly?

"Who are you? This is the inner courtyard of my Jia family, where the young ladies conduct their affairs. How dare an outsider trespass?" Lai Xi's wife saw that the young man was an unfamiliar face. Although he had an extraordinary bearing, his clothes were ordinary and he did not seem like a distinguished guest. She immediately shouted in a fierce but inwardly weak voice, trying to pin the accusation of "trespassing on the inner courtyard" on him.

"I am Lin Chen, a disciple of the West Courtyard." Lin Chen walked forward slowly, and the old women blocking his path unconsciously parted to the sides, as if intimidated by an invisible aura. He walked to Tan Chun's side, standing shoulder to shoulder with her, and said calmly to Lai Xi's wife, "I overheard from afar that this old woman seemed to be instructing the Third Miss on how to 'not offend anyone'?"

His tone was calm, but the word "instructing" carried a hint of sarcasm. Lai Xi's wife choked, her face flushing red: "This old servant...this old servant is just telling the truth! The Third Miss is young, and some things..."

"Young, therefore ignorant?" Lin Chen interrupted her, turning his gaze to the old woman Fei, "A young lady shouldn't be involved in affairs? Then, in your opinion, who should manage the affairs of this manor? Should it be managed by you 'loyal servants'?"

"You...you're slandering me!" the old woman Fei shrieked.

"Slanderous accusations?" Lin Chen's lips curled into a faint smile. His gaze swept over the crowd, finally settling on the few old women who were clearly Lai Xi's and Granny Fei's confidantes and had been the most vocal in their clamor earlier. "I just heard that the Third Miss wanted to investigate old accounts and clarify expenditures, and some people jumped out crying and wailing as if they had suffered a great injustice. Others praised her while subtly criticizing her, saying that the Miss was young and naive. Still others spoke sarcastically, implying that the Miss shouldn't have offended anyone... I'd like to ask, the Third Miss is acting according to the rules of the household, checking expenditures, and cleaning up old malpractices. What wrong has she done? And who has she offended? Has she offended those corrupt officials who line their own pockets, or those arrogant, deceitful rats who take advantage of their age?"

His voice wasn't loud, but every word was clear, like ice beads falling onto a jade plate, striking everyone's heart. The words "moth" and "giant rat," in particular, were merciless, hitting the nail on the head. Many of the old women who had initially joined the clamor lowered their heads guiltily.

Lai Xi's wife and Granny Fei trembled with anger, Lai Xi's wife especially reckless: "What a sharp-tongued brat! You, a member of a collateral branch, dare to spout nonsense here? Do you really think you're someone important just because you beat up a few rascals? Let me tell you, this mansion is a dangerous place! You can't just barge in! If you know what's good for you, get out of here, or else..."

"Or else what?" Lin Chen's eyes suddenly turned cold, and he took a step forward.

He did not release his full power, but the inner energy of a mid-stage eighth-rank martial artist, refined and condensed like mercury through the initial tempering of the "Qi Control Technique," along with the spiritual pressure fused with the Vajra Fist intent, still transformed into an invisible, heavy pressure that instantly enveloped Lai Xi's family and Granny Fei! At the same time, he deliberately incorporated a trace of the dignified and sharp spirit of Princess Zhaoyang Zhao Lingxi, which contained the characteristics of "imperial nobility," into this pressure.

"Thump!" "Thump!"

Lai Xi's wife and Granny Fei felt their breath catch in their throats, as if an invisible hand had gripped their throats. Their chests tightened, their legs went weak, and they involuntarily collapsed to their knees! All color drained from their faces, and their eyes were filled with indescribable fear. They weren't martial artists, but having spent years mingling with high-ranking families, they had seen many people of status and power. The oppressive aura emanating from Lin Chen, so heavy and imbued with an air of nobility, made them instinctively tremble. It was as if they were facing a long-established, powerful figure, not a young boy!

The surrounding women and wives were so frightened they dared not breathe, retreating hastily, their eyes filled with awe as they looked at Lin Chen. The earlier clamor had vanished without a trace.

Tan Chun, standing beside Lin Chen, felt it most clearly. Although she knew nothing of martial arts, she knew Lin Chen was a martial artist, yet she hadn't expected that a single step forward from him could frighten two long-time, unruly servants to their knees! This strength, this aura… She looked at Lin Chen's calm profile, her heart filled with shock and an indescribable surge of emotion. When she was isolated and helpless, suffering constant harassment, he had suddenly appeared, so forcefully and directly calming the situation and rescuing her from the predicament.

"Third Miss, you are managing affairs according to the household rules and investigating long-standing malpractices for the long-term benefit of the Jia family. If anyone disagrees, they can go and explain the situation to the Old Madam, Master, and Madam. But if anyone dares to outwardly comply but inwardly defy you, incite trouble, or even commit insubordination and disrespect..." Lin Chen's gaze, sharp as ice, swept over the two kneeling figures and the trembling old women. "Then don't blame the household rules for being ruthless!"

Having said this, he withdrew his imposing manner and turned to Tan Chun, giving her a slight nod: "Third Miss, you've been frightened. Please continue. If anyone is so ungrateful and dares to make a fuss again, I, Lin Chen, am willing to punish them on your behalf."

Lai Xi's wife and Granny Fei, as if granted a pardon, slumped to the ground, panting heavily, not daring to utter another word. The other old women lowered their heads and shrank back, their arrogance completely gone.

Tan Chun took a deep breath, suppressing the tremor in her heart, and curtsied to Lin Chen. Her voice had regained its usual clear and steady tone: "Thank you for speaking up, Young Master Lin. You are right. Now that I have accepted this task, I will handle it impartially, regardless of what others say."

She turned, her gaze sharpening again, sweeping over the crowd: "You all heard what Young Master Lin said. Checking the accounts and clarifying the expenditures is imperative! Anyone with objections can now report to the Old Madam and Madam. If there are no objections, then everyone should return to their duties. In three days, I will see detailed expenditure ledgers for each household for the past three years! Disperse!"

The old women felt as if they had been granted a pardon, hurriedly bowing and retreating. Lai Xi's wife and Granny Fei were helped up by their acquaintances and slunk away, not daring to look back.

The crowd dispersed, and the flower hall returned to its usual quiet. Shi Shu and Cui Mo finally breathed a sigh of relief, their eyes filled with gratitude as they looked at Lin Chen.

Tan Chun approached Lin Chen and bowed respectfully again, saying, "Thank you so much for helping me out today, Young Master Lin. I am deeply grateful."

"You're too kind, Third Miss. It was nothing more than a small act of kindness," Lin Chen returned the bow, his tone gentle.

"It was nothing to you, Young Master, but for me, it was a lifesaver." Tan Chun looked up at Lin Chen, her eyes bright, devoid of the previous scrutiny and distance, filled only with sincere gratitude and undisguised admiration. "I've long heard of your esteemed name, Young Master. Your prowess at the banquet was astonishing. Seeing you today, I realize that you are not only highly skilled in martial arts but also possess a strong sense of justice and extraordinary courage. I admire you greatly."

"You flatter me, Third Miss," Lin Chen shook his head. "Your courage in facing criticism and taking responsibility to rectify the family's conduct is truly admirable. However, the household is riddled with problems; a single misstep can have far-reaching consequences. You must be more careful in your future actions."

Hearing this, Tan Chun felt a warmth in her heart, knowing his concern was genuine. She gave a bitter smile: "What you say is very true, young master. However, having taken on this task, I have no way out. You have also seen the current state of the manor; income is insufficient to cover expenses, and problems are rampant. If this continues, I fear it will collapse. Although I am of low status and a woman, I cannot stand by and watch our ancestral legacy crumble. But..." She paused, a hint of helplessness flashing in her eyes, "I have few capable people around me, and the resistance is considerable. Situations like today will likely be common in the future."

Lin Chen looked at the unwavering resolve and worry in her eyes, which seemed beyond her years, and sighed inwardly. This third young lady was indeed extraordinary. He pondered for a moment and said, "If you do not mind, young lady, I may be able to offer my humble assistance. I dare not speak of anything else, but if there are any more instances of people relying on their age and power, bullying others, or even secretly sabotaging or using force to coerce others, I can certainly handle them."

His words were a clear statement: he was willing to support Tan Chun's reforms to some extent and provide military support.

Tan Chun's eyes lit up, a surge of indescribable excitement welling up within her. She had been lamenting the lack of capable advisors, and Lin Chen's words were like rain after a long drought! Suppressing her joy, she solemnly said, "Young Master's righteousness is deeply appreciated! To be honest, I need capable people to help me reorganize the inner court and eliminate long-standing problems. Young Master is courageous, resourceful, and exceptionally strong—exactly the kind of assistance I've been seeking! If you don't mind the small tasks, I may need your help in many ways in the future."

"I am willing to serve you, Miss." Lin Chen cupped his hands in greeting. Cooperating with Tan Chun would not only grant him a shrewd and capable ally within the Jia family, but also allow him to gain a deeper understanding of, and even influence, the Jia family's internal operations, acquiring more resources and information—a win-win situation for him personally.

"Excellent!" Tan Chun finally showed her first genuine smile of the day, radiant like a spring flower in full bloom. "If you ever need anything in the future, or hear any rumors, you can come directly to Qiushuangzhai to find me, or inform Shishu or Cuimo. If I encounter any difficult situations, I will also send someone to inform you. Let's... cooperate fully."

"It's a deal." Lin Chen nodded.

"Ding! Detected that the important plot character 'Jia Tanchun' has developed a strong appreciation, gratitude, and trust for the host, establishing a preliminary cooperative relationship. The Succubus Physique passive effect has been triggered. Gained Jia Tanchun's favorability +15 (Appreciation). Gained a wisp of pure energy (bright, decisive, and containing a reformist spirit), which has automatically nourished the martial arts foundation, slightly improving mental agility and decisiveness. Bond Trait: Keen Insight (Slightly enhances the host's insight into potential opportunities and risks)."

The system notification sounded, and an energy completely different from Daiyu's elegance, Xiangyun's frankness, Baochai's mellowness, Xiren's gentleness, Miaoyu's clarity, and the princess's grace flowed in. This energy was bright and sharp, full of the reformer's boldness and wisdom, invigorating Lin Chen and making his thinking seem clearer and more agile. The "Keen Insight" trait was particularly useful, allowing him to better discern situations and people in this complex environment.

“Then I, Tan Chun, will not disturb you any longer, young master. Thank you again for today’s matter.” Tan Chun bowed again, clearly in high spirits.

“Take care, young lady. Farewell.” Lin Chen cupped his hands, turned and left, his figure quickly disappearing at the end of the flower-lined path.

Tan Chun gazed in the direction he had left, remaining motionless for a long time. Shi Shu stepped forward and whispered, “Young lady, this young master Lin… seems truly remarkable. With his assistance, your future endeavors will surely be much smoother.”

“Yes.” Tan Chun let out a soft breath, her eyes gleaming. “He is not only strong, but more importantly, he understands reason and knows what is important, unlike those brutes who only know how to be ruthless. With him on our side, many things will be much easier…” She paused, a slight smile playing on her lips, “…give the order that from now on, all expenses for young master Lin’s side be increased by 30% from the allowance for the direct descendants of the household. Any rare food or supplies should also be sent from Tingzhu Pavilion. It is imperative…not to be negligent.”

"Yes, Miss," Shishu quickly replied.

Tan Chun turned, gazing at the setting sun, her eyes filled with unprecedented confidence and fighting spirit. The thorny path of reforming the Jia family's backyard seemed to have become clearer and brighter because of someone's appearance.

Inside Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen sat cross-legged, sensing his newly acquired energy and characteristics, a slight smile playing on his lips.

His cooperation with Tan Chun had already taken its first step. Within the vast machine of the Jia family, another pawn belonging to him had quietly begun to exert its power.

Night deepened. But certain changes had already quietly sprouted in the darkness.


Chapter 23 Advanced Check-in at Longcui Nunnery, Miaoyu's Heart Flutters

Having established an initial partnership with Tan Chun and gained new energy and the ability to "recognize talent," Lin Chen's understanding of the internal dynamics of the Jia family had become even clearer. Tan Chun's reforms would inevitably affect the interests of many, especially the stewardesses who embezzled through old practices, and their overt or covert supporters. Choosing to side with Tan Chun would undoubtedly attract more hostility, but it also granted him access to deeper levels of involvement in the household affairs and to acquire resources. Risk and opportunity coexisted, and he was never afraid of challenges.

Back at Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen didn't immediately begin his cultivation. He sat cross-legged, carefully sensing the "reformist spirit" emanating from Tan Chun. This energy was bright and sharp, filled with a decisive force for breaking with the old and establishing the new, making his thinking clearer and more agile, and seemingly elevating his observation and judgment of his surroundings. Combined with his "keen eye for talent," he could vaguely sense that beneath the seemingly calm surface of the Jia family mansion, various entanglements of interests and shifting sentiments were unfolding before his "eyes" in a clearer pattern.

"Next, I should head to the Green Bamboo Nunnery for my second advanced sign-in of the month," Lin Chen thought to himself. Ever since triggering the "Princess's Exclusive Opportunity" on the street and obtaining the "Qi Control Technique" and the Breakthrough Pill, his anticipation for the advanced sign-in had only grown stronger. The Green Bamboo Nunnery was a pure Buddhist sanctuary, and also the place where people like Miaoyu, with her mysterious background and noble character, cultivated themselves. Signing in there would likely yield valuable items related to his state of mind, spirit, or special cultivation aids, which was exactly what he needed at the moment.

The initial stages of cultivating the *Qi Control Technique* have been achieved, greatly benefiting the refinement and control of internal energy. However, this method demands a high level of mental discipline, requiring constant mental clarity and freedom from external distractions. While the Tranquilizing Tea is beneficial, its quantity is limited, and its effects are primarily focused on "suppression" and "calming the mind," falling slightly short in terms of "enhancing" mental cultivation. Obtaining more advanced calming methods or resources through daily check-ins would be a tremendous aid in cultivating the *Qi Control Technique* and even mastering the succubus physique.

Moreover, it was time to have further contact with Miaoyu, this aloof and eccentric woman who practiced Buddhism while still keeping her hair. Their chance encounter by the well, though brief, had left a deep impression on Lin Chen with its clear, solitary, and Zen-like energy, and Miaoyu's subtle reaction. Although her favorability rating was only 5 points and her energy supply efficiency was low, her "Zen Mind Ripples" characteristic suggested she wasn't entirely indifferent to him. Deepening their bond would not only provide a more stable source of special energy, but might also allow him to access some of the secrets of Buddhism or the cultivation world.

Having made up his mind, Lin Chen changed into a more elegant, moon-white fine cloth robe, concealing his aura to appear even more calm and reserved, devoid of any worldly airs. He carried no gifts, going empty-handed. Given Miaoyu's temperament, ordinary worldly possessions would likely only invite her disdain.

It was afternoon, the winter sun warm and gentle. The plum grove on the east side of the Grand View Garden remained serene, red, white, and green plum blossoms quietly blooming under the warm winter sun, their subtle fragrance wafting in the air. The grey walls of the Longcui Nunnery, nestled deep within the plum grove, appeared even more tranquil and otherworldly.

Lin Chen arrived at the temple gate but didn't knock immediately. Instead, he paused for a moment, listening intently. The faint sounds of wooden fish drums and chanting emanated from within, their rhythm gentle and soothing. A faint scent of sandalwood and cold plum blossoms filled the air, creating a unique atmosphere that unconsciously calmed the mind.

He raised his hand and gently knocked three times on the ebony door, unhurriedly.

The sounds of the wooden fish drums and chanting paused slightly. A moment later, the temple door creaked open a crack. A young nun, about ten years old, with delicate features and dressed in grey robes, peeked out. She was the maidservant beside Miaoyu, named "He'er."

He'er was slightly taken aback when she saw a composed and handsome young man standing outside the door. She clasped her hands together and said in a childish voice, "Amitabha, esteemed benefactor, what brings you to our humble abode? My master is chanting sutras and does not receive guests."

"My name is Lin Chen. I once met your nunnery's Master Miaoyu. Today, passing by your esteemed temple, I was moved and came to pay my respects, wishing to consult with you on Buddhist principles and Zen. May I ask if you may let me in?" Lin Chen's tone was calm and his demeanor respectful.

He'er blinked, seemingly finding the name "Lin Chen" somewhat familiar. Seeing his distinguished bearing and polite manner, unlike the noisy worshippers, she said, "Please wait a moment, esteemed benefactor, while I inform my master." With that, she gently closed the door, her footsteps fading into the distance.

Lin Chen stood quietly outside the door, unhurried. He could sense that after He'er went in to announce his arrival, the gentle sound of the wooden fish drum inside the nunnery seemed to pause slightly before resuming its normal rhythm. It seemed Miaoyu already knew he had arrived.

About the time it takes to brew a cup of tea later, the nunnery door opened again. This time, it was Miaoyu herself who opened the door.

She was still dressed in a moon-white monk's robe, with a blue silk cloak over it. Her long hair was loosely tied back with an ebony hairpin, a few strands falling beside her cheeks, making her skin appear even whiter, almost translucent. Her beautiful face was expressionless, her eyes calm and still, like two bottomless, icy pools. However, beneath that calm surface, Lin Chen's exceptionally sharp senses, honed by cultivating the *Qi Control Technique* and his succubus physique, detected an extremely subtle, almost imperceptible ripple.

“Young Master Lin,” Miaoyu said, pressing her palms together and bowing slightly, her voice clear and cold like a jade chime, “I wonder what brings you to my humble abode today?” Her tone was distant, carrying a clear sense of detachment, but she didn’t directly dismiss him.

“I apologize for my intrusion, disturbing my master’s meditation,” Lin Chen returned the greeting, his attitude still respectful. “To be honest, I’ve recently been practicing a mental cultivation method that emphasizes a clear mind, undisturbed by external things. However, worldly affairs keep me busy, and my emotions fluctuate, making it difficult to reach a state of tranquility. I’ve long admired your profound Buddhist teachings and clear Zen mind, so I’ve come to trouble you, hoping to receive some guidance or perhaps find a quiet place in your temple to calm my mind.”

His words were half true and half false. It was true that cultivating the *Qi Control Technique* required a clear mind, but his main purpose was to use this as an excuse to approach Miaoyu and complete his attendance check. Moreover, this reason was relatively easy for a cultivator like Miaoyu to accept, and not so “vulgar.”

As expected, Miaoyu's eyes flickered slightly upon hearing this. Her cool gaze lingered on Lin Chen for a moment, as if trying to discern the truth in his words. The young man before her possessed a calm and reserved demeanor, with clear eyes, truly unlike those worldly people filled with ambition and scheming. Moreover, the peculiar aura emanating from him, the one that stirred her "Zen-like heart," seemed even more profound today, yet also even more... intriguing.

"The Buddhist path is vast, guiding those with affinity. Since you have come to cultivate your mind, please enter," Miaoyu stepped aside, her tone still calm, but at least she opened the door.

"Thank you, Master," Lin Chen stepped inside. The instant he entered the nunnery, a thought stirred within him:

"System, designate 'Longcui Nunnery' for your second advanced check-in of the month."

"Command confirmed. Host detected as being within the [Longcui Nunnery] area and interacting with the important plot character 'Miaoyu,' meeting the advanced check-in requirements. Initiating advanced check-in..."

The system notification sounded, and an invisible detection wave quietly spread. This time, Lin Chen could clearly feel that this wave resonated strangely with the serene, peaceful, and Zen-like "field" permeating Longcui Nunnery. When the wave swept over Miaoyu, it seemed to cause a very subtle reaction in her aura, causing her brows to furrow almost imperceptibly, but she quickly regained her composure.

A few breaths later, the fluctuations subsided.

"Ding! Advanced check-in successful!"

"Congratulations, host, you have obtained:"

**Purifying Heart Mantra (Mid-Grade Yellow Rank, Special Mind Technique)** This is an introductory method for calming the mind from a branch of Buddhism. It's a non-combat technique specifically used to cleanse the mind, suppress distracting thoughts, enhance mental cultivation, and resist external demonic influences (mental interference, allure, illusions, etc.). Long-term practice can purify the mind and strengthen the will, greatly aiding in comprehending profound techniques and breaking through cultivation bottlenecks. It complements the host's already mastered *Qi Control Mantra*.

**Jade Dew Tea x2 (High-Grade Mortal Rank, Spiritual Tea)** Made by collecting morning dew from flowers before sunrise, supplemented with the tender buds of several calming and soothing spiritual herbs, and processed using a special method. Drinking it not only clears the mind and brightens the eyes, relieving fatigue, but also slightly purifies internal energy and nourishes the soul. It provides additional benefits for cultivating techniques like the *Purifying Heart Mantra* and *Qi Control Mantra*, which demand high levels of mental and internal energy cultivation. Long-term consumption has a very small chance of improving comprehension.

Special Reward: Due to the high compatibility between the check-in location and the interactive character, you will receive an additional 'Zen Glimmer' x1 (a one-time consumable). After use, it will significantly enhance your understanding and affinity for Buddhist techniques, Zen principles, and states of mind related to 'stillness' and 'concentration' for one hour.

A generous reward! And perfectly suited to his current needs! The *Heart-Cleansing Mantra* is precisely the calming method he desperately needs, complementing the *Qi Control Mantra*—one internal, one external—together! Jade Dew Tea is an excellent aid to cultivation, its effects likely even surpassing the Heart-Cleansing Tea! As for the 'Zen Glimmer,' although it's a one-time item, it might have miraculous effects when used to comprehend certain key bottlenecks!

Lin Chen was inwardly delighted, but showed no sign of it on his face. He simply followed Miaoyu across the spotless bluestone path in the courtyard to a small meditation room in the side wing of the nunnery. The room was small and simply furnished: a table, a chair, a cushion, and a low table with an antique ceramic incense burner on it, burning a faint scent of sandalwood. Several sprigs of winter plum blossoms could be seen outside the window, elegant and pleasing to the eye.

“Please sit, young master,” Miaoyu said, gesturing for Lin Chen to sit on the cushion. She herself took a seat in the chair opposite him, her posture elegant yet aloof. “The matter of cultivating the mind that you speak of, young master, is vast in Buddhist teachings, and my knowledge is shallow; I dare not presume to offer guidance. However, having entered this path, it is a matter of affinity. You may sit quietly and feel the atmosphere here. If you have any questions, I may offer some answers.”

Her words were perfectly balanced, neither refusing nor overly enthusiastic, maintaining an appropriate distance while giving Lin Chen space.

"Thank you, Master." Lin Chen sat cross-legged on the futon, adjusted his breathing, and indeed began to close his eyes and meditate. He didn't immediately begin cultivating, but instead calmed his mind, trying to sense the serene Zen atmosphere permeating the quiet room. At the same time, he also spared a sliver of his attention to Miaoyu.

Seeing that he had truly begun to meditate, his expression serene, his breathing long and even, without the slightest impatience or anxiety, a barely perceptible hint of surprise flashed in Miaoyu's eyes. She also sat quietly, her gaze occasionally sweeping over Lin Chen's composed face before quickly shifting away to look at the winter plum blossoms outside the window, lost in thought. The room fell silent for a moment, broken only by the wafting sandalwood incense and the almost inaudible breathing of the two.

After about the time it takes for an incense stick to burn, Lin Chen slowly opened his eyes, looked at Miaoyu, and said, "Master, during my meditation just now, I felt a little more at peace, but distracting thoughts still floated like duckweed on water, rising again as soon as I tried to suppress them. Is there any method to completely clear the lake of my mind, leaving it undisturbed?"

Upon hearing this, Miaoyu withdrew her gaze from the window and looked at Lin Chen. Seeing his clear eyes and earnest question, which was not unfounded, she pondered for a moment and slowly said, "The mind is inherently like a mirror, obscured by dust. Distracting thoughts are like dust, impossible to wipe away completely, simply because the effort is still focused on the 'dust.' Consider this: a mirror is inherently clear; why should it be wiped? Only by letting the mind not dwell on appearances, not be swayed by circumstances, not follow thoughts when they arise, and not chase after them when they fall, like bird tracks in the sky or bubbles on water, coming and going as they please. In time, the lake of the mind will naturally become clear; this cannot be achieved by force."

These words contained Zen wisdom, directly pointing to the essential principles of practice: "non-attachment" and "non-discrimination." Lin Chen listened, and combining this with the key points of the *Purifying Heart Mantra* he had just acquired, a sudden realization dawned on him. His previous meditation had been somewhat driven by the intention to "calm down," and this intention itself was a form of attachment, becoming a new distraction. Miaoyu's words, "not following, not pursuing," perfectly captured the state of "observing the mind without mind, the mind naturally becomes empty and still" in the *Purifying Heart Mantra*.

"Master's words are like a sudden awakening," Lin Chen said sincerely, a flash of understanding in his eyes. "I was attached to appearances. Thank you for your guidance, Master."

Seeing his immediate understanding and extraordinary comprehension, Miaoyu's surprise deepened. Her tranquil mind seemed to have been stirred by an even clearer pebble. This young man was not only calm but also possessed such high comprehension? She nodded slightly: "Young Master's comprehension is extraordinary; you understand so quickly. This is not due to my own skill, but rather to your innate wisdom."

The atmosphere between the two seemed to have subtly warmed slightly due to this brief exchange of Zen-like questions and answers. The sense of distance, though not completely gone, was at least no longer so cold and rigid.

Taking the opportunity, Lin Chen took out a small, exquisite white jade jar from his sleeve (actually from his system space), one of the two "Jade Dew Tea" he had received for signing in. He gently placed it on the low table and pushed it toward Miaoyu.

"Master, your cultivation is arduous. This is a small amount of 'Jade Dew Tea' that I obtained by chance. Although it is not a rare delicacy, it is said to have the effects of calming the mind and nourishing the body and soul. I offer it to you as a token of my gratitude, and I hope you will not find it offensive," Lin Chen said sincerely. This Jade Dew Tea was beneficial to his cultivation, and its effects would likely be even greater for someone like Miaoyu who was also a cultivator. Presenting it to her was both thoughtful and unconventional, and perhaps it could further open up opportunities.

Miaoyu's gaze fell upon the white jade jar. Its surface was warm and smooth, faintly radiating spiritual energy. Although she didn't value material possessions, she was quite knowledgeable about tea ceremony and knew of the benefits of certain spiritual teas for cultivation. She had never heard of this "Jade Dew Tea," but judging from the vessel it was in and the subtle aura emanating from it, she knew it was no ordinary tea. This young man actually possessed such a thing? And he gave it to her so easily?

A slight ripple stirred in her heart. Was it a sincere gift, or did he have ulterior motives? If the latter, using such spiritual tea as bait, his intentions must be considerable. If the former… why was he treating her like this?

Miaoyu remained silent for a moment, not immediately accepting the tea, but instead raising her eyes, her cool gaze meeting Lin Chen's directly: "This tea is precious, why does young master gift it to this humble nun? We've only met a few times."

"Tea, however precious, is but an external possession," Lin Chen calmly met her gaze, his expression serene. "My master's guidance just now has been beneficial to my cultivation, that's one point. My master's secluded cultivation here, his strict adherence to precepts, is admirable; gifting her this tea is perfectly appropriate, that's the second point. As for the third..." He paused, a hint of sincerity flashing in his eyes, "I feel that this tea resonates with my master's temperament; in her hands, it might unleash its full potential. Giving it to someone worthy is not a waste of talent."

His words were frank, acknowledging gratitude while also expressing his approval of Miaoyu's cultivation and character. The final phrase, "given to someone worthy," directly revealed his appreciation for her. There was neither flattery nor intimacy, only an equal gift based on mutual respect.

The lingering doubts in Miaoyu's heart dissipated considerably under his open gaze and words. Having cultivated for many years, she had seen all sorts of people, and could discern sincerity from pretense to some extent. The young man's eyes were clear and honest, devoid of the unpleasant greed, desire, or scheming often found in cultivation. Moreover, his peculiar attraction to her didn't seem to stem from base, superficial desires, but rather from a deeper, spiritual resonance.

She lowered her eyes, her slender fingers gently tracing the warm jade jar. The slightly cool touch of the jar, combined with the subtle, elegant aroma of tea wafting to her nose, seemed to infuse her usually cold and desolate heart with a touch of warmth. How long had it been? Since her master's passing, she had been cultivating here alone, with only her companion, He'er, and no one else had offered her such a sincere gift, nor had anyone engaged in such a conversation about cultivation insights.

“Young master’s kindness is beyond my comprehension…” Miaoyu finally spoke, her voice still cool, but less chilling. She put away the jade jar and took out a small packet of tea, wrapped in plain white Xuan paper and exuding a similarly elegant fragrance, from a small brocade pouch she always carried. She pushed it towards Lin Chen.

“This is my homemade ‘Plum Blossom and Snow Tea,’ made with the first snow of winter and the stamens of the old plum blossoms at Longcui Nunnery. It’s nothing special, but it can purify the mind and cleanse the soul. Since you enjoy tea, young master, please accept it as a… return gift.”

This was undoubtedly Miaoyu’s initiative in extending goodwill, even a form of acceptance. Given her proud nature, for her to reciprocate was extremely rare.

"Thank you, Master." Lin Chen solemnly accepted the tea. He could sense the extremely pure fragrance of plum blossoms and the refreshing coolness of snow water within the "Plum Blossom Snow Tea." Although not as wondrous as Jade Dew Tea, it was still a rare and excellent calming beverage. More importantly, it represented Miaoyu's change of heart.

The two exchanged tea leaves and smiled at each other. Though it was only a very faint smile, fleeting even on Miaoyu's face, the atmosphere in the room had completely transformed from distant and cold to peaceful, tranquil, and even carrying a subtle, unspoken understanding.

"Ding! Detected a significant shift in the host's perception of the important plot character 'Miaoyu,' from indifference and curiosity to fondness and acceptance, deepening the emotional bond. The succubus passive effect triggered. Gained +10 favorability with Miaoyu (curiosity → favorability). Received a large influx of pure, aloof, and Zen-inspired energy, nourishing the martial arts foundation, significantly improving the purity of internal energy and mental stability, and greatly increasing the speed of comprehension of the *Qingxin Jue*. The bond trait 'Zen Heart Ripples' effect enhanced, allowing a faint perception of Miaoyu's general emotions (requires close proximity), and a slight increase in resistance and comprehension of Buddhist techniques and concepts."

The system notification and the subsequent surge of cool energy cleared Lin Chen's mind, instantly deepening his understanding of the newly acquired *Qingxin Jue*. The increased favorability with Miaoyu brought extremely high-quality energy, perfectly matching the environment of the Longcui Nunnery and her own characteristics, greatly benefiting him.

After sitting for a while longer, and sipping half a cup of the clear spring water brought by He'er, Lin Chen, seeing that the time was right, rose to take his leave. Today's gains had already far exceeded his expectations; he shouldn't be greedy.

Miaoyu didn't linger, personally seeing him to the temple gate.

"If you ever feel uneasy in the future, or wish to find a quiet place, you may come again," Miaoyu suddenly said softly as they parted. Her words were almost an invitation.

"I will certainly come again to disturb your peace, Master," Lin Chen said sincerely, bowing.

Watching Lin Chen's figure disappear at the end of the plum grove path, Miaoyu stood before the temple gate for a long time. The warm jade dew tea in her hand seemed to still retain the warmth and scent of that young man. The frozen lake in her heart, today, seemed to have been stirred not just by a single pebble, but by clear and warm ripples.

She looked down at her pale hands, her fingertips seemingly still lingering on the unintentional touch during the exchange of tea leaves—the heart-stirring warmth and… strange attraction emanating from the other's fingertips.

"Lin Chen…" she murmured, a flicker of confusion and ripples, imperceptible even to herself, crossing her clear, cool eyes.

Back at Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen immediately took out the *Qingxin Jue* (Heart-Clearing Mantra) and Jade Dew Tea. He practiced for a while according to the method, feeling his mind clear and free of distractions, his efficiency far exceeding his previous levels. He then brewed a cup of Jade Dew Tea; the tea was a clear, bright green, with a mellow taste and a refreshing coolness that permeated his entire body. It not only nourished his inner energy and spirit but also gave him a new understanding of the operation of the *Yuqi Jue* (Qi Control Mantra).

"The bond with Miaoyu has been initially established," Lin Chen said, finishing his tea, his eyes shining brightly. "Next, it's time to deal with the internal strife within the Jia family and formally choose sides. Jia She… I'm afraid he'll soon be unable to restrain himself."

He looked out at the deepening twilight, a cold smile curving his lips. With Tan Chun's cooperation, his goodwill with Miaoyu, the newly acquired *Qingxin Jue* and Jade Dew Tea, and his steadily improving strength, no matter what methods Jia She used, he was confident he could make him suffer a crushing defeat.


Chapter 24 Internal Strife within the Jia Family, Lin Chen Takes Sides

Upon returning from the Green Bamboo Hermitage, Lin Chen carefully stored away the *Qingxin Jue* (Heart-Cleansing Mantra) and the Jade Dew Tea, not rushing to practice immediately. While the newly acquired method and the spiritual tea were certainly welcome, he was acutely aware that the Jia family mansion, due to Tan Chun's reforms and his own rising power, was already rife with undercurrents, ready to unleash a storm at any moment. Especially since Master She, who already harbored resentment towards him, would likely not stand idly by as his position in the mansion became increasingly secure, and his connections with Tan Chun and even Jia Zheng's faction grew ever closer.

Sure enough, the afternoon after Lin Chen's return from the Green Bamboo Hermitage, an expected, yet more sudden, "visitor" arrived at the Tingzhu Pavilion.

The visitor was not Jia She himself, but his most trusted retainer, Shan Pinren. This man was about forty years old, with a fair complexion, a long three-pronged beard, and wore a brand-new sapphire blue silk robe and a square headscarf. He carried a bamboo folding fan. Although dressed as a scholar, his eyes gleamed, revealing a shrewd and calculating air. Behind him followed two servants carrying gift boxes covered with red silk, which appeared quite heavy.

"Young Master Lin, it's been many days! Your demeanor is even more refined than before!" Shan Pinren greeted everyone with a beaming smile as soon as he entered the courtyard, bowing and greeting everyone with an attitude bordering on obsequiousness.

Lin Chen was standing quietly under the plum tree in the courtyard, comprehending the subtle rhythms of the Qi Control Technique's manipulation of the primordial energy of heaven and earth. Hearing the voice, he turned around, his expression calm: "Mr. Shan, your presence is an honor. I apologize for not welcoming you sooner. May I ask what brings you here?"

"Not at all," Shan Pinren replied with a smile, gesturing for a servant to place the gift box on the stone table in the courtyard. "Master She has heard that the young master has been diligently practicing martial arts recently, making rapid progress, and is very pleased. Considering the young master's parents passed away early, and he has been supporting the family alone, it is truly not easy. Master She often says that the name 'Lin' cannot be written with the same brush. Since the young master is of the Jia family bloodline and is so accomplished, he should naturally take good care of him. This small gift is a token of Master She's appreciation; I hope the young master will accept it."

As he spoke, he stepped forward and unveiled the red silk. In the gift box on the left were two pieces of lustrous Shu brocade with intricate patterns, one a clear blue after rain, the other apricot red, both rare and exquisite. In the gift box on the right was an exquisite sandalwood box. Upon opening it, ten ingots of snowflake-patterned silver were neatly arranged inside, each ingot weighing fifty taels. Beside it was a small brocade pouch, from which several pearls the size of longan fruits, with an inner luster, were poured out.

Five hundred taels of silver, two bolts of fine Shu brocade, and several pearls. This "small gift" was considered a substantial sum for a member of the collateral branch of the Jia family, even somewhat excessive.

Lin Chen's gaze swept over the gifts, his face showing no joy, but rather a barely perceptible furrowing of his brow. Jia She's sudden show of goodwill, and such generosity, was definitely not a simple act of "favor." He looked up at Shan Pinren: "Master She's kindness is beyond my comprehension. I cannot accept such a generous gift without having done anything in return. Please, Mr. Shan, take this back and convey my gratitude to Master She."

Shan Pinren's smile remained unchanged, as if he had expected his refusal. He stepped closer and lowered his voice, saying, "Young Master, why be so formal? Master She truly appreciates your talent. You must understand the current situation in the manor. Although Second Master (Jia Zheng) manages the household affairs, he is ultimately a civil official, and in matters of economics, career advancement, and interpersonal relationships, he is inevitably... somewhat pedantic. Master She, on the other hand, inherits the title of First-Class General, and has extensive connections both in and out of court. There are many things that Second Master cannot handle, but Master She can. Master She has said that you are a prodigy and should not be confined to this small backyard. If you are willing, Master can personally arrange a substantive post for you, whether in the military or in a local position. With your abilities, it is not impossible that you will one day be ennobled and appointed as a general. Why... submit to others and be at their beck and call?"

These words were almost blatant attempts to win him over and sow discord. Ostensibly, they promised him a high position and promising future, but secretly they were trying to drive a wedge between Lin Chen and Jia Zheng, implying that Jia Zheng was "out of touch" and could not offer him a future, while Jia She could. They also subtly pointed out that although Lin Chen seemed to be valued by Jia Zheng, he was actually still "subordinate and at the mercy of others."

If it were an ordinary member of a collateral branch of the family, faced with such a generous gift and promise of a bright future, they would likely have been tempted. But Lin Chen, with his discerning nature, was not blind to the trap and scheming within. Jia She was greedy, incompetent, and jealous of talent. His so-called "appreciation" and "promotion" were nothing more than an attempt to recruit Lin Chen as a henchman to counterbalance Jia Zheng's faction, and perhaps even to be used as a pawn when necessary. Moreover, Lin Chen's cooperation with Tan Chun in reforming the inner court had deeply impacted the interests of Jia She's faction (including those stewards who were dependent on him), making the conflict between the two sides irreconcilable. Accepting such an offer now would be tantamount to seeking favor with a tiger, destroying one's own defenses.

“Mr. Shan, you flatter me.” Lin Chen’s expression remained unchanged, but his tone softened. “Master Zheng has been kind to me, and the Jia family is where I have made my living. Although I am not talented, I understand the meaning of loyalty and righteousness. My future is a matter of fate, and I dare not trouble Master She with such concerns. As for these gifts, please take them back as they are. Lin Chen is accustomed to poverty and cannot accept them.”

His refusal was decisive and left no room for negotiation.

Shan Pinren's smile finally faltered, a hint of malice flashing in his eyes, but he quickly forced a smile again, saying, "Why must you be so resolute, young master? Master She's sincerity is genuine..."

"Mr. Shan, there's no need for further words," Lin Chen interrupted him, his gaze calm yet unwavering. "Those who walk different paths cannot work together. Lin Chen's mind is made up; please leave."

Having said all that, there was no room for negotiation. Shan Pinren's face darkened completely. He stared at Lin Chen for a moment, then suddenly sneered, "Fine, a fine example of 'those who walk different paths cannot make plans together'! Young Master Lin, I hope you won't regret your choice today! Let's go!"

With that, he flicked his sleeve, leaving with two servants, not even bothering to pack up the gifts, and stormed off in a huff.

Watching Shan Pinren's departing figure, a cold glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. Refusing Jia She's offer of enticement meant that the two sides had completely broken off relations. Given Jia She's temperament, what followed would likely not be enticement, but coercion, or even… more direct methods.

"What's meant to come will come," Lin Chen muttered to himself, turning and going back into the house. He didn't put away the valuable gifts, leaving them scattered on the stone table as if they were just a few unsightly odds and ends.

That night, the moon was dark, the wind was high, and all was silent.

The Listening Bamboo Pavilion was completely dark, except for a tiny, flickering candlelight shining through the bedroom window, illuminating a figure who seemed to be reading under the lamp.

At the third quarter of midnight, a time when people are most drowsy, three shadowy figures, almost blending into the darkness, appeared silently atop the wall of Tingzhu Pavilion like phantoms. All three were dressed in black night clothes, their faces covered with black veils, revealing only a pair of gleaming eyes. Their movements were swift and their coordination impeccable, clearly indicating they were well-trained. The leader, slightly taller and with a deep, resonant aura, was clearly a mid-stage eighth-rank martial artist! The other two also possessed early-stage eighth-rank cultivation.

The three lay prone on the wall, observing for a moment. Seeing the courtyard silent except for a single light in the bedroom, a savage glint flashed in the leader's eyes, and he gave a hand gesture. The three figures moved with the speed and agility of three giant night owls, silently landing in the courtyard. They then formed a triangular formation, slowly approaching the lit bedroom, each already holding a poisoned hidden weapon gleaming with a bluish light, or a short, drawn blade, its cold gleam sharp.

The leader of the men in black approached the window, listening intently. He could only hear even, deep breathing coming from inside, as if the occupant was fast asleep. A fierce glint flashed in his eyes. He nodded to the two men behind him, then suddenly raised his foot, ready to kick the door open!

Just as his foot was about to touch the door—

a sudden change occurred!

"Buzz!"

A faint, yet soul-stirring, humming sound suddenly rang in the ears of the three! Immediately afterward, they felt an indescribable, mountain-like, and chillingly murderous mental pressure surge in from all directions like an invisible tide, instantly engulfing them! This was Lin Chen's mental shock, a fusion of the "Vajra Wrathful Gaze" fist intent and the spiritual essence of the *Qi Control Technique*, which he had deliberately activated!

Although this mental shock had limited effect on the black-clad man with the highest cultivation level, the mid-stage eighth rank, it still involuntarily slowed his movements by half a beat, causing a momentary lapse in concentration. The two black-clad men at the early stage of the eighth rank were even more severely affected, letting out a muffled groan, their vision blurring, their blood churning, and their hidden weapons nearly slipping from their hands!

At the same time, in the seemingly unguarded bedroom, the single candle flame suddenly went out! A figure in azure, like lightning tearing through the darkness, crashed through the window, carrying an unparalleled, terrifying fist force capable of topple mountains, descending upon the dazed mid-stage eighth-rank black-clad man!

The sixth move of the Vajra Fist—Vajra Pushing the Mountain! Lin Chen, having accumulating power for a long time, unleashed his full force! Driven by the *Qi Control Technique*, his inner energy surged like boiling magma along the unique power path of the Vajra Fist. On his fist, the pale golden inner energy solidified, faintly forming a tiny, rapidly rotating vortex that even caused the surrounding air to emit a low whistling sound!

The mid-stage eighth-rank black-clad man was, after all, a veteran. Though mentally stunned and distracted, his reaction in this life-or-death situation was incredibly swift. With a roar, he frantically circulated his internal energy, crossed his palms, and hastily met the attack upwards! The palm wind howled, carrying a chilling and vicious aura, clearly indicating he was no ordinary opponent.

"Boom!!!"

The fist and palm collided, like a thunderclap from a clear sky! A violent shockwave erupted from the two men, sending snow and dust billowing into the air! The mid-stage eighth-rank black-clad man felt an unimaginable force, combining ferocity and shock, slam into his arms like a destructive force. His prized, chilling palm power crumbled instantly before this supremely yang fist energy, like ice meeting boiling water!

"Crack! Crack!"

A sickening sound of bones cracking rang out! The man in black screamed in agony as the bones in both arms shattered inch by inch. He was struck as if by a charging bull, spitting blood, and flew backward, crashing heavily against the courtyard wall, leaving spiderweb-like cracks in the surface. He then slumped down, unconscious.

It all happened too fast! From Lin Chen's sudden attack to the mid-stage eighth-rank man in black collapsing severely injured, it was only a breath's time!

The other two early-stage eighth-rank men in black, barely recovering from the shock, were terrified at the sight. They didn't dare linger and turned to flee.

"Trying to leave? Too late!"

Lin Chen's icy voice seemed to emanate from the depths of hell. His figure blurred as he unleashed his Lightness Step to its fullest extent, leaving an afterimage in his wake. His true form appeared like a ghost behind a man in black, his fingers like halberds, swift as lightning, striking the man's vital acupoint in the back! His fingertips condensed internal energy, sharp as needles—a pressure point technique incorporating the characteristics of "Qi Control"!

The man in black felt a numbness in his back, his internal energy instantly stagnating, and he collapsed limply to the ground.

Another man in black had already leaped onto the wall, about to jump down, when he suddenly felt a chill on the back of his neck. A strange "sword tip," cold and neither gold nor jade, with a dark blue aura flowing around its edges, was silently pressed against the back of his neck. The "sword tip" wasn't a physical object, but rather a concentrated burst of internal energy, forced from Lin Chen's fingertips, faintly resembling the phantom of the "Azure Edge Sword"! It was a "qi sword" he had condensed using the *Qi Control Technique* to simulate the aura of a fragment of the "Azure Edge Sword"! Although it merely possessed the form and its power was far inferior to a real sword, its sharpness and intimidating force were more than enough to deal with a panicked early-stage eighth-rank martial artist.

"Don't move, and don't even think about committing suicide. Otherwise, I'll make you face a life-or-death dilemma." Lin Chen's calm voice came from behind him, causing the man in black to freeze as if plunged into an ice cave, his body stiffening, no longer daring to move an inch.

The battle lasted no more than ten breaths. Of the three formidable assassins, one was severely wounded, and two were captured—a swift and decisive victory.

Lin Chen dispelled the "qi sword" from his fingertips, casually sealing the pressure points of the black-clad man on the wall. He then lifted him and the other man on the ground and tossed them into the courtyard. He walked to the severely wounded and unconscious mid-stage eighth-rank black-clad man, knelt down, and pulled off his black mask, revealing a sinister middle-aged face he didn't recognize. He then searched the man's pockets, finding only loose silver coins, tinder, and poison; nothing to identify him.

Lin Chen wasn't in a hurry. He stood up and walked to the black-clad man who had been acupunctured but could still speak, looking at him calmly.

The black-clad man's eyes were filled with fear. He tried to bite open the hidden poison sac in his mouth, but found his jaw weak and unable to exert any force. Clearly, Lin Chen's strange finger strike had sealed the veins in his jaw as well.

"Who sent you?" Lin Chen spoke, his voice not loud, but carrying a chilling aura. "Jia She? Or someone behind him? Talk it out, and I'll give you a quick death. Don't talk..." His gaze swept over his unconscious companion on the ground, both arms shattered, "...I'll make you suffer ten times more than him."

The black-clad man trembled, his fear intensifying. They were all desperate criminals who had lived on the edge of death; they weren't afraid of death, but they feared a fate worse than death. This young man before him was ruthless, his strength ridiculously high, and he was definitely not a soft-hearted person.

"I...I'll talk!" the man in black hissed, his voice distorted with fear. "It's...it's Master She! Master She sent us! He said...he said that if he takes your life or cripples your martial arts, each of them will be rewarded with a thousand taels of silver! And...that leader is a master Master She invited from the north, named 'Poison Palm' Han Tao!"

It really was Jia She! Lin Chen's eyes flashed even colder. A thousand taels for his life? He really was willing to spend that much.

"Anything else?" Lin Chen said coldly. "Jia She sent you to kill me, surely not just to vent his anger? Who is he colluding with? Besides you, is there any other plan? Speak!"

"I...I don't know much..." the man in black stammered. "I...I only vaguely heard Han Tao mention that Master She seems to have some...connections with...a certain prince in the capital, and the Xue and Shi families of Jinling City. It seems...it seems they're plotting something big, requiring a lot of money, and...it also involves martial arts resources...We're just doing this for money, we really don't know the details!"

A prince in the capital? The Xue family? The Shi family? Martial arts resources? Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. This was a lot of information. It seemed Jia She's ambitions were far greater than just eliminating him, an eyesore, but also some kind of conspiracy involving the court and powerful families, concerning enormous interests! The Xue family were imperial merchants, the Shi family were marquises; if they were truly colluding with Jia She...

"Do you have any evidence? Letters? Tokens? Or do you know the time and place of their next meeting?" Lin Chen pressed.

"Yes! Yes!" the man in black said hastily, as if grasping at a straw, "Han Tao has an oilcloth pouch hidden close to his body. Inside it seems to be... a handwritten order from Master She, and... half a jade pendant, said to be a token! The next meeting seems to be in three days, at the 'Drunken Immortal Pavilion' in the west of the city, room number three, with... and a steward surnamed Wang!"

Lin Chen immediately went to the unconscious Han Tao and searched carefully. Sure enough, he found a small pouch carefully wrapped in oilcloth in the lining of Han Tao's inner garment. Opening it, he found a note stamped with Jia She's private seal, which read: "Han Yishi is instructed to act as he sees fit; a generous reward will follow. She." The handwriting was messy, but the seal was clear. The other half of the jade pendant was dark green, carved with intricate cloud patterns, and had a warm, smooth texture; it was clearly no ordinary item and must be some kind of token.

“Very good.” Lin Chen put away the note and jade pendant, then looked at the man in black. “You can leave now.”

The man in black was stunned, seemingly unable to believe it.

“Go back and tell Jia She,” Lin Chen’s tone was calm, yet carried a chilling coldness, “I have accepted the man he sent. I, Lin Chen, will remember this ‘generous gift.’ Tell him… to watch his step.”

Having said that, he released the pressure points from the man in black.

The man in black, as if granted a pardon, scrambled over the wall and fled, not daring to look back.

Lin Chen looked at the unconscious Han Tao and the other man in black whose pressure points had been sealed, hesitated for a moment, and decided not to kill them. He carried the two to the woodshed, bound them with sturdy cowhide rope, and gave them some sleeping potion to ensure they couldn't move for a while. These two might still be useful.

Having done all this, dawn was approaching. Lin Chen changed out of his clothes, which were stained with dust and a trace of blood, washed his hands and face, and cleaned up the traces of the fight in the courtyard. Then, he took out Jia She's warrant and half a jade pendant, examined them carefully for a moment, and a cold smile curled at the corner of his lips.

“Jia She, you brought this upon yourself.”

Without the slightest delay, he quietly left Tingzhu Pavilion before dawn and went straight to the study in the east wing of Rongxi Hall. At this hour, Jia Zheng was usually already up or reading in his study.

Inside the study, Jia Zheng was indeed already there, reading an ancient book by candlelight. Seeing Lin Chen arrive without prior notice, his expression grave, and his clothes still damp with morning dew, Jia Zheng frowned slightly and put down his book: “Brother Chen? What brings you in such a hurry?”

Lin Chen stepped forward, bowed, and then presented the warrant and half a jade pendant with both hands: “Uncle, last night some villains infiltrated Tingzhu Pavilion, intending to harm me. I have already repelled and captured them. This is the evidence found on the leader of the villains; please take a look, Uncle.”

Jia Zheng's expression hardened. He took the warrant and jade pendant, examining them closely by candlelight. When he saw the private seal bearing the character "赦" (pardon) and the familiar handwriting, his face turned ashen, and his fingers trembled slightly. He then picked up the half of the jade pendant and examined it closely, his eyes blazing with disbelief and rage.

"This...this jade pendant...it's the 'Double Blessings of Fortune and Longevity' pendant, specially bestowed by the Imperial Household Department last year for the Empress Dowager's birthday! There were only eight pairs in total, awarded to several imperial relatives and senior officials! Lord She...he actually used this as a token to collude with bandits of the Jianghu?!" Jia Zheng's voice trembled, filled with both anger and chilling disbelief. Brotherly discord had reached such a point! It even involved collusion with unknown forces, and might even implicate an imperial gift—this was a grave offense of disrespect!

"My nephew also extracted information from the captured thieves that Master She seems to be connected with a certain prince in the capital, as well as the Xue and Shi families of Jinling. Their ambitions are substantial, involving money and martial arts resources. They will meet with a steward surnamed Wang in three days at the Drunken Immortal Pavilion, room number three in the west of the city," Lin Chen added.

Upon hearing this, Jia Zheng took several deep breaths, forcibly suppressing his surging anger. His gaze towards Lin Chen was filled with complex emotions: relief, lingering fear, and deep guilt.

“Chen-ge’er, you…you’ve been frightened. Your uncle is truly sorry for this. I never imagined he…could be so ruthless!” Jia Zheng said in a deep voice, clutching the warrant and jade pendant tightly in his hand. “Don’t worry, your uncle will definitely give you an explanation! I will immediately present this evidence to the old lady and secretly report it to old friends in the capital. As for those thieves…”

“The seriously injured ringleader and another thief have been captured by my nephew and are being held in the woodshed of Tingzhu Pavilion,” Lin Chen said.

“Good! You’ve done very well!” Jia Zheng nodded, a sharp glint in his eyes. “For now, don’t make a fuss about this. Just tell everyone it was a thief attack, and they’ve been dealt with. Leave the rest to your uncle. You…must also be careful. Don’t go out easily these days unless absolutely necessary. I will send more reliable guards to protect Tingzhu Pavilion.”

"Yes, thank you, Uncle," Lin Chen replied. He knew that with this irrefutable evidence, Jia Zheng would not let go of this opportunity to strike at Jia She and purge his own family. And he, through this incident, not only completely won Jia Zheng's trust, but also placed a sufficiently heavy weight on the power scales within the Jia family.

As he left the study, the sky was already turning light. Lin Chen walked calmly in the cool morning breeze. Last night's attack, counterattack, and shift in allegiances had completely changed his situation within the Jia family. He was now irreconcilably at odds with Jia She's faction. And with Jia Zheng and Tan Chun's side, he had truly become allies bound together by interests and trust.

"The prelude to the internal strife has only just begun," Lin Chen muttered to himself, a cold glint in his eyes. "Jia She, the Xue family, the Shi family, the princes of the capital… this swamp is indeed deep. Good, let me see just how treacherous and unpredictable this world of martial arts and romance truly is."

He walked steadily towards Tingzhu Pavilion. Behind him, in the study of Rongxi Hall, the lights were bright, and Jia Zheng's figure cast a heavy shadow on the window paper, clearly making hasty preparations for the impending storm.


Chapter 25 An unexpected encounter with the princess, a martial arts sparring session

The incident of Jia She sending assassins to raid Tingzhu Pavilion at night, under Jia Zheng's strong intervention and deliberate suppression, did not cause much of a public uproar within the Jia family. The official explanation was that it was an attack by outsiders, which had been repelled by the guards, and patrols were increased throughout the mansion. However, it was clear to everyone that the atmosphere within the mansion had suddenly become much more tense. Travel between Rongxi Hall and the East Courtyard (Jia She's residence) noticeably decreased; servants hurried about, speaking cautiously, afraid of incurring any repercussions. Stewards in some sensitive positions were quietly replaced, all with confidants of Jia Zheng's faction or reliable individuals promoted by Tan Chun. Grandmother Jia seemed to have also noticed this, summoning Jia Zheng for a private talk, after which she claimed illness and began to recuperate, rarely receiving outside visitors, leaving most of the mansion's affairs to Jia Zheng and Tan Chun.

After this incident, Lin Chen's status in Jia Zheng's eyes had risen from "a collateral descendant worth cultivating" to "a core asset entrusted with important matters and must be protected at all costs." Around Tingzhu Pavilion, numerous skilled guards had appeared, both overtly and covertly. These were all reliable men Jia Zheng had drawn from his own ranks or through old connections, all possessing at least a ninth-rank cultivation level, with the leading squad leader being a skilled early-stage eighth-rank fighter. Their supplies and necessities were also raised to the highest standard, even surpassing those of some direct descendants. Tan Chun, on the other hand, frequently used the pretext of discussing "reorganizational matters," sending servants or coming in person to exchange information and communicate. Lin Chen had clearly become a crucial pillar for Jia Zheng's faction in terms of both "military strength" and "resourcefulness."

Lin Chen accepted this without hesitation. He needed this protection and resources to deal with Jia She's potentially more ferocious counterattack, as well as the larger, lurking forces behind Jia She (the princes of the capital, the Xue family, and the Shi family). At the same time, he also used this relatively stable environment to fully digest his recent gains.

The combined effects of the *Qingxin Jue* (Heart-Clearing Mantra) and Jade Dew Tea made his mind increasingly clear, greatly enhancing his control over his internal energy and his cultivation of the *Yuqi Jue* (Qi Control Mantra). He experimented with integrating the *Qingxin Jue*'s concept of "observing the mind without intention, the mind naturally becomes empty and still" into the *Yuqi Jue*'s refinement of internal energy and guidance of external primordial energy, and surprisingly discovered that the combination yielded remarkable results. His internal energy became more condensed and pure, and his sensitivity to and attraction of the primordial energy of heaven and earth became increasingly clear and stable. Although the primordial energy he drew was still thin, the trickling flow into his dantian, merging with his own internal energy and the energy provided by the [Affection Nourishment], allowed his cultivation to steadily and solidly improve at a speed far exceeding that of ordinary eighth-rank martial artists.

His training in the Vajra Fist hadn't lagged behind either. The fist power, incorporating the characteristics of "Qi control," possessed a greater agility and penetrating force than its raw power. Especially the sixth form, "Vajra Pushing the Mountain," which, after repeated practice and drawing on his insights from sparring with Han Tao, had gradually reached a minor level of mastery, where "the fist strikes like a mountain, pushing mountains and cutting off rivers." When used at full power, combined with his powerful internal energy at the mid-stage of the eighth rank, it was enough to pose a serious threat to a late-stage eighth-rank martial artist.

His steadily increasing strength brought with it a more composed and confident mindset. He knew that his struggle against Jia She and the forces behind him was far from over. But passive defense was not his style. While consolidating his own power and helping Tan Chun stabilize the inner court, he also consciously began to gather information about the Xue family, the Shi family, and even the "Prince of the Capital" who might be involved. However, the secrets of these powerful families and nobles were not easily uncovered, and progress was slow.

On this day, the sky was high and the clouds were light, and the rare warm winter sun shone down, dispelling the chill of the past few days. Lin Chen circulated the "Qi Control Technique" nine times in the courtyard, feeling his internal energy flowing smoothly and warmly, and his vitality was extremely abundant. Although long-term meditation in the courtyard could improve his strength, it ultimately lacked some of the "movement" he needed to temper his mind. He suddenly remembered that the "Qi Control Technique" mentioned that to cultivate this method, one must often be close to nature, feel the wonder of the flow of the primordial energy of heaven and earth, and seek stillness within movement—this was the right path.

With this thought in mind, he changed into a neat blue outfit, and without any attendants, left the Jia mansion alone, heading towards Qixia Mountain west of Jinling City. Although Qixia Mountain wasn't a famous mountain, its deep forests, clear springs, and remoteness made it an ideal place for quiet contemplation, internal energy cultivation, and physical exercise.

He used his light and agile steps, leaping swiftly through the forest at incredible speed, landing silently and startling a few crows. Breathing in the crisp mountain air, feeling the vitality of the vegetation and the weight of the rocks, his internal Qi Control Technique circulated spontaneously, much more actively than in the city, and his perception of the surrounding energy became clearer. Sensing something, he found a flat boulder near a mountain stream, sat cross-legged, and attempted a deeper level of meditation, guiding his internal energy to merge with the natural energy of the mountain forest.

He didn't know how much time had passed—perhaps an hour, perhaps longer. Lin Chen was immersed in a profound state of subtle resonance with heaven and earth, his mind clear and his inner energy flowing freely. Suddenly, his ears twitched, catching a faint yet incredibly swift sound of wind breaking through the air, rapidly approaching from the depths of the forest. The sound wasn't from birds or beasts, but… the movement of a human!

There was someone! And their movement was remarkably skillful, at least that of an eighth-rank martial artist!

Lin Chen suddenly opened his eyes, his gaze sharp as lightning, and looked toward the source of the sound. Almost simultaneously, a pale purple figure, like a fleeting shadow, floated down from the branch of an ancient pine tree ten feet away and landed lightly on the open ground by the stream three feet in front of him.

The newcomer was also dressed in practical, close-fitting clothing, though in a women's style—pale purple, tailored to fit her perfectly, outlining her slender and graceful figure. She wore a matching brocade cloak, the hood pulled back, revealing a stunningly beautiful face, possessing both elegance and a touch of heroism. Her eyebrows were like distant mountains, her eyes like autumn waters, her nose straight, and her skin, bathed in the winter forest light, was so white it seemed translucent. It was none other than Princess Zhaoyang, Zhao Lingxi, whom she had briefly encountered on the streets of Jinling a few days prior.

She also seemed to have just finished some kind of cultivation or exploration; fine beads of sweat glistened on her forehead, and her breathing was slightly rapid, yet still long and steady. Upon suddenly seeing Lin Chen sitting cross-legged on the boulder by the stream, a flicker of surprise flashed in her eyes, quickly turning into realization, and a barely perceptible hint of…inquiry and interest.

“Young Master Lin?” Zhao Lingxi spoke, her voice still clear and melodious, but lacking the imposing air she had displayed on the street that day, replaced by a refreshing mountain air. “I didn’t expect to meet you here. Young Master, what a refined taste you have! Are you also practicing your Qi in the mountains?”

Lin Chen had already risen from the rock and bowed respectfully. “So it’s Miss Zhao. I had nothing better to do, so I came for a stroll in the mountains. I apologize for disturbing you.” He was also slightly surprised. This princess seemed to truly enjoy “incognito visits,” and her actions were unconventional; she had actually come to these mountains alone. Judging from her state, she had clearly been practicing some advanced movement technique, moving at an extremely fast speed. If it weren’t for his minor mastery of the *Qi Control Technique* and his keen senses, he would have almost failed to detect her.

“It wasn’t a disturbance,” Zhao Lingxi smiled slightly, her gaze falling on Lin Chen with scrutiny. “But you, young master, your aura was quite profound just now, subtly harmonizing with this mountain forest. Clearly, your cultivation method is quite extraordinary. That day on the street, your movements were agile and your attacks decisive. Judging from your aura today, it seems you’ve improved even further? Could it be that you’ve recently encountered another fortuitous event?”

Her eyes were sharp; she could even perceive the change in Lin Chen’s aura. Lin Chen felt a chill run down his spine. This princess was not only of noble birth, but her knowledge and perception of martial arts far surpassed that of ordinary people.

“You flatter me, young lady. It’s just a few things I’ve learned, and I’m just practicing haphazardly,” Lin Chen said vaguely, not wanting to elaborate.

Zhao Lingxi didn't press further, but instead observed him with interest. Suddenly, she said, "That day, young master, you came to my rescue, and I didn't have a chance to properly thank you. Our chance encounter in the mountains today is fate. I see you are also a martial artist; I wonder… if we could spar a few moves and become friends through martial arts?"

Her eyes shone with a bright and open light, the kind of brilliance a true martial artist displays when facing a worthy opponent. Clearly, she was deeply interested in Lin Chen's strength and wanted to test him personally.

Lin Chen was slightly taken aback. Sparring with a princess of the dynasty? This was no ordinary matter. But seeing the pure passion and anticipation for martial arts in Zhao Lingxi's eyes, the martial artist's chord within him was gently plucked. Sparring with an opponent of the same level, or even potentially higher (Zhao Lingxi's cultivation level, judging from his senses, was probably at the mid-eighth rank, or even higher), was an excellent opportunity to test his own skills and identify his shortcomings. Moreover, it was also a way to further understand and get to know this mysterious princess.

“It is my wish, I dare not ask.” Lin Chen hesitated for a moment, then nodded in agreement, a hint of fighting spirit rising in his eyes. “I beg you to show mercy, young lady.”

“It’s just a friendly spar, nothing more.” Zhao Lingxi’s smile deepened, clearly quite satisfied with Lin Chen’s straightforwardness. She took off her cloak and hung it on a nearby tree branch, revealing a well-fitting purple outfit underneath, which accentuated her tall and dashing figure. “Young master, please.”

The two stood facing each other, about three zhang apart. The mountain breeze blew through the forest, bringing the fragrance of grass and trees and the dampness of the stream. The atmosphere instantly became solemn.

Without any formalities, Zhao Lingxi moved first! Her feet shifted, as if shrinking the distance to an inch, covering the three-zhang gap in an instant. Her right palm, like a butterfly flitting among flowers, was light and graceful, yet carried a majestic and imposing force, striking towards Lin Chen's shoulder! Before the palm wind even arrived, an invisible, chilling aura had already enveloped him, as if he were facing not a young girl, but a monarch wielding power. This palm technique clearly possessed characteristics of royal martial arts.

Lin Chen dared not be careless. He used his light and agile steps, his body moving like a willow catkin in the wind, sliding sideways in the blink of an eye. At the same time, his right hand, fingers pointing outwards, struck Zhao Lingxi's wrist pulse point with lightning speed. The finger strike was sharp, containing a penetrating effect of "Qi control." It was a pressure point technique incorporating the "Qi Control Technique."

Zhao Lingxi exclaimed "Eh!" seemingly surprised by Lin Chen's agility and the purity of his finger strength. Her palm strike changed from a clap to a flick, her sleeves fluttering like clouds brushing across a mountain, subtly deflecting Lin Chen's finger force with softness. Simultaneously, her left palm silently emerged from under his ribs, striking towards Lin Chen's chest and abdomen, its power concealed yet concealing a hidden force.

Lin Chen neither dodged nor evaded, lowering his stance and lowering his left fist upwards in a powerful "Vajra Starting Hand" strike! The fist was as solid as a mountain, carrying the unparalleled ferocity of the Vajra Fist, yet at the instant the fist touched the opponent's palm, his inner energy subtly trembled, unleashing the penetrating power of the "Zhen" technique!

"Bang!"

Fist and palm collided, producing a muffled sound of impact! Both men staggered simultaneously, each taking a half-step back. Lin Chen felt a balanced yet overwhelming palm force penetrate his body, his breath slightly caught in his throat, and he was secretly astonished by the purity and strength of his opponent's inner energy. Zhao Lingxi's beautiful eyes also lit up, sensing that besides the fierce and violent force of Lin Chen's punch, there was also a strange, penetrating shockwave, causing her hand to feel slightly numb and her blood to surge.

"Excellent fist technique! Excellent power!" Zhao Lingxi exclaimed, her fighting spirit intensifying. She moved again, this time holding nothing back, pushing her movement technique to its limit. Her entire body seemed to transform into a pale purple phantom, circling Lin Chen rapidly. Palms, fingers, fists, and legs rained down like a storm. Each strike was upright and precise, the moves exquisite, the internal energy flowing with the sounds of wind and thunder, clearly indicating a martial art of extremely high rank, which she had mastered to a high degree.

Lin Chen, his spirits lifted, unleashed his light and agile steps and Vajra Fist to their fullest potential. His body darted and weaved within the limited space, narrowly escaping attacks and counterattacking with fierce and domineering fist techniques.
