He sometimes used "Vajra Subduing Demons" to meet attacks head-on, sometimes "Vajra Probing Claws" to seize targets, and sometimes incorporated the characteristics of "Qi Control" into his fist power, increasing its variation and penetration. The two fought with speed and skill, the sounds of clashing energy filling the air, stirring up fallen leaves and dust, and startling nearby birds and beasts into scattering.

Zhao Lingxi's martial arts were expansive and powerful, imbued with righteous indignation, and possessed an aura of dominance, clearly a legitimate royal art. Her internal energy was pure and profound, her control over power extremely refined, and her movements swift and experienced (obviously frequent sparring or actual combat), putting immense pressure on Lin Chen.

Lin Chen's martial arts skills are self-taught (granted by the system and learned by himself), combining the power of the Rock-Splitting Fist, the ferocity of the Vajra Fist, the agility of the Light Step, and the newly acquired "Qi Control" characteristic. His style is unpredictable and varied, combining strength and softness. In particular, his use of "internal force" often has unexpected aspects, which makes Zhao Lingxi quite troubled.

The two exchanged nearly a hundred blows, neither able to gain the upper hand, resulting in a stalemate! Although Lin Chen was slightly inferior in cultivation level (Zhao Lingxi was indeed at the mid-stage of the eighth rank, with an extremely solid foundation), he managed to make up for the difference with his exquisite fist techniques, agile movements, and unique understanding of "internal force."

"Exhilarating!" Zhao Lingxi, far from being annoyed by the prolonged battle, grew even more excited. With a clear shout, her palm technique suddenly changed, shifting from its previous swift and varied movements to a slow and deliberate strike. This seemingly slow palm strike seemed to manipulate the surrounding airflow; as the palm wind passed, the air emitted a low groan, and a heavy, mountain-like pressure swept over them! Clearly, it was her trump card.

Lin Chen's eyes narrowed; he knew the crucial moment had arrived. He ceased his feints, fixed his stance, took a deep breath, and fully activated the "Qi Control Technique," his inner energy surging like a boiling river towards his right fist. The Vajra Fist intent, the Stone-Splitting Power, and the Qi Controlling Spirituality were forcibly fused together in this single punch!

Vajra Fist Sixth Form—Vajra Pushing the Mountain! Full Power Unleashed!

"Hah!"

The fist struck like a raging Vajra, capable of shattering mountains and halting rivers! On the fist's edge, pale golden inner energy solidified, forming a rapidly spinning micro-vortex that frantically devoured and churned the surrounding air and sparse primordial energy, carrying an unstoppable, terrifying will to crush everything, fiercely meeting the mountain-like palm strike!

"Boom—!"

This collision far surpassed any previous one! It was like a thunderclap from a clear sky! A violent shockwave emanated from the two figures, violently spreading outwards, uprooting the thick layer of fallen leaves and loose soil, forming a clear circular shockwave! The nearby stream rippled with the impact.

Lin Chen felt an irresistible force surge through his fist, his body trembling violently, his blood churning. He involuntarily staggered back seven or eight steps before barely regaining his footing. A sweet taste rose in his throat, which he forcibly suppressed. His right arm was numb and tingling, his inner energy disordered.

Zhao Lingxi, too, swayed, staggering back five or six steps before regaining her footing. An unnatural blush appeared on her beautiful face, and her chest heaved slightly, clearly indicating she was also unwell. Her gaze towards Lin Chen had shifted from initial admiration and inquiry to undisguised shock and admiration.

“Young Master, your fist technique is superb! Your cultivation is remarkable!” Zhao Lingxi slowly regulated her breathing, her eyes gleaming with admiration. “With only early-stage eighth-rank cultivation (she could tell Lin Chen had recently broken through to the eighth rank), you actually withstood my mid-stage eighth-rank ‘Suppressing Mountain Palm’ without being at a disadvantage! Young Master’s fist technique is powerful and exquisite, and your application of force is truly ingenious. Lingxi is truly impressed!”

"Your palm technique is magnificent and powerful, your inner strength profound. I, Lin Chen, humbly admit defeat." Lin Chen suppressed his surging blood and bowed. He knew that although the previous exchange seemed evenly matched, he had actually suffered a slight disadvantage, given the difference in their cultivation levels. But to have fought to this extent was already something to be proud of.

"Young Master, there's no need for such modesty," Zhao Lingxi shook her head, stepped forward, and said earnestly, "I've practiced martial arts since childhood with the palace servants, learning only superior techniques, and with the aid of medicinal resources, I've achieved my current level. Young Master, you've learned without a teacher, yet you've reached such a level; that's truly remarkable. Our sparring just now has been incredibly beneficial, giving me new insights into the variations of internal energy."

Her words were sincere, devoid of any arrogance befitting royalty, which only enhanced her composure. Lin Chen's impression of her improved even further.

"You flatter me, Miss. It is my honor to spar with you," Lin Chen said.

The two exchanged a smile, the intensity and tension of their earlier sparring transforming into a peaceful mutual appreciation. They walked together to the stream, scooped up water to wash their faces, and the cool stream invigorated them.

“Young Master mentioned the other day that you were exploring cultivation on your own.” Zhao Lingxi sat on a smooth bluestone by the stream, turning her head to look at Lin Chen, her eyes clear. “I observed that while your fist techniques, body movements, and even the circulation of your internal energy are all self-contained and exquisitely refined, it seems… you lack some systematic inheritance and guidance? Especially your control over the tempering and external projection of your internal energy; it seems you've only just grasped the basics, and in some areas, there's room for further refinement.”

Her insight was indeed sharp; she immediately saw Lin Chen's “shortcoming”—a lack of systematic inheritance, and he was still exploring advanced techniques such as “qi control.” Lin Chen’s heart stirred slightly. This princess seemed to intend to offer guidance?

“Your insight is truly remarkable,” Lin Chen readily admitted. “I have indeed been exploring on my own, and many key points I have only grasped the form of, not the essence of. Your background… is vast and your knowledge profound; if you would be so kind as to offer some guidance, Lin Chen would be eternally grateful.”

Zhao Lingxi smiled slightly, and without holding back, picked up a twig and began to draw on the sandy ground by the stream: "Young master, you see, releasing internal energy is not simply about forcing internal force out of the body. It requires guiding it with divine will, following specific routes along the meridians, simulating the rhythm of the circulation of primordial energy in heaven and earth... For example, like this..." As she spoke, she drew, explaining some basic techniques and key points of releasing internal energy and condensing qi into a powerful force in a simple and easy-to-understand way. Although not a specific cultivation method, it was a high-level theory and experience, and many parts of it perfectly corroborated the confusion Lin Chen had encountered when cultivating the "Qi Control Technique," making him suddenly enlightened and clarifying many previously unclear points!

Lin Chen listened intently, occasionally asking questions, which Zhao Lingxi patiently answered. One taught diligently, the other learned focused intently, and before they knew it, the sun was beginning to set.

"Thank you so much for your guidance, Miss. Today's words are more valuable than months of arduous practice!" Lin Chen expressed his heartfelt gratitude. Zhao Lingxi's guidance had elevated his understanding of the *Qi Control Technique* and his mastery of internal energy manipulation to a new level.

“It is good that this can be of some help to you, young master.” Zhao Lingxi put down the branch, gazing at the setting sun, a hint of melancholy in her voice. “Martial arts cultivation is like rowing against the current. Although I have had some opportunities, I still feel the road ahead is long and fraught with confusion. To be able to exchange ideas and discuss the Dao with someone as talented as you, young master, is a pleasure for me.”

She turned to Lin Chen, her gaze earnest: “Young master is no ordinary person; one day you will surely rise to great heights. However, this Jinling City, this Jia family… the waters are too deep, the whirlpools too numerous. You must be careful. Especially… some people, some things, are too complicated. If it is not necessary, it is best not to get too deeply involved.”

These words were clearly a reminder and a sign of concern. Lin Chen’s heart skipped a beat; he knew she was referring to Jia She and the forces that might be involved behind him.

“Thank you for your reminder, young lady. I will remember it,” Lin Chen said solemnly.

"It's getting late, I should head back." Zhao Lingxi stood up and put her cloak back on. "Young master, farewell."

"Take care, young lady."

Watching the pale purple figure disappear into the twilight forest like a fleeting glimpse of a swan, Lin Chen stood there for a long time. Today's sparring and conversation had given him a deeper understanding of Princess Zhaoyang. Not only was she of noble birth and possessed exceptional martial arts talent and broad knowledge, but more importantly, she was open-minded, discerning, and helpful.

"Ding! Detected that the host has engaged in an in-depth martial arts exchange with the important plot character 'Princess Zhao Lingxi'. The princess has developed a high level of appreciation and recognition for the host's strength, comprehension, and temperament, deepening the emotional bond. The passive effect of the Succubus Physique has been triggered. Gained +15 points of favorability from Zhao Lingxi (Admiration). Received a large influx of pure energy, exuding elegance, imbued with dragon energy and sharpness, significantly nourishing the foundation of martial arts, deepening the understanding of the 'Qi Control' characteristic, and greatly enhancing the perception of 'Power'. The bond characteristic 'Noble Lineage' effect has been strengthened."

The system notification and the surge of energy that followed greatly invigorated Lin Chen. The princess's favorability jumped from 10 points to 25 points (Admiration), and the energy supply efficiency improved from below average to average. More importantly, the martial arts insights gained from this exchange were irreplaceable by any elixir or arduous training.

“Princess Zhaoyang…” Lin Chen murmured, his eyes gleaming. His encounter with this princess seemed to be leading him down a path more magnificent, yet also more treacherous. But wasn't this exactly what he wanted?

Strength, opportunity, challenge, and even… the deeper, unseen entanglement of fate with the women of the Red Chamber and with this world of high martial arts—all these fueled the flames in his heart to burn even more fiercely.

He turned and strode towards Jinling City. The setting sun cast a long shadow of him, blending him into the mountains and streams, serene and resolute.

When he returned to Tingzhu Pavilion, the lanterns were just coming on. Everything in the courtyard seemed normal, but Lin Chen could sense that several new faces had appeared among the guards, both overt and covert, their auras more refined. Jia Zheng's movements were clearly quickening.

He returned to his quiet room, sat cross-legged, and instead of immediately beginning his cultivation, carefully reviewed and processed the details of his sparring and discussions with Zhao Lingxi that day. He especially focused on his insights into projecting internal energy, condensing qi, and utilizing "momentum," comparing them with the *Qi Control Technique*, *Heart Cleansing Technique*, and *Diamond Fist*. Many previously unclear points gradually became clear.

"Next, it's time to concentrate and attempt to break through to the late stage of the eighth rank," Lin Chen said, opening his eyes and taking out his treasured "Breakthrough Pill." "With today's insights and this pill, my chances should be much greater."

Outside the window, night deepened. The myriad lights of Jinling City, along with the undercurrents within this grand mansion, flowed silently in the cold night. The young man inside Tingzhu Pavilion was already prepared to face even greater storms and climb even higher peaks.


Chapter 26 Signing in at Xiaoxiang Pavilion, Daiyu presents a handkerchief

A thoroughly enjoyable sparring session in Qixia Mountain, and a profound discussion with Princess Zhao Lingxi, yielded rich rewards for Lin Chen. He gained a deeper understanding of the cultivation of the *Qi Control Technique* and the control of internal energy's "qi" characteristics, and his understanding of the "momentum" of martial arts began to sprout. More importantly, he established an initial trust and appreciation with this princess of special status and extraordinary insight, deepening their bond and bringing tangible benefits.

Back at the Jia residence, Lin Chen remained discreet, continuing his secluded life. He carefully digested what he had gained in the mountains, combining it with the *Qingxin Jue* (Heart-Clearing Mantra) and Jade Dew Tea. His mind became increasingly clear, and his internal energy flowed more smoothly and effortlessly. With the Breakthrough Pill in hand, his chances of breaking through to the late stage of the Eighth Rank increased significantly, but he was not in a hurry, knowing that a solid foundation and natural progression were the right path.

That day, as dawn broke and the thin mist lingered, Lin Chen finished his night of meditation, feeling refreshed and full of energy. The system's cold notification sounded precisely in his mind:

"Host detected in the inner courtyard of the Jia family mansion. Would you like to complete today's basic check-in?"

"Check-in." Lin Chen's mind stirred slightly. These days, daily check-ins mostly yielded only mortal-grade pills, gold, silver, and miscellaneous items, but little by little, they added up to resources. Occasionally, he could even get something useful like "Rejuvenation Powder."

"Ding! Check-in successful in the inner courtyard of the Jia family mansion! Obtained: 2 Concentration Pills (High-grade mortal-grade pills, which can slightly stabilize the mind, soothe restless blood and qi, and assist in cultivation, especially suitable for adjusting one's state before breaking through a bottleneck)."

A Concentration Pill? Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. Although this pill wasn't of high grade, its effects were quite practical, exactly what he needed to consolidate his cultivation level and prepare for breaking through his bottleneck in the future. The system always seemed to provide just the right assistance when he needed it. He put the pill into his pocket.

His gaze involuntarily drifted towards Xiaoxiang Pavilion. Since the day Daiyu gave him medicine and took meticulous care of him, the veil of mystery between them seemed to have been quietly lifted. Although they hadn't seen each other much afterward due to their respective affairs (Daiyu was frail and needed rest, and Lin Chen was busy with cultivation and dealing with Jia She), that intangible connection and bond had grown clearer and deeper with the continuous, cool, and ethereal energy from [Love's Nourishment]. Daiyu's high favorability rating of 65 points (Unwavering in This Life) weighed heavily on his heart, impossible and unwilling to be ignored.

It was time to go see her. And while he was at it, he should also complete his daily check-in at Xiaoxiang Pavilion. He hadn't been there since his first check-in, when he received the "Light and Agile Steps" skill.

Thinking of this, Lin Chen changed into a clean, moon-white fine cloth robe, concealing his aura to appear even more gentle and serene. He brought no gifts, going empty-handed. For Daiyu, perhaps a sincere sentiment was more precious than any external object.

The Xiaoxiang Pavilion was still surrounded by verdant bamboo, quiet and serene. Morning mist swirled among the bamboo leaves, adding to its otherworldly charm. The air was filled with the familiar scent of herbs and plum blossoms, but today, there seemed to be an added faint trace of the unique aroma of ink and paper.

The courtyard gate was ajar. Lin Chen gently knocked.

A short while later, the door opened, and Xueyan peeked out. Seeing Lin Chen, her face lit up with surprise and delight: "Young Master Lin? You've finally arrived! Please come in, the young lady is inside." Her tone was familiar and warm, clearly no longer treating him as an outsider.

Lin Chen nodded his thanks and stepped into the courtyard. The courtyard was still tidy, but the few slender plum trees in the corner were now in full bloom, their red and white blossoms dazzling in the morning mist. His gaze swept over them, and a thought flickered in his mind: "System, check in at Xiaoxiang Pavilion."

"Ding! Check-in successful at Xiaoxiang Pavilion! Obtained: Concentration Pill x2 (High-grade Mortal Rank)."

Another Concentration Pill? Lin Chen was slightly surprised, but quickly understood. This item might be quite beneficial to Daiyu's overthinking, frail, and emotionally troubled body. He could leave it for her.

Xueyan had already lifted the curtain to the main room. Lin Chen stepped inside.

The room was elegantly furnished; every table, chair, bed, and couch exuded a scholarly air. On the desk by the window lay snow-white Xuan paper, and an inkstone, its ink still fresh. Daiyu stood with her back to the door, before the desk, holding a purple-tipped brush, slightly bent over, seemingly writing something. Today she wore a light blue casual jacket and skirt, over which she wore a moon-white vest. Her figure was still slender, but compared to her pale and haggard appearance of the past few days, her complexion seemed better, with a faint, healthy blush on her cheeks. Her long black hair was loosely tied in a bun, secured only by a simple white jade hairpin, a few strands falling beside her neck, swaying gently with her writing.

Hearing footsteps, Daiyu paused slightly, but did not immediately turn around. She simply said softly, "Xueyan, is the medicine ready? Put it in the outer room first. I'll finish writing these two lines." Her voice was clear and cold, carrying a hint of imperceptible fatigue, yet exceptionally focused.

"Miss, it's Young Master Lin," Xueyan gently reminded her.

Daiyu's hand, holding the brush, trembled almost imperceptibly. She slowly straightened up, but didn't turn around immediately, as if calming herself. After a moment, she put down the brush, pressed down the still-wet ink with a paperweight, and then slowly turned around.

Morning light streamed through the window, illuminating her exquisitely beautiful face. Her eyebrows were like distant mountains shrouded in mist, her eyes like autumn waters rippling, her nose delicate, her lips pale—she still possessed that pitiful beauty. But at this moment, in those eyes that always held a faint melancholy, the instant she saw Lin Chen, it was as if starlight suddenly shone, dazzlingly bright. In that light, there was surprise, joy, a hint of barely perceptible shyness, and even more so, a deep, almost overflowing affection.

“Lin…Brother Lin,” Daiyu spoke, her voice even softer than before, as if afraid of disturbing something. “You…why are you here?” She subconsciously raised her hand to tuck a stray strand of hair behind her ear, a subtle gesture that betrayed a hint of unease within her.

“I came to see you.” Lin Chen stopped a few steps away from her, his gaze gently falling on her face as he carefully examined her complexion. “You seem to be in better spirits these past few days.”

His gaze was focused and honest, filled with undisguised concern. Daiyu's cheeks flushed slightly under his gaze. She lowered her eyes and whispered, "Yes, after taking the medicine Brother Lin brought last time, and...and listening to Brother Lin's advice to think less and worry less, I've been sleeping better these past few days." She paused, then mustered her courage to look up at him. "Brother Lin, your...your injury from that day, is it much better? I was so worried..."

"It's completely healed," Lin Chen smiled slightly, his tone relaxed. "The medicine you sent was very effective. I also want to thank you for taking care of me that day."

Hearing him mention that day, Daiyu couldn't help but recall the scene of her applying medicine and wiping his sweat, leaning on his shoulder and sobbing. Her blush deepened, and she quickly looked away, glancing at the desk. "Brother Lin, what are you saying? That was just what I should...should do."

The room fell silent for a moment, broken only by the rustling of bamboo leaves outside the window and their slightly rapid breathing. A subtle, heart-pounding atmosphere filled the air.

Lin Chen's gaze fell upon the still-wet ink on the Xuan paper on the desk. On it, written in elegant, vigorous, and unrestrained small regular script, was a seven-character quatrain:

"Autumn wind and rain startled me from my dream, beneath the crimson gauze window I tuned my old zither.

The icy strings are broken, few understand my music; tears fall on the bare branches, revealing my bleeding heart.

Fortunately, the winter plum endures the cold, not vying with peach and plum for spring's fragrance."

"If in the future I achieve my lofty ambitions, I will protect the beautiful maiden from worldly defilement."

The poem is austere and solitary. The first four lines fully express the sorrow of loneliness and the difficulty of finding a kindred spirit. The last four lines shift the focus, using the winter plum blossom as a metaphor for the poet, implying an indomitable will. The final two lines convey a profound sense of longing: "If in the future I achieve my lofty ambitions, I will protect the beautiful maiden from worldly defilement..." Who is this "beautiful maiden"? What is this "lofty ambition"? The poem's subtle anticipation and...emotions almost leap off the page.

Lin Chen's heart trembled slightly. This poem was clearly written for him. Daiyu used the poem to express her aspirations, and even more so, to convey her emotions. The loneliness ahead, the present fortune (fortunate to have the winter plum blossoms), the future expectations (to protect Qing'e)... every word and phrase was a true reflection of her sensitive and thoughtful heart, yet one that was open to him.

Seeing Lin Chen staring at the poem, Daiyu was initially flustered, instinctively reaching out to cover it, but stopped halfway. She looked up at Lin Chen, her eyes filled with a hint of trepidation, a hint of expectation, and even more so, a desperate courage.

"Brother Lin...what do you think of this poem?" she asked softly, her voice trembling almost imperceptibly.

Lin Chen didn't answer immediately. Instead, he shifted his gaze from the poem, looking deeply into Daiyu's eyes. Those eyes, like autumn waters, clearly reflected his image, and also reflected all her innermost thoughts.

"The poem is very good," Lin Chen said slowly, his voice low and firm. "The plum blossom endures the years, not following the vulgar. Qing'e...should also have lofty ambitions to protect herself, untouched by worldly dust."

His words were both praise and a response. A response to her feelings, and also a statement of his own attitude.

Daiyu's eyes widened instantly, shimmering with tears, as if starlight shattered and rippled within them. Her lips trembled slightly, wanting to say something, but she choked up, simply gazing intently at Lin Chen, as if trying to etch his image into the depths of her soul.

After a long silence, she seemed to have made up her mind. Turning around, she took out a neatly folded, plain white silk handkerchief from a small, locked sandalwood box beside her desk. The handkerchief was soft, its edges embroidered with simple lotus scrolls in the same color thread, elegant and refined. She presented the handkerchief to Lin Chen with both hands, her fingers turning slightly white from the pressure, her cheeks flushed like the morning glow.

"Brother Lin..." Daiyu's voice was almost inaudible, yet it carried a resolute determination, "This handkerchief... I... I embroidered it myself. It's not very well embroidered, the stitches are rough... If... if you don't mind, then... then please keep it." She didn't dare look Lin Chen in the eye, only lowering her head, staring at the handkerchief in her hands.

Lin Chen took the handkerchief. It was soft and slightly cool to the touch, carrying Daiyu's unique, cool, and delicate fragrance. He slowly unfolded it.

The handkerchief was plain white, without any intricate patterns. Only in one corner, in fine silk thread matching the handkerchief, was a delicately embroidered character, "Chen." The embroidery wasn't perfectly neat; slight skewing and repetitive stitches were visible, clearly indicating it had been embroidered and unraveled repeatedly before finally taking shape. However, it was precisely this imperfection that made the embroidery all the more sincere and heartfelt; every stitch, every thread, seemed to be imbued with the embroiderer's entire spirit and emotion.

"Chen"...that was his name.

Lin Chen held the silk handkerchief, light as a feather yet weighing a thousand pounds, and the softest part of his heart was deeply touched. He looked up at the girl before him, her head bowed, her neck flushed with shyness, as if awaiting judgment from fate. How much courage did she need to take this step? In this era of strict social norms, for a woman to secretly give a handkerchief to a man outside the marriage, embroidering his name on it, was almost tantamount to a secret pledge of love, entrusting her entire reputation and future to him.

"Ding! Detected that the important plot character 'Lin Daiyu' has reached the peak of her feelings for the host, and has voluntarily given the host a personal item (embroidered handkerchief) to express her emotions, resulting in a qualitative change in the emotional bond. The passive effect of the Succubus Physique has been triggered to its maximum. Gain Lin Daiyu's favorability +20 (Eternal Love → Love for Three Lifetimes). Receive a massive infusion of pure, cool, and spiritual 'Origin Energy of Affection,' continuously nourishing and stabilizing your martial arts foundation like never before. The total amount and purity of your internal energy have greatly increased, and your comprehension of the 'Heart-Cleansing Technique' and 'Qi-Controlling Technique' has entered a new level, resulting in a qualitative change in your spiritual power. Special effect: Unlock one of the ultimate bond characteristics of the Succubus Physique—Heart-to-Heart Connection (Beginner). A weak telepathic connection can be established between the host and Lin Daiyu (requires intense emotional fluctuations on both parties and close proximity). The host's use of mental skills such as 'Soothing the Mind' has double the effect on Daiyu, and Daiyu's emotional fluctuations (positive) can slightly replenish the host's spiritual power."

The system notification was more solemn than ever before, and the energy that surged in was as vast as the sea! That cool and ethereal primal energy seemed to carry all of Daiyu's affection and trust, cleansing and nourishing Lin Chen's limbs, meridians, dantian, and even his spiritual sea! Although his cultivation did not break through immediately, his foundation was solidified to an unimaginable degree, his internal energy became more refined and condensed, and its total amount also increased significantly. More importantly, his spiritual power seemed to be cleansed and sublimated, becoming more solid and sharp, and he gained a completely new and more essential understanding of the "stillness" of the *Qingxin Jue* and the "movement" of the *Yuqi Jue*. Furthermore, the ultimate bond characteristic of "heart and soul intertwined" established a spiritual connection between him and Daiyu that transcended common sense.

Lin Chen took a deep breath, suppressing the turmoil in his heart. He stepped forward, reached out his hands, and gently grasped Daiyu's trembling, cold little hands.

Daiyu trembled, instinctively trying to pull away, but Lin Chen held them even tighter. She finally raised her head, her eyes brimming with tears, like pear blossoms glistening with morning dew, delicate and moving, yet carrying a poignant beauty.

“This handkerchief is very fine.” Lin Chen looked into her eyes and said clearly and solemnly, word by word, “The stitching is excellent, and the handwriting is beautiful too. I will keep it carefully, and always will.”

He paused, then took her hand in his, using the warmth of his palm to warm her cold hand.

“Yu’er,” he called her name so intimately for the first time, “I promised you that I would protect you, and protect the people you care about. That promise will always stand. Not just because of the promise, but because… of you.”

“I accept this handkerchief. I accept your sentiments as well.” He carefully folded the handkerchief embroidered with the character “Chen,” placing it close to his heart. “From this day forward, your sorrows, your joys, your safety, your future, will all be inextricably linked to me, Lin Chen. I will use everything I have to protect it, to ensure it…remains forever untarnished.”

These words were the most direct and resolute vow and response. There were no flowery words, no empty promises, only a heavy responsibility and undisguised affection.

Daiyu’s tears finally welled up and slid down her fair cheeks. But these were no longer tears of sorrow, but of relief, joy, the immense happiness of having her wish fulfilled, and… a sense of peace that had settled. She gripped Lin Chen’s hand tightly, as if grasping the only lifeline, the only warmth in her life.

“Brother Lin…” she choked back tears, a thousand words condensed into those three words, unable to utter another.

Lin Chen reached out his other hand and gently wiped away her tears with his fingertips. His movements were tender, as if handling the most precious and fragile thing in the world.

“Don’t cry,” he whispered. “From now on, I’m here.”

Daiyu nodded vigorously, her tears turning into a smile. That smile was like ice thawing, spring returning to the earth, radiant and incomparably beautiful, instantly illuminating the entire cold and quiet Xiaoxiang Pavilion.

The two stood there silently, hands clasped tightly, eyes locked, seeing only each other. Time seemed to stand still; the wind outside the window, the rustling of bamboo leaves, even their own heartbeats became distant background noise.

After what seemed like an eternity, Daiyu finally turned her gaze slightly, her face still flushed, and said softly, "Brother Lin, please sit. Xueyan, bring tea."

"Yes, Miss!" Xueyan, who had been waiting outside the door, her heart pounding with excitement yet afraid to disturb them, quickly responded and went off with light steps.

Lin Chen sat down in a chair by the window, and Daiyu sat opposite him. A step separated them, yet they felt closer than ever before. The atmosphere was no longer ambiguous and tense, but had transformed into a tranquil, tender warmth.

"Brother Lin, how have you been these days? What about Master She…?" Daiyu asked with concern.

"It's alright, nothing serious will happen for now." Lin Chen brushed it off simply, not wanting her to worry too much. "But you, you need to take good care of yourself. You look better, but there's still a hint of worry between your brows. Have you been thinking too much?"

"No..." Daiyu instinctively wanted to deny it, but under Lin Chen's knowing gaze, she changed her mind. "It's just... sometimes I can't help but think about... what will become of us? The old lady, the master and mistress... and this mansion, with so many people gossiping..." A trace of worry reappeared in her eyes.

"Leave all that to me." Lin Chen's tone was steady, carrying a reassuring strength. "You just need to take care of yourself and be happy; that's the greatest help you can give me. As for the rest..." A sharp glint flashed in his eyes, "I have my own plans."

Looking at his calm and resolute face, Daiyu felt her worries gently brushed away by a warm hand. She softly hummed in agreement, asking no further questions, but the reliance and trust in her heart grew even deeper.

Xueyan brought tea—the finest Longjing, its aroma clear and refreshing. The two quietly sipped their tea, occasionally exchanging a few words in hushed tones, discussing poetry, the garden scenery, or the books Daiyu had recently read. There were no vows of eternal love, no sweet words, yet a quiet warmth and a sense of peaceful contentment filled the air.

Until the sun was high in the sky, Lin Chen noticed weariness in Daiyu's eyes and rose to take his leave.

"Brother Lin..." Daiyu saw him to the courtyard gate, hesitating to speak.

"Rest well, I'll come see you again in a couple of days," Lin Chen said gently.

“Mm.” Daiyu nodded, looking at him, then suddenly mustered her courage and whispered quickly, “That handkerchief…you must keep it close to your body, don’t…don’t lose it.” Her face flushed crimson, and she dared not look at Lin Chen again, turning and fleeing back into the house as if escaping.

Lin Chen watched her hasty yet swift retreating figure, a smile unconsciously playing on his lips. He reached into his robes and gently pressed the silk handkerchief, warm and fragrant against his heart, his eyes softening.

Leaving Xiaoxiang Pavilion, walking back to Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen’s emotions remained unsettled. Daiyu’s desperate gift of the handkerchief, her tearful yet smiling face, her unreserved trust and affection—like the finest wine, it intoxicated him, and also made the responsibility on his shoulders clearer and heavier.

"A love destined for three lifetimes... souls intertwined..." He savored the system's notification. This emotion had transcended mere liking and admiration, reaching a profound bond on a soul level. This not only brought a massive amount of energy and special abilities, but also meant that Daiyu's fate was now inextricably linked to his. He would never allow any tragedy to repeat itself in her life.

"Strength... I still need even greater strength!" Lin Chen clenched his fist. Whether dealing with Jia She's threat, protecting Daiyu, or facing the storms of a wider world in the future, formidable strength was his only reliance.

He quickened his pace, returned to Tingzhu Pavilion, immediately closed the courtyard gate, and sat cross-legged. The silk handkerchief in his arms seemed to still carry Daiyu's lingering warmth, and the faint telepathic connection brought by "souls intertwined" made his mind exceptionally peaceful and resolute. He took out his treasured "Barrier-Breaking Pill" and then swallowed a "Mind-Concentrating Pill" that he had just signed in with.

It's time to break through to the late stage of the eighth rank. With the continuous nourishment of Daiyu's "original energy of affection," the spiritual transformation brought about by "heart-soul connection," the insights gained from sparring with the princess, and the assistance of the Breakthrough Pill and Concentration Pill, this breakthrough is inevitable!

The pills entered his stomach, transforming into a surging heat and a cooling, calming power. Lin Chen cleared his mind of distractions, circulating the *Qi Control Technique* and the *Heart Cleansing Technique*, guiding the boiling internal energy and vast affection energy within his body, launching the most fierce assault against the now clearly visible barrier between the mid and late stages of the eighth rank!

Inside Tingzhu Pavilion, the energy surged, with the faint sound of wind and thunder. In Xiaoxiang Pavilion, Daiyu leaned against the window, unconsciously stroking another plain handkerchief in her hand, gazing towards Tingzhu Pavilion, her eyes like water, a faint, reassuring smile on her lips.


Chapter 27 Late Stage of Rank 8, Great Advancement in Strength

Inside the Listening Bamboo Pavilion, the quiet room was silent.

Lin Chen sat cross-legged on a futon, his eyes slightly closed, his breathing deep and long. This resonated strangely with the faint, warm scent of Daiyu emanating from the plain handkerchief in his arms, and with the profound, ethereal tranquility brought about by their "heart-to-soul connection." This tranquility was not lifeless, but rather like a deep, still pool, beneath which lay a powerful force about to erupt.

The effects of the Breakthrough Pill and the Concentration Pill had already dissipated. The Breakthrough Pill, like a spark thrown into boiling oil, instantly ignited the already exceptionally active and powerful internal energy within Lin Chen's body, which was infused with the "Energy of Affection." That scorching, violent, yet vibrant medicinal power, like thousands of tiny fire dragons, surged and crashed wildly through his meridians, constantly softening and impacting the invisible barrier between the mid- and late-stage Eighth Rank. The Concentration Pill, like a cool spring, followed closely behind, soothing the agitation of his blood and qi and the fluctuations in his spirit caused by the violent circulation of his internal energy, allowing his mind to remain as clear and focused as an ice mirror.

The *Qi Control Technique* was operating at full power, his dantian like a massive vortex, frantically absorbing and refining two streams of energy—inner energy from the Breakthrough Pill and his own surging internal energy; outer energy from the invisible, cool, and ethereal bond he shared with Daiyu in his "Heart-Soul Intertwined" state; and the thin yet inexhaustible primordial energy of heaven and earth, painstakingly drawn from the surrounding air using the *Qi Control Technique*. Guided by the exquisite *Qi Control Technique*, these three energies intertwined, refined, and merged, ultimately transforming into a pure, condensed torrent of internal energy, subtly shimmering with a pale golden luster and possessing a touch of agile "Qi control" characteristics.

This torrent of internal energy surged along his broadened and resilient meridians, growing stronger and more solid with each cycle. Lin Chen could clearly "see" that the invisible barrier separating their realms, under the relentless onslaught from both inside and outside, was now riddled with fine cracks, its light flickering uncertainly, as if it might shatter completely at any moment.

The time has come!

Lin Chen's mind was as calm as iron, completely banishing all distractions. He concentrated his will, guiding the surging torrent of internal energy within his body, now at its limit, like a reservoir overflowing with water. He infused the fierce fist intent of the *Vajra Fist*, the penetrating power of the *Stone-Splitting Fist*, the agile control of the *Qi Control Technique*, and the spiritual power brought by the "Heart-Soul Connection" into this final assault! This was not a simple aggregation of power, but a fusion of all his current understanding, insights, and emotional bonds with the martial path, transforming them into an indestructible blade of the "Dao"!

"Break!"

A silent roar resounded within him! The torrent of internal energy, accumulated to its peak, carrying his unwavering martial will, surged forth like a bursting river, like an awakened dragon, with a terrifying force capable of shattering mountains and breaking the void, launching its final and most ferocious attack on the already crumbling barrier!

"Boom—!"

It was as if a colossal, earth-shattering roar truly resounded in his mind! That resilient barrier, under the immense pressure of this attack that gathered all of Lin Chen's essence, energy, and spirit, finally succumbed and shattered! Like a collapsing dam, a new, more vast, purer, and subtly transformed internal energy, like a breached ocean, surged forth from behind the broken barrier, instantly engulfing and filling every inch of his newly opened, wider, and more resilient dantian and meridians!

"Buzz..."

A continuous, melodious resonance, like the tinkling of metal and jade, resonated within his body. This was a sign of a fundamental improvement in his bones, muscles, and internal organs, honed and refined by a higher level of internal energy. His blood and qi surged like the Yangtze River, their roar powerful, his vitality as vigorous as a furnace. His five senses and six perceptions underwent another transformation; within a radius of several dozen feet, the crawling of insects, the falling of leaves, and even the subtle sounds of earthworms turning the soil were clearly audible. His vision could discern the finest veins on a leaf several feet away. His perception of the primordial energy of heaven and earth had more than doubled, and he could vaguely distinguish the extremely thin yet real differences in the five elements within the primordial energy.

Eighth-Rank Late Stage, achieved in one fell swoop! Moreover, the solidity of his foundation and the purity and power of his internal energy far surpassed that of ordinary eighth-rank late stage martial artists; he had even touched the threshold of the eighth-rank peak!

Lin Chen didn't stop immediately. Instead, he continued circulating the *Qi Control Technique* and the *Heart Purification Technique*, guiding his newly generated, surging inner energy through his expanded meridians to complete nine perfect cycles, thoroughly stabilizing his realm and familiarizing himself with his surging power. Only when the final cycle ended, and his inner energy returned to his dantian, transforming into a calm and profound "sea of ​​qi" with a faint golden luster, did he slowly and deeply exhale a long breath.

The breath was long and condensed, actually forming a foot-long pale golden arrow of energy in the air before him. It remained condensed for several breaths before slowly dissipating, as if even the air itself had been torn apart by this energy.

He slowly opened his eyes. His gaze was piercing, with a golden glint within. His every glance exuded a profound and imposing aura, like a dormant volcano containing terrifying energy capable of scorching the heavens and boiling the seas. His demeanor had become more composed and reserved, but the confidence and control that emanated from that restraint were even more chilling.

"Late stage of the eighth rank..." Lin Chen lightly clenched his fist, the air easily bursting in his palm with a crisp sonic boom. He felt a surge of power within him, as if a single leap could propel him to the top of a tree several meters high; a casual strike could shatter stone tablets. The total amount of his internal energy had nearly doubled compared to before his breakthrough, and its purity had significantly improved. Its circulation was smooth and effortless, and his control over his power had reached a level of "subtle mastery." With a thought, his internal energy could precisely reach any part of his body.

More importantly, at the moment of his breakthrough, his understanding of the *Qi Control Technique* deepened further. The internal energy within his dantian seemed to truly possess a rudimentary "object manipulation" spirituality. With a slight thought, he attempted to retrieve an empty teacup from a low table a meter away.

At first, there was some sluggishness, and the internal energy dissipated quickly after leaving his body. However, he focused his mind and, following the techniques taught by Zhao Lingxi and his own insights, used his internal energy to simulate the rhythm of the flow of primordial energy in heaven and earth, subtly guiding it with his divine will.

The teacup trembled slightly, then, as if supported by an invisible hand, slowly rose from the table and hovered in mid-air! Although it was only about a foot off the ground and swayed slightly, its control was not perfect, but this was indeed a real, unrestricted "manipulation of objects from a distance"! It was a sign that the "qi-controlling" characteristic was beginning to manifest!

"Good!" A gleam of joy flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. Although it was still very rudimentary and consumed a lot of energy, it meant that he had truly stepped into the threshold of "Qi Control," laying a solid foundation for breaking through to the seventh rank and mastering "Qi Flight" in the future.

He gently placed the teacup back on the table, closed his eyes again, and introspectively examined himself, carefully experiencing the various changes after the breakthrough. His spiritual power, nourished by both "Heart-Soul Connection" and the breakthrough in his realm, had also undergone a qualitative change. Not only had its total amount greatly increased and his perception become more acute, but its concentration was also extremely high. He could even vaguely "see" the faint glow of internal energy flowing within his meridians, reaching a completely new level of control over his body.

"Ding! Congratulations, host, on successfully breaking through to the late stage of the eighth rank! Your cultivation realm is stable, your foundation is solid, and your inner energy is pure. New realm-related permissions unlocked."

"Detected that during the breakthrough process, the host has deeply comprehended the wonders of combining 'emotional bonds' and 'martial will,' deepening their mastery of the *Qi Control Technique* and initially grasping 'telekinesis.' Reward: Soul Nourishing Wood x1 (Mid-grade Yellow Rank, a rare and precious material. Crafted from the heart of a thousand-year-old Soul Nourishing Wood, thumb-sized, warm and smooth, with a pale purple color. Wearing it long-term can slowly nourish the soul, stabilize mental strength, resist external evil invasion, and has excellent therapeutic effects on mental trauma. It can be carved into ornaments and given to those with deep bonds for even better results, and may trigger special bond effects). Attached: Simple Carving Method."

"Ding! Detected a significant increase in host strength, reaching a new stage, triggering a strength enhancement reward! Obtained: Thunderclap Fist (Low-grade Profound Rank, Fragment of Fist Technique Manual x1). This is the introductory chapter of a fist technique created by an ancient thunder-path master after observing the Nine Heavens Thunderclap. It contains a trace of the true essence of heavenly thunder, extremely yang and powerful, swift and violent, with unparalleled power. Upon reaching minor mastery, the fist strikes like a thunderclap, capable of shaking the mind and dispelling evil spirits. Note: This is only a fragment of the first form; further fragments require collecting the remaining pieces or seeking other opportunities. Cultivation requires a strong physique and abundant internal energy; it is recommended to attempt this at a cultivation level of late-stage eighth rank or higher."

A series of system notifications, accompanied by two new streams of information, flooded into Lin Chen's mind. Lin Chen's heart trembled, followed by a surge of immense joy.

Soul-Nourishing Wood! This is a treasure for nourishing the soul! In this world of high martial arts, the importance of spiritual power is no less than that of internal energy cultivation. Soul-Nourishing Wood can not only help him stabilize his newly transformed, yet not fully mastered, spiritual power, but also continuously nourish his soul, enhancing his spiritual strength and resistance. This will greatly benefit his cultivation of the *Heart-Cleansing Technique* and *Qi-Controlling Technique*, as well as his future comprehension of more profound techniques. More importantly, the system notification suggests that it can be given to someone with a "deep bond," and may trigger special bond effects… Daiyu! She is physically weak, prone to overthinking, mentally exhausted, and constantly plagued by anxiety and fear. This Soul-Nourishing Wood is nothing short of a life-saving medicine for her! Perhaps it can fundamentally improve her physical condition and mental state!

The fragment of the "Thunderclap Fist" was a godsend! He felt that while the Vajra Fist was powerful and fierce, its variations and explosive force were somewhat lacking in certain situations. This Thunderclap Fist, imbued with the "True Meaning of Heavenly Thunder," possessing extreme Yang energy and unparalleled speed and power, perfectly filled that gap! At the lower level of the Profound Rank, its grade far surpassed the Vajra Fist, and its power was unimaginable. Although it was only a fragment of the first form, if he could master it, it would undoubtedly become his new trump card!

He immediately retrieved the two rewards from his system space.

The Soul-Nourishing Wood felt warm and smooth to the touch. About the length of a thumb, it was an irregularly shaped cylinder, pale purple throughout, with fine grain, and emitted a very faint, calming fragrance. Just holding it in his hand made him feel refreshed; the slight mental fatigue vanished, and his thinking became much sharper and clearer.

The fragment of the Thunderclap Fist technique was a piece of dark yellow "paper"—neither gold nor jade, cool to the touch, and of a peculiar texture. On it, silvery-white lines imbued with Daoist principles outlined a blurry figure in a strange fist stance, with tiny arcs of electricity dancing around its body. Beside it were hundreds of tadpole-like, indistinct ancient runes, yet directly pointing to the true meaning of "thunder." Lin Chen stared intently, and the runes seemed to come alive, transforming into rolling thunder and streaks of lightning in his mind. A violent, swift, destructive, yet boundless "thunder intent" surged forth, shaking his mind and causing his blood to boil.

"What domineering fist intent!" Lin Chen quickly averted his gaze, daring not to look any longer. With his current cultivation level and spiritual power, forcibly comprehending this fragment of a Xuan-rank fist technique would be more harmful than beneficial. He needed to wait until his realm was more stable and his spiritual power stronger before attempting it slowly.

He carefully put away the fragments of the Thunderclap Fist manual. Then he picked up the Soul-Nourishing Wood and examined it closely. The system's provided simple carving method allowed him to use his own internal energy as a "knife," supplemented by specific visualization, to leave simple patterns on the Soul-Nourishing Wood. This would help it better condense soul power and also convey his feelings. The shape of the carving was also important. For women, flowers, safety buckles, and small pendants were preferred.

Lin Chen pondered for a moment, then made up his mind. He sat cross-legged, placed the Soul-Nourishing Wood in his palm, and circulated the *Heart-Cleansing Technique*, calming his mind like still water. Then, he channeled a wisp of pure internal energy, infused with the characteristics of "Qi Control," and concentrated it at his fingertips, visualizing Daiyu's beautiful figure holding a poem manuscript under the window of the Xiaoxiang Pavilion that day, and the elegant "Chen" character on the plain handkerchief.

His fingertips pulsed with inner energy, like the finest carving knife, slowly tracing the warm, nourishing wood. He carved with utmost slowness and meticulous care. Wood shavings flew, and gradually, the outline of a magnolia bud, pure and elegant, emerged on the pale purple wood. The magnolia was Daiyu's symbol—noble, aloof, and fragrant beyond compare. Below the bud, with extremely fine strokes, he carved a tiny "Chen" character, identical to the one on the handkerchief, as if this magnolia bloomed because of him.

With the carving complete, Lin Chen let out a long sigh of relief, fine beads of sweat appearing on his forehead. The process, seemingly simple, required exquisite control over his mental energy and internal energy, making it quite mentally taxing. He picked up the finished Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant. The magnolia bud was lifelike, the character "Chen" subtly hidden beneath the blossom. The entire piece of wood seemed to have "come alive" from the carving; its pale purple luster became even more warm and restrained, and its tranquil, soul-nourishing fragrance became even clearer. Holding it in his hand, he could clearly feel its calming and nourishing effects on his mental energy.

"Yu'er should like it." Lin Chen's lips curled into a slight smile as he strung the item together with a sturdy, multicolored silk thread and carefully tucked it into his robes. He planned to give it to her at a suitable time next time they met.

Having done this, he closed his eyes again, consolidating his late-stage eighth-rank cultivation, familiarizing himself with his surging power, and attempting to apply the technique of "telekinesis" to more precise applications, such as controlling the trajectory of flying pebbles or remotely adjusting candle flames.

Unbeknownst to him, dawn had broken, and sunlight streamed through the paper windows, filling the quiet room.

Lin Chen finished his cultivation, feeling refreshed and in better condition than ever before. He pushed open the door and walked into the courtyard. Taking a deep breath of the crisp morning air, a surge of pride welled up within him.

Late stage of the eighth rank, Soul-Nourishing Wood, a fragment of the Thunderclap Fist manual, the bond of "heart and soul" with Daiyu, the alliance with Tanchun, the nascent friendship with Princess Zhaoyang… Unbeknownst to him, in this world of high-martial arts and the Dream of the Red Chamber, he was no longer the vulnerable, orphaned member of a collateral branch, subject to bullying and living in constant fear. He had gained the ability to protect himself, had someone to care about, something to protect, and above all… the confidence and ambition to climb to even greater heights of martial arts.

“Jia She… the Xue family… the Shi family… the princes of the capital…” Lin Chen gazed into the distance, his eyes calm yet sharp. “Whatever you’re plotting, no matter how deep this water runs, I, Lin Chen, having come here, will not back down.”

“Next, it’s time to take the initiative, investigate the ‘Drunken Immortal Pavilion’ agreement, and… see what Princess Zhaoyang’s so-called ‘discussion’ is all about.”

He turned and went back into the house, changing into a sharp, tailored outfit. His strength had greatly improved; it was time to meet those lurking demons and monsters in the shadows.


Chapter 28 The Appointment at Drunken Immortal Pavilion: Uncovering the Conspiracy

Inside Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen held the warm, smooth Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant in his hand, his mind already made up. This item was crucial to Daiyu's mental well-being and needed to be delivered as soon as possible. But right now, there were more pressing matters to attend to.

"Drunken Immortal Pavilion, Room Number Three..." The information he had extracted from the assassin that night flashed through Lin Chen's mind. Three days later, Jia She's confidant would meet with a "manager surnamed Wang" here. Now, the three days had passed. Jia She had been suppressed by Jia Zheng with swift and decisive action and was temporarily lying low, but the forces behind him would not let it go. This appointment at Drunken Immortal Pavilion was a crucial clue to continue investigating the mastermind behind him and uncovering a larger conspiracy; it could not be missed.

He changed into a dark gray cloth robe for ease of movement, concealed his aura, and made himself appear as an inconspicuous ordinary citizen. He carefully concealed the Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant, then placed the newly acquired, yet-to-be-examined fragment of the "Thunderclap Fist" manual, along with the half of the "Double Blessings of Fortune and Longevity" pendant used as a token, into his robes. After a brief check to confirm he carried several poisoned hidden weapons (scavenged from Han Tao and his companions) and sufficient silver, he quietly left Tingzhu Pavilion.

West of Jinling City, Zuixianlou. Although not the most upscale restaurant in the city, its relatively secluded location, with its backyard leading directly to the docks, made it a popular resting and business meeting place for merchants and martial arts practitioners from all over the country. A melting pot of people and well-connected with the world, it was also a preferred location for many shady transactions.

As noon approached, the Drunken Immortal Pavilion was already quite lively. The main hall on the first floor was filled with all sorts of people: merchants, porters, bodyguards, and down-on-their-luck scholars, playing drinking games, engaging in loud conversations, and creating a noisy atmosphere. The air was thick with the mingled smells of wine and meat, sweat, and cheap cosmetics.

Lin Chen lowered his bamboo hat and found an empty table in a corner of the first floor. He ordered a pot of the most ordinary strong liquor and two small dishes, sipping slowly, his gaze seemingly casually sweeping around. His spiritual senses were sharp; after breaking through to the late stage of the eighth rank, his perception far surpassed that of ordinary people. He could easily pick up the hushed conversations at the various tables in the hall and distinguish those "special" guests with calm demeanor and shifty eyes.

After about the time it takes for an incense stick to burn, footsteps sounded on the stairs. A middle-aged man in a silk robe, with a slightly sallow complexion and a mustache, led two wiry guards with bulging temples up the stairs. Lin Chen's gaze sharpened; he recognized the man in the lead as another trusted retainer of Jia She, named Bu Shiren (not human), who handled Jia She's shady financial dealings and "special" purchases. It seemed he was Jia She's contact for today.

Bu Shiren's eyes swept warily across the hall. Finding nothing amiss, he led his guards straight to the second floor. Lin Chen waited until his figure disappeared around the corner of the stairs, and after a moment, dropped a few copper coins, stood up, and seemingly casually walked towards the outhouse in the backyard. As he passed the stairs, his steps didn't stop, but his figure flashed like a ghost, silently avoiding the sight of the waiters and diners, and ascended to the second floor.

The second-floor private room area was noticeably quieter. The corridor was covered with a worn carpet, and on either side were private rooms with bamboo curtains or tightly closed doors. Room number three, located at the end of the corridor, was relatively secluded. Lin Chen concealed his aura, his footsteps as light as a feather, and walked to the door of room number two (adjacent to number three), listening intently. It was empty. He released a slight burst of internal energy from his fingertips, dislodging the loosely bolted door, slipped inside, and gently closed the door behind him.

The room was simply furnished, with a table and a few chairs, and a window facing the street. Lin Chen walked to the partition wall adjacent to room number three and listened intently. Activating the *Qingxin Jue* (a technique from the *Clarity of Mind*), his mental energy was highly concentrated, and combined with his keen senses at the late-stage eighth rank, the sounds from the adjacent room, though separated by the partition, were clearly audible.

"...Mr. Bu, I apologize for keeping you waiting. I was delayed on the road; please forgive me." A slightly hoarse voice, tinged with a hint of mercenary arrogance, rang out—it was the "manager surnamed Wang."

"Steward Wang, you're too kind. Please have a seat." Bu Shiren's voice carried its usual smoothness. "Did you bring the goods...?"

"Of course, of course." Steward Wang seemed to clap his hands, and a slight sound of a wooden box opening could be heard. "This is a new shipment from 'Blackwater Manor' last month. The quality is even better than last time, a full three hundred catties. There's also a new batch of 'Jade Marrow Ointment' from the 'South,' which is quite effective for cultivating internal martial arts. Here are samples, Mr. Bu, would you like to inspect them?"

A moment of silence followed, as if Bu Shiren was inspecting the goods. Then he said with satisfaction, "Excellent, of superior quality. Manager Wang has done a good job; Master She is very pleased. This is the payment for this shipment; please check it." The crisp sound of silver ingots clinking together rang out.

"Thank you, Mr. Bu! Thank you, Master She!" Manager Wang's tone was filled with joy. "I wonder... when will the next batch be needed? How much? Things are getting a bit tense over there 'in the south' lately; they're pressing us hard, and the price... I'm afraid it will have to go up by ten percent."

Bu Shiren pondered, "Price is negotiable. As long as the goods are good, Master She won't treat you unfairly. As for the next batch... at least five hundred catties! Also, what about the 'black iron' and 'cold star sand' we mentioned last time? The prince is pressing us hard; the production schedule for this batch of military equipment... no, this batch of 'farm tools' can't be delayed!"

Prince! Military equipment! Black iron! Cold star sand! Lin Chen's heart trembled. Jia She had indeed colluded with a prince, secretly forging military equipment! Black iron was an excellent material for forging heavy armor and armor-piercing arrowheads, while cold star sand was a rare mineral used for inscribing runes and enhancing the sharpness and magical power of weapons—both strictly controlled by the court! They dared to purchase and forge them privately! And judging from the tone, the scale was considerable, definitely not something done overnight.

“Black Iron and Cold Star Sand…” Manager Wang lowered his voice, sounding somewhat troubled, “Mr. Bu, as you know, these two items are investigated even more strictly than salt and iron. One of the channels in the ‘southern’ region was recently shut down, and things are very tense right now. However… it seems that Master Xue (Xue Pan?) has some connections. I heard he has some ties with the independent cultivators in the East China Sea, so perhaps he can get some, but the price…”

The Xue family! They're involved too, as expected! Lin Chen's eyes grew colder. That fool Xue Pan is probably being used as a pawn without even realizing it. The Xue family's reputation as imperial merchants is perfectly used to conceal the circulation of these contraband goods.

"Master She has his own plans regarding the Xue family," Bu Shiren said. "You just need to contact them; price is not an issue. But you must be quick and reliable! The Prince's important matter cannot be delayed! Also..." He lowered his voice further, "The Shi family has sent word that their people in the Northern Frontier have discovered the ruins of what appears to be a former dynasty's 'armory,' which may contain ancient cultivators' elixirs, secret manuals, and even... clues to 'Foundation Establishment Pills'! The Prince takes this very seriously and has already dispatched experts to investigate. You in Jinling should also keep an eye out for such news, especially those seemingly insignificant but ancient martial arts families or relics, such as... the increasingly declining Lin family in the Western City..."

The Lin family? Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. Did it refer to the original owner's family, or the branch of Lin Ruhai, Daiyu's father? Or... something else entirely? The former dynasty's armory, Foundation Establishment Pills... this was far more complicated. Foundation Establishment Pills were elixirs that helped martial artists break through the bottleneck of the Innate Realm (seventh rank to sixth rank). They were priceless and extremely rare; their appearance would inevitably cause bloodshed. The prince behind Jia She certainly had grand ambitions, not only coveting military equipment but also wanting to seize ancient cultivation treasures to enhance his own power!

"The Lin family?" Steward Wang seemed somewhat bewildered. "Which Lin family? Oh, you mean that orphan who lives with the Jia family? What's there to be concerned about? He's just a lucky junior who knows a bit of martial arts."

"What do you know!" Bu Shiren scoffed, his tone tinged with coldness. "That kid is incredibly strange. In a short time, his strength has increased by leaps and bounds. Even Instructor Han was defeated by him. Master She suspects that he either had some kind of extraordinary encounter, or... he has someone backing him! The Prince ordered that such a suddenly rising and uncontrollable variable should either be recruited for his own use, or... eliminated as soon as possible to avoid ruining things. Don't you have some 'friends in the martial arts world' under your command? Find an opportunity to test his strength. If he really did get some kind of fortuitous encounter... hehe."

A cold glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. Sure enough, he had already been targeted and placed on the "eliminate" list. His opponent had even begun to suspect he possessed some extraordinary talent. It seemed he needed to be even more careful in the future, and his strength needed to be improved as quickly as possible to cope with more perilous situations.

"Yes, yes, yes, I understand," Steward Wang quickly replied. "That lad is staying at the Jia family's Tingzhu Pavilion. I will arrange for people to be there, waiting for an opportunity..."

"Fool!" Bu Shiren cursed. "The Jia family is under the Second Master's watchful eye. Sending lowlifes will only alert them. Find an opportunity outside! For example, when he leaves the city, or goes somewhere less crowded... Remember, be swift and decisive, leaving no trace!"

"Yes, yes, yes, I am foolish! Mr. Bu is far more astute!"

Then, the two whispered and discussed details such as the time, place, and code for the delivery, as well as how to launder money and transfer goods through the Xue and Shi families. Lin Chen focused his mind, memorizing these key pieces of information.

About the time it takes to drink a cup of tea later, the sounds of packing and people getting up to leave came from the private room.

Lin Chen quickly retreated behind the door, listening intently. Footsteps sounded, and Bu Shiren left first with his guards. A moment later, Steward Wang, carrying a small bundle, also sneaked out and headed downstairs.

Lin Chen didn't immediately follow Steward Wang. This man was just a henchman; capturing him wouldn't yield any core secrets, and would only alert his backers. He waited a moment, estimating that Bu Shiren was far enough away, before quietly opening the door and slipping out, preparing to leave.

However, just as he reached the second-floor stairwell and was about to descend, he suddenly caught a glimpse of two familiar figures sitting in a corner of the lobby near the back door!

One of them, dressed in a light purple outfit with a matching cloak and a low-hanging hood that obscured most of her face, possessed a unique, elegant yet spirited air, along with an understated air of authority that made Lin Chen instantly recognize her—it was Princess Zhaoyang in disguise, Zhao Lingxi! Opposite her sat a middle-aged man dressed as an ordinary wealthy merchant, with an unremarkable face but exceptionally shrewd eyes, who was quietly reporting something to her. Zhao Lingxi nodded slightly, her fingers unconsciously tapping lightly on the table, her gaze seemingly intentionally or unintentionally sweeping towards the stairs, where her eyes met Lin Chen's for a fleeting moment.

Why was she here? Was it a coincidence? Or… was she also eyeing Zuixianlou, or rather, Jia She's connection? Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. This princess's actions were indeed unpredictable, personally appearing in such a chaotic place. Their eyes had only met for a fleeting moment, but given her shrewdness, she had probably already recognized him.

Lin Chen didn't stop, calmly walking down the stairs, blending into the bustling crowd in the lobby, heading towards the restaurant entrance as if he were just an ordinary customer. He could feel a clear, probing gaze following him until he stepped out of Zuixianlou and disappeared into the flow of people outside.

After walking a distance from Zuixianlou, Lin Chen slowed his pace, his mind racing. Today's trip to Zuixianlou had been incredibly fruitful, but it had also unearthed many more mysteries and dangers.

Jia She colluded with a prince to smuggle military materials (black iron and cold star sand), with grand ambitions. The Xue and Shi families were involved, providing channels and cover. They were also searching for clues about the former dynasty's armory and Foundation Establishment Pills, and had even noticed the "Lin family" (likely the original owner's family or related relics). He, however, was seen as a "variable" and a potential threat, and was placed on a kill list.

The appearance of Princess Zhaoyang, Zhao Lingxi, further complicated matters. She was clearly also investigating the matter and may have noticed his conflict with Jia She's faction. Was she friend or foe? Was it mere curiosity, or did she have ulterior motives?

"These are turbulent times..." Lin Chen muttered to himself. But he felt no fear; instead, a strong fighting spirit surged within him. Crisis is opportunity. The "former dynasty's armory" and "Foundation Establishment Pill clues" that his opponents coveted were, for him, enormous opportunities. If he could snatch chestnuts from the fire, he would gain unimaginable benefits.

"The most urgent task is to improve our strength as quickly as possible to deal with any possible attacks. At the same time, we need to find a way to contact Princess Zhaoyang, figure out her intentions, and perhaps we can use her strength against her, or even... cooperate." Lin Chen made up his mind. The princess's identity is special; if he could gain her trust and support, it would be an extra layer of protection in dealing with the prince behind Jia She.

He didn't linger, quickening his pace as he returned towards the Jia mansion. The soul-nourishing wood pendant in his arms emitted a warm, gentle aura, reminding him that beyond the cold intrigue and bloodshed, there was a tender attachment he needed to protect.

Back at Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen carefully organized his observations and experiences of the day, recording them on a specially made, water-soluble slip of paper, leaving only the key information. He then summoned a trusted servant, personally appointed by Jia Zheng, and instructed him: "Present this letter to Master Zheng immediately. It must be delivered personally; it must not pass through any other hands. Tell him it concerns what I saw at Zuixianlou, as well as matters concerning 'Blackwater Manor,' 'Black Gold Iron,' 'The Xue Family,' 'The Shi Family,' 'The Prince,' and 'The Former Dynasty's Armory,' etc."

The servant's expression hardened; knowing the matter was of great importance, he solemnly accepted the item, carefully hid it close to his body, and hurried away.

Lin Chen knew that Jia Zheng, upon receiving this information, would be furious and even more vigilant, utilizing his connections in the court and local areas to launch an investigation and countermeasures. This would alleviate some of the pressure on him and temporarily distract Jia She's faction.

After seeing the servant off, Lin Chen returned to his quiet room and sat cross-legged. He didn't immediately begin cultivating; instead, he took out half of the "Double Blessings of Fortune and Longevity" pendant, his gaze deep and thoughtful.

“The Prince… the Xue family… the Shi family… the former dynasty’s armory…” He repeatedly processed this information, and a vague plan gradually took shape in his mind. Perhaps, he could use this opportunity not only to resolve his own crisis, but also… to take the initiative and seize greater benefits.

“Strength, it’s all about strength.” Lin Chen gripped the jade pendant tightly, his eyes flashing with a sharp light. “The Thunderclap Fist… it’s time to try and comprehend it. Also, the ‘cooperation’ with the princess should be put on the agenda.”

He took a deep breath, suppressing his chaotic thoughts, and took out the strange fragment containing the first form of the Thunderclap Fist. This time, he didn’t immediately try to visualize the runes containing thunderous intent. Instead, he first circulated the *Heart Cleansing Technique* and the *Qi Control Technique*, adjusting himself to his optimal state, highly concentrating his spiritual power. Then, he carefully probed the fragment with an extremely weak wisp of spiritual power.

"Boom!"

A faint flash of lightning seemed to explode in his mind! A blurry, dynamic image of a figure dancing, surrounded by arcs of electricity, flashed by, followed by a violent, swift, and destructive intent that surged into his mind! But this time, Lin Chen was prepared. He used the *Cleansing Heart Technique* to stabilize his mind, the *Qi Control Technique* to guide his internal energy, and his powerful late-stage eighth-rank spiritual energy to firmly lock down the foreign "thunderous intent," forcibly binding, analyzing, and comprehending it.

Although he still felt his blood surging and his brow throbbing, at least he didn't lose his composure like the first time. He slowly closed his eyes, repeatedly visualizing the fleeting image of the lightning-clad figure in his mind, comprehending the true meaning of that "lightning-fast" and "thunderous" fist technique.

Outside the window, the sun's shadow slanted westward, and crows returned to their nests. Inside the Listening Bamboo Pavilion, the young man was immersed in his initial understanding of the Supreme Thunder Technique, his aura sometimes as calm as an abyss, sometimes faintly carrying the sounds of wind and thunder. Meanwhile, beneath the night sky of Jinling City, various forces continued their quiet operations and clashes, each following its own path. A far greater storm was slowly brewing in a corner unseen by anyone.


Chapter 30: Investigating the Lin Family Ancestral Home, Discovering a Secret

Since the secret meeting at Lanyue Pavilion and returned, three days have passed. 

During these three days, Jinling City appeared calm on the surface, but beneath the surface, there were turbulent undercurrents. Jia She, suspected of Jia Zheng's secret interference in the "Heishui Manor" deal, the loss of an important token (half a jade pendant), and the disappearance of his subordinates "Poison Palm" Han Tao and others made him suspect Jia Zheng was secretly sabotaging. Shocked and furious, he was also afraid to act rashly because Jia Zheng had cleared the gates with an iron fist and controlled most of the power in the mansion. He had no choice but to hide in the eastern courtyard, secretly intensifying contact with Prince Zhongshun, the Xue family, and the Shi family to discuss countermeasures. Within the Jia household, with Jia Zheng's full support and Lin Chen's military backing, Tanchun's efforts to rectify internal affairs and audit accounts proceeded smoothly. Although resistance remained, the situation was settled, many longstanding problems were eliminated, and the servants gradually got used to the decisive and efficient stewardship style of this "Third Miss." 

Lin Chen, on the other hand, had completely entered a different state. During the day, he was still the collateral son who occasionally visited the library and spent most of his time listening to the "quiet cultivation" at the Bamboo Pavilion, occasionally meeting Tanchun and Shishu to exchange trivial information about the "internal residence rectification." But secretly, he had already begun to operate quietly as the holder of the "Xuanlong Token." 

Through the secret code left by "Shadow Seven" (the mysterious expert at the early seventh rank, whom Lin Chen never saw in person, only contacting him through special means), he obtained the first encrypted information about the "Xuanzhen Armory" in an inconspicuous old bookstore in the west market of Jinling City. The intelligence showed that the Xuanzhen Arsenal was indeed left behind by a rogue cultivator from the previous dynasty with the honored title "Xuanzhen Shangren." His cultivation had reached the Transformation Realm, and three hundred years ago, he passed away, sealing his lifelong collection of techniques, pills, divine weapons, and most importantly, the "Foundation Establishment Pill" formula and several finished products in a secret realm. The clues to the entrance were related to an ancient map called "Fragments of the Map of Mountains and Rivers and the State." It is said that this painting was last seen in the hands of an official surnamed Lin from the previous dynasty, who was the great-grandfather of the late Salt Inspector Lin Ruhai, who was also of the same clan as Lin Chen of the Western Courtyard. 

The clue indeed pointed to the Lin family! Moreover, it seemed more toward Daiyu's father's branch, but it was also connected to the Lin family ancestors of the Western Courtyard. Lin Chen understood in his heart that it was no wonder Prince Zhongshun and Jia She had noticed the "Lin family of the Western City." 

That afternoon, Lin Chen requested leave from Jia Zheng, citing "sorting through his parents' belongings and paying respects to his ancestors," saying he wanted to leave the city to visit the Lin family ancestral tomb and abandoned ancestral home in the western suburbs. Jia Zheng suspected no reason, only reminded him to be extra cautious, and sent two reliable guards to accompany him, ostensibly for protection but in reality for surveillance (of course, this surveillance was now an official form of protection). Lin Chen accepted this calmly. 

In the western suburbs, in the mountain hollow where the Lin family's ancestral graves stand, the vegetation withered and the place looked even more desolate. Several graves were neglected and overgrown with dead grass. Lin Chen solemnly worshipped at his parents' graves, then cleaned the other faintly recognizable ancestral graves, making a thorough appearance. The two guards kept watch from a distance, not approaching. 

After finishing the worship, Lin Chen followed the original owner's vague memories deeper into the ruins near the foot of the mountain. That was the old residence where the Lin family had lived in the west courtyard for many years. Since the original owner's parents moved into the Jia family's side courtyard in the city, it had been abandoned for many years, leaving only broken walls and ruins. 

The old house was small, with three courtyards. Now the roof had collapsed, the walls crumbling, weeds overgrown inside the courtyard, and only a few old trees had bare branches left.
