The cold winter wind passed through the dilapidated doors and windows, making a mournful sound and adding to the bleakness. 

Lin Chen sent two guards to wait outside the residence, saying he wanted to go in alone to take a look and find some old items as keepsakes. Knowing his martial arts skills were strong and seeing the place truly remote, the guards nodded and waited at the gate. 

Stepping into the ruins, a wave of stale dust and musty smell hit his face. Lin Chen activated his mental power, coordinating with the 'Clear Heart Technique' to carefully sense everything around him. To ordinary people, this was just an ordinary abandoned residence. But with his keen spiritual senses and his 'keen eye for talent,' he could faintly sense that within the ruins, there seemed to be a faint, almost imperceptible 'field' that was different from ordinary yin energy. That 'field' was faint, as if it could dissipate at any moment, yet it was exceptionally tough, faintly connected to the earth vein energy. 

Following the description Ying Qi provided about the possible existence of the "Xuanzhen Arsenal," he paced slowly through the ruins, his gaze sweeping over every broken beam and pillar, collapsed walls, and stone steps buried by wild grass. 

The front yard and the central courtyard found nothing. Arriving at the spot that should have been the main house of the rear residence, the place was the most severely damaged, with only half a charred wall and broken tiles scattered all over the floor. Lin Chen's gaze fell on a seemingly ordinary bluestone pedestal in the corner, covered with withered vines and thick dust. The base was originally the base that supported the building's columns, with an ancient and simple design, nothing special. 

But as Lin Chen approached, he felt that faint trace of 'field' seemed to become a bit clearer here. He crouched down, brushed off the withered vines and dust from the base, revealing the lotus pattern carved below, already blurred and blurred. Lotus flowers are commonly used in Buddhist and Taoist fashions, so they were nothing special. But upon closer inspection, Lin Chen noticed that the number of petals on the lotus was not the usual eight or twelve, but rather ...... Seven petals. 

Seven-Petal Lotus? Lin Chen's heart stirred. Ying Qi's intelligence mentioned that 'Master Xuanzhen' loved the number 'seven' most in his life. His cultivation techniques, formations, and even the layout of his cave residence often subtly matched the wonders of 'seven.' Could this seven-petal lotus be some kind of mark? 

He tried to gently press his palm against the center of the seven-petal lotus pattern, slowly channeling a strand of internal breath, while using the 'Qi Control Technique' method to simulate a special frequency fluctuation described in the intelligence from the 'Xuanzhen Arsenal.' 

At first, there was no reaction at all. But just as Lin Chen thought his judgment was wrong and was about to withdraw his inner energy— 

"Buzz......" 

A faint humming sounded, as if coming from deep underground! At the center of the bluestone base, each petal of the seven-petaled lotus pattern began to glow with a faint, milky-white halo like a firefly! Though it was only a fleeting flash, Lin Chen clearly felt a faint vibration on the stone surface beneath his palm, as if triggering some extremely hidden mechanism. 

Immediately after, not far from his feet, a patch of ground paved with blue bricks, seemingly indistinguishable from the surrounding ground, silently sank downward, revealing a dark cave entrance just wide enough for one person to pass through! Inside the entrance, a faint flow of air surged out, carrying a long-sealed, cold, and damp aura. Yet there was no foul stench; instead, there was a faint, extremely thin, invigorating spiritual energy. 

Found it! Lin Chen was shaken. Beneath the ruins of the Lin family ancestral home, there really is another world to the world! The entrance to this cave is so well concealed, and only a special technique can open it—it's definitely not an ordinary cellar. Most likely, it's related to clues from the 'Xuanzhen Arsenal.' 

He glanced back toward the mansion's entrance; the two guards noticed nothing unusual. Without hesitation, he flashed and silently slipped into the cave entrance. The moment he entered, the sunken blue brick floor above slowly closed up again, returning to its original state. From the outside, it looked no different from the surrounding ground, even the dust covering it was identical, clearly showing a ingenious mechanism. 

Below the cave entrance was a narrow, sloping stone staircase, bottomless and unfathomable. The stone steps were covered in thick dust, clearly untouched for a long time. The stone walls on both sides were damp, with a bit of moss growing there. The thin spiritual energy in the air became clearer. Though still faint, in this era of declining Dharma, it was already extremely rare. 

Lin Chen unleashed his Light Spirit Step and silently descended the stone steps. He did not light a fire striker; relying on the extraordinary eyesight and spiritual perception of a late-stage eighth-rank martial artist, he could see in the dark. The stone steps spiraled downward, descending about twenty or thirty zhang. Ahead, the space suddenly opened up, revealing a natural stone cave several zhang in diameter. 

In the center of the grotto, there was a small spiritual spring, gushing clear water. Around the spring, spiritual energy swirled—the very source of that thin spiritual energy. Beside the spring stood a black stone tablet, half a person's height, smooth as a mirror. On the stele, a certain silvery-white mineral was inlaid with a complex pattern resembling a star map or mountain range, with a few lines of ancient seal script characters nearby. 

Lin Chen approached to take a closer look. The patterns were interlaced with lines, with seven stars shining especially brightly at the center, connected along specific trajectories, faintly forming the shape of the Big Dipper. Below the pattern, ancient seal script read: 

"The secret is hidden in the Seven Stars, the earth's veins are hidden in Jinling. The map divides yin and yang; when united, it reveals its traces. The descendants of the Lin family, holding this fragmented map, can glimpse a glimpse of heaven's secrets. However, the armory is a crucial place; only those with fate and virtue may enter; those who force it will surely face heavenly punishment. Wait for the destined to stay, never betray your ancestors. ” 

Sure enough, it was the 'Fragment of the Mountains and Rivers and the State'! Or rather, it was a clue to part of the fragmented map! The star map embedded in this stele should be part of the 'Yin Map' or 'Yang Map'. And 'the map divides yin and yang; when combined, it reveals its traces' means there is another fragment of the map. Only when the two are united can the true entrance to the armory be found. The stele prompts, 'Descendants of the Lin family, holding this fragment of the map,' indicating that this clue was indeed left by the Lin family descendants, and it would likely require the Lin family's bloodline or a specific token to activate or retrieve it. 

Lin Chen tried to bring his palm close to the stele, slowly circulating his inner breath, while simultaneously visualizing the Lin family ancestor. The stele showed no response. He then took out the half piece of the "Blessing and Longevity Double Pendant" from his bosom. This jade pendant was an imperial gift, perhaps related to the royal family or the previous dynasty? But it was still useless. 

It seems that he is not the "Lin family descendant" recognized by this stone tablet? Or perhaps he needs a more specific token, such as something left behind by Daiyu's father, Lin Ruhai? Or perhaps a specific bloodline concentration? 

Lin Chen frowned. The clue was right in front of him, yet he couldn't obtain it—this feeling was unpleasant. But he didn't lose heart, carefully examining the stone tablet and the entire grotto. The spiritual spring gushed forth, providing only a thin layer of spiritual energy, but seemed nothing special. The walls of the grotto were also quite ordinary, not as if there was any mechanism behind it. 

His gaze finally settled on the few lines of ancient seal script characters on the stone tablet. "Descendants of the Lin family"...... Could it be that it doesn't just refer to bloodline, but rather to descendants who "inherit certain specific legacies or wills of the Lin family"? For example, someone who has cultivated some kind of Lin family ancestral technique, or possesses a signature ancestral artifact? 

The Lin family's ancestors...... In the original owner's memory, her parents were ordinary people who had never practiced martial arts and left no ancestral heirlooms. However, Daiyu's father, Lin Ruhai, was a top scholar and had served as a salt inspector, so he might know more family secrets and might have left special items for Daiyu. 

Thinking of this, Lin Chen was moved. He might not be able to directly take the "fragment" clue from the stele, but he could rub it down! If he could obtain relevant tokens or information from Daiyu in the future, perhaps he could unlock the mysteries within. Even if he couldn't, copying this clue and handing it to Princess Zhaoyang would still be a merit, further earning her trust and support. 

He immediately took out the blank "jade slip" he carried with him for recording key techniques (a rare and common way for low-level cultivators, but he had obtained a few from system check-ins and the Jia family library), and also a small piece of "Shadow Ink" (also rare, used for brief image recording). He placed the jade slip close to the star map on the stone tablet, carefully guiding the ink with his spiritual power, and inscribed the star map and text on the stele with perfect accuracy. 

This process was quite mentally exhausting, but Lin Chen had the 'Clear Heart Technique' to guard his spiritual platform and the support of late-stage eighth-grade spiritual power, so he completed it smoothly. After finishing the rubbing, he carefully examined the jade slip to confirm the information was complete and correct, then carefully put it away. 

After finishing all this, he did not linger. This place was not suitable to linger long; the two guards were still outside, waiting so long without seeing him leave, raising suspicion. He retraced his steps back and arrived below the entrance. Looking up, the closed blue brick floor was perfectly sealed; from the inside, there was no flaw, and no mechanism had been activated. 

Lin Chen had already anticipated this. He held his breath and focused, activating the 'Qi Control Technique,' channeling his inner breath into his palms, slowly lifting them upward, while sensing the changes in the 'field' on the blue brick floor. With the profound perception of the 'Qi Control Technique,' he quickly found the faint energy nodes of the blue brick ground, the surrounding earth veins, and the mechanism at the cave entrance. He used his inner breath to simulate the frequency fluctuations opposite to those when he activated it, and gave a gentle vibration. 

With a soft "click," the blue brick ground above his head sank again. Lin Chen's figure flashed and he leapt out of the cave entrance. Almost as soon as his feet touched the ground, the ground had returned to its original state. 

He brushed the dust off his clothes, then casually picked up a rusty, inconspicuous copper ring (apparently an old door knocker) from the ruins, held it in his hand, pretended to find an "old item," and then walked toward the mansion gate with a calm expression. 

"Sorry to keep you both waiting. After searching for a while, I only found this old item, which is a keepsake." Lin Chen said to the two guards. 

The guards suspected he was there, and thought the young master's filial piety was commendable. The three of them left the ruins together and returned to the city. 

Back at Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen immediately shut the door tightly, took out the jade slip recording the "Star Map Remnant Stele," and examined it carefully again. The Star Map was mysterious, the writing archaic, and the information contained was far beyond what could be comprehended in a short time. But at the very least, he had obtained a crucial clue. 

"We need to inform the princess of this matter as soon as possible, and find a way to investigate Daiyu's side regarding any tokens or information left behind by the Lin family ancestors." Lin Chen pondered in his heart. He summoned the special contact token left by Ying Qi—a seemingly ordinary Qingfu coin. On its back, several codes were engraved with a specific technique, saying, "Important matter, contact us quickly," then placed it in an inconspicuous gap on the windowsill. 

That night, at midnight, the Qingfu coin quietly disappeared, replaced by a new note with only four words: "Tomorrow night, the same place." ” 

The next night, on the top floor of Lanyue Pavilion. 

Under the gentle glow of the luminous pearl, Zhao Lingxi looked at the jade slip rubbing Lin Chen presented, her stunning face showing an unmistakable look of surprise. She carefully examined the star chart and the text, especially the phrases "The map divides yin and yang; when united, traces appear" and "The Lin family descendants hold this fragmented map," her eyes flickering with light. 

"Young master, you truly did not disappoint me!" Zhao Lingxi looked up, her gaze at Lin Chen full of admiration. "This is undoubtedly the 'Yin Map' constellation section of the 'Map of Mountains and Rivers and the Nation'! According to the information I obtained, the other part of the 'Yang Map' should be a map indicating the direction of specific mountains, rivers, and earth veins. Only when the two are combined can we locate the entrance to the 'Xuanzhen Arsenal.' Unexpectedly, the clues to this hidden map are actually hidden beneath the Lin family ancestral home in the Western Courtyard! ” 

"Your Highness, identifying this 'Lin family descendant' will likely be difficult." Lin Chen said, "I have tried but it seems I cannot activate or retrieve clues from the stone tablet." ” 

"It's fine." Zhao Lingxi carefully put away the jade slip, "The fact that you were able to make a copy is already a great achievement. This 'Lin family descendant' may not refer solely to bloodline. Perhaps a specific token is needed, or a particular cultivation method, perhaps...... It is related to the late Lord Lin Ruhai's lineage. I will order people to continue investigating Lord Lin Ruhai's belongings, especially the items that might be left to his daughter, Miss Daiyu. Young master, you must also pay close attention to Miss Daiyu's side. If there are any clues about the Lin family ancestors or special items left by Lord Lin, be sure to be vigilant. ” 

"Yes, sir." Lin Chen agreed; this was exactly what he had been thinking. 

"Also," Zhao Lingxi pondered, "your operation this time may be secretive, but Jia She's side has suffered repeated setbacks and lost important tokens. I fear they might become desperate and jump over the wall. I received secret news that Prince Zhongshun has secretly sent two experts at the early seventh rank to infiltrate Jinling, most likely targeting you or to speed up the search for clues from the armory. You must be very careful. Unless necessary, try to minimize outings recently. Listening to Zhuxuan's defenses, I will have Yingqi secretly strengthen them." ” 

Seventh-rank experts! And two! Lin Chen's heart tightened. This was not something an eighth-rank martial artist could compare to. There was a qualitative gap between seventh and eighth ranks, but their initial Qi control far surpassed their combat power. If two seventh-rank experts really targeted him, it would indeed be dangerous. 

"Thank you for the reminder, Your Highness. Lin Chen will be extra cautious." Lin Chen said solemnly. 

"Mm." Zhao Lingxi nodded, then took out a small white jade bottle from her sleeve and handed it to Lin Chen. "Inside are three 'Xuanyuan Pills,' a secret palace recipe. They are remarkably effective for stabilizing realms, purifying internal energy, and healing internal injuries. They are beneficial to your current cultivation. Please keep them for now, just in case." ” 

"Thank you, Your Highness, for your generous gift." Lin Chen took it. It felt warm to the touch, with a medicinal fragrance deep within—definitely no ordinary product. 

"Since we have already cooperated, there's no need for such formality." Zhao Lingxi waved her hand, gazing out the window into the deep night, her gaze deep and far-reaching. "A storm is brewing, and the wind fills the building. Prince Zhongshun, Jia She, the Xue family, the Shi family, and those mysterious spirits lurking in the shadows...... Jinling City is probably about to lose its peace. Young master, are you ready? ” 

Lin Chen also looked out the window. In the darkness, countless lights shone like stars. He gripped the Xuanlong Token and jade bottle tightly in his sleeve, his eyes calm and unruffled, yet filled with a rock-solid determination. 

"Your Highness, rest assured. Lin Chen has never feared wind or rain. ” 

The night breeze blew through the window, carrying a chill, yet it could not dispel the silent understanding and determination between the two in the elegant room. An even greater storm surrounding the relics of the previous dynasty, the power struggles at court, and the feuds of the aristocratic families seemed to have quietly begun in this quiet deep night.


Chapter 31 A Seventh-Rank Master Attacks, Lin Chen Breaks Through in a Critical Situation

After returning from Lanyue Pavilion and receiving the "Xuanyuan Pill," Lin Chen received the princess's warning about two seventh-rank experts infiltrating Jinling, and his nerves had quietly tightened. He knew that the conflict with Jia She and Prince Zhongshun had completely intensified, shifting from covert scheming to blatant military threats. A seventh-rank martial artist could be considered a master in this high-martial world, able to fly short-range by controlling qi, releasing internal breath and condensing his form, with combat power far surpassing eighth-rank. Sending two people at once specifically to deal with this "variable" showed the opponent's fierce killing intent and their determination to win. 

The defenses at Ting Zhuxuan, arranged in secret by Ying Qi and the additional subordinates Jia Zhengming had deployed, were as solid as iron barrels. Open sentries and hidden sentries, mechanisms and early warnings—everything was in place. Ordinary eighth-rank martial artists would never have the chance to sneak in silently. But Lin Chen knew well that for truly skilled seventh-rank experts skilled in concealment and with strange movements, these defenses were not impenetrable. He could not rely solely on external protection for his own safety. 

Back in the meditation room, he immediately took a 'Xuanyuan Pill.' As the pill entered his stomach, it transformed into a gentle, mellow yet overwhelming torrent of medicinal power, quickly merging into his limbs and bones. Unlike the violent and blazing nature of the Barrier-Breaking Pill, the Xuanyuan Pill's power was more balanced and peaceful, like spring rain nourishing his meridians, dantian, and even his spirit. In just the time it takes to drink a cup of tea, he felt his internal energy become even more refined and refined. The tension in his mind, which had been tense from recent days of travel, cultivation, and crisis responses, was completely relieved, returning to his best state. The princess's gift was truly extraordinary. 

Having adjusted to peak condition, Lin Chen wasted no time. He knew well that passive defense was never a long-term solution, nor was it his character. Rather than waiting for the enemy to come knocking, it was better to actively improve his strength and, with absolute power, crush all conspiracies and ambushes. 

He took out the fragment of the first move of the "Thunderfist." Previously, due to insufficient cultivation, lack of spiritual power, and tight time, he had only tasted it briefly and gained some insights. Now, facing the deadly threat of a seventh-rank expert, he had to take a risk and try to truly master this supreme Xuan-level lower-grade fist technique, turning it into his own to find a glimmer of hope in this desperate situation. 

Calming his mind, he activated the 'Clear Mind Technique,' and his spiritual platform became clear and clear. The 'Qi Control Technique' moved in response, his internal breath lively and full of spirit. He placed the fragment on his lap, not directly visualizing the rune patterns containing thunder intent, but first immersed his mind into the fist technique true meaning he had previously captured—'lightning strikes in time' and 'thunderous power.' 

In his mind, the final strike from that day at Qixia Mountain and Princess Zhaoyang, the scene merging with the "Vajra Pushing the Mountain" fist intent, and the fleeting glimpse of thunder and figure on the fragmented page, slowly overlapped. The extreme strength and ferocity of the Vajra Fist, the penetrating vibrations of the Stone-Splitting Fist, the agile control of the Qi Control Technique...... All these insights flowed through his heart. He no longer tried to forcibly comprehend those profound runes, but instead tried to simulate and reconstruct that "thunder" realm through his martial understanding. 

What is thunder? It is the dull oppression when dark clouds press down, the fleeting brilliance of lightning tearing through the sky, the deafening roar that cleanses the world, the vibrant vitality contained after destruction...... It is ultimate speed, violent power, the majesty that shatters all laws! 

He slowly raised his right fist, his internal energy rapidly traversing, compressing, rubbing, and stirring through the specific meridians of his arm along a mysterious path, completely different from what he had learned before! Strands of pale golden inner energy condensed on the edge of his fist. It was no longer the heavy heaviness of the Vajra Fist, nor the gentle and agile style of the Qi Control Technique, but instead began to carry a faint, almost invisible silver-white electric light, almost imperceptible to the naked eye, flickering with a silvery white electric light! In the air, faint "crackling" sounds could be heard—this was the faint ionization phenomenon between the air caused by the high-speed friction of internal breath and the simulating effects of lightning. 

Still not enough! Lin Chen's temples bulged slightly as he concentrated his spiritual power and furiously squeezed out the internal energy in his dantian. Ignoring the slight swelling pain in his meridians, he poured more and purer internal energy into his right fist, while visualizing thunder striking down and all things lying dormant, merging that "heavenly might" will into his fist intent! 

"Ssshhh—" 

The silver-white electric light on the fist suddenly brightened and pulsed even more violently! A violent, swift, and destructive pressure quietly spread from Lin Chen's right fist, making the air in the quiet room seem to freeze and grow heavier. The oil lamp flame on the table was nearly extinguished by this invisible pressure, flickering violently. 

"First Form of Thunder Fist—Lightning Appears!" 

Lin Chen shouted in his heart and suddenly threw a punch forward, blowing it out in the void! No target, just unleashing this concentrated punch intent and inner energy into the void ahead! 

"Boom!" 

A louder, shorter, and more violent explosion far sharper, shorter, and more violent than the "Vajra Mountain Pushing" suddenly erupted in the meditation chamber! It was not a physical impact, but a sonic boom caused by the violent friction, compression, and explosion of internal energy and air! A visible fist-shaped energy, pale golden and wrapped with silver-white electric light, shot out from Lin Chen's fist tip like lightning, instantly striking the heavy stone wall three zhang away! 

"Bang!" 

A muffled thud! The stone wall shook violently, and dust began to fall in a puff. The fist-shaped energy did not penetrate the wall (Lin Chen deliberately controlled the direction of its power), but the wall that was struck suddenly revealed a bowl-sized pit, about an inch deep, with charred edges and fine cracks! Around the pit, the stone surface showed strange signs of enamelization, as if instantly scorched by high temperatures, with a few wisps of extremely faint blue smoke curling up. 

It worked! Although it was only in form and far from the true power described in the "Thunder Fist"—"the punch produces thunder accompanied by sound, lightning splits the sky, and all things are destroyed"—this punch already possessed a trace of the essence of the "Thunder Fist"—extreme speed, violent bursts, and the unique defense break and burn effects with lightning attributes! Its power was at least thirty percent stronger than his full-power "Vajra Mountain Pushing"! And it was even faster and harder to defend against! 

"Pfft!" Lin Chen's face also turned pale. He groaned softly and staggered back half a step. His right arm was numb and weak, most of his dantian energy instantly drained, and a strong sense of weakness swept over him. Forcibly activating the Xuan-level fist technique, even if only the first form of the basics, was a huge burden on his current cultivation and control over internal energy. One strike had nearly consumed nearly half of his internal energy, and his meridians ached faintly. 

But in his eyes, a radiant light of wild joy burst forth! Worth it! This punch was enough to become his trump card against a seventh-rank expert! Though it shouldn't be used lightly, at this critical moment, it could definitely be the decisive blow and turn the tide! 

He quickly sat cross-legged, took another Xuanyuan Pill, and gripped a low-grade spirit stone, fully channeling the 'Qi Control Technique' and 'Clear Heart Art' to recover energy and calm the turbulent qi and meridians. At the same time, he carefully recalled every detail of his earlier punch, feeling the feeling of the 'thunder intent' flowing and erupting through his meridians, striving to master it quickly, reduce consumption, and increase his proficiency. 

Just as he had restored his internal energy to seventy percent and his spirit refocused, preparing to attempt a second exercise with even weaker control— 

Outside the quiet room, in the night sky, a sharp, short, piercing whistle, as sharp as the night owl's cry, sounded out of nowhere! Immediately after, there were several dull sounds of flesh collapsing, as well as the muffled sounds of weapons drawn and clashing energies! 

Enemy attack! And it was a top-tier expert! They actually broke through all the outer defenses and directly reached the core area of Tingzhu Pavilion! 

Lin Chen suddenly opened his eyes, a cold light shooting in them! Without a moment's hesitation, his figure flashed like lightning, breaking open the door and darting into the courtyard. 

Under the cold moonlight, the courtyard was already in complete disarray. Four guards sent by Jia Zheng, all at the early eighth-rank cultivation, lay on the ground, their fate unknown. Another six or seven ninth-rank guards formed a formation, barely holding off two ghost-like shadows weaving through the crowd. 

The two shadows were both dressed in black nightwear and black headscarves, exposing only a pair of cold, merciless eyes like venomous snakes. Their movements were astonishingly fast, weaving through the crowd as if strolling leisurely. With every strike, one guard would fall screaming in agony—some with broken tendons, some with injuries to vital points. They had no weapons in their hands, relying only on their bare palms—sometimes slapping, sometimes tapping, sometimes grabbing. Their moves were concise and ruthless, their internal breath condensed like needles, penetrating with incredible power—clearly following a sinister and sinister assassination path. They were the two early-stage seventh-rank experts sent by Prince Zhongshun that the princess had warned them about! 

"Form a circular formation! Protect the young master!" A guard leader with the highest cultivation, a mid-eighth rank (Jia Zheng's trusted aide), shouted fiercely, trying to steady the formation. But facing two seventh-rank assassins with strange movements and overwhelming strength, the so-called formation was as fragile as paper. 

"Sss!" A dark shadow flashed past the head guard's side like light smoke, his fingertip lightly tapping under his ribs. The guard leader was instantly struck as if by lightning, his whole body trembled violently, blood spat out, and he staggered backward, already suffering serious internal injuries. 

Another shadow, like a venomous snake having spotted its prey, instantly locked onto Lin Chen, who had just burst out the door. With a flicker, it dodged two guards blocking its way and, like a bone-changing, lunged toward Lin Chen! Before he arrived, a chilling, bone-chilling palm wind that seemed capable of freezing blood had already descended from a distance, sealing off the space several feet around Lin Chen! 

Fast! Ruthless! Accurate! With the first move, it was a decisive strike! Clearly, the opponent had already anticipated Lin Chen's strength, and immediately used the Qi control technique of a seventh-rank martial artist, pressing down with momentum and aiming for a decisive blow! 

Faced with this chilling palm force that could instantly render an ordinary eighth-rank martial artist unable to resist, Lin Chen's pupils contracted, but his mind remained as calm as an icy mirror. The 'Clear Heart Art' operated on its own, dispelling the spiritual chill and stagnation brought by the palm wind. Inside his body, the 'Qi Control Technique' was frantically activated, his powerful inner breath instantly spreading throughout his body. The Light Spirit Step was executed to its peak, his figure like willow catkins in the wind. At the instant the cold palm wind struck his body, he twisted and twisted strangely, narrowly avoiding the fiercest center of the palm wind, only being struck on his shoulder by the edge of the force. 

"Sss!" The clothes on his shoulder tore, and a thin layer of white frost instantly formed on his skin, radiating a bone-chilling cold and burning pain. But Lin Chen used the momentum to retreat, dissipating most of the force. At the same time, he swept his right hand across his waist, and several poison-tempered bone-piercing nails rained down like a storm toward the incoming shadow! They were the hidden weapons obtained from Han Tao and the others. 

"Petty trick!" The shadow snorted coldly, neither dodging nor dodging. With a sweep of his sleeve, a supple yet powerful internal energy surged forth, drawing all the bone-piercing nails into his sleeve. Then, with a swift swing, he reflexed toward Lin Chen at even faster speed and at a more cunning angle! At the same time, he moved again, like maggots on the instep, relentlessly pursuing him. He struck out with another palm strike, his palm even more fierce. Earlier, faint sounds of ghostly wailing and wolf howling echoed in the air, chilling to the core. 

Lin Chen flashed again, narrowly avoiding the reflected hidden weapon, but the immediate palm wind had already blocked all his escape routes. No way to escape! 

"Young master, be careful!" Not far away, the injured head guard's eyes were bloodshot. He mustered his inner breath, trying to rush in for help, but was forced back by another seventh-rank assassin with a casual palm, coughing up blood again. 

Life and death hanging by a thread! A ruthless glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes, and he no longer retreated. He stomped his feet hard, sat down on his horse, and his internal energy, which had just recovered most of his internal energy, surged like boiling lava, wildly surging toward his right fist along the mysterious path of the "Thunderfist"! In his vision, that bolt of lightning tearing through the sky flashed again! 

"Thunder Fist—Lightning Appears!" 

No charge, no flashiness. Just as the chilling palm wind was about to strike his chest, Lin Chen exhaled and threw a punch! On the edge of his fist, within the pale golden inner breath, silver-white lightning flashed again. Though weaker than during the meditation chamber, it was more concise and swift! The punch rang out, producing a short, piercing sonic boom, as if a faint bolt of thunder was truly exploding at the fist! 

"Hmm?" For the first time, the seventh-rank assassin's eyes showed surprise and doubt. He hadn't expected this late-stage eighth-rank kid to unleash such a swift and violent punch, with a faint lightning attribute in his fist! This fist technique was definitely not the Jia family or any ordinary martial art of the martial world! 

But after all, he was a seventh-rank expert with rich combat experience. The shock lasted only a moment; his palm movement remained unchanged, his inner energy surged, and a faint layer of black ice crystals appeared in his palm, fiercely facing the fist wrapped in electric light! He intended to crush this seemingly strange fist technique with absolute cultivation and the quality of his internal energy! 

"Bang—crack!" 

Fists and palms collided! There was no earth-shattering roar, but a dull, almost dull explosion rang out, accompanied by a clear, glass-like shattering sound! The black ice crystal in the seventh-rank assassin's palm shattered inch by inch the moment it met Lin Chen's fist! A violent, scorching, and strangely piercing punch force like a tiny thunderclap ruthlessly tore through his chilling palm force and slammed into the meridians in his palm! 

"Ah!" The seventh-rank assassin let out a muffled groan, his body trembling violently. The punch forced him to retreat three steps into the air before he steadied himself. His entire right arm instantly felt numb and stinging, his palm charred black, wisps of blue smoke billowing out, and the chilling internal breath was thrown into chaos by the supremely yang and strong thunder force. His blood and energy churned, his throat tingled with a sweetness, and he nearly spat out blood! His eyes were filled with disbelief and terror. 

Lin Chen, on the other hand, was even worse. Although he had barely shaken a seventh-rank assassin with the strangeness and burst of the "Thunder Fist," the gap in cultivation between them was simply too great. The opponent's chilling palm force, which had been mostly weakened, still partially penetrated his body. He felt a bone-chilling, extremely vicious force pierce his right arm, wildly destroying his meridians and invading his heart vessels. His chest felt as if struck hard, his vision darkened, and he could no longer suppress a mouthful of blood, spurting out with a "wah," staining his chest red. He was thrown backward like a kite with a broken string, crashing heavily against the courtyard wall before finally sliding to the ground. For a moment, he couldn't get up, and his right arm hung limp, almost losing consciousness. 

One strike left both sides wounded! Lin Chen was seriously injured, but even that seventh-rank assassin was in a tough spot—his right arm was temporarily half disabled, his internal energy chaotic, and his combat power was reduced by at least thirty percent! 

Everything happened so fast—from Lin Chen rushing out the door to the two exchanging blows, it took only two or three breaths. Another seventh-rank assassin had just forced the head guard back again, and was shocked to see this, clearly not expecting his companion to suffer at the hands of an eighth-rank kid. 

"Useless!" Finish this quickly! " He shouted in a low voice, ignoring the remaining guards. With a flicker, he lunged at the severely wounded, seemingly powerless Lin Chen, his eyes filled with murderous intent as his fingers formed claws, aiming straight for Lin Chen's throat! If this claw was firmly seized, Lin Chen would surely die! 

"Young master!" The remaining guards saw this and their eyes bulged with rage, desperately trying to stop them, but the fierce force he casually unleashed sent him staggering unsteadily. 

Lin Chen leaned against the wall, staring at the deadly claws rapidly expanding before him. There was not a trace of fear in his eyes, only a flash of determination and madness. He struggled to raise his left hand, as if trying to block, but his movements were slow and powerless. 

A cruel sinister smile appeared in the seventh-rank assassin's eyes, as if he could already see Lin Chen's throat being crushed. 

However, just as his claws were about to touch the skin on Lin Chen's neck— 

Mutation Regeneration! 

Lin Chen's seemingly powerless left hand, between his index and middle fingers, had quietly caught a wooden pendant the size of a longan, pale purple all over and exuding a serene fragrance—the very very soul-nurturing wooden pendant he had carved himself, originally intended for Daiyu! 

"Explosion!" 

Lin Chen silently shouted in his heart, pouring his last remaining pure inner energy and all his spiritual power into the Soul-Nurturing Wood pendant without reservation, simultaneously unleashing the cool, spiritual primordial energy of Daiyu from the "Heart-Soul Seal" bond contained within, as well as all the spirit and protective will he had poured into carving it! 

"Buzz——!" 

The Soul-Nurturing Wood Pendant suddenly erupted with an unprecedented dazzling purple light! The purple light was not dazzling, but carried a pure power that cleansed the soul and suppressed evil spirits, instantly enveloping the three-foot space around Lin Chen! Moreover, a cool, resolute, and indomitable will spread along with the purple light! 

The deadly claw of the seventh-rank assassin, upon touching the purple light, seemed to collide with an invisible yet incredibly tough barrier, producing a dull thud and unable to advance further! Moreover, the pure soul power and protective intent contained within the purple light were like red-hot branding irons, fiercely burning his spirit and palm, which had condensed killing intent and chilling inner energy! 

"Ah——!" The second seventh-rank assassin let out a scream even more piercing than his companion, as if struck by lightning. He abruptly withdrew his palm, only to see the hand he was grabbing at Lin Chen. The palm was already torn and charred, with wisps of black energy (formed from chilling internal energy and killing intent) purified and dispersed by the purple light! He clutched his trembling, painful right hand and retreated, his eyes filled with fear as he looked at the purple light, as if he had seen some kind of nemesis. 

This Soul-Nourishing Wood originally had the power to nourish the soul and repel external evil. Lin Chen had carved it with his own heart, infused it with his obsession to protect Daiyu and the energy of 'heart-soul resonance.' At the critical moment of life and death, he unleashed it with all his spirit and inner breath, unleashing unimaginable power. It exerted a strong counterbalance and backlash against martial artists who practiced sinister techniques and harbored murderous intent! 

The purple light lasted less than a breath before quickly fading. The Soul-Nurturing Wood Pendant also cracked its surface, its surface covered in cracks, its spiritual light completely lost, clearly completely ruined. But in less than a breath, Lin Chen gained a crucial moment to catch his breath and severely damaged the second assassin's right palm and spirit. 

The two seventh-rank assassins: one had a half-crippled right arm, his internal energy chaotic; the other had a severe right palm wound, his mind intimidated. As for Lin Chen, though severely injured and lying on the ground, his right arm nearly crippled, his internal energy depleted and his spirit drained, his eyes still shone brilliantly. His left hand barely supported his body, leaning against the wall to sit up, coldly staring at them. 

The scene was momentarily deadlocked. The remaining guards took the opportunity to gather in front of Lin Chen. Although all wounded, their eyes burned with a resolve to fight to the death. 

"Retreat!" The seventh-rank assassin who had been injured first by Lin Chen's thunder fist and had his right arm half-crippled flashed with a trace of unwillingness and apprehension. He made a snap decision and shouted in a low voice. Today's assassination attempt had already failed. Lin Chen was constantly using endless tricks and playing tricky trump cards. If the fight continued, even if he managed to kill him, their side would likely pay an unbearable price. Moreover, with such a commotion happening here, reinforcements from the Jia family and the authorities could arrive at any moment. 

The other assassin also realized there was no way to stop it. He glared fiercely at Lin Chen, as if trying to etch his image into his bones. Then, with a flicker, the two of them blended into the night like ghosts, rising and falling in a few moments, vanishing without a trace, leaving behind a mess and wounded man. 

Only after confirming the two assassins had truly withdrawn did Lin Chen's tense mind suddenly relax, his vision went black, and he could no longer hold on and completely fainted. Before losing consciousness, he seemed to hear hurried footsteps and shouts from afar, as if Jia Zheng had arrived with a large force. 

Inside the Listening Bamboo Pavilion, silence returned, with only the thick stench of blood and burnt filling the cold night sky, lingering for a long time.


Chapter 32 The Jia Family in Turmoil, Reactions from All Sides

The attack on Tingzhu Pavilion resulted in the deaths and injuries of several eighth-rank guards, the annihilation of two seventh-rank assassins, and the death of Lin Chen, who was critically wounded and unconscious. This news, like a spark thrown into boiling oil, instantly ignited chaos within the usually quiet Jia mansion.

Jia Zheng was the first to arrive at the scene. When he led a large group of trusted guards, carrying lanterns and torches, into the ravaged and blood-soaked Tingzhu Pavilion, the sight before him filled him with rage and chilling fear. Several of his carefully selected eighth-rank guards, whom he relied upon heavily, lay dead or wounded on the ground. The courtyard walls were cracked, the ground charred black, and the lingering energy and chilling killing intent testified to the ferocity of the battle. Lin Chen, the nephew on whom he had placed so much hope and who had just rendered great service to the family, now leaned against the broken wall, his face ashen, his breath faint, his right arm twisted at an unnatural angle, his chest stained crimson with blood, and he lay unconscious.

"Quickly! Carry him inside! Summon the imperial physician! No, summon Physician Wang! Go now!" Jia Zheng's voice trembled with shock and anger. He personally stepped forward, carefully checking Lin Chen's breathing and pulse. Though weak and irregular, there was still a glimmer of hope, and he breathed a slight sigh of relief before immediately issuing the order in a stern voice. Physician Wang was the head of the Imperial Medical Academy, highly skilled in medicine, and had some personal connections with Jia Zheng; he was also extremely discreet.

"Block the news! No one is to divulge tonight's events! Anyone who disobeys will be punished according to family law, even to death!" Jia Zheng's gaze was like a knife, sweeping over the stewards and guards who had rushed to the scene, as well as the servants who had been alerted and were peeking around. His tone was icy. "Properly settle the wounded and injured brothers, and double their compensation. Strengthen the defenses of all courtyards in the mansion, especially Rongxi Hall, Qiushuangzhai, and Xiaoxiang Pavilion! Send another elite squad to surround Tingzhu Pavilion; not a single fly is allowed to enter!"

With this series of orders, the entire Jia mansion sprang into action like a startled beast. The servants were silent and hurried about, no one daring to utter a single word. The wounded were carried away for treatment, the dead were collected, and bloodstains were quickly cleaned up. A large number of elite guards were stationed in the key courtyards, especially Tingzhu Pavilion, which was completely surrounded, with guards every three steps and sentries every five, the atmosphere as tense as an iron barrel.

Physician Wang was quickly summoned; Li Gui, Jia Zheng's trusted confidant, personally rode to fetch him. The elderly physician, his hair and beard white, frowned deeply upon seeing Lin Chen's injuries. After a careful examination, he said in a deep voice, "Lord Zheng, your nephew's injuries are extremely severe. His right arm is fractured, his meridians are severely damaged, and a chilling, poisonous internal energy lingers within, constantly eroding his life force. He was struck in the chest; although most of the force was dissipated, his internal organs were shaken, and his meridians were also damaged. Furthermore, he forcibly activated a powerful martial art that was extremely energy-consuming and had a strong backlash, leaving his internal energy nearly depleted and his mental energy severely depleted… If it weren't for his exceptionally solid foundation and far superior vitality compared to ordinary people, he would likely be beyond saving by now."

Jia Zheng was horrified and immediately said, "Old Physician Wang, please, no matter what, you must save him! Whatever medicine he needs, just ask; my Jia family will spare no expense, even if it means going bankrupt!"

"You flatter me, Your Excellency," sighed Physician Wang. "I will do my best. However, this injury... requires a gradual approach. I will first use acupuncture to stabilize his heart and dispel some of the cold palm force. Then I will prescribe a dose of 'Nine-Turn Rejuvenating Soup' for internal use, combined with 'Bone-Regenerating and Muscle-Regenerating Ointment' for external application. As for the damaged meridians and depleted internal energy... they require quiet rest, supplemented with high-quality warming and nourishing elixirs to slowly recuperate. Perhaps... they can recover seventy or eighty percent. However, even if his right arm is reattached, it will likely be difficult to regain its former state, and his martial arts cultivation... will probably be greatly diminished."

Greatly diminished martial arts cultivation? Jia Zheng's heart sank. Lin Chen's greatest value lay in his astonishing martial arts talent and strength. If he were crippled because of this, it would not only be a personal loss for him, but also a major blow to the Jia family's ability to resist Jia She and cope with future changes. But he did not show it on his face, only solemnly saying, "Thank you for your trouble, Elder Wang. As long as his life can be saved, everything else can be discussed later."

Without another word, Physician Wang immediately took out his gold needles and began the treatment. For a moment, the quiet room was so still you could hear a pin drop; only the old physician's steady breathing and the occasional soft tinkling of the needles could be heard.

Rongqing Hall, at Grandmother Jia's residence.

Although Jia Zheng tried his best to suppress the news, how could such a momentous event be kept from Grandmother Shi, who had managed the Jia household for decades? The news quickly reached her ears through her network of spies throughout the mansion.

"What?!" Grandmother Jia, who had been reclining on the couch with Yuanyang massaging her legs, sat bolt upright upon hearing the news. The Buddhist prayer beads she was holding fell to the floor with a "clatter," and her face instantly turned ashen. "Tingzhu Pavilion attacked? Chen-ge'er critically injured? Who...who dared to be so audacious?!"

"Reporting to Grandmother, the specific perpetrators have not yet been identified. However, the Second Master has ordered a news blackout and a thorough investigation. We heard from...the guards who survived that the attackers were two men in black, extremely skilled in martial arts, and ruthless—at least...seventh-rank masters!" Amber reported in a low voice, her own voice trembling.

"Seventh-rank masters?!" Grandmother Jia gasped, a fierce glint in her eyes. "Very well, very well indeed! To attack and kill members of my Jia family in the inner quarters of my mansion, and by two seventh-rank masters no less! This is an act of mortal enmity against my Jia family!" She trembled with rage. "Go! Call Lian'er here! No, go and fetch the Second Master! I want to ask him, is there no law left in this mansion!"

"Grandma, please calm down." Yuanyang quickly stepped forward, patting Grandma Jia's back to soothe her, and whispered, "Second Master is currently handling the aftermath at Tingzhu Pavilion, and he's probably... unable to leave for the time being. This matter... is likely no small thing, involving many serious implications."

Grandma Jia, having weathered many storms, forcibly suppressed her anger upon hearing this. After a moment of respite, her eyes turned icy cold: "Where's Lian'er? Have him investigate! Use every connection you can to find out who's behind this! Also, relay my message: take that three-hundred-year-old ginseng and that box of 'Snow Lotus Jade Toad Pills' from my private treasury and send them to Chen-ge'er! Tell Second Master to spare no effort to save Chen-ge'er! The children of the Jia family are not for outsiders to bully!"

"Yes, Grandma." Amber quickly agreed and hurried away.

Grandmother Jia leaned back on the couch, closed her eyes, and began to twirl her prayer beads again, her knuckles turning white from the effort. A seventh-rank master… in Jinling City, that was already a powerful force. To send two seventh-rank masters to raid the Jia mansion at night, the opponent's power was likely far beyond imagination. Was it She'er? No, She'er didn't have the ability, nor the guts. Was it someone from outside? Who was it? Prince Zhongshun? Or… other adversaries? Chen'er… what terrifying vortex had this child been drawn into?

At this moment, Grandmother Jia's perception of Lin Chen completely changed. He was no longer merely a promising collateral descendant worth cultivating, but a key figure who could influence the future fate of the Jia family and was already deeply mired in danger. She had to protect him, not only to preserve a glimmer of hope for the Jia family, but also to show the outside world that the Jia family had not fallen!

Autumn Breeze Studio.

Tan Chun was checking the accounts she had compiled over the past few days with Shi Shu and Cui Mo when she suddenly heard a commotion outside. A maidservant rushed in, panicked, reporting that something had happened at Tingzhu Pavilion—someone had died, and Young Master Lin was seriously injured.

Tan Chun's account book slammed onto the table with a thud, her face instantly turning deathly pale. "What did you say? Young Master Lin…seriously injured? Is…is it serious?" Her voice trembled, and she barely managed to stand, leaning on the table for support.

"The specifics…this servant doesn't know either. I only heard that some very powerful thieves have arrived. The Second Master has already gone there and deployed many guards, surrounding Tingzhu Pavilion and forbidding anyone from approaching," the maidservant said timidly.

Tan Chun's heart sank, an indescribable panic and anger welling up inside her. She immediately realized that this was no simple intrusion, but a premeditated assassination attempt targeting Lin Chen! Moreover, the fact that the assailant could seriously injure Lin Chen in the heavily guarded inner quarters of the Jia family mansion indicated terrifying strength. Was it someone behind those stewards whose interests had been threatened? Or… her uncle, Jia She? Or perhaps an even more terrifying force?

"Shishu, Cuimo, prepare immediately! I need to see the Second Master, no, I need to go to Tingzhu Pavilion!" Tan Chun tried to remain calm, but her slightly trembling voice betrayed her inner turmoil.

"Miss, you mustn't!" Shishu quickly stopped her. "The Second Master has already ordered the area sealed off. Going now would be inconvenient. And… it's too dangerous. What if the thief has accomplices…"

“Young Master Lin suffered this calamity because he helped me reorganize the internal affairs. How can I sit idly by?” Tan Chun’s eyes were filled with tears, yet her resolve was unwavering. “At least, I need to know how he is now. Go, bring me my token; I’m going to see the Second Master!”

She knew deeply that Lin Chen was no longer just her ally, but her most important pillar and support on this difficult path of reform. If he fell, those demons and monsters that had just been suppressed would surely return, and all her efforts might be in vain. For both public and private reasons, she absolutely could not allow anything to happen to him.

Xiaoxiang Pavilion.

Daiyu had felt uneasy since the afternoon, as if something bad was about to happen. She leaned against the soft couch by the window, intending to read and calm her mind, but her thoughts kept wandering. Lin Chen’s calm face kept involuntarily appearing before her eyes, and the resolute look in his eyes when he held her hand and said, “From now on, I’m here.”

Suddenly, a strange palpitation struck, causing a sharp pain in her chest, as if gripped tightly by an invisible hand. She cried out softly, her face turning deathly pale, and the book slipped from her hands.

“Miss! What’s wrong?” Xueyan, who was doing needlework nearby, was startled by the sound and quickly rushed to support her.

“N-nothing…” Daiyu clutched her chest, panting. The sudden palpitation and pain had come and gone quickly, but the lingering panic and unease chilled her to the bone. She suddenly remembered the Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant Lin Chen had given her (Lin Chen had intended to give it to her, but the assassination attempt had delayed her, and she hadn’t received it yet). Instinctively, she reached for it, but grasped at empty air, and the unease intensified.

Just then, Zijuan, pale-faced, rushed in from outside. Forgetting all etiquette, she whispered urgently, "Miss, something terrible has happened! Tingzhu Pavilion… has been attacked by thieves! Several guards are dead! Young Master Lin… he was severely injured protecting Tingzhu Pavilion, and his life hangs in the balance!"

"Boom!"

It was as if a thunderbolt had exploded in her mind! Daiyu felt dizzy, her vision blurred, and her delicate body swayed, almost fainting. Xueyan and Zijuan quickly supported her on either side.

"You… what did you say?" Daiyu grabbed Zijuan's arm, her fingers icy cold, her voice trembling uncontrollably, "Brother Lin… severely injured? Life hanging in the balance? How could… how could this be…" Tears instantly blurred her vision, and immense fear, like an icy tide, engulfed her. The scene of him spitting blood by the well that day, superimposed with the news of his severe injury and life-or-death situation, caused her heart to ache terribly, making it almost impossible to breathe.

“Miss! Miss, don’t worry! Take care of yourself!” Seeing her like this, Zijuan was both anxious and frightened, repeatedly saying, “Second Master has already rushed over and even summoned the imperial physician. He will definitely save Young Master Lin! You must take care of yourself. If you also get sick from worry, even if Young Master Lin recovers, he will still be heartbroken!”

Daiyu, however, seemed not to hear her words, only staring blankly in the direction of Tingzhu Pavilion, tears streaming down her face like broken beads. The lingering ache in her heart seemed to have found its source now—was it the “soul connection” with Lin Chen, allowing her to sense his pain and danger when he was critically injured?

“I want to see him…” Daiyu struggled to get up, but Xueyan and Zijuan firmly stopped her.

“Miss, you can’t go! Second Master has already ordered a blockade; no one is allowed in! It’s chaotic there now. If you go, not only will you not see Young Master Lin, but you might also cause trouble and give thieves an opportunity!” Zijuan said urgently.

Daiyu slumped down, her body ice-cold, tears streaming down her face. For the first time, she hated her own weakness and powerlessness. Her Lin brother was protecting her from the wind and rain, even fighting bloody battles, while she was trapped in this Xiaoxiang Pavilion, unable to do anything, not even to see him.

“Zijuan, Xueyan…” After a long silence, Daiyu finally stopped crying, her voice hoarse yet carrying an unusual firmness. “Go find my bottle of ‘Nine-Flower Jade Dew Pills,’ and the ‘Hundred-Year Blood Ganoderma’ that Uncle sent the other day. And…and bring me that small sandalwood box at the very bottom of my makeup box.”

“Miss, what is this…” Zijuan asked, puzzled.

“Brother Lin was injured protecting us. I…I can’t go, but these things might be useful to him.” Daiyu wiped away her tears, her eyes gradually clearing, yet carrying an unprecedented resolve. “Find a way to secretly send these things to Tingzhu Pavilion, to…to Master Zheng, or his most trusted confidant. Just say…it’s a small token of our appreciation from Xiaoxiang Pavilion, and it must be used for Brother Lin.”

She couldn't go herself, but she wanted to do her part. The "Nine-Flower Jade Dew Pill" was a holy medicine for healing, and the "Hundred-Year Blood Ganoderma" was an even more potent tonic, capable of replenishing vital energy and prolonging life. As for the small sandalwood box… inside was an ancient jade piece left to her by her mother, said to have calming and soul-stabilizing effects, which she had never shown to anyone. At this moment, she couldn't care less.

"Yes, Miss, we'll go and take care of it right away." Seeing her resolute expression, Zijuan and Xueyan knew they couldn't dissuade her, so they quickly agreed and hurried off to prepare.

Daiyu sat alone by the window, gazing in the direction of Tingzhu Pavilion, her hands clenched tightly, her nails digging deeply into her palms, yet she felt no pain. Only one thought occupied her mind: Brother Lin, you must hold on! You must get better!

East Courtyard, Jia She's residence.

Unlike the fury in Rongqing Hall, the panic in Qiushuangzhai, and the grief in Xiaoxiang Pavilion, the atmosphere in the East Courtyard was strange.

Jia She slumped in a reclining chair in the warm pavilion, toying with two jade balls in his hands, his face grim and uncertain. Madam Xing sat beside him, her brows also furrowed. Only the couple and Jia She's most trusted servant, Xing'er, were in the room.

"Master, there's quite a commotion at Tingzhu Pavilion... The Second Master went there personally, and he mobilized a large force, surrounding Tingzhu Pavilion like an iron barrel. Physician Wang has also been summoned. Judging from the situation, that boy... he's probably seriously injured," Xing'er reported in a low voice.

"Hmph, with two seventh-rank experts involved, how could he possibly escape unscathed?" Jia She sneered, his eyes showing little joy, but rather a hint of apprehension and annoyance. "I just didn't expect that kid to be so resilient. He didn't die? And he even injured one of our men?"

"Yes. I heard... I heard from the guards who survived that he was incredibly strange. His fist techniques could even generate lightning, and their power was immense. One of them who went to kill him... apparently suffered a considerable loss. The other was also injured in the hand by some strange method he used. Both of them were wounded, which is why they retreated." Xing'er's voice was even lower.

"Lightning? A strange method?" Jia She's face darkened further, the jade ball spinning rapidly in his hand. "This lad is indeed strange! It seems his opportunity is even greater than we imagined! If such a person cannot be used by me, I must eliminate him as soon as possible! Otherwise, the consequences will be endless!"

"Master, what about now..." Madam Xing said worriedly, "Second Prince will definitely suspect us, and the Old Madam will probably..."

"So what if he suspects me?" A glint of malice flashed in Jia She's eyes. "Without evidence, what can he do to me? The old lady? She knows everything perfectly well, but for the sake of the Jia family's 'harmony,' as long as things don't come to a complete break, she won't openly support the second son in making a move against me! However… after this incident, the second son will undoubtedly be more vigilant, and the guards around that kid will be tighter. It will be difficult to make a move again."

He paused, a glint of light flashing in his eyes: "Prince Zhongshun is probably getting impatient. The clues about the Xuanzhen Armory… can't be delayed any longer. Xing'er, immediately contact the people the prince sent, as well as the Xue and Shi families, and tell them… the plan has changed, and we need to speed things up. Also, keep a close eye on the Lin family ancestral home in the west courtyard, and that girl Lin Ruhai left behind! I refuse to believe we can't get them to talk!"

"Yes, Master!" Xing'er responded and withdrew.

Jia She slammed the jade ball onto the table, his eyes flashing with murderous intent: "Lin Chen... you'd better be crippled. If you dare block my way again, next time, I'll make sure you're reduced to ashes!"

As night deepened, the lights in the Jia mansion's courtyards shone brighter and more somber than usual. An attack, like a boulder thrown into a deep pool, had completely disrupted the delicate balance within the mansion, ushering in a more intense and dangerous power struggle.

Meanwhile, inside Tingzhu Pavilion, the heavily injured and unconscious Lin Chen was unaware that he had become the center of this storm. The Imperial Physician Wang's acupuncture treatment was nearing its end, and the steaming, fragrant "Nine-Turn Rejuvenating Soup" was being carefully poured into his mouth.


Chapter 33 Daiyu visits the sick, their hearts and souls intertwined.

As night faded and the first light of dawn appeared, the darkness of night receded.

Inside Tingzhu Pavilion, a heavy and oppressive atmosphere remained. The strong smell of medicine mingled with a faint stench of blood, lingering in the morning breeze. Outside the courtyard, guards stood watch every few steps, their armored faces grim, their eyes scanning the surroundings warily. Inside, the door to the quiet room was tightly shut. Only Physician Wang, his two personal apprentices, and Jia Zheng's confidant, Li Gui, stood guard outside. Even the maids carrying water and medicine were only allowed to be escorted to the door, where Li Gui personally received and brought them inside.

Lin Chen remained unconscious, lying on the bed covered with thick, soft bedding. His face was ashen, his breath weak, only the extremely slow rise and fall of his chest proved that he was still stubbornly alive. His right arm had been skillfully reattached and immobilized by Physician Wang, and covered with a thick layer of the pungent-smelling "Bone-Regenerating and Muscle-Regenerating Ointment," completely wrapped up. He had already taken two doses of the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenating Soup," supplemented with acupuncture to dispel some of the cold palm force entrenched in his meridians, but the vicious cold energy, like a maggot clinging to his bones, was difficult to eradicate and continued to relentlessly erode his life force. The turmoil in his internal organs, the depletion of his internal energy, and the immense exhaustion of his mind were like three mountains weighing heavily on his body.

Physician Wang hadn't slept all night and was now sitting on a low stool by the bed, taking Lin Chen's pulse with his eyes closed, his brows furrowed. Jia Zheng hadn't left either, standing by the window with his hands behind his back, gazing at the pale morning light streaming in from outside. His face was ashen, his eyes bloodshot, showing both anger and deep exhaustion and worry.

"Old Wang, how is it?" Seeing Physician Wang slowly withdraw his hand, Jia Zheng immediately turned around and asked in a hoarse voice.

Physician Wang opened his eyes, sighed deeply, and shook his head: "His life has been saved for now. The Nine-Turn Rejuvenating Soup has kept him alive, and my golden needles have temporarily suppressed the spread of the Yin-Cold Palm Force. But this child's injuries are more complex and severe than I anticipated. The Yin-Cold Palm Force is extremely vicious, having invaded deep into his internal organs and bone marrow. It cannot be cured by ordinary medicines. It requires extremely Yang and strong, or rare and precious herbs containing life force, to slowly remove it. Otherwise, even if the external injuries heal, his foundation will be damaged, and his lifespan will be hindered. His internal energy is depleted, and his meridians are severely damaged. Without months or even years of nourishment, it will be difficult to recover. The most troublesome thing is the injury to his mind and spirit... Before he fell into a coma, he seemed to have forcibly activated some kind of secret technique that was extremely mentally taxing, causing his sea of ​​consciousness to tremble and his soul to dim. This is the most dangerous part. If he cannot wake up on his own, or receive elixirs to nourish his soul, I'm afraid... he will remain asleep like this, becoming a living dead."

Jia Zheng's heart sank, his fingers trembling slightly. A living dead? No, absolutely not! He abruptly turned to Physician Wang: "Elder Wang, what kind of rare and precious treasures do you require? No matter how precious or rare, my Jia family will find a way to obtain them! Elixirs, substances to nourish the soul—do you know where to find them?"

"Master Zheng, it's not that I'm making excuses," Physician Wang said with a wry smile, "These treasures, capable of removing the insidious palm strikes of a seventh-rank master and nourishing the soul, are things that are rare and hard to come by. The imperial treasury might have some, but… how could they be easily obtained? In the martial world, perhaps some hidden sects or ancient families possess them, but they are by no means easy to acquire. I know of only one thing that might be able to diagnose the problem."

"What is it? Please tell me, Elder Wang!"

"The 'Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill' left behind by the 'Master Xuanzhen' of the previous dynasty!" Physician Wang said in a deep voice. "This pill has the power to seize the creation of heaven and earth. It is said to be able to bring the dead back to life, heal flesh and bones, and also has the miraculous effects of cleansing the marrow and nourishing the soul. If we could obtain one of these pills, your nephew's injury would surely be cured, and he might even turn misfortune into blessing, further strengthening his foundation. However... this pill was lost long ago with the passing of Master Xuanzhen. We only know its name but never see its trace. I only saw a record of it in a tattered ancient book when I was young."

Master Xuanzhen? The Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill? Jia Zheng's heart skipped a beat. Lin Chen's secret report yesterday mentioned that Prince Zhongshun and Jia She were plotting against the "Xuanzhen Armory," which might contain items like the "Foundation Building Pill." Could the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill" be among them? This thought weighed heavily on his mind. If the pill was indeed in the armory, it was something Prince Zhongshun and Jia She were determined to obtain; for him to snatch it from their jaws was an impossible task.

"Besides this, are there any other methods?" Jia Zheng pressed, unwilling to give up.

"I can prescribe common medicines to nourish the meridians and replenish qi and blood, which will prevent his injuries from worsening and allow for gradual healing. But to cure him completely, especially the injury to his soul... requires a great stroke of luck," Physician Wang shook his head. "Perhaps... calling upon him with familial love and continuously nourishing him with pure and gentle spiritual power might offer a glimmer of hope to stabilize his soul and awaken him on his own. But this method is elusive and its effectiveness is uncertain."

Familial love? Spiritual power? Jia Zheng fell silent. Lin Chen's parents had died early; in this world, who could he call "familial love"? Could he rely on those distant relatives in the West Courtyard? As for spiritual power... that was even more elusive.

Just then, Li Gui's voice, deliberately lowered yet barely concealing his excitement, came from outside the door: "Master, Miss Lin from Xiaoxiang Pavilion has arrived. She's requesting an audience outside the courtyard, saying... she heard that Young Master Lin was injured and is worried, so she came to visit. She also... she brought some medicine."

Daiyu? Jia Zheng was taken aback. Why was she here? And with medicine? Yes, this girl is thoughtful and gets along well with Chen-ge'er. Hearing this, she must be very worried. But her health is already weak, and coming now... Jia Zheng initially wanted to refuse, but remembering what Physician Wang had said about "the call of kinship and love," his heart stirred again. Daiyu and Chen-ge'er seem... to have an extraordinary bond? Perhaps, her arrival might really be of some use?

"Please invite Miss Lin in. She can stay in the outer room; please don't disturb her by entering the inner chamber," Jia Zheng instructed after a moment's thought. He then said to Physician Wang, "Master Wang, what do you think…?"

"It's alright. Letting a close relative talk might be helpful. But she shouldn't stay long, and she shouldn't become too emotional," Physician Wang nodded.

A moment later, the curtain rustled softly, and Daiyu, supported by Zijuan, walked in with unsteady steps. She had clearly also been awake all night; her face was paler than usual, her eyes slightly red, her lips pale, and she wore a plain moon-white jacket and skirt, over which a silver fur cloak made her appear even thinner and weaker. She clutched a small sandalwood box tightly in her hands, her knuckles white from the force.

As soon as she entered, her gaze, filled with barely concealed panic and pain, was fixed on the tightly closed inner door, as if she could see through the door to the person inside, whose fate remained uncertain. Tears instantly filled her eyes, but she forced them back.

"Master Zheng, Physician Wang." Daiyu forced herself to bow, her voice hoarse and thick with nasal congestion. "I heard that Brother Lin... is seriously injured, and I am deeply worried, so I have come to visit. This... this is a small token of my affection. It's the 'Nine-Flower Jade Dew Pill' left by my mother, and a 'Hundred-Year Blood Ganoderma.' Perhaps... perhaps it will be of some use to Brother Lin's injury." She gently presented the sandalwood box to Jia Zheng, her movements gentle yet carrying an undeniable solemnity.

Jia Zheng looked at her pale, fragile yet forcedly strong appearance, and his heart ached slightly. This girl, even in her own condition, still cared about Brother Chen. This sentiment was truly rare. He took the wooden box; it felt slightly heavy in his hand, knowing that it contained precious items, especially the "Nine-Flower Jade Dew Pill" and the "Hundred-Year Blood Ganoderma," which were rare and precious healing medicines even for the Jia family. Daiyu's willingness to offer them showed her heart.

“Yu’er is so kind. Chen’er will be grateful if he knew,” Jia Zheng said gently, handing the wooden box to Li Gui for safekeeping. “Imperial Physician Wang is treating him inside. You…would you like to go in and see him? But he hasn’t woken up yet, you…”

“I…I want to see him.” Daiyu raised her head, her eyes pleading, tears finally streaming down her face. “Even…even just a glance, a few words would be enough.”

Jia Zheng looked at Imperial Physician Wang, who nodded slightly.

"Zijuan, accompany the young lady inside. Be careful not to disturb her," Jia Zheng instructed.

"Yes, Master," Zijuan quickly replied, supporting Daiyu as they gently pushed open the door to the inner room and entered.

The room was dimly lit, and the smell of medicine was even stronger. Daiyu immediately saw the familiar figure lying quietly and lifelessly on the bed. His face was so pale, his lips bloodless, and even in his unconscious state, his brows were slightly furrowed, as if he were enduring immense pain. His right arm was wrapped like a dumpling, and his exposed left hand had several abrasions on the back. He looked so fragile, as if he could break at any moment, a stark contrast to the imposing figure who had stood before her in the Xiaoxiang Pavilion that day, defeating her enemy with a single punch.

“Brother Lin…” Daiyu’s throat tightened, and she uttered a soft call, tears streaming down her face like a broken string of pearls. She broke free from Zijuan’s support, staggering to the bedside, but afraid of hurting him, she only dared to extend her trembling, icy little hand, gently, gently grasping his intact left hand.

It was ice-cold to the touch, lifeless. Only the faint pulse in his palm proved he was still alive.

“Brother Lin, wake up… look at Yu’er…” Daiyu held his hand, pressing it against her cold cheek, tears soaking the back of his hand. Looking at his tightly closed eyes and pale face, her heart ached as if a thousand needles were piercing it. “You said… you would protect me, you said… how could you… how could you lie here and ignore me?”

“Brother Lin, does it hurt? That bad guy hit you, it must have hurt so much, right? Yu'er is useless, Yu'er can't do anything, I can only watch you suffer…” She sobbed softly, her words incoherent, pouring out all the fear, heartache, and helplessness she had suppressed all night. Her usual aloofness, her sharp tongue, had all transformed into the softest, most helpless sorrow.

Zijuan watched from the side, her heart aching, secretly wiping away her tears, not daring to disturb her.

Perhaps it was her tears falling on his hand, or perhaps it was her deeply affectionate calls that touched something. Or perhaps it was the invisible bond of their “mutual souls,” resonating quietly in this moment of such close proximity and such intense emotional turmoil for Daiyu.

Suddenly, Daiyu felt the cold hand she held in her palm, its fingertips trembling very slightly.

She abruptly stopped crying, her tear-filled eyes widening as she stared intently at Lin Chen's face, afraid it was just her imagination.

There was no movement.

Just as disappointment overwhelmed her once more, she clearly felt the hand she was holding twitch slightly. Then, Lin Chen's tightly closed eyelashes began to tremble slightly, his brows furrowing even more deeply, as if struggling in the darkness, trying to break free from something.

"Brother Lin? Brother Lin! Did you hear that? Did you hear Yu'er speaking?" Daiyu was both surprised and delighted. She quickly leaned down and whispered in his ear, using all her strength to call out again, "Brother Lin, it's me, Yu'er. I'm here. Please open your eyes and look at me, okay?"

Meanwhile, in the endless darkness and chaos, Lin Chen's consciousness was sinking into the abyss of nothingness brought about by his severe injuries and immense mental exhaustion. All around him was boundless cold, pain, and deathly silence. He felt like a fallen leaf, constantly sinking in a vortex of darkness, his consciousness growing increasingly blurred, as if he might vanish completely at any moment.

Just then, a faint, cool, and familiar warmth, like a firefly in the darkness, suddenly shone in the depths of his consciousness. The warmth was weak, yet it carried an indescribable intimacy and longing, as if it were the most cherished imprint on his soul. Immediately afterward, a voice, tinged with tears yet incredibly clear, that shook his very soul, pierced through layers of darkness and chaos, like celestial music, resounding directly into the depths of his soul:

"Brother Lin... wake up... look at Yu'er..."

It's Yu'er! It's Daiyu's voice! She's crying? Why is she crying? Is it because of... me?

This thought, like a thunderclap, cleaved through the darkness of despair! A powerful desire to protect her, to stop her from crying, erupted from the deepest recesses of his soul like a volcano! I can't die! I can't just leave her like this! I promised to protect her!

Accompanied by this intense will to live and unwavering protectiveness, the bond of "heart and soul intertwined" was fully activated! A cool, ethereal, yet boundless sorrow and deep affection—the pure spiritual power belonging to Daiyu—flowed like a burst dam, ceaselessly surging into Lin Chen's depleted and trembling sea of ​​consciousness along the invisible threads of this bond!

This spiritual power, unlike ordinary internal energy, acted directly on the soul, carrying all of Daiyu's worries, love, longing, and reluctance. It was like the gentlest spring water, nourishing Lin Chen's parched and cracked sea of ​​consciousness; and like the steadfastest anchor, firmly securing his dissipating soul.

"Buzz..."

Deep within Lin Chen's sea of ​​consciousness, the faint "mark" formed through the "heart-soul connection" suddenly shone brightly! It not only frantically absorbed and merged with the spiritual power surging from Daiyu, but also spontaneously fed back a pure, gentle, and vibrant energy, flowing along the bond to Daiyu, nourishing her spirit depleted by grief and tears.

Internal and external resonance, soul-soul harmony!

In this unprecedentedly deep state of "heart-soul connection," astonishing changes also occurred within Lin Chen's dormant body.

Deep within the dantian, the nearly depleted primordial inner energy, reduced to a mere wisp, began to slowly rotate on its own under the stimulation of this pure vitality emanating from the resonance of the soul, like a frozen spring beginning to seep forth again. This newly generated inner energy was no longer merely the agility of the "Qi Control Technique" or the ferocity of the Vajra Fist intent, but rather carried a trace of the cool, refined, and unyielding qualities brought about by the "connection of heart and soul."

More importantly, the chilling palm force entrenched deep within her meridians, internal organs, and bone marrow, under the cleansing influence of this strange internal energy containing "protection" and "vitality," and the invisible comfort of Daiyu's spiritual power, began to slowly melt and disintegrate, like ice and snow meeting the warm sun! Although the process was extremely slow, it was undoubtedly a miraculous beginning! The "extremely yang and vigorous or vital natural treasures" that Physician Wang spoke of, and Daiyu's "power of heart and soul," into which she poured all her emotions, weren't they precisely the purest and most fitting "vitality"?

Meanwhile, the meridians in her right arm, severely damaged and nearly severed from forcibly activating the "Thunderbolt Fist," began to tingle and itch under the nourishment of this new internal energy. This was a sign that the severed meridians were slowly healing and regenerating under the combined effects of the "Bone-Regenerating and Muscle-Regenerating Ointment" and the new internal energy!
