Beside the bed, Daiyu suddenly felt the hand she held in her palm gradually warming up. Lin Chen's breathing was no longer so faint as to be almost imperceptible, but had become slightly longer and more steady. His tightly closed eyelashes trembled even more violently.

Finally, under Daiyu's tense, expectant, and incredulous gaze, Lin Chen's long eyelashes, like butterfly wings, gently parted slightly.

His eyes, still dim but with a hint of renewed life, slowly opened. They held the bewilderment of someone just waking, and the indescribable complex emotions that welled up when he met Daiyu's tear-filled eyes, which then burst with immense joy.

"Yu...er?" A dry, hoarse, almost inaudible voice escaped from his pale lips.

"Brother Lin! You're awake! You're really awake!" Daiyu could no longer hold back, bursting into tears of joy. Tears streamed down her face like a broken string of pearls, but this time, they were tears of happiness. She gripped his hand tightly, as if afraid that if she let go, he would disappear again. "It's me, it's Yu'er! I'm here, I've always been here!"

Lin Chen looked at her tear-streaked yet radiant face, feeling the cool, trembling yet strong grip on his palm, and the cool, ethereal, and infinitely affectionate spiritual power flowing from their "heart-to-heart connection." The hardest part of his heart melted completely. An indescribable warmth and strength rose from the deepest part of his heart, flowing through his limbs and dispelling the cold and pain.

He tried to force a smile to comfort her, but it aggravated his injuries. He groaned, his brow furrowing again.

"Brother Lin, don't move! Don't speak! Lie still!" Daiyu was startled and quickly let go of his hand, but then didn't know where to put it, feeling helpless. She just looked at him with pleading eyes, her heart filled with heartache.

"I...I'm fine." Lin Chen looked at her flustered appearance and mustered all his strength to squeeze out a few more words, his gaze never leaving her face. It was so good to see her again.

Outside, Jia Zheng and Physician Wang heard the commotion and rushed in. Seeing that Lin Chen had actually opened his eyes, though weak, he had clearly regained consciousness, both of them were both surprised and delighted.

"Brother Chen! You're awake?!" Jia Zheng rushed forward, his eyes filled with excitement.

“Uncle… Zheng.” Lin Chen looked at Jia Zheng, nodded slightly, then looked at Physician Wang, “Thank you… Physician Wang.”

“It’s good that you’re awake, it’s good that you’re awake!” Physician Wang was also greatly relieved. He stepped forward to take Lin Chen’s pulse again, and after a moment, an incredulous look appeared on his face. “How strange! How amazing! Although the young master’s pulse is still weak, the erosion of that cold palm force has been contained, and even… is slowly receding? His internal energy has also begun to regenerate on its own? Although his spirit is still dim, it has stabilized and is no longer in danger of collapsing! This… this is simply a miracle!”

He looked at Daiyu, who was still holding Lin Chen’s hand by the bedside, her eyes only on Lin Chen, and seemed to be deep in thought. Could it really be this girl’s “call of kinship” that had such a miraculous effect?

Jia Zheng was also shaken, his gaze towards Daiyu filled with complex emotions. This girl, her influence on Chen-ge’er is so profound? Could this be the “great opportunity” that Physician Wang spoke of?

"Master Wang, now that Chen-ge'er has awakened, what should be done to care for him?" Jia Zheng asked, suppressing his thoughts.

"Now that the young master has awakened, he has passed the most dangerous hurdle," said Physician Wang, stroking his beard. "Next, he still needs to rest. I will adjust the prescription, focusing on internal medicine to warm and nourish the meridians and replenish qi and blood, and external application to promote bone and muscle regeneration. The young master must remain calm and composed, and must not engage in any more physical activity or exert himself. As for the chilling palm force, since it has shown signs of subsiding, continue to use gentle internal energy to slowly dispel it, supplemented by the young master's own vitality. Given time, he should recover completely. However, the injury to his right arm and meridians will take time to heal, so please do not be impatient."

"Thank you for your concern, Master Wang," Lin Chen said softly.

"Young Master, please take good care of yourself. I'll go out and write a prescription." Physician Wang nodded, then said to Daiyu, "Miss Lin, now that the young master has awakened, you can rest assured. However, he still needs to recuperate, and you are also weak, so you shouldn't stay too long. Don't tire yourself out."

"Yes, thank you, Physician Wang," Daiyu replied softly, her gaze still fixed on Lin Chen's face, unwilling to look away.

Jia Zheng also said, "Yu'er, you should go back and rest. Old Wang and I, along with the servants, will take care of Chen-ge'er here. Don't worry, now that he's awake, he's fine."

Daiyu knew that staying any longer would be pointless and might even disturb Lin Chen's rest. She bit her lip, giving Lin Chen one last deep look, her eyes filled with unspoken words, which ultimately condensed into one sentence: "Brother Lin, please take good care of yourself. Yu'er... will come to see you again tomorrow." With that, she released his hand, the warmth of his touch seemingly still lingering in her palm.

"Mmm." Lin Chen responded softly, watching her leave with Zijuan's support, turning back every few steps.

The room returned to silence. Jia Zheng gave Li Gui a few instructions and then withdrew, telling Lin Chen to rest well.

Lin Chen lay alone on the bed. He slowly closed his eyes, feeling the faint yet tenacious rebirth of his inner energy. Nourished by the cool energy of "Heart-Soul Connection," it flowed gently, dispelling the chill and repairing the damage. In his mind, Daiyu's tearful yet smiling face, and her heart-wrenching cries, remained crystal clear.

"Yu'er..." he murmured, an unprecedented resolve and strength filling his heart.

This time, it was Daiyu who had pulled him back from the brink of death. This kindness, this debt, he would never forget. And those who had hurt him, those who had tried to kill him... Jia She, Prince Zhongshun, and those two seventh-rank assassins...

In the shadows unseen by anyone, a chilling yet burning killing intent flashed in Lin Chen's eyes.

Just wait. The day I recover will be the day you... pay for your blood debts!

He calmed his mind and began to guide the newly generated internal energy, following the methods of the "Heart-Cleansing Technique" and the "Qi-Controlling Technique," to slowly circulate and nourish his body. Although slow, each step was solid and firm.


Chapter 34 The Turmoil in the Jia Family Mansion: Undercurrents Surge

Lin Chen's awakening was like a shot of adrenaline, slightly dispelling the heavy gloom that hung over the Jia family mansion. However, beneath this surface calm lay an even more turbulent and unpredictable undercurrent. The attack on Tingzhu Pavilion was like a boulder thrown into a deep pool; the ripples it created were spreading at an alarming speed to every corner inside and outside the mansion, affecting the sensitive nerves of everyone involved.

Inside Tingzhu Pavilion, security remained tight, but the atmosphere was no longer as deathly silent and despairing as before. Imperial Physician Wang adjusted the prescription, increasing the proportion of herbs that warmed and nourished the vital energy and meridians, and adding several auxiliary herbs to help dispel cold and calm the mind. Jia Zheng even mobilized several aged ginseng and lingzhi mushrooms from the treasury, personally overseeing Lin Chen's diet and medication daily. Nourished by the extraordinary vitality brought by the "Heart-Soul Connection," and aided by top-tier medicine, Lin Chen's recovery speed far exceeded Imperial Physician Wang's expectations. In just a few days, his pale face regained some color, his weak breathing became stable and long, and although his right arm was still immobile and his internal energy was recovering slowly, he was at least out of danger, and his mind was completely clear.

However, each time he circulated his inner energy and observed his surroundings, he sensed the lingering, chilling aura within his meridians—a cold, insidious energy that, though suppressed by the power of his "heart and soul," remained stubbornly persistent—and the thin, stagnant internal energy in his dantian, like a mere pool of water after a storm. Lin Chen's heart hardened with iron. He knew that this injury had severely damaged his foundation; without months, even years, of arduous training, he would struggle to recover, let alone reach a higher realm. And the Prince Zhongshun and Jia She behind those two seventh-rank assassins would never give him that time.

"Strength…still not enough." In the quiet room, Lin Chen leaned against the headboard, his gaze calmly fixed on the swaying bamboo shadows outside the window. In his left hand, he clutched the Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant, now devoid of its former luster and riddled with cracks. It was this very object that had bought him a precious moment in the face of death, and had also severely wounded the second assassin. But it was also utterly destroyed. Daiyu's profound affection and protection had, in this way, shared life and death with him.

He carefully tucked the pendant into his bosom, treasuring it close to his skin. It was more than just an object; it was a testament, a promise.

Flat footsteps sounded outside the window. It was Tan Chun, who had come daily to report on the progress of the "reorganization," but in reality, to check on his injuries. Today, she wore a light green brocade cotton-padded jacket, over which was a lotus-colored vest. Her brows held a mixture of heroic spirit and weariness, but her eyes were brighter and more determined than usual. Clearly, Lin Chen's attack and serious injuries had not deterred her; instead, they had stirred the indomitable tenacity and fighting spirit within her.

"Third Sister is here, please sit." Lin Chen gestured for Shi Shu to bring over an embroidered stool.

“Brother Lin, you look much better today.” Tan Chun sat down by the bedside, carefully examining his complexion. A hint of relief appeared in her eyes, which quickly turned serious. “Physician Wang said that it’s fortunate your condition has stabilized. However, recovery cannot be rushed; you need to rest quietly. Brother, don’t worry about anything in the manor; I’ll take care of things.”

“Thank you for your concern, Third Sister.” Lin Chen nodded. “Has the manor been peaceful these past few days? Have those scoundrels started causing trouble again?”

Tan Chun sneered, a sharp glint in her eyes: "When news of my brother's serious injury spread, some people were secretly delighted for the first two days, thinking they had an opportunity. A few fools even tried to tamper with the purchases and monthly allowances, or slack off. I caught them red-handed, and those who deserved it were beaten, those who deserved it were kicked out, and even their backers were given a warning. Now, at least on the surface, no one dares to provoke them anymore. But..."

She paused, then lowered her voice: "The East Courtyard is unusually quiet. My uncle (Jia She) is claiming illness and hasn't left the house, even skipping his morning and evening greetings. His stewards are all cowering. But I always feel this isn't fear, but rather a sign of something sinister brewing. Also, Aunt Xue came to speak with Mother the other day, and in her words, she seemed quite 'regretful' about my brother's injury, mentioning that my eldest brother (Xue Pan) has recently met several 'heroes of the martial world,' who are quite skilled… I always feel there's more to her words than meets the eye."

Xue Pan? Heroes of the martial world? Lin Chen sneered inwardly. It seemed the Xue family was starting to get restless, and might even have already made contact with the people sent by Prince Zhongshun. The so-called "heroes of the martial world" were probably accomplices of those two seventh-rank assassins, or perhaps a new force.

"Keep a close eye on the Xue family, especially Xue Pan's movements and their dealings with the Shi family," Lin Chen said in a deep voice. "The quieter the East Courtyard is, the more careful we need to be. I'm injured, so they won't dare to act openly in the short term, but they're more likely to sabotage us or do dirty work behind the scenes. Your reorganization of internal affairs has affected the interests of many people. They won't dare to openly oppose you, but they might make trouble for the people around you or the things you handle. Be extremely cautious."

"Don't worry, brother, I understand." Tan Chun nodded. "I've carefully reviewed the duties of Shi Shu, Cui Mo, and several other capable maids and servants, and I've also secretly planted some spies. I'm keeping a close eye on the accounts, the tally cards, and the keys. But..." A hint of worry appeared on her face. "The old lady seems quite furious about your injury, but she also... has some doubts. The other day she summoned me for questioning, and her words implied that she felt you were too outspoken, which led to this trouble. Although I tried my best to explain, the old lady's thoughts... are ultimately unpredictable."

Lin Chen had anticipated Grandmother Jia's attitude. This matriarch, who had managed the Jia family for decades, valued family stability and outward harmony above all else. Lin Chen's rise and subsequent attack undoubtedly disrupted the delicate balance within the household, bringing to the forefront certain conflicts she was unwilling to delve into. She was both angry at the outsider's intrusion and inevitably resentful of Lin Chen for "causing trouble." This conflicting feeling made her stance delicate.

"It's alright. As long as Grandmother Jia doesn't openly obstruct your actions, that's fine. In crucial moments, she ultimately stands on the side of the Jia family's overall interests," Lin Chen said. "You just need to do your job well, manage the inner quarters impeccably, and let Grandmother Jia see your abilities and achievements. She will naturally know how to make her choice."

The two whispered a few more words about the key points of personnel changes and accounting audits within the household, and how to use this incident to further marginalize or eliminate those stewards too deeply involved with Jia She, the Xue family, and the Shi family. Tan Chun's actions were becoming increasingly sophisticated and decisive, and many of her ideas coincided with Lin Chen's, which pleased him greatly.

After seeing Tan Chun off, Lin Chen slowly closed his eyes and began to try to guide his faint internal energy with the *Qingxin Jue* (a method of internal energy cultivation), combined with the continuous nourishment from Daiyu's cool and ethereal spirit transmitted through the "Heart-Soul Connection," to slowly cleanse and dissipate the cold palm force in his meridians. The process was slow and painful, like being cut by a dull knife, but his will was as firm as iron, and he didn't even furrow his brow.

He knew he had to recover as soon as possible, at least to have some ability to protect himself. Because an even greater storm might be coming soon.

Xiaoxiang Pavilion.

Since returning from Tingzhu Pavilion that day, Daiyu seemed to have lost most of her soul. She was listless all day, had no appetite, and often stared blankly out the window or unconsciously stroked the sandalwood beads that Lin Chen had given her, which had a calming effect. Only when Zijuan and Xueyan brought her medicine and mentioned that "Young Master Lin looks better today" and "Physician Wang said he is recovering well" would a faint light flash in her eyes, and she would obediently finish her medicine.

Her body, already weakened by the intense emotional turmoil and mental exhaustion of that day, coupled with sleepless nights, had become even thinner in recent days. Her chin was sharp, making her eyes appear even larger and more vacant, shrouded in a lingering melancholy. Strangely, though her spirit was weary, a profound and indescribable strength seemed to have been infused into her inner being. This was a feedback from Lin Chen, stemming from the complete activation of their "Heart-Soul Connection" during the crisis, a deep resonance with Lin Chen's soul, and a clear understanding of the future and the duty to protect.

She knew that her brother Lin was struggling between life and death, and she couldn't collapse, couldn't let him worry about her anymore. She forced herself to take her medicine on time and try to eat as much as possible, even if it tasted like cardboard. She even began to try, when no one was around, to adjust her breathing and calm her mind using the "Heart-Clearing Mantra" that Lin Chen had briefly mentioned. Although she didn't quite get the hang of it and the results were minimal, her intentions and efforts made Zijuan and Xueyan feel both heartache and relief.

That afternoon, she leaned against the soft couch by the window, holding a copy of Zhuangzi, but hadn't turned a page for a long time, her gaze fixed blankly on the few green bamboo stalks in the courtyard. Suddenly, a maidservant announced, "Miss, Miss Bao and Miss Yun have arrived."

Daiyu snapped out of her reverie and quickly invited them in.

She saw Xue Baochai dressed in a honey-colored cotton-padded jacket, a rose-purple two-tone gold and silver fur-trimmed vest, and a pale yellow silk cotton skirt, dignified and elegant, her face showing just the right amount of concern. Shi Xiangyun wore a bright red felt cloak over a slightly worn autumn-colored embroidered cotton skirt, still as radiant and smiling as ever, though a hint of worry had crept into her eyes.

"Sister Lin, are you all better? We were so worried when we heard you couldn't get out of bed for the past few days!" Xiangyun entered and immediately took Daiyu's hand, looking her up and down. Seeing her pale face and thin frame, her eyes immediately reddened. "How did you get so thin? You must not have been eating or resting properly again!"

Baochai also stepped forward and said gently, "Pin'er, your health is important. I still have some of the best bird's nest soup. I'll have Ying'er send it over later. You can stew it with rock sugar every day; it's very nourishing and replenishes your energy. If there's anything else you want to eat or need, just tell me, or tell Yun'er."

Daiyu felt a warmth in her heart, knowing their concern was genuine. She gently shook her head, "Thank you for your concern. I'm fine; I just caught a chill a few days ago and felt a little unwell. I'm much better now." She didn't want to talk too much about her illness, nor did she want them to worry about her, so she asked instead, "What brings you all here today?"

Baochai sat down opposite Daiyu and sighed, "It's because things haven't been peaceful in the manor lately. We heard that Young Master Lin of the West Courtyard was seriously injured, and we were worried about you. We also feared that you, Daiyu… might be saddened, so we arranged to come and see you. We also have some things we'd like to say to you."

Xiangyun sat down next to Daiyu, angrily saying, "Sister Lin, you don't know how awful the rumors are! Some say it's enemies coming to our door, others say it's internal strife in the manor… and those heartless servants are gossiping behind our backs, saying that Brother Lin… he's been punished by heaven, or he brought this trouble upon himself! It's infuriating! If I find out who's spreading these rumors, I'll tear their mouths apart!"

Daiyu's face paled, her fingers tightening slightly. She knew that Lin Chen's attack would inevitably cause all sorts of speculation and gossip in the manor. Although she hadn't heard the vile rumors herself, she could imagine them. Her heart ached with anger and sorrow, and she felt indignant for Lin Chen.

Baochai glanced at Xiangyun, signaling her to say less, then turned to Daiyu seriously and said, "Pin'er, rumors stop with the wise; there's no need to pay them any mind. However, Lin Gongzi's injury this time is indeed no small matter. Although I live in seclusion, I've heard that the attacker's martial arts are extremely high; he's probably...someone with a background. The Jia family is now a powerful family that attracts attention, and Lin Gongzi is...quite outstanding, inevitably drawing attention and even jealousy. You and he...have always been close, so you need to be even more careful. If it's not necessary, avoid going there as much as possible, and...don't worry too much, lest you harm your health."

Her words were tactful, but the meaning was clear: Lin Chen had gotten into serious trouble, potentially involving many people. Daiyu's closeness with him might be dangerous and could easily attract gossip; she should maintain a proper distance.

Xiangyun disagreed: "I don't like what Sister Bao is saying! Brother Lin is a very good person, and he was injured while protecting our manor! Those bad people wanted to harm him, is that his fault? Should we not go see him and care for him, or should we avoid him? What would that make us? Sister Lin, don't listen to Sister Bao, you still have to go! I'll go see Brother Lin tomorrow!"

Baochai, though rebuked by Xiangyun, remained unperturbed, merely shaking her head slightly. Her gaze towards Daiyu held a subtle, probing intent: "Yun'er, you're straightforward. I'm only doing this for your own good, Daiyu. This household, though seemingly prosperous, is actually... seething with undercurrents. We young ladies, after all, have limited power. Self-preservation is the best long-term strategy. As for Young Master Lin, Master Zheng and the imperial physicians are taking care of him. Daiyu, taking care of yourself is the best thing you can do for everyone."

Daiyu lowered her eyes, her long eyelashes casting faint shadows on her pale cheeks. She knew Baochai's words stemmed from worldly, rational considerations—the "propriety" and "wisdom" expected of a "lady of a prominent family." In the past, she might have felt bewildered and secretly saddened.

But at this moment, feeling the outline of the shattered Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant against her chest, yet still seemingly retaining Lin Chen's warmth, and sensing the cool, inseparable bond deep within her soul—a bond of "heart and soul"—her heart was clearer and more resolute than ever before.

She raised her eyes, her gaze clear and bright, and looked at Baochai. Her voice was soft, yet carried an undeniable calm and strength: "I appreciate Sister Bao's kindness. However, Brother Lin saved my life and, more importantly, has a duty to protect me. He is now seriously injured and bedridden. If I were to distance myself out of fear of gossip or for self-preservation, wouldn't I become an ungrateful and cowardly person? Though I am dull-witted, I know the saying 'a drop of kindness should be repaid with a spring of gratitude,' and I know that 'true friendship is revealed in times of trouble.' I cannot help but worry about Brother Lin's safety; I will not change my sisterly duty because of external storms. As for anything else... I only wish for peace of mind."

Her words were spoken calmly and deliberately, yet each word was clear and resolute. She not only expressed her attitude towards Lin Chen but also subtly responded to Baochai's "advice," and subtly revealed a sharp edge beneath her usual gentleness.

Baochai paused slightly, her gaze towards Daiyu now carrying a genuine surprise and deep thought for the first time. The Daiyu before her seemed somewhat different from the sentimental, aloof, and easily troubled girl of the past. Beneath her pale face, there appeared an indescribable, calm, and resolute strength.

Xiangyun, however, nodded repeatedly, clapping her hands and saying, "Well said! Sister Lin, I support you! This is how we sisters should be! Who cares what others say? As long as we have a clear conscience, that's enough!"

Baochai remained silent for a moment, then sighed softly, saying nothing more, only, "In that case, Daiyu, take good care of yourself. If you encounter any difficulties, or if you want to talk to someone, feel free to come to Hengwu Garden to find me."

After sitting for a while longer and exchanging pleasantries, Baochai and Xiangyun rose to take their leave. After seeing them off, Daiyu stood alone by the window, watching their departing figures, her heart clear and serene.

She knew that what she had said to Baochai today would inevitably reach the ears of some people. This might make her situation in the household even more precarious, and might even trigger new troubles. But she did not regret it.

“Brother Lin, please rest and recover. The storms outside... I'm not afraid.” She murmured to herself, her fingertips lightly brushing the sandalwood beads on her wrist, as if she could feel the faint yet resolute heartbeat of the person far away through those cold beads.

Rongqing Hall, in Grandmother Jia's quarters.

Lady Wang, Lady Xing, Aunt Xue, along with Li Wan, Feng Jie (Feng Jie had been "sick" for many days and was barely able to get up today), and others were all present. Grandmother Jia reclined on the couch, her legs gently massaged by Yuanyang, her eyes closed in meditation, slowly turning the prayer beads in her hands. The atmosphere in the room was somewhat somber.

"...That's how it is. The thief that night was incredibly skilled in martial arts, appearing and disappearing without a trace, leaving very few clues. Second Master has used all his connections to secretly investigate, but so far there's no lead." Madam Wang reported the progress of Jia Zheng's investigation in a low voice, her tone grave. "Chen'er's injuries were extremely serious, but fortunately, thanks to Physician Wang's skillful treatment and the Old Madam's precious medicine, he has only just stabilized, though his recovery will take time. The manor's security has been strengthened several times over, with additional personnel deployed to each courtyard, especially to the young ladies' residences and the storerooms."

Grandmother Jia slowly opened her eyes, her gaze sweeping over everyone before finally landing on Madam Xing: "She'er's daughter-in-law, is She'er feeling any better? Why didn't he come today?"

Madam Xing quickly rose, forcing a smile, "Reporting to Madam, Master... he is still dizzy and has chest tightness. The imperial physician said it was due to the shock and anger attacking his heart, and he also caught a cold. He needs to rest for some time. He specifically sent his daughter-in-law to apologize to you, saying he will come to pay his respects as soon as he feels better."

"Shock and anger attacking his heart?" Grandmother Jia gave a noncommittal snort. "He has every reason to be anxious and angry! Such a shocking thing has happened in our otherwise peaceful household! If word gets out, where will the Jia family's reputation be? How can he, the eldest son of the family, who inherits the title of First-Class General, manage the household?"

Madam Xing's face paled, and she stammered, unable to reply.

Seeing this, Aunt Xue quickly tried to smooth things over, saying, "Grandma, please calm down. No one could have predicted this sudden disaster. Fortunately, our ancestors have blessed us, and Chen-ge'er is a lucky man who is no longer in danger. The most important thing now is to find the murderer and uphold the family law. Furthermore, we must appease the servants and prevent them from spreading rumors and disrupting the rules of the household."

Grandmother Jia glanced at Aunt Xue, her tone softening slightly: "Aunt is right. But this murderer... able to move freely in Jinling City and within our Jia family compound, seriously injuring our family members, is no ordinary thief. Zheng'er can't find anything; I think... the waters run deep." She paused, her gaze sweeping meaningfully over Lady Wang and Feng Jie. "Feng Jie, you're not well, so you shouldn't be troubled. But regarding the household affairs, although Tan Chun is diligent, she's still young, and many things still require your help as her sister-in-law and aunt. Especially the expenses of each household and the interactions of people; you must carefully oversee everything, lest anyone take advantage of any loopholes, or... spread any rumors that shouldn't be spread."

Feng Jie forced herself to be cheerful and replied, "Grandmother, don't worry, your granddaughter-in-law understands. I will definitely help my third sister take good care of the household."

Grandmother Jia nodded, then said to Lady Wang, "Please take good care of Chen'er. Don't be stingy with the medicine and tonics he needs. His parents died early, and now he's suffered this great calamity for the Jia family. We can't let the child's heart be broken. As for outside... those rumors and gossip, suppress them as they should. Our Jia family hasn't reached the point where we can be bullied and talked about like this!"

"Yes, I understand," Lady Wang replied respectfully.

"Alright, I'm tired. You may all leave." Grandmother Jia waved her hand and closed her eyes again.

The others bowed and withdrew. Outside Rongqing Hall, Aunt Xue walked side-by-side with Madam Wang, whispering, "Sister, I think Grandmother's words today had a hidden meaning. She seems quite dissatisfied with the East Courtyard."

Madam Wang sighed and whispered, "Grandmother is so shrewd. She understands some things perfectly well, but she just doesn't want to make a scene. This matter with Chen'er is probably... not so simple. I only hope it doesn't get too far and cause unrest in the household."

Aunt Xue's eyes flickered, lost in thought, but she simply echoed, "Sister is right. We women only ask for peace and tranquility in our homes."

Each lost in their own thoughts, the two parted ways. Inside Rongqing Hall, Grandmother Jia continued to strum her prayer beads, gazing at the deepening twilight outside the window, her brow furrowed, remaining silent for a long time.

"Yuanyang, do you think... is the Jia family truly about to undergo a major upheaval?" After a long silence, Grandmother Jia suddenly asked in a low voice, her tone tinged with an indescribable weariness.

Yuanyang gently massaged her legs, softly saying, "Grandmother is blessed with good fortune and will surely protect the peace and prosperity of the household. Please rest assured and take good care of yourself."

Grandmother Jia said nothing more, but the fingers that were fiddling with the prayer beads tightened slightly, their knuckles turning white.

Night once again enveloped the Rongguo Mansion. The lights in Tingzhu Pavilion shone quietly amidst heavy guards. Under the window of Xiaoxiang Pavilion, Daiyu sat alone by the lamp, her needlework untouched for a long time. In Qiushuangzhai, Tanchun was still reviewing account books under the lamp, her expression resolute. The East Courtyard was deathly silent, except for a single lamp in the study, illuminating Jia She's unpredictable face. And in the Buddhist hall of Rongqing Hall, the ever-burning lamp flickered, illuminating Grandmother Jia's serene face, which seemed to have aged considerably in an instant.


Chapter 35 Zhaoyang Returns, Laying Out the Armory

Under the continuous nourishment of "Heart and Soul Interlocking," the assistance of top-tier medicine, and his own tenacious will to live, Lin Chen's injuries were slowly but steadily recovering at a pace far exceeding Physician Wang's expectations. The fixation on his right arm had been removed; although he still couldn't exert force and his meridians occasionally ached, he could at least perform slight movements. The recovery of his internal energy was the most difficult. That night's battle had almost emptied his dantian, and the forced use of "Thunderclap Fist" had damaged his meridians. His newly generated internal energy was like a thin stream struggling to flow on a cracked riverbed; despite his daily arduous training, progress was minimal. The only consolation was that the chilling palm force entrenched deep within his meridians and internal organs had been largely removed by the combined power of "Heart and Soul Interlocking" and the "Qi Control Technique." The remaining portion, though still stubborn, was no longer fatal; it only required gradual attrition.

Late that night, all was quiet. Lin Chen sat cross-legged on the couch in the quiet room, guiding his meager internal energy according to the "Heart-Cleansing Technique," coordinating it with the cool and tranquil spiritual power of Daiyu transmitted through "Heart-Soul Connection," performing his daily cycle of circulation in an attempt to repair a severely damaged, small meridian. Just as his internal energy reached the vicinity of the "Shanzhong" acupoint, touching that point particularly sluggish due to the lingering force of the cold palm strike, a familiar aura, dignified yet subtly sharp, appeared silently outside the quiet room window, like ripples blending into the night.

There was no knocking, no message; the presence simply lingered, carrying a sense of anticipation.

It was Princess Zhaoyang, Zhao Lingxi. She had come in person again, bypassing all the visible and hidden sentry posts around Tingzhu Pavilion, appearing directly outside his meditation room. Her strength and familiarity with the Jia family's defenses were astonishing.

Lin Chen slowly finished his practice, opened his eyes, a knowing glint in them. The princess's visit must be about the "Xuanzhen Armory." His near-death injuries and miraculous recovery, coupled with his unusually rapid pace of healing, had likely attracted her attention and prompted a deeper assessment.

He rose gently, walked to the window, and silently pushed it open. In the moonlight, Zhao Lingxi, dressed in a light purple outfit and a matching cloak, stood quietly in the shadow of a plum tree outside the window. Her posture was upright, and her beauty was even more striking in the moonlight. However, the aloofness and authority of a superior woman between her brows were heavier than usual. Her gaze towards Lin Chen was scrutinizing, and carried a hint of barely perceptible complexity.

"Your Highness's late arrival is unforgivable; please forgive my lack of hospitality," Lin Chen said with a slight bow, his voice calm. His internal energy was weak, and his face still showed signs of illness, but his eyes remained serene, showing no trace of the dejection or panic of someone recovering from serious injury.

Zhao Lingxi's gaze lingered for a moment on his pale yet serene face, then swept over his right arm, still slinged to his chest, a flicker of surprise crossing her eyes. She nodded slightly, her figure blurring as she vanished into the room like a wisp of smoke, the window closing silently behind her.

"The speed of your recovery, young master, is astonishing," Zhao Lingxi said, stopping in the room and getting straight to the point. "It seems you harbor many secrets. However, my visit today is not to pry into your secrets."

"Please speak, Your Highness," Lin Chen said, walking to the table and gesturing for her to sit down. He then sat opposite her. Only a cup of tea remained on the table, long since cold.

Zhao Lingxi didn't sit, but stood by the window, gazing at the deep night outside, and slowly said, "Prince Zhongshun colluded with Jia She to illegally purchase weapons, plotting treason. His crime deserves death. The Xue and Shi families, as his allies, will also not escape the law. However, bringing down a prince and implicating two powerful families is not something that can be done overnight; it requires overwhelming evidence. They are secretive and cunning; conventional methods will not be able to find them."

She turned to Lin Chen, her gaze intense: "The Xuanzhen Armory is the key. Prince Zhongshun is determined to obtain it, seeing it as crucial for accumulating strength, winning hearts and minds, and even refining 'Foundation Building Pills' to train assassins. Jia She, the Xue family, and the Shi family have also invested heavily in it. If we can find the armory first, we can not only cut off their vital source of wealth and resources, but also obtain irrefutable evidence of their illegal activities, and even… perhaps find clues directly pointing to Prince Zhongshun's treason!"

"Your Highness intends for me to search for the Xuanzhen Martial Armory?" Lin Chen raised an eyebrow.

"That's right." Zhao Lingxi nodded, taking out an object wrapped in special silk from her sleeve. She placed it on the table and slowly unfurled it. Inside was not a jade slip, but a leather map that was clearly ancient, with tattered edges and a peculiar texture. Complex mountainous terrain and strange symbols were outlined on it with dark red ink. In the center of the map were seven star-shaped markings arranged in the shape of the Big Dipper. Four of these were brighter, while the other three were dim and blurry. One of these points faintly corresponded to a star position on the star map that Lin Chen had copied on the ancestral stone tablet in the west courtyard!

“This is the ‘Yang Map’ of the ‘Map of Mountains and Rivers’—a fragment of a geomantic map!” Zhao Lingxi said in a deep voice. “I obtained it with great effort from a secret chamber belonging to a former official of the previous dynasty. Although incomplete, combined with the celestial clues from the ‘Yin Map’ that you obtained from the Lin family ancestral home, we might be able to deduce the approximate location of the entrance to the Xuanzhen Armory!”

Lin Chen’s heart skipped a beat as he looked at the tattered map. A Yang Map! It really was a Yang Map! The Yin Map depicts celestial phenomena and constellations, while the Yang Map shows mountains and rivers; only when the two are combined can it form a complete “Map of Mountains and Rivers” and be used to locate the armory. The princess had actually found the fragment of the Yang Map and had even brought it to share with him! The sincerity of this “cooperation,” or rather, the value she placed on him, far exceeded his previous expectations.

“Your Highness entrusts such an important item to me; aren’t you afraid…” Lin Chen looked at Zhao Lingxi.

"Afraid you'll embezzle it? Or perhaps you lack the ability?" Zhao Lingxi's lips curled slightly, revealing a faint yet sharp smile. "Young Master is a wise man. What benefit would it bring you to embezzle this map? You have no power, no resources. Even if you find the armory, how would you open it? How would you retrieve the treasure? How would you deal with the inevitable pursuit by Prince Zhongshun and other forces? Cooperating with me, at least you can get a share, receive my protection, and even take revenge. As for ability..."

Her gaze fell on Lin Chen's sling-on right arm, her tone turning serious: "Young Master, you were able to repel two seventh-rank assassins despite your severe injuries, and recover to this state in such a short time. This willpower, potential, and... opportunity are my greatest gamble. I believe you can bring me surprises. Moreover, you are still recovering from your injuries and your strength has not yet returned. You need a secluded place to recuperate, and you also need sufficient resources for healing and improvement. This Xuanzhen Armory might be the opportunity for you to rise from the ashes and take another step forward!"

Her words were clear. The princess valued his potential, his character, and the potential "opportunity," using him as a key piece in finding and opening the armory, while also promising corresponding rewards and support. This was a high-stakes gamble with both risks and opportunities.

Lin Chen remained silent for a moment, then gently stroked the leather map. It was cold to the touch, its texture rough, and the dark red lines and symbols seemed to contain some ancient power and information. He looked up at Zhao Lingxi: "What does Your Highness need me to do? When shall we depart?"

"As soon as possible," Zhao Lingxi replied. "Prince Zhongshun, having failed in his assassination attempt and with his young master severely injured but not dead, must be even more anxious and will accelerate their search for the armory. We must get there before them! According to my calculations, combined with the fragments of the Yin-Yang map, the approximate location of the armory's entrance should be near a dangerous place called 'Hidden Dragon Abyss,' deep within Qixia Mountain, about three hundred miles southwest of Jinling City. The mountains there are high and the forests dense, rarely visited by humans, teeming with poisonous miasma and ferocious beasts, and there are even rumors of ancient formations remaining there—it is extremely perilous."

Qixia Mountain? Qianlong Abyss? Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. That was the very place where he had sparred with Princess Zhaoyang and attempted to cultivate the *Qi Control Technique*. He hadn't expected the entrance to the Xuanzhen Martial Armory might be nearby. This might not be a coincidence.

"I will arrange for Shadow Seven, along with two other reliable individuals, to accompany you. Shadow Seven is skilled in concealment, assassination, and trap formations, and can help you scout ahead and avoid danger. The other two are a medical expert who can help you heal your injuries and deal with the poisonous miasma in the mountains; and an expert in vein exploration and mining, with considerable knowledge of ley lines and ancient tomb mechanisms, who may be able to help you locate the entrance." Zhao Lingxi continued, "You will need to disguise yourselves as merchant caravans going into the mountains to gather herbs or explore for minerals, and secretly leave the city in batches to meet up outside Qixia Mountain. I will prepare identity documents, clothing, and necessary elixirs, supplies, and self-defense equipment for you. Remember, the primary objective of this trip is to explore and confirm the location of the entrance; do not attempt to open it lightly unless absolutely necessary. Once the entrance is confirmed, immediately send a secret message to me, and I will personally lead a team to meet you."

The arrangements were meticulous. With Ying Qi, a seventh-rank expert, along with a medical master and a mining expert, this team was strong enough to handle most dangers. The princess's personal assistance also demonstrated the importance she placed on the matter.

"My injuries haven't healed, and my right arm is injured, significantly reducing my strength. I'm afraid I'll be a burden to the team," Lin Chen stated frankly.

"It's alright." Zhao Lingxi seemed prepared, and took out an exquisite white jade bottle from her bosom, handing it to Lin Chen. "Inside are three 'Life-Creating Pills.' These pills are a secret treasure of the Imperial Palace, possessing the power to seize the essence of heaven and earth. They can stimulate the body's potential in a very short time, greatly accelerate wound healing, replenish depleted vital energy, and even slightly purify internal energy, stabilizing the foundation. However, their potency is potent and cannot be overused. Each pill should be taken at least seven days apart, and there will be a period of weakness for several days afterward. You can take them at crucial moments, or to restore some combat power if necessary. Furthermore, these pills are also remarkably effective against the residual poison from the Yin-Cold Palm Force in your meridians."

Life-Creating Pills! Lin Chen's heart trembled again. He had seen scattered records of this pill in some ancient books; it was a true holy medicine for healing, said to have the effect of bringing the dead back to life. It was extremely difficult to refine, and even the royal family had very few in stock. The princess was actually willing to give him three at once! This investment was undeniably significant.

"Thank you, Your Highness." Lin Chen solemnly accepted the jade bottle. With this pill, his recovery speed would be greatly accelerated, and he would have an extra trump card to save his life in critical moments.

"Young Master, there's no need for thanks. Since we're in the same boat, we should help each other." Zhao Lingxi waved her hand, her expression turning solemn again. "This matter is of utmost importance; Young Master, you must be careful. Prince Zhongshun may have noticed our movements, and there might be an ambush within Qixia Mountain. The Xue family, the Shi family, and even Jia She might all send people to secretly obstruct us. It's easy to dodge a spear in the open, but hard to guard against an arrow in the dark. Young Master, you must be vigilant at all times."

"I understand." Lin Chen nodded.

“Furthermore…” Zhao Lingxi paused, a rare hesitation creeping into her voice, but she quickly regained her composure. “That Miss Lin in the manor… seems quite concerned about her, young master. She is frail and troubled, and your trip might cause her worry. Perhaps… I should send someone to secretly keep an eye on her?”

Daiyu! Lin Chen’s heart tightened slightly. The princess had indeed noticed his relationship with Daiyu, and seemed to have investigated quite thoroughly. Her offer to keep an eye on her was both a gesture of goodwill and a subtle reminder—Daiyu could become his weakness, or perhaps a breakthrough point for others to use against him.

"Thank you for your kindness, Your Highness." Lin Chen took a deep breath, suppressing his concern and guilt towards Daiyu, and calmly said, "As for Miss Lin... I will make proper arrangements. Your Highness only needs to focus on the armory matter. As for her safety... I believe that within the Jia family, as long as Your Highness and Master Zheng remain in power, no one will dare to truly harm her."

His words were both a response and a clarification of Daiyu's relationship with Jia Zheng, as well as the current power structure of the Jia family, implying that the princess should not interfere too much to avoid creating unnecessary complications.

Zhao Lingxi gave him a deep look, seemingly seeing through the undercurrents beneath his calm exterior, but she didn't point it out, only nodding slightly: "It's good that you understand, young master. Three days later, at midnight, a carriage carrying medicinal herbs for the Ciyun Nunnery will be waiting at the back gate of the western city. You may take this carriage out of the city; a new identity document and attire will be on board. After leaving the city, someone will meet you and guide you to rendezvous with Ying Qi and the others. The specific route and password are in this brocade pouch."

She then took out an inconspicuous gray brocade pouch and placed it on the table.

"Lin Chen obeys." Lin Chen put away the brocade pouch and jade bottle, rose, and bowed solemnly.

"May your journey, young master, be a resounding success." Zhao Lingxi returned the bow, then said nothing more. With a flicker, she vanished through the window, disappearing into the vast night, as if she had never been there.

The room returned to silence, save for the tattered leather map on the table and the faint, pure plum blossom scent that belonged to the princess, bearing witness to the secret conversation that had just taken place—a conversation that could have influenced the future of the court and individual destinies.

Lin Chen walked to the table, unfurling the tattered scroll of the "Yang Map" once more. His fingers slowly traced the dark red lines, his gaze lingering on the markings of "Qixia Mountain" and "Hidden Dragon Abyss," his eyes deep and thoughtful.

The Xuanzhen Armory… Prince Zhongshun… Princess Zhaoyang… Foundation Establishment Pills… Relics of the Previous Dynasty…

A contest surrounding this astonishing treasure had begun. And he, a collateral descendant of the Jia family, still severely injured and weakened, had, by a twist of fate, become the most crucial and dangerous pawn in this contest.

Will he become a weapon in someone else's hands, ultimately falling to the ground? Or will he seize this opportunity to acquire resources, restore his strength, and perhaps even… turn the tables and rule the world?

Lin Chen gently closed the map, two deep flames burning in his eyes. He carefully stored the map, the brocade pouch, the jade bottle, and the now-ineffective Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant close to his body. Then, he sat cross-legged, took out a "Life-Creating Pill," and swallowed it without hesitation.

The pill entered his stomach, instantly transforming into a gentle yet overwhelmingly warm current that spread rapidly! Wherever it passed, the damaged meridians, like parched land receiving rain, greedily absorbed the medicinal power, experiencing waves of tingling and soothing sensations. The thin internal energy in his dantian, like a pond infused with living water, began to rapidly grow and strengthen. The stinging and stiffness in his right arm also quickly lessened under the medicinal power. Even more miraculously, the remaining chilling palm force, before this medicinal power containing boundless vitality, melted away rapidly like snow under the scorching sun!

A powerful surge of energy, accompanied by a rush of blood and a slight dizziness (due to the overdose of the medicine), returned to Lin Chen's body. Although far from his peak, he was no longer the helpless, severely injured man he once was.

"Three days..." Lin Chen slowly regulated his breathing, guiding the surging medicinal power to repair his battered body. "Enough time for me to make some preparations."

His mind quickly planned his next move. First, he needed to thoroughly familiarize himself with the fragments of the "Yang Map" and the clues to the "Yin Map," attempting to deduce the location of the entrance. Second, he needed to use these three days to recover his strength as much as possible, at least to master the first move of the "Thunderclap Fist" more thoroughly, just in case. Then, he needed to arrange his "aftermath" at the Jia family mansion—how to explain to Jia Zheng and Tan Chun, how to comfort Daiyu, how to create a plausible excuse to "leave the mansion"...

Most importantly, he needed to figure out how to not only complete the princess's mission during his perilous trip to Qixia Mountain, but also to seize the greatest possible benefit for himself!

"The Mysterious Armory..." Lin Chen murmured, a cold yet expectant smile curving his lips.


Chapter 36: Pre-departure Arrangements, Daiyu's Gift

After swallowing the "Life-Creating Pill," Lin Chen immediately entered a deep meditative state. The immense medicinal power contained in the pill surged and washed over his body like a river bursting its banks, frantically repairing his damaged meridians, nourishing his depleted dantian, and, with an overbearing force, accelerating the dissipation and purification of the remaining yin and cold palm force poison, like snow melting under the blazing sun.

He sat there for a full day and night.

When Lin Chen opened his eyes again, the quiet room was filled with morning light. He exhaled a long breath of stale air, carrying a faint chill, feeling completely refreshed. The stagnation, stinging, and weakness within his body had largely dissipated. Although his internal energy hadn't fully recovered, the "shallow water" in his dantian had expanded and deepened several times over, regaining its vibrant vitality. While its flow was still slow, it no longer felt weak or powerless. His right arm was still immobilized, but the excruciating pain of broken bones and severed meridians had vanished, leaving only a slight numbness and weakness characteristic of the healing process. The most noticeable change was in his complexion; it was no longer as deathly pale as paper, but had regained a rosy glow, and his eyes were clearer and brighter.

"The Life-Creating Pill truly lives up to its name," Lin Chen thought to himself, praising it inwardly. The effect of this single pill was almost equivalent to over a month of arduous cultivation, and it had even eradicated eighty to ninety percent of the most troublesome residual Yin-cold poison. Although he was still far from his peak condition, he at least had basic mobility and a certain degree of self-defense ability. The princess's "investment" was indeed generous.

He slowly stood up, stretched his limbs, and felt the changes in his body. His right arm still couldn't exert force, but daily activities were no longer a problem. The circulation of the *Heart-Cleansing Technique* and the *Qi-Controlling Technique* was also much smoother than before. He tried to mobilize a wisp of internal energy, condensing it at the fingertips of his left hand. A wisp of pale golden energy, carrying a faint "Qi-controlling" spirituality, quietly emerged; though small, it was solid and stable.

"Finally... I have a fighting chance." A sharp glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. There were still two days until the agreed-upon departure point at Ciyun Nunnery. In these two days, he had to make thorough preparations, arrange everything inside and outside the Jia family mansion, especially... appease that person.

First, he needed to "ask for leave" from Jia Zheng. He explained that his injuries had stabilized, but the lingering cold poison and hidden damage to his meridians required a secluded place for quiet recuperation using special techniques and medicine to completely eradicate them; otherwise, it might leave lasting problems and delay his martial arts future. To gain Jia Zheng's trust, he even asked Physician Wang to "cooperate," stating that Lin Chen indeed had a strange, lingering poison within him that hadn't been completely eliminated. Staying in the mansion for too long, with its many people and noise, was detrimental to his healing. He suggested that finding a quiet place with auspicious energy might have miraculous effects.

Although Jia Zheng was reluctant and worried about Lin Chen's safety, seeing his rapid recovery and improved complexion, and hearing what Physician Wang said, and considering that Lin Chen's future was crucial to the Jia family's future, he finally agreed. He repeatedly instructed Lin Chen to keep his whereabouts secret, bring sufficient guards, and send regular messages to report his safety. Lin Chen agreed to everything, saying he was going to a secluded mountain villa left by his parents years ago, guarded by reliable old servants, and safe. He also wanted peace and quiet and didn't want to bring many men, only one or two trusted confidants. Jia Zheng couldn't persuade him otherwise, so he selected two of his most alert and loyal guards, both with mid-stage eighth-rank cultivation, and assigned them to Lin Chen. He also secretly gave him a voucher that allowed him to withdraw large sums of money from certain secret banks within the Great Chu Kingdom, just in case.

Next was Tan Chun. Lin Chen didn't completely hide the truth from this shrewd and capable ally. He only said he was ordered by "a certain important person" to go out on an extremely important and dangerous mission. This mission concerned whether he could completely overthrow Jia She and his backers, and also whether he himself could recover quickly and advance further. He instructed Tan Chun to stabilize the inner quarters during his absence and continue to push forward with reforms, but to act more tactfully and cautiously, avoiding being too radical, lest she give others a handle against her. She was especially wary of the Xue family, the Shi family, and the East Courtyard, who might use his "disappearance" or "seclusion" to cause trouble. He left the two guards he had arranged with Jia Zheng with Tan Chun, ostensibly for protection, but in reality to strengthen her power and allow her to maintain closer contact with Jia Zheng through these two men.

Tan Chun, intelligent and perceptive, though unaware of the specifics of Lin Chen's task, sensed its gravity from his solemn tone. She didn't press further, but simply grasped his hand, her eyes filled with tears yet radiating unwavering resolve: "Brother Lin, go forth with peace of mind. I will take care of you in the manor. You… must take care and return safely. Tan Chun… will wait for you."

Having made these arrangements, it was already the eve of his departure. The final and most difficult hurdle—Daiyu.

Lin Chen didn't send anyone to invite her; instead, he went to Xiaoxiang Pavilion himself. He knew that this meeting was perhaps more important than any explanation or arrangement.

Inside Xiaoxiang Pavilion, the lights were dim. Daiyu sat on a soft couch by the window, holding a book in her hand, but she hadn't turned a page for a long time, her gaze fixed blankly on the deep night outside. Since returning from visiting the sick that day, she had remained like this, terribly thinner, the subtle sorrow between her brows seemingly etched into her very bones. Zijuan and Xueyan, who were waiting outside the door, were both surprised and delighted to see Lin Chen, and quickly lifted the curtain.

"Brother Lin?" Hearing footsteps, Daiyu turned her head and saw Lin Chen. Her eyes brightened briefly, then quickly dimmed again, filled with deep worry. She struggled to get up, "Why are you here? You're not fully recovered yet, you shouldn't be moving around..."

"Lie down and don't move." Lin Chen quickly stepped forward, gently pressing her shoulder, and sat down on the embroidered stool beside her bed. By the lamplight, he carefully looked at her face. In just a few days, she seemed to have lost even more weight; her chin was so sharp it was heartbreaking. Her already large eyes appeared even more vacant against her thin cheeks, and there were faint dark circles under her eyes, clearly indicating she hadn't slept.

“I’m fine, look, much better.” Lin Chen softened his voice, even raising his uninjured left hand and gently clenching it into a fist to show her.

Daiyu’s gaze fell on his right arm, still slinged across his chest. Her eyes instantly filled with tears again, but she forced them back, nodding slightly and whispering, “Yes, your complexion is better… but Physician Wang said your internal injuries haven’t healed and you need long-term recuperation…”

“Yu’er,” Lin Chen interrupted her, his tone becoming serious, “I came tonight to tell you something.”

Daiyu’s heart tightened. She looked up at him, a hint of unease flashing in her eyes.

“My injuries require me to go away and recuperate for a while,” Lin Chen said slowly, looking at her honestly. “I need to go to a secluded place, with special techniques and medicine, to completely eradicate the hidden dangers. Otherwise, it might affect my future martial arts foundation.”

“Go away? Where to? For how long?” Daiyu asked anxiously, her voice trembling.

“I’m going to… a secluded mountain retreat left by my parents years ago. It’s far away and very hidden. The time… maybe several months, maybe longer, I can’t say.” Lin Chen didn’t mention Qixia Mountain, nor did he give a definite return date. He didn’t want to give her unrealistic hopes, and he was afraid she would worry too much. “But I will come back as soon as possible, I promise.”

Daiyu’s tears finally broke free and slid down her pale cheeks. She bit her lower lip tightly, trying not to cry out, but just stared at Lin Chen with her tear-filled eyes, as if trying to imprint his image deeply into her heart.

“Why… do we have to go so far? Can’t we stay in the manor? I can go with you every day, I can…” She choked, her words incoherent.

"Yu'er, listen to me." Lin Chen reached out his left hand and gently wiped away the tears on her face, his movements tender, but his eyes were incredibly firm. "This injury has taught me a lot. In this world, without sufficient strength, you can't even protect yourself, let alone... protect those you want to protect. I need to become stronger so that I can truly protect you for the rest of my life. This trip isn't just for healing, but also to find an opportunity... that will allow me to recover faster, and even become stronger. I can't miss this opportunity."

He paused, looking at the fear, reluctance, and deep longing surging in her eyes, his heart ached, but his tone became even more steady: "I promise you, I will come back safe and sound. Before I return, you must promise me you will take good care of yourself, take your medicine on time, eat well, and not worry too much. Only when you are well can I be at ease abroad, without any worries. Okay?"

Daiyu's tears streamed down her face, but she did not try to stop him. She understood Lin Chen, knowing that once he made a decision, he would never change it. She also sensed the unspoken meaning in his words—this trip was probably not just about healing, but perhaps also involved unspeakable dangers. But since he didn't say, she didn't ask. She simply reached out her cold, trembling hand and tightly grasped Lin Chen's left hand, as if using all her strength.

"Brother Lin... I promise you, I'll be alright, I'll wait for you to come back." She choked back tears, each word a struggle, her tears flowing even more fiercely. "But you must also promise me, you must be careful, you must... come back safe and sound. No matter what happens, remember, there's someone waiting for you in Xiaoxiang Pavilion. If... if anything happens to you, Yu'er... Yu'er will never live alone!"

This vow weighed heavily, carrying a poignant and resolute weight. Lin Chen's heart trembled. He grasped her hand tightly in his own, solemnly saying, "I promise you. For you, I will definitely come back."

Their eyes met, blurry with tears, yet a silent vow and bond seemed to converge and solidify in the air. That "mutual soul connection" resonated quietly at that moment, and a cool, ethereal spiritual power emanated from Daiyu, carrying endless reluctance, worry, and deep love, flowing into Lin Chen's mind and infusing his weary spirit with a warm and unwavering strength. Simultaneously, Lin Chen also reciprocated with a surge of pure, vital energy derived from the "Life-Creating Pill," quietly nourishing Daiyu's spirit, weakened by grief.

After a long while, Daiyu's emotions calmed down slightly. Suddenly remembering something, she struggled to sit up, walked to the bedside, and took a small object wrapped in a plain white silk handkerchief from under her pillow. Clutching it tightly in her palm, she returned to Lin Chen.

"Brother Lin, this... is for you." She gently placed the object in Lin Chen's palm. The handkerchief unfolded, revealing a jade pendant, its surface warm and smooth, its color like mutton fat, carved into the shape of a budding magnolia flower. The pendant was exquisitely carved, the jade of superior quality, and, most remarkably, a faint, cool, and tranquil aura flowed within it, clearly indicating it was no ordinary piece.

"This was left to me by my mother... It's said to be part of my maternal grandmother's dowry, an ancient jade from the previous dynasty's palace, said to have calming and protective properties. I... I've always worn it close to my body." Daiyu lowered her head, her cheeks slightly flushed, her voice barely audible. "Your journey will be perilous; wearing it might... might protect you. You... please don't find it offensive."

Lin Chen looked at the jade pendant in his palm, still warm from Daiyu's touch and carrying her faint fragrance, and his heart was deeply touched. This jade pendant held extraordinary significance for her; it wasn't just a mother's heirloom, but a personal item imbued with deep affection. She had actually given it to him...

He held the jade pendant tightly in his hand, feeling its warm, smooth texture and the tranquil power flowing within it—a power shared with the cool, aloof aura emanating from Daiyu. He solemnly said, "Yu'er, I accept this token of my affection. I will always wear this pendant close to my body; seeing it is like seeing you. It will surely protect me and bring me back safely."

Daiyu, seeing him accept it, felt a surge of relief mixed with a touch of shyness in her eyes, and softly murmured an "Mmm."

"And..." Lin Chen suddenly remembered something, taking from his robes the now-damaged, cracked soul-nourishing wood pendant, and handed it to Daiyu, his voice tinged with guilt, "This... I originally intended to give it to you. That night, during the attack, I was fortunate enough to survive thanks to it. But... it's ruined. When I return, I will find a better piece of wood and carve a new one for you."

Daiyu gazed at the once warm and lustrous wooden pendant, now cracked and dull, and felt a pang of pain in her heart. She could imagine how Lin Chen, in that life-or-death moment, had risked everything with this pendant he had intended to give her. She reached out her trembling hand and took the broken pendant, gently stroking the cracks, as if she could feel the harrowing moment and Lin Chen's resolute determination.

"No, this… is good enough." She looked up, a strange light shining in her tear-filled eyes, clutching the broken pendant tightly in her own palm. "This is what Brother Lin protected with his life; it's better than any new one. I'll keep it safe and wait for you to return."

The two sat quietly for a while longer, exchanging trivial words, but every word seemed imbued with a deep reluctance to part. Night deepened, and Lin Chen knew he couldn't stay any longer.

“Yu’er, I must go.” He stood up, giving her one last, deep look. “Remember my words, take good care of yourself. Wait for me.”

“Mm.” Daiyu also stood up, seeing him to the door. Just as the curtain was about to fall, she suddenly tiptoed and swiftly, gently, placed a cool, trembling kiss on Lin Chen’s cheek. Then, like a startled deer, she quickly took a step back, her face flushed, lowering her head, not daring to look at him, only saying in a voice as soft as a whisper, “Brother Lin… take care.”

That fleeting touch, carrying the unique sweetness and shyness of a young girl, was like a weak current, instantly spreading throughout Lin Chen’s body. He was stunned for a moment, then a huge warm current and an indescribable flutter surged in his heart. He took a deep breath, firmly engraving this flutter and longing in his heart.

"Wait for me," he said one last time, then resolutely turned and strode away without looking back. He feared that if he turned back and saw her tear-filled eyes and her forced strength, he would be unable to take another step.

Stepping out of the Xiaoxiang Pavilion, the night wind was biting, dispelling the tenderness and romance in his heart. Lin Chen's eyes regained their calm and sharpness, like a drawn sword. He looked up at the dark night sky, the twinkling stars seemingly guiding his way.

In his arms, the warm magnolia jade pendant and the broken Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant clung tightly together, one warm, one cool, seemingly symbolizing protection and destruction, also bearing two heavy burdens of affection and longing.

"It's time to set off." Lin Chen murmured to himself, his figure unfurling as he silently disappeared towards Tingzhu Pavilion. At midnight tomorrow, at the back gate of Ciyun Nunnery, an adventure concerning life and death, future prospects, and a colossal treasure was about to begin.

Meanwhile, at the window of Xiaoxiang Pavilion, Daiyu stood quietly, watching the direction he had gone, until his figure completely merged into the night and could no longer be seen. She tightly clutched the broken Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant in her hand, pressing it against her chest, as if she could feel the lingering warmth and presence of Lin Chen within it.

"Brother Lin, you must... come back safely." She prayed silently into the boundless night. Tears streamed down her face again, but this time, her eyes held not only sorrow but also an unprecedented determination and the courage to wait.

Inside Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen did a final check of his belongings. Several sets of clothes, enough gold and silver for several months, the family head's certificate, the jade bottle and brocade pouch containing the "Life-Creating Pill" bestowed by the princess, the oilcloth bag containing fragments of the "Thunderclap Fist" manual, the half-piece of the "Double Blessings of Fortune and Longevity" token, and most importantly—the fragments of the "Yang Diagram" and the jade slip containing rubbings of the "Yin Diagram." He carefully concealed these vital items close to his body. He also strung the magnolia-shaped jade pendant given to him by Daiyu with a sturdy red cord and wore it close to his neck, against his heart. The broken Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant was wrapped in another clean silk handkerchief and also tucked into his robes.

Having done all this, he sat cross-legged and began his final meditation, adjusting his state to its peak. In his mind, the terrain of Qixia Mountain, the rumors of Qianlong Abyss, the information from the fragments of the Yin-Yang Diagram, the potential dangers and opponents… all flashed through his mind, which he then calmly analyzed and deduced.

As the midnight drumbeats echoed from the city, Lin Chen slowly opened his eyes. His eyes were clear and bright, devoid of any hesitation or doubt.

He changed into an inconspicuous dark gray robe, concealing his presence to the extreme, like an ordinary night traveler. He took one last look at the quiet room that held countless memories, struggles, efforts, and warmth from his transmigration, then gently pushed open the back window.


Chapter 37 First Encounter with Danger in Qixia Mountain

At 1:45 AM, at the back gate of Ciyun Nunnery in the western part of the city.

The night was deep and all was silent. This place was on the edge of Jinling City, near the city wall. The surrounding area consisted mostly of low-rise houses, abandoned vegetable gardens, and a few dilapidated temples. Ciyun Nunnery was small and not very popular. At this moment, its back gate was tightly shut, with only a single, flickering lamp hanging under the eaves, swaying gently in the night breeze, casting a dim, yellowish glow that added to the desolation.

A slightly worn, blue-awning carriage sat quietly in the shadows at the corner of the alley. A hunched-over coachman, wearing a straw hat, sat atop the shaft, seemingly having waited for a long time, dozing with his whip in hand. The carriage was pulled by an ordinary chestnut horse, its head drooping, occasionally snorting.

Lin Chen appeared silently at the alley entrance, his gaze sweeping over the carriage and the coachman. The coachman appeared ordinary, but his breathing was long and steady, and although his body was hunched, his posture hinted at a readiness to strike. Clearly, he was no ordinary coachman; he must have been sent by the princess. He concealed his presence and walked forward with unhurried steps.

Hearing footsteps, the "driver" looked up, revealing a weathered and wrinkled ordinary face under his straw hat, but his eyes were sharp and bright. He quickly glanced at Lin Chen, especially lingering on his right arm in a sling for a moment, before lowering his eyes and whispering, "Are you Master Zhang who gathers herbs? The nunnery master asked me to take you to 'Huichuntang' to get your medicine."

The code was correct. Lin Chen nodded, "Exactly. Thank you."

The "driver" said no more, jumped off the carriage shaft, and lifted the curtain. Lin Chen climbed into the carriage. The interior was quite spacious, covered with clean straw mats. In one corner were several bulging burlap sacks covered with oilcloth, emitting a faint herbal scent, clearly used for camouflage. Apart from that, there was nothing else.

The "driver" lowered the curtain, sat back on the shaft, and gently flicked the reins. The carriage began to move slowly, the wheels making a "rolling" sound on the cobblestone road that carried far in the quiet night.

The carriage did not head towards the city gate, but instead followed a secluded path along the base of the city wall, circling around for a short distance before finally arriving near an old, abandoned water gate that was partially collapsed. The city wall here was low, overgrown with weeds, and even beggars avoided it. Although the water gate was closed, the city wall, dilapidated from years of neglect, had several breaches, barely wide enough for one person to pass through.

The coachman stopped the carriage and whispered, "Master Zhang, go out from here and walk three miles downstream along the moat. There's a willow grove by the river, and a small boat with a black awning in it. Someone on board will take you to meet your companions. This carriage and the 'medicinal herbs' inside will be taken care of later. Take care."

"Thank you." Lin Chen nodded, lifted the carriage curtain, and in a flash, he leaped out through the breach in the city wall, landing as lightly as a cat without making a sound. He glanced back; the coachman had already driven the carriage away silently in the direction he had come from.

The air outside the city, carrying the scent of earth and river water, was even colder. Lin Chen calibrated his bearings and hurried downstream along the overgrown riverbank. He continued to conceal his presence, but his pace was quick, his figure weaving swiftly through the night. The sling on his right arm caused slight discomfort, but after the nourishment of the Life-Creating Pill and a night of rest, the pain had greatly lessened and barely hindered his journey.

After walking about three miles, he indeed saw a dense willow grove by the river. The night wind blew, and the willow branches swayed, rustling softly. Lin Chen focused his senses and quickly spotted an inconspicuous, black-awning boat moored in a secluded bend near the water's edge. There were no lights on the boat; it was quiet, as if no one was there.

He took a few steps closer, lowered his voice, and following the coded signal in the brocade pouch, gently clapped his hands three times, paused for a moment, and then clapped twice more.

The cabin curtain fluttered slightly, and a lean, dark figure flashed out like a ghost, landing on the bow without making a sound. The newcomer was also dressed in black, his face veiled with a black cloth, revealing only a pair of gleaming eyes. Slightly shorter than Shadow Seven, his aura was equally deep and powerful—clearly a seventh-rank early-stage expert! His gaze swept over Lin Chen from head to toe, lingering for a moment on the Xuanlong Token hanging on his chest before he nodded slightly and lowered his voice, saying, "Are you Young Master Lin? I am Shadow Nine, here to meet you on the orders of Her Highness the Princess and Lord Shadow Seven. Please aboard."

"Thank you." Lin Chen nodded, leaping lightly onto the bow. His body swayed slightly, but he quickly regained his balance. His internal energy had recovered to less than 30%, his right arm was injured, and his movement techniques were hampered.

Ying Jiu seemed to sense his poor condition, a flicker of surprise crossing her eyes, but she didn't ask further, simply saying, "Young Master, please come into the cabin to rest. We'll set off immediately, sailing downstream, and should reach the first rendezvous point before dawn."

Lin Chen lifted the curtain and entered the cabin. The cabin was cramped, barely enough for three or four people to sit facing each other. It was also covered with straw mats, and a water pouch and a bag of dry rations were placed in one corner. He sat down on the mat, and Ying Jiu followed, sitting opposite him. She didn't say much, simply closing her eyes to rest. The boat lurched slightly, having already left the shore, sailing downstream at a considerable speed, yet with remarkable stability, clearly the work of a skilled boatman.

The cabin was silent, save for the gurgling sound of water flowing beneath the hull. Lin Chen also closed his eyes and began circulating the *Cleansing Heart Technique*, regulating his breathing to recover while recalling the route and rendezvous information recorded in the brocade pouch.

According to the plan, they will travel downstream by boat for about eighty li (approximately 40 kilometers) and arrive at a remote dock called "Blackwater Ferry" before dawn. Ying Qi, the medical expert, and the mining expert will disguise themselves as merchants buying mountain goods and wait there. After meeting up, they will abandon the boat and go ashore, taking a land route to blend into caravans or mountain dwellers heading to villages and towns around Qixia Mountain. They will then seize the opportunity to infiltrate the deep mountains and search for the location of "Hidden Dragon Abyss."

The journey was smooth and uneventful, without any checks or unexpected incidents. It seemed that the Ciyun Temple route, and this unassuming awning boat, were secret passages established by the princess over many years, safe and reliable. As dawn approached, the boat quietly approached a desolate riverbank shrouded in mist. The bank was overgrown with reeds, with no dock, only a muddy path worn into the ground leading to the woods on the shore.

"Young Master, we've arrived. From here, go ashore, through that grove of trees ahead, about two li, and you'll reach Blackwater Ferry. Lord Ying Qi and the others should be waiting at the teahouse near the ferry," Ying Jiu whispered, leaping ashore first and cautiously surveying his surroundings.

Lin Chen followed closely behind. The two figures spread out, quickly disappearing into the misty forest. Although Lin Chen was not in good condition, his foundation in the Lightness Step was still intact, allowing him to barely keep up with Ying Jiu's speed. Ying Jiu seemed to have deliberately slowed his pace, accommodating his injuries.

The two-mile journey was quickly completed. Passing through the forest, the view suddenly opened up, revealing a murky river that had bended, forming a small river bend ferry crossing. Beside the ferry crossing were several crooked thatched huts, one of which had a worn-out "tea" banner hanging above its door—the only teahouse in Blackwater Ferry. It was still early, and the ferry crossing was deserted. Only a few early-rising boatmen were squatting by the river washing their boats. The teahouse was also empty, except for three people sitting at a table in the corner.

In the center sat a tall, thin man, dressed in a worn indigo robe and a square headscarf, resembling a middle-aged scholar. His face was ordinary, but his eyes held a glint of shrewdness—this was Ying Qi. To his left sat an elderly man with graying hair and a gaunt face, carrying a large rattan trunk. He held a bowl of steaming coarse tea, sipping it slowly with a calm expression; this was likely the skilled physician. To his right was a dark-skinned, stocky middle-aged man with a weathered face, wearing a coarse cloth jacket and straw sandals. Before him lay an open bundle containing oddly shaped stones, a compass, and other items. He was carefully examining one of the stones—this was the expert in vein finding and mineral exploration.

Seeing Lin Chen and Ying Jiu enter the teahouse, a glint flashed in Ying Qi's eyes, but he quickly regained his composure. He put down his teacup, rose, and gave a slight bow, his voice calm: "Is this Manager Zhang from Jinling City? My surname is Qi, and I've been waiting here for quite some time.
