These two are Doctor Chen and Master Shi, whom we've invited to help on this trip."

"Mr. Qi, I apologize for keeping you waiting. We were delayed on the road." Lin Chen returned the bow and walked over to sit in an empty seat. Ying Jiu remained silent, finding a place to sit near the entrance, his eyes warily scanning the area outside.

"It's alright, it's good that you arrived safely." Ying Qi gestured for the teahouse owner to bring a few more bowls of tea, then said in a low voice, "This place is not safe to linger. We are ready. Please have some tea and snacks, young master, and we will depart immediately. Today we need to travel sixty li to enter 'Wild Man Gully' on the outskirts of Qixia Mountain, where we have a secret hideout."

Lin Chen nodded, picked up the rough porcelain teacup, and slowly sipped the scalding tea. The tea was bitter, but it dispelled the chill of the morning mist. His gaze swept over Doctor Chen and Master Shi. Doctor Chen remained calm, but as Lin Chen sat down, his gaze lingered for a moment on Lin Chen's face and his right arm in a sling, his brow furrowing almost imperceptibly. Master Shi, on the other hand, continued to stare intently at his stones, oblivious to his surroundings, as if his eyes were only on the minerals.

A moment later, the group rose and left the teahouse. Ying Qi had already hired a worn-out mule cart, piled high with sacks and bamboo baskets, disguised as a transport of mountain goods. Doctor Chen's rattan trunk and Master Shi's bundle were also placed on it. Ying Jiu remained seated on the cart's shaft, driving. Lin Chen, Ying Qi, Doctor Chen, and Master Shi squeezed into the carriage.

The mule cart creaked and groaned as it set off along the rugged dirt road, heading west towards the rolling mountains. Qixia Mountain was now in sight; its verdant peaks, shrouded in mist, appeared mysterious and profound in the morning light.

The cart was jolted, and the atmosphere was silent. Ying Qi closed her eyes to rest. Doctor Chen took a small, delicate copper hand warmer from a wicker box, lit the charcoal inside, and placed it on his lap to warm his hands, seemingly feeling the chill. Master Shi, meanwhile, kept fiddling with his compass and several pieces of ore, muttering to himself, seemingly calculating something.

Lin Chen closed his eyes and continued to regulate his breathing. The injury to his right arm was healing rapidly under the influence of the Life-Creating Pill, but the recovery of his internal energy was not something that could be achieved overnight. He tried to guide his faint internal energy, slowly circulating it along the path of the *Qi Control Technique*, while simultaneously diverting a sliver of his mind to comprehend the profound information contained within the fragment of the "Yang Diagram" and the rubbing of the "Yin Diagram" in his possession, attempting to sketch the possible location of the "Hidden Dragon Abyss" in his mind.

Suddenly, Doctor Chen, who had been silent all along, spoke, his voice aged yet steady: "This...Young Master Zhang, judging from your complexion, although your external injuries are stable, your internal energy is weak and unstable, there is a hidden blockage in your middle jiao, and your lung meridians also carry a metallic aura. This is likely due to forcibly activating some kind of domineering martial art that consumes a great deal of vital energy and also damages your lung meridians. In addition, there is a trace of cold and vicious energy remaining in your body. Although it has been suppressed by spiritual medicine, if it is not removed in time, it may damage your heart meridians and kidneys. Young Master, your right arm bones are healing well, but your meridians are severely damaged. You need to nourish them slowly with gentle internal energy. You cannot rush things, otherwise your meridians will be fragile and easily leave hidden injuries, affecting your future strength."

His words were professional and direct, clearly an examination of Lin Chen's injuries. Lin Chen opened his eyes, looking at the unassuming old doctor with a hint of surprise. To be able to diagnose his injuries so accurately simply by observing his complexion, this Doctor Chen's medical skills were probably even superior to Imperial Physician Wang's.

"Doctor Chen's insight is remarkable; what he says is correct," Lin Chen nodded. "Doctor Chen, do you have any effective remedies?"

"I wouldn't call it a miracle cure." Doctor Chen took a small, blue porcelain bottle from his wicker trunk and handed it to Lin Chen. "This is my own concoction, the 'Hundred Herbs Rejuvenating Pill.' It's mild in nature, primarily for strengthening the body's foundation and nourishing the meridians. It will be quite beneficial to your current condition, young master. Take one pill each morning and evening with warm water. Combined with your own breathing exercises, it should accelerate the recovery of your internal energy and solidify your foundation. As for the lingering Yin-cold poison… we need to find a place with a mild climate or a Yang-attribute spiritual spring, and treat it gradually with acupuncture and specific pills. It won't be a quick fix. Once we reach our destination, I will give you a more detailed examination."

"Thank you, Doctor Chen." Lin Chen took the bottle. It was slightly warm to the touch, and the medicinal fragrance was subtle, clearly indicating it was of good quality. The medical expert the princess had arranged was indeed living up to his reputation.

"It's my duty." Doctor Chen waved his hand, said no more, picked up his hand warmer again, and closed his eyes to rest.

Master Shi, who had been fiddling with the compass, suddenly looked up at the approaching mountains outside the car window. A strange light flashed in his eyes as he murmured, "The earth's energy has changed... In the mountains ahead, there's a faint, chilling aura of metal and water... and a trace... an extremely faint 'spiritual movement' unlike any other in the earth's veins? Strange, very strange..."

Ying Qi, hearing this, also opened his eyes and looked at Master Shi: "Master Shi, can you determine the approximate location? Is it related to our target?"

Master Shi concentrated for a moment, then looked down at the compass in his hand. The needle trembled slightly, pointing southwest. He pondered, "The general direction is southwest, but we'll need to get closer to confirm. But that 'spiritual' aura is elusive, as if veiled by something, extremely difficult to detect. Moreover, this chilling aura of Geng metal and the cold, damp air of the marshland doesn't seem to be naturally formed; it's more like... traces left behind by some kind of formation or human intervention? This Qixia Mountain is indeed extraordinary."

Formation? Human intervention? Ying Qi and Lin Chen exchanged a glance, both seeing the gravity in each other's eyes. It seemed that the location of the Xuanzhen Armory was more complex and dangerous than anticipated.

The mule cart continued on, the road becoming increasingly rugged, the forests on both sides growing denser and deeper. The sun rose higher, but the light in the forest dimmed, the air filled with the damp scent of decaying vegetation and earth, occasionally punctuated by the cries of unknown birds and beasts, adding to the desolate atmosphere.

Around noon, the mule cart stopped at the entrance of a cave halfway up the mountain, almost completely covered by vines. Ying Qi gestured for everyone to get out.

"This is one of the temporary shelters for 'Wild Man Gully,' opened up by hunters years ago but later abandoned. It's relatively dry inside, with simple stone beds and a stove. We'll rest here for an hour, eat some dry rations, and then continue our journey. Tonight we need to reach 'Old Bear Ridge,' deeper in, where there's a safer base." As Ying Qi spoke, he pushed aside the vines at the cave entrance and went inside first.

The cave wasn't deep, about two or three zhang (approximately 6-9 meters). As he had said, it was quite dry, with some firewood piled in a corner and a simple stone stove. The group unloaded some supplies from the mule cart, started a fire, boiled water, and ate their dry rations with the water.

Lin Chen swallowed a "Hundred Herbs Rejuvenating Pill" given to him by Doctor Chen, and ate a hard, flatbread. He felt a warm glow in his stomach and his spirits lifted. He walked to the cave entrance and gazed at the vast, desolate mountains outside. Following the vague guidance of the "Yang Map" and the "Yin Map," combined with Master Shi's intuition, "Hidden Dragon Abyss" was likely located to the southwest, deep within those treacherous mountains known locally as "Ghost's Bane." There, clouds lingered year-round, poisonous miasma lurked, and ferocious beasts were roamed; various bizarre and terrifying tales circulated, deterring ordinary hunters and herb gatherers from venturing deep.

"The road ahead is fraught with peril..." Lin Chen murmured to himself. But in his eyes, there was no fear, only an increasingly calm resolve.

After resting, the group set off again. This time, they didn't travel by carriage but on foot. Shadow Nine led the way, Shadow Seven brought up the rear, and Lin Chen, Doctor Chen, and Master Shi walked in the middle. Master Shi stopped frequently to use his compass and a strange, horn-like object to probe the earth's veins and adjust their direction.

The mountain path became increasingly difficult; often there was no path at all, requiring them to hack their way through the undergrowth. Lin Chen, with his injured right arm, struggled to climb, but gritted his teeth and persevered. Doctor Chen, though elderly, walked with unusual steadiness, clearly no ordinary old man. Shadow Seven and Shadow Nine, on the other hand, moved as if on flat ground.

As the sun began to set, they had ventured deep into the heart of Qixia Mountain. The surrounding forests grew even taller and denser, blocking out the sun and dimming the light. A thin mist, carrying a sweet, fishy smell, began to fill the air. Master Shi warned, "Be careful, it's miasma. Although it's not strong, inhaling too much will cause dizziness. Cover your mouth and nose with this." He handed each of them a small piece of coarse cloth soaked in some kind of herbal juice.

After walking for about another hour, a bottomless, mist-shrouded cliff appeared ahead. The roar of rushing water echoed from below, indicating a raging torrent. This was the edge of "Ghost's Sorrow." According to the map, they needed to find a very hidden, almost vertical "Ape Path" on one side of the cliff to descend and reach the deeper valley.

Just as the group stopped to search for the legendary "Ape Path," Shadow Nine, who had been scouting ahead, suddenly returned like a ghost, his face grave. He whispered, "My lord, there's something ahead! About a hundred feet away, we've spotted signs of human activity. There are quite a few, at least ten or more, searching something on the cliff edge. They don't seem like mountain people or hunters, but rather... well-trained martial artists! Some of them have a rather strong aura!"

Someone? And they arrived even before them? Are they Prince Zhongshun's men? Or some other force coveting the armory? A chill ran through the group.

Shadow Seven's eyes flashed coldly. He made a hand gesture, and the group immediately crouched low, using the dense bushes for cover, and silently crept towards the direction Shadow Nine had indicated.

Soon, they saw the scene ahead. On a relatively flat clearing at the edge of the cliff, there were indeed more than a dozen figures. These people were all dressed in uniform dark gray close-fitting clothes, with swords at their waists. Their movements were efficient, carefully searching every clump of grass and crevices along the cliff edge. Leading them was a burly, bald man with a fierce aura—clearly a peak eighth-rank martial artist! Beside him stood two elderly men with even more profound auras and bulging temples—two early seventh-rank experts! The others were all at the eighth or ninth rank in cultivation.

These people were definitely not benevolent! Judging from their attire and style of operation, they seemed to be private soldiers or assassins kept by some powerful organization.

"It's them! The Black Tiger Gang!" Ying Qi lowered his voice, his tone icy. "The Black Tiger Gang is one of the largest underworld gangs near Jinling, known for its ruthless and lawless actions. Behind them... seems to be the Xue family. It appears the Xue family and Prince Zhongshun's side are acting quickly, and have sent quite a few men."

The Black Tiger Gang? The Xue family? Lin Chen's eyes narrowed. It seemed the "heroes of the martial world" Xue Pan had befriended were likely members of the Black Tiger Gang. They had indeed come for the Xuanzhen Armory, and it seemed they had already found some clues and were searching for the entrance.

"My lord, what should we do? Should we..." Ying Jiu's eyes flashed with murderous intent, and he made a throat-slitting gesture. Although the enemy outnumbered them and had two seventh-rank members, their side also had two seventh-rank members, Ying Qi and Ying Jiu, plus Lin Chen (injured but able to hold them off), Doctor Chen (a poison expert?), and Master Shi (familiar with the terrain). They weren't without a fighting chance. If they could launch a surprise attack, they might be able to annihilate them all.

Ying Qi pondered for a moment, then shook his head: "No. The enemy outnumbers us, and two of them are seventh-rank. If we engage in combat, the commotion will be too great, potentially alerting other forces in the mountains or attracting even more terrifying beasts. Moreover, our target is the armory; we shouldn't linger here and expose our whereabouts."

He looked at Lin Chen: "Young Master, your injuries haven't healed; it's not advisable to fight head-on. Let's bypass them and find a way down from the other side. This cliff stretches for over ten miles; their search area is limited. Let's speed up and find the entrance before them."

Lin Chen nodded in agreement. This was indeed not the time for a direct confrontation. His injuries hadn't healed, and his right arm was injured. Forcing a fight would not only have a low chance of victory but could also worsen his injuries and hinder their subsequent actions.

Just as the group was about to quietly retreat and find another way, suddenly, from the depths of the churning mist below the cliff, a muffled, thunderous roar, like a boulder tumbling down, came from the depths. Immediately afterward, a strange, scorching current, mixed with sulfur and a certain sweet, fishy smell, shot upward from the cliff!

The two seventh-rank elders of the Black Tiger Gang's expressions changed simultaneously, exclaiming, "The earth's veins are shifting! Is this a sign the entrance is opening? Quick! Go down and check!"

The bald, burly man also shouted excitedly, "Everyone, stay alert! Follow the two elders!"

Immediately, led by the two seventh-rank elders and the bald, burly man, the dozen or so Black Tiger Gang members all took out grappling hooks and nimbly climbed down the cliff, clearly prepared beforehand.

"Earth ley lines shifting? The entrance opening?" Shadow Seven's eyes flashed with brilliance. "Could the armory entrance be right below this cliff? And showing signs of opening at this very moment? Is it a coincidence, or..."

"Regardless of whether it is or not, this is a rare opportunity!" Master Shi also became excited, his compass needle spinning wildly, pointing down the cliff. "That 'spiritual' energy has become exceptionally clear! It's right below!"

"Go! Follow them! Be careful and stay hidden, wait for the right opportunity!" Ying Qi made a decisive move. Now that the others had already gone down, they couldn't fall behind. Perhaps they could use them to scout ahead, or even... let the two sides fight, and the third side benefit!

Without further hesitation, the group immediately crept towards a hidden crevice on the other side of the cliff, about a hundred feet away from where the Black Tiger Gang members had descended. There, the vines were denser, the terrain more complex, and it was harder to be discovered.

Lin Chen, with his right arm in a sling, followed behind Ying Qi, his gaze calmly fixed on the misty, bottomless cliff. He could feel that the fragment of the "Yang Diagram" and the jade slip of the "Yin Diagram" in his arms seemed to be faintly warming, resonating slightly with the strange "spiritual" energy emanating from below the cliff.

The entrance to the Xuanzhen Armory was truly beneath this "Ghost's Sorrow" cliff? And, just as they arrived, it showed signs of opening?

Was it an opportunity, or a trap?

Lin Chen took a deep breath, suppressing the turmoil in his heart. Whether ahead lay treasure or a mountain of knives, having come this far, there was no choice but to move forward.

He tightened his grip on the magnolia jade pendant at his chest, then touched the broken Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant in his robes. The last trace of hesitation in his eyes vanished.

With a swift movement, he and Ying Qi and the others silently disappeared into the hidden crevice, climbing down the bottomless, mist-shrouded cliff.


Chapter 38 Underground Passage, Ancient Tomb Discovered

The cliff was steep, almost vertical. Mist, like a veil, swirled among the jagged rock walls and gnarled ancient vines, reducing visibility to almost nothing. The roaring sound of water and the scorching air below, mixed with the smell of sulfur and a sweet, fishy odor, created an eerie atmosphere that unsettled the mind.

Lin Chen, his right arm in a sling, gripped a crevice in the rock with his left hand, searching for footholds as he followed Ying Qi, climbing down with difficulty and caution. Although the pain in his right arm had greatly subsided, he still dared not exert any force, relying entirely on the strength of his left arm and legs, along with the subtle application of internal energy from the *Qi Control Technique* to adjust his center of gravity, barely managing to keep up with the group. Doctor Chen was not far above him, his movements seemingly slow but exceptionally steady, often using his medicinal hoe or a few specially made needles to gain leverage or create footholds at dangerous points. Master Shi followed closely behind, his eyes still bright and alert even in the dim light, constantly observing the patterns and crevices of the rock face, as if reading the book of the earth. Ying Jiu brought up the rear, responsible for guarding the back.

The movements of the Black Tiger Gang members below were clearly audible. The sound of iron hooks and ropes scraping against the rocks, heavy breathing, occasional shouts and commands, and the increasingly close, strange sounds and gusts of air originating from the earth. Clearly, they too were rapidly descending towards the core of the "earthquake disturbance."

"Be careful, the rocks here are loose, there's miasma!" Shadow Seven, who was scouting ahead, warned in a low voice, while simultaneously shifting to the side to avoid a small plume of grayish-white mist with a pungent, acidic smell that suddenly erupted.

Everyone immediately held their breath or covered their mouths and noses with the herbal cloth. The mist seemed corrosive; when it hit the nearby vines, they quickly turned black and withered. Clearly, below this cliff, not only was the terrain treacherous, but there was also a naturally formed poisonous miasma trap.

The deeper they went, the dimmer the light became, the thicker the mist grew, yet the temperature gradually increased, and the sulfurous smell in the air became increasingly strong. The roaring sound of water grew louder, as if a rushing underground river flowed below. And that strange, unsettling "spiritual" energy became clearer, like an invisible pulse, slowly throbbing in the darkness, attracting and warning every creature that approached.

"Almost there! It's just below!" Master Shi's voice carried suppressed excitement. The compass needle in his hand had almost stopped spinning, pointing straight down to a fixed point.

After descending another twenty or thirty feet, Ying Qi suddenly stopped and gestured. The group immediately lay prone behind a large, protruding rock, holding their breath.

About ten zhang below, the mist dissipated, revealing a relatively open, smooth stone platform sculpted by the river. The platform clung to the cliff face, half-hidden in shadow. At the end of the platform was a dark, towering cave entrance, several zhang high and over two zhang wide! The entrance was irregularly shaped, with obvious signs of human excavation along its edges, and remnants of ancient, weathered relief carvings, though long since blurred. Inside the cave, a faint, flowing, pale blue light emanated, the source of the "spiritual" energy! Even more strangely, scattered on the ground near the entrance were some broken stones, several brand-new climbing tools, and even a pool of undried blood!

The Black Tiger Gang members had clearly gone inside! And it seemed they had encountered something, a battle had broken out?

"This is it! The armory entrance!" Master Shi whispered, his fingers trembling slightly with excitement. "Judging from the decorations at the entrance, it's undoubtedly the style of the 'Xuanzhen' lineage from the previous dynasty! That light... it's the 'spiritual essence' left over from an ancient formation! They triggered the restriction, and it seems they suffered a loss, but they've already gone in!"

Shadow Seven's sharp gaze swept around the area around the entrance, then looked down at the roaring, steaming underground river below. He said in a deep voice, "They left two people guarding the entrance. It's that bald man and another mid-stage eighth-rank cultivator. The others, including those two seventh-rank elders, should have gone in. Judging from the direction of the bloodstains, they're heading deeper into the cave."

Everyone looked, and sure enough, in the shadows of the entrance, two figures could be vaguely seen leaning against the rock wall—the bald, burly man and another wiry man. They appeared to be resting, but their eyes were warily scanning the area outside the cave and towards the underground river, their weapons never leaving their hands.

"We must get in as soon as possible. If we're too late, we might not even get a taste of the soup." A murderous glint flashed in Ying Jiu's eyes. "Those two, leave them to me and Lord Ying Qi. Young Master, Doctor Chen, Master Shi, you take this opportunity to sneak in."

"No." Ying Qi shook his head. "A forced attack will surely alert those inside the cave. Moreover, this cave entrance is strange; that pale blue light is probably some kind of residual protective barrier. The Black Tiger Gang members were able to get in either by finding a way or by paying a price. If we rashly force our way in, we risk disaster."

"Then what should we do?" Ying Jiu frowned.

Ying Qi looked at Master Shi: "Master Shi, in your opinion, besides this main entrance, are there any other entrances? Or, is there a way to break this barrier?"

Master Shi stared at the pale blue light flowing around the cave entrance, then looked at the surrounding terrain and rock walls, and pondered, "This restriction... seems to be formed by combining the earth's veins and water vapor with an ancient formation. It has been neglected for many years, and its power is only a fraction of what it used to be. However, if it is touched rashly, it will still trigger a counterattack. The Black Tiger Gang was able to enter either by using a special 'formation-breaking talisman' or a token, or... by finding a weak point in the restriction, or by forcibly breaking through at the cost of lives. As for the other entrances..." His gaze swept over the rushing underground river, then looked at the cliff face above the cave entrance, which was completely covered by vines, "Normally, the former residences of powerful figures from previous dynasties would often have multiple passages, both visible and hidden. The underground river might be one, but the current is rapid, the water vapor is strong, and there might be water beasts and poisonous insects, making it extremely dangerous. On the cliff face... there might be ventilation shafts or drainage channels, but due to the passage of time, they have probably been sealed off by rocks and soil long ago."

A silence fell over the crowd. A direct assault was out of the question, the underground river was too treacherous, and other entrances seemed unlikely. Should they wait for the Black Tiger Gang to emerge before attempting to seize the territory? That would introduce even more uncertainty.

Just then, Lin Chen, who had been silently observing, suddenly spoke, his voice calm: "Perhaps… we can go in from above."

"Up there?" Everyone paused, looking up to see a steep cliff face covered in slippery moss and vines above the cave entrance, seemingly unaffected.

Lin Chen raised his left hand, pointing to a section of rock wall about three zhang (approximately 10 meters) above the cave entrance, completely obscured by dense vines: "There, there's a slight airflow disturbance. Although very subtle, in this relatively enclosed canyon environment, any airflow disturbance is worth noting. Moreover, when the hot airflow caused by the earth's ley lines surged upwards just now, I noticed that the vines there swayed slightly differently from the surrounding area, as if... there was emptiness behind it."

Upon hearing this, everyone immediately focused their attention. Sure enough, upon closer inspection, the leaves of that vine, swaying in the occasional passing hot wind carrying the smell of sulfur, exhibited an extremely subtle difference in frequency and direction compared to the surrounding vines. It would be virtually impossible to notice without deliberate observation.

"It's a ventilation shaft! Or some kind of side passage!" Master Shi's eyes lit up. He immediately took out the horn-shaped object, held it to his ear, and listened carefully to the rock wall for a moment. A look of delight appeared on his face. "That's right! There's definitely a cavity behind it! And the airflow is inward, albeit weak! Young Master, you have such keen insight!"

Shadow Seven also showed admiration, then decisively said, "Go in from there! Shadow Nine, try to distract the guards at the entrance, create some noise, but don't fight them head-on. We'll take the opportunity to go up. Be quick and quiet!"

"Yes!" Shadow Nine responded, his figure blurring as he silently slipped towards the underground river below, like a bat disappearing into the shadows.

A moment later, a loud "plop" echoed from a spot in the underground river near the stone platform, followed by splashing water and several short, eerie growls resembling those of a water beast (clearly, Shadow Nine had imitated or triggered something). The bald, burly man guarding the cave entrance and another person abruptly stood up, warily looking towards the underground river, gripping their weapons tightly.

"What was that sound?"

"It sounds like something fell into the water? Or is there something in the water?"

"Be careful, let's go check it out!"

Taking advantage of the moment their attention was diverted, Ying Qi shouted, "Go!"

He leaped up first, his body lightly touching the steep cliff face with a few quick movements. Like a nimble ape, he silently reached the vine-covered rock face. A flash of cold light appeared in his hand, and a short, thin blade, as thin as a cicada's wing, swiftly and silently sliced ​​through the entangled vines, revealing a narrow, dark opening, barely wide enough for one person to crawl through! It was indeed a hidden ventilation shaft or side passage!

Without hesitation, Ying Qi shrank back and slipped inside. Lin Chen followed closely behind. The opening was narrow and sloping downwards, the inner walls damp and slippery, covered in moss. He could only use his left hand and knee for leverage, inching his way in. His right arm was slung across his chest, making movement extremely difficult; a slight misstep could cause him to get stuck or slip. Doctor Chen and Master Shi also entered one after another, their movements slow but steady.

The passage wasn't long; after climbing about ten zhang (approximately 33 meters), the path suddenly opened up, revealing a naturally formed stone cave, about two zhang (approximately 6.6 meters) square. The air inside was stuffy, but not stale, indicating the presence of ventilation. A thin layer of dust covered the ground, and scattered in the corners were weathered and rotten wooden planks and pottery shards, suggesting past human activity. Most striking was a narrow, man-made stone doorway carved into the rock wall on one side of the cave, allowing only one person to pass at a time. The doorway was ajar, and a faint, pale blue light, similar to that at the cave entrance, shone through the crack, along with… the faint sounds of fighting, shouts, and the echoes of clashing weapons!

The Black Tiger Gang members were behind this stone door! And, it seemed, they were engaged in combat?

"It's inside! They've triggered the mechanism, or encountered a guardian!" Shadow Seven leaned close to the stone door, listened intently for a moment, and whispered, "The fighting is intense. At least three seventh-rank experts are battling, and others are attacking. Judging from the sounds, the Black Tiger Gang seems... to be at a disadvantage?"

Lin Chen also focused his senses. Amidst the sounds of fighting were roars, screams, and a dull thud, like heavy objects pounding the ground—a sound unlike anything a human could make. In the air, besides the "spiritual" energy brought by the pale blue light, there was a strong stench of blood, and a... chilling, violent, deathly aura!

"It's a tomb guardian puppet! Or a mechanical puppet!" Master Shi's expression changed slightly, and he exclaimed in a low voice, "The former residences of powerful figures from the previous dynasty often used formations combined with mechanisms and puppets for protection. Judging from this murderous aura, they're definitely not benevolent! The Black Tiger Gang has bitten off more than they can chew!"

"Perfect, let them wear us down first." A cold glint flashed in Ying Qi's eyes. "We'll wait until they're both exhausted, or break through the defenses, then we'll make our move. Doctor Chen, Master Shi, you two stay hidden here and set up simple warning and defenses. Young Master, come closer with me and see the situation. Be careful not to reveal your presence, and don't touch anything that might have traps."

Doctor Chen and Master Shi nodded and immediately took out some medicinal powder, special silk thread, small bells, and other items from their respective "treasure chests," and began setting them up at the entrance and corners of the cave. Ying Qi, meanwhile, led Lin Chen silently towards the half-open stone door, peering inside through the crack.

Behind the door lay an even more expansive, towering cavern, several meters high. The cavern was irregularly circular, spanning dozens of meters in circumference. The floor was paved with neat, massive bluestone slabs, but many were now broken and upturned. The cave walls were carved with numerous faded murals and runes, exuding an aura of antiquity and age. Inlaid in the cavern ceiling were dozens of fist-sized, strange stones emitting a soft, pale blue light—the source of the "spiritual energy," casting a hazy, ethereal blue glow over the entire cavern.

At this very moment, a fierce battle was unfolding within this eerie blue cave!

A dozen or so members of the Black Tiger Gang were forming a defensive formation, desperately resisting the attacks of three "monsters"! These three monsters were towering, nearly ten feet tall, their bodies covered in heavy, rusted bronze armor. Through the gaps in the armor, one could vaguely see their black, withered, and lifeless bodies. They had no heads, or rather, their heads were replaced by grotesque bronze beast-head masks, with two eerie green flames burning in the eye sockets. Each held a massive, equally rusted bronze battle axe or halberd; each swing carried a whistling wind and immense force, slamming into the stone slabs and creating deep craters, sending stone chips flying everywhere!

Corpse puppets! Bronze corpse puppets! And judging from their movements, power, and the intense killing intent and chilling aura emanating from them, they were no ordinary creatures. Each one was likely close to a seventh-rank martial artist! Even more terrifying was that they seemed oblivious to pain and death, their attacks ferocious and direct, focusing solely on offense and neglecting defense—a suicidal fighting style!

The two early-stage seventh-rank elders of the Black Tiger Gang were currently each facing a bronze corpse puppet, struggling immensely. Their martial arts were clearly based on agility and unpredictability, or a subtle and vicious approach. Faced with these terrifyingly powerful, incredibly defensive, and seemingly invincible corpse puppets, many of their exquisite techniques were ineffective. They could only rely on their agility to maneuver, occasionally striking the puppets only leaving shallow white marks on their bronze armor or sending up a few sparks—incapable of causing any real damage. On the contrary, each powerful attack from the corpse puppets forced them to dodge hastily, constantly facing perilous situations.

The other bronze corpse puppet was being held back by the bald, burly man (peak eighth rank) and three other eighth-rank late-stage gang members. The four worked in perfect coordination, their swords flashing as they relentlessly attacked the puppet's joints and armor gaps—potentially weak points—but with little success. A single sweep of the puppet's axe sent one of them flying backward, coughing up blood; had his companions not intervened in time, he would likely have been cleaved in two. On the ground lay the corpses of four or five Black Tiger Gang members, their deaths gruesome; some were severed at the waist, others had their heads shattered, their blood staining the bluestone slabs a dark red.

"Damn it! This thing is unbreakable!" roared a seventh-rank elder, narrowly dodging the bronze battle axe's blows. Several beams of poisoned black light shot from his sleeve, striking the puppet's chest, but only producing a few soft clanging sounds before being deflected. The puppet moved without hesitation, sweeping back with its halberd, forcing the elder to retreat again.

"Use fire! Use a lightning talisman! Try it!" another seventh-rank elder shouted, pulling a yellowed talisman from his robes. He bit his tongue, spitting a mouthful of blood onto it. The talisman instantly ignited, transforming into a fist-sized crimson fireball, radiating scorching heat, and hurtled towards the corpse puppet's face.

"Boom!" The fireball exploded, flames engulfing the corpse puppet's head. The corpse puppet's movements faltered slightly, letting out a muffled roar, seemingly from the depths of hell. The eerie green flames burning on its head flickered violently, appearing to dim slightly, and its bronze mask glowed slightly red from the heat. But only for a moment, the eerie green flames surged again, and the corpse puppet swung its battle axe, hacking wildly at the elder, seemingly enraged, its attack even more ferocious!

"Ordinary fire talismans have limited effect! This corpse puppet likely has a Yin Sha core within it; without destroying the core, it's difficult to kill!" The elder's expression grew even more grim.

The bald giant also tried fire, but to no avail. One of his gang members risked getting close, pouring a can of oil onto the corpse puppet's leg and setting it ablaze. The puppet only burned its leg, slowing its movement slightly, before stomping down and crushing the gang member into a bloody pulp. The flames were largely extinguished by the intense, malevolent aura emanating from the puppet.

"This won't do! Third Brother, use that thing!" The seventh-rank elder fighting the old man with the fire talisman suddenly shouted sharply.

The one called "Third Brother" was the same early-stage seventh-rank elder who had been fighting the third corpse puppet alongside the bald giant (one of the two who had been guarding the cave entrance earlier?). Hearing this, a flicker of pain crossed his face, but seeing the precarious situation, he gritted his teeth, pulled a palm-sized, jet-black wooden puppet covered in blood-red runes from his robes, bit his finger, smeared his blood on the puppet's brow, muttered incantations, and then hurled the puppet to the ground!

"Pfft!"

The puppet landed and instantly exploded, transforming into a thick, inky black mist! The mist churned and rapidly condensed, forming a ghostly figure that bore a striking resemblance to the bronze corpse puppet, but slightly smaller, entirely black, and wielding a black mourning staff! As soon as this ghostly figure appeared, it let out a sharp, eerie howl, whipping up gusts of cold wind, and pounced on the nearest bronze corpse puppet! It seemed to ignore physical attacks, passing directly through the cleaving blows of the bronze battle axe, and lunged at the corpse puppet, its mouth snapping open to bite into the gap in the puppet's neck armor!

"Ghost Puppet Technique! This is a forbidden technique of the Southern Frontier's witchcraft sect! The Black Tiger Gang is indeed tainted!" Master Shi outside the door exclaimed softly, a look of apprehension in his eyes.

The dark, ghostly figure seemed to have a special resistance to things imbued with Yin energy. With a bite, the bronze corpse puppet's movements abruptly halted, the Yin energy emanating from its body fluctuated violently, and the eerie green flames flickered erratically. A sizzling sound came from the gap in the neck armor where the bite had struck, as if it were being corroded.

It worked! The Black Tiger Gang members' spirits lifted. The bald, burly man seized the opportunity to roar, and he and another eighth-rank expert launched a full-scale attack on the corpse puppet entangled by the ghostly figure, their swords and blades striking, targeting its joints, eye sockets, and other vital points.

However, at that moment, another unexpected change occurred!

Deep within the cave, from the place where the eerie blue light shone brightest, a sudden, even louder, rattling sound, like gears turning, emanated. Immediately afterward, the ground trembled violently, and the previously dim murals and runes on the cave walls began to glow faintly! An even more ancient, desolate, and majestic aura permeated the depths of the cave!

"Not good! The core restriction has been triggered! Something even more powerful is about to emerge!" Master Shi's expression changed drastically.

At the deepest part of the cave, from the source of the pale blue light—an ancient altar, three zhang tall, carved entirely from some unknown white jade and shaped like a lotus flower—slowly rose from the ground! Around the altar, nine thick bronze pillars, engraved with intricate cloud and thunder patterns, spiraled. As the altar rose, the gems atop the nine bronze pillars suddenly burst forth with dazzling golden light!

"Roar—!"

A terrifying roar, seemingly from the primordial era, shook the entire cave, sending dust plumes down its spines, erupted from beneath the altar! Immediately afterward, the ground in the center of the altar cracked open, and an enormous, ferocious claw covered in dark golden scales and bound by black chains shot out from the fissure, slamming heavily onto the ground!

"Boom!"

The entire cave shook violently! The bluestone slabs cracked inch by inch! A violent, destructive pressure swept out like a tsunami! Whether it was the Black Tiger Gang members, the three bronze corpse puppets, or even the dark, ghostly figure, under this pressure, they all swayed and staggered like leaves in a storm, their movements instantly slowed!

"This is... a guardian beast of the tomb?! Or... an ancient demon suppressed?!" Ying Qi's voice was filled with disbelief and horror. "What kind of monster is being suppressed in this Xuanzhen Armory?!"

Lin Chen also felt a tightness in his chest, his blood churning. The fragment of the "Yang Tu" scroll and the magnolia jade pendant in his arms simultaneously emitted a violent burning sensation! The broken Soul-Nourishing Wood pendant also trembled slightly, emitting a trace of coolness, protecting his mind and barely resisting the terrifying pressure.

The cave was deathly silent. Everyone was stunned by this sudden and terrifying change. The dark golden claws slowly retracted into the crack, but the earth-shattering pressure still enveloped the entire space. From within the crack, the faint clanging of chains being dragged could be heard, along with even heavier, muffled thunderous breathing. It was as if a peerless ferocious beast that had slumbered for thousands of years was slowly awakening, about to break free!

"Run! Run! This is no place to linger!" The seventh-rank elder, the leader of the Black Tiger Gang, no longer cared about the armory's treasures. His face contorted with terror, he roared and turned to flee towards the cave entrance he had come from! Corpse puppets, ghost puppets—they were all insignificant before this soon-to-be-born ferocious creature!

The others, as if waking from a dream, abandoned their opponents and fled for their lives. Even the three bronze corpse puppets, seemingly driven by some command or instinct, abandoned the pursuit and slowly retreated, guarding the crack in the altar. Their eerie green flames flickered, as if on guard, or perhaps… in worship?

"An opportunity!" Ying Qi's eyes flashed with a sharp light. He made a decisive move. "While they're in chaos and the ferocious beast hasn't fully awakened, let's go in! Our target isn't the beast, nor the altar, but the side chamber! That's most likely where the burial goods or storage are! Quick!"

Before he finished speaking, he was already like an arrow released from a bow, rushing into the cave. But instead of heading towards the altar or the escape route for the Black Tiger Gang, he darted along the cave's edge towards a small, inconspicuous doorway to the right of the altar, half-hidden by shadows and collapsed stone pillars! Lin Chen, Doctor Chen, and Master Shi immediately followed!

In the chaos, the Black Tiger Gang members were only concerned with escaping. Although the two seventh-rank elders caught a glimpse of them, they were preoccupied with their own survival and couldn't afford to intercept them. The three bronze corpse puppets seemed to be drawn to the crack in the altar, failing to react to the group that had slipped past along the edge.

The group moved with incredible speed, reaching the small doorway in the blink of an eye. A faint, pale blue light emanated from the doorway, but there were no signs of any protective energy. Shadow Seven didn't hesitate, flashing inside. Lin Chen followed closely behind.

Just as he stepped into the doorway, out of the corner of his eye, he seemed to glimpse a pair of large, lantern-like vertical pupils, burning with dark golden flames, slowly opening from the crack in the altar, sweeping their gaze towards him.

That single glance sent a chill down Lin Chen's spine, as if he had fallen into an ice cave, his hair standing on end, as if he were being stared at by the Grim Reaper! If it weren't for the coolness brought by the "Heart-Soul Connection" and the protective glow of the magnolia jade pendant, he would have almost lost his composure!

He dared not turn back, forcibly suppressing the surging fear within him, and stepped into the doorway, shutting out those terrifying eyes.

Beyond the doorway was a downward-sloping, narrower, and deeper passageway. The passageway's walls were smooth, seemingly meticulously polished, and inlaid with stones that emitted a faint glow. A faint scent, like sandalwood mixed with herbs, filled the air, completely different from the bloody, malevolent, and ferocious atmosphere of the cave outside.

The group hurried along the passageway, the clamor of the Black Tiger Gang's fleeing members, the heavy footsteps of the bronze corpse puppets moving, and the increasingly loud clanging of chains and the low growls of ferocious beasts coming from the direction of the altar all seeming to be filtered out, becoming distant and indistinct.

After running about a hundred paces, at the end of the passageway appeared a tightly closed, ancient bronze door. The door had no keyhole, only two recessed palm prints and some illegible runes.

"This is it! It requires a specific bloodline, cultivation technique, or token to open!" Master Shi said solemnly, looking at the runes on the gate.

Ying Qi looked at Lin Chen. Lin Chen took a deep breath, stepped forward, and looked at the bronze gate. The burning sensation of the "Yang Diagram" fragment and the "Yin Diagram" jade slip in his arms grew stronger, as if they were cheering. He raised his intact left hand, hesitated for a moment, and then looked down at the warm magnolia jade pendant on his chest. A memento left by Daiyu's mother, perhaps…

He shook his head and pressed the jade pendant back into his clothes. He didn't want to take the risk. Then, he tried to circulate the *Qi Control Technique*, injecting a wisp of internal energy into the sunken palm print. At the same time, he visualized the two characters "Xuanzhen" and the celestial phenomena and ley lines of the "Mountains and Rivers Map."

At first, there was no reaction. But when he guided his inner energy along a specific trajectory within the "Yin Diagram" astrological chart, and then slowly injected it—

"Buzz..."

The bronze door suddenly emitted a deep buzz! The dim runes on the door, like lit lamps, gradually lit up with a soft white light! The sunken palm print also began to radiate warmth.

"There's a reaction! Continue!" Shadow Seven whispered.

Lin Chen concentrated and continued to inject his inner energy. The white light grew brighter, and the buzzing sound grew louder. Finally, after a slight "click," like gears meshing, the heavy bronze door slowly and silently opened a crack inward.

A richer, purer spiritual energy, mixed with the scent of aged dust and a faint medicinal fragrance, rushed in. Behind the door, a soft light shone.

The storage room of the Xuanzhen Armory, or rather, the true core and secret place of this underground ancient tomb, finally opened its doors to them.

"Enter!" Ying Qi shouted, flashing inside first. Lin Chen and the others immediately followed.

Just as the last person—Doctor Chen—entered, the bronze door slowly and automatically closed, separating the inside from the outside into two worlds. It also completely shut out the terrifying roars from the depths of the cave, the Black Tiger Gang's rout, and the imminent awakening of its unparalleled ferocity.

Inside was a small but exceptionally clean and elegant stone chamber. The walls were also made of luminous stone, casting a soft, bright light. In the center of the chamber were several antique-style rosewood shelves, displaying jade boxes, wooden cases, porcelain vases, scrolls, and a few dusty, ancient weapons. In a corner, there was a small stone platform, upon which rested an exquisite jade box, entirely purple-gold and carved with a nine-dragon-playing-with-a-pearl pattern, radiating the most intense spiritual energy and alluring precious light.

The treasures of Master Xuanzhen were right before their eyes.

However, having just experienced the life-or-death ordeal in the cave, standing here in this tranquil and peaceful secret stone chamber, the group felt little joy, but rather even more vigilant. Who knew what traps and trials were hidden within this seemingly peaceful chamber?

“Be careful, don’t touch anything lightly, especially that jade box,” Ying Qi said in a deep voice, his sharp gaze sweeping over every corner of the stone chamber.

Lin Chen’s gaze fell on the wooden shelf closest to him. There, on it lay an open jade box, inside which lay a pill the size of a longan, pale purple in color, with nine natural cloud patterns on its surface, emitting a strange and invigorating fragrance. Beside the jade box was an ancient jade slip, on which a few small characters were written in ancient seal script.

“Nine-Turn…Resurrection Pill?!”


Chapter 39 Fierce Battle Underground, Narrowly Obtaining the Resurrection Pill

"Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill?!"

Lin Chen's voice was filled with undisguised shock, his gaze fixed on the open jade box. That pale purple pill, adorned with nine natural cloud-like patterns and exuding a refreshing medicinal fragrance—wasn't it the miraculous healing elixir that Physician Wang had mentioned, capable of reviving the dead, healing bones, cleansing marrow, and nourishing the soul? This pill was precisely the perfect remedy for his current injuries! Not only could it completely eradicate the lingering Yin poison, repair his damaged meridians and dantian, but it could also nourish his soul, which had been severely depleted from using the "Thunderbolt Fist" and "Heart-Soul Connection," and might even, in a way, elevate his martial arts foundation to a higher level!

Almost instinctively, Lin Chen's gaze was fixed on the pill. The remaining Yin poison within his body, his weakened meridians, his depleted internal energy, and his weary soul all desperately craved the immense life force this pill could provide.

"Young Master, you mustn't!" However, Ying Qi's low shout was like a bucket of cold water, instantly freezing Lin Chen's outstretched hand in mid-air. "This place is strange everywhere. These pills are displayed so openly here; there must be something fishy going on! Perhaps it's bait, or… a test!"

Master Shi hurriedly added, "Lord Ying Qi is absolutely right! What kind of person is Master Xuanzhen? Would his legacy be obtained so easily? This jade box is open, the pills are exposed, yet there's no protection whatsoever—that's illogical! It must be a trap! Touching it will surely lead to disaster!"

Doctor Chen narrowed his eyes, carefully examining the pill, and then sniffed the medicinal fragrance in the air. He said in a deep voice, "The pill is genuine, and its medicinal properties are well preserved. It is priceless. But precisely because of this, it is even more suspicious. Young master, please do not act rashly."

Lin Chen took a deep breath, forcibly suppressing the desire in his heart and the urge of his body, and slowly withdrew his hand. He knew Ying Qi and the others were right. This quiet room seemed peaceful, but who knew what kind of deadly danger lurked in the shadows? The ferocious creature about to break free of its seal in the cave, and the terror of the bronze corpse puppet, were still vivid in his mind. The treasures of the Xuanzhen Armory were not so easily taken.

He forced himself to look away from the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill" and instead looked at the rest of the stone chamber. On several sandalwood shelves, besides the jade boxes containing pills, there were many other items. There were sealed jade scrolls, ancient-style swords and weapons imbued with spiritual energy, porcelain bottles containing unknown pills, and some peculiar minerals and jade artifacts. But without exception, they were all shrouded in an extremely faint, almost imperceptible pale golden light membrane. Only the jade box containing the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill" was open, without the protective light membrane.

“Look, all the other items have protective barriers, but this pill doesn’t,” Ying Qi pointed to the pale golden membrane. “This further indicates something is wrong. Perhaps it’s an enticement to touch it and trigger a mechanism. Or perhaps…” His gaze fell on the ancient jade slip next to the pill, “Does that jade slip record the correct method for obtaining the pill, or… instructions for a trap?”

Lin Chen looked at the jade slip. Besides “Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill,” there seemed to be several lines of even smaller characters on the ancient seal script, but he was too far away to see them clearly.

“Master Shi, can you see the smaller characters on that jade slip?” Lin Chen asked.

Master Shi gazed intently. Having spent years studying ancient texts and esoteric writings, he was quite knowledgeable in ancient seal script. After a moment, he slowly recited: "'Nine Revolutions of Resurrection, seizing the power of heaven and earth. Those destined will obtain it, but must undergo my test. Beside the elixir lies a jade box containing a token. Hold the token, break the seal, and the elixir may be yours. Those who forcibly take it will be struck by heavenly lightning and earthly fire, their soul and spirit annihilated!'"

Indeed, there was a test! And it mentioned a "token" and "breaking the seal"! Could that "jade box beside the elixir" refer to the purple-gold jade box carved with nine dragons playing with a pearl? But that jade box looked far more precious, and its protection was likely far more stringent.

“The token is in the jade box, and the jade box is sealed. To obtain the token and break the pill's seal (if the pill truly has a seal), one must first open the jade box. And the jade box's seal is likely even more difficult to break.” Ying Qi frowned deeply. “This is a vicious cycle. Or perhaps, the so-called 'test' is simply requiring one to open the jade box and obtain the token through skill?”

“Perhaps… this test isn't about strength, but rather… some specific condition?” Lin Chen pondered. He recalled that when he opened the bronze gate, he succeeded by simulating the “Yin Diagram” star trajectory using the internal energy of the *Qi Control Technique*. This Master Xuanzhen seemed to have considerable knowledge of “astronomy,” “earth veins,” and “arrays,” and his test might be related to this.

He turned his gaze back to the purple-gold jade box. The jade box rested quietly on the stone platform, its nine dragons playing with a pearl carving lifelike, radiating a soft yet noble purple-gold halo. He tried to slowly probe the jade box with a wisp of spiritual energy, attempting to sense the fluctuations of the seal on it.

However, the instant his spiritual energy touched the purple-gold aura on the jade box's surface—

"Buzz!"

The jade box trembled violently! The nine carved dragons seemed to come alive, their mouths opening simultaneously, each spewing out a beam of light, each a different color and as thin as a hair! Red, orange, yellow, green, cyan, blue, purple, black, and white—the nine colors of light intertwined in the air, instantly forming an incredibly complex and ever-changing three-dimensional light net, completely enveloping the jade box! Above the light net, faint phantoms of fading stars and reversed landscapes appeared, emanating a breathtakingly vast and profound aura of power!

"The Nine Palaces Star Array Restriction!" Master Shi gasped, his face filled with horror. "This is a legendary ancient array! Based on the Nine Palaces and guided by the stars, its variations are endless, containing the wonders of spatial teleportation, the five elements' mutual generation and restraint, and spiritual illusion and killing! Only those proficient in array formations and possessing extraordinary cultivation can break it! Forcibly breaking the array will inevitably result in a backlash from the nine-colored divine light, annihilating both body and soul!"

The Nine Palaces Star Array Restriction! Lin Chen also felt a chill run down his spine. The aura emanating from that net of light was far more terrifying than any restriction he had encountered before, even faintly comparable to the oppressive power of the ferocious creature deep within the cave! This was something they simply couldn't break at their current level.

Should they just give up on the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill" like this? Lin Chen was unwilling. This pill was too important to him. With it, he could not only recover quickly, but might even break through to a higher realm, possessing the ability to directly confront a seventh-rank expert, thus better protecting Daiyu and dealing with the threats from Jia She and Prince Zhongshun.

His gaze returned to the pale purple pill lying quietly in the jade box. Without the protective light membrane, its fragrance was alluring. The jade slip stated, "Those who forcibly take it will be struck by heavenly lightning and earthly fire, their soul and spirit annihilated," but… what if there was no "forcible take"? What if… he had a way to obtain the pill without triggering any of the "forcible take" judgment mechanisms?

An extremely bold, even fanciful, idea suddenly surfaced in his mind.

"Lord Ying Qi, Master Shi, Doctor Chen," Lin Chen spoke slowly, his voice carrying a hint of determination, "I need that 'Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill.' It is crucial to me. I might… have a way to try and obtain it, but I need you to buy me time, and, no matter what happens, do not act rashly, and do not approach me."

"Young Master, you…" Ying Qi looked at him, his eyes showing confusion and worry.

"Don't worry, I won't force it." Lin Chen's gaze was calm, yet carried an undeniable firmness. "I just want to test an idea. If it fails, I'll give up immediately, and we'll leave right away. If it succeeds… perhaps we can not only obtain the pills but also gain a deeper understanding of the restrictions here."

Ying Qi gave him a deep look, seeing that his eyes were clear and not impulsive. After a moment's thought, she finally nodded: "Alright. We'll protect you. But young master, you must prioritize your own safety. If things don't go well, retreat immediately. This place is not safe to stay long; the commotion from the cave could spread over at any time."

“Understood.” Lin Chen nodded. He walked to the wooden shelf where the “Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill” was placed, sat cross-legged, and kept a distance of about ten feet from the jade box and the pill. He slowly closed his eyes, circulated the *Qingxin Jue* (Heart-Cleansing Technique), and his mind became as calm as an ancient well.

He didn't try to “take” the pill, nor did he use his inner energy or spiritual power to touch the jade box. What he did was begin to circulate the *Yuqi Jue* (Qi Control Technique).

Not a simple circulation of Qi, but rather immersing his mind completely in the comprehension of this technique, to simulate and resonate with that “spiritual energy.” The spiritual energy in this stone chamber, the “spiritual essence” in the cave, and even the vast array intent emanating from the “Nine Palaces Starry Sky Restriction” were, to some extent, all related to “Qi,” to the flow, change, and combination of the primordial energy of heaven and earth.

He tried to align his internal energy fluctuations as closely as possible with the frequency of the pale blue "spiritual essence" permeating the stone chamber. Simultaneously, he visualized the earth's vein patterns in the fragments of the "Yang Diagram" and the trajectories of the constellations in the "Yin Diagram," attempting to construct a vague, internally and externally integrated "field" within his mind.

This was an extremely delicate and mentally taxing process. He had to completely suppress his aura and thoughts, as if transforming into a part of the flowing "energy" within the stone chamber, without any intention to "take" or "destroy," simply existing naturally, resonating with the surrounding environment.

Initially, there was no reaction. The stone chamber remained tranquil, the elixir lying quietly in the jade box. Ying Qi and the others nervously watched their surroundings, especially the nine-colored light net of the purple-gold jade box and the direction of the stone door.

But Lin Chen remained calm and focused. He incorporated the serene and tranquil spiritual power of Daiyu, brought about by the "Soulmate Connection," into this resonance, further purifying and etherealing his mind. The magnolia-shaped jade pendant on his chest also emitted a faint, comforting, and warm aura.

Time passed slowly. The stone chamber was so quiet you could hear a pin drop, save for the soft breathing of the few people inside. The roars of the ferocious beasts and the clanking of chains coming from the direction of the cave seemed to gradually fade, whether due to the distance or the beasts having temporarily subsided, it was unclear.

Just as Ying Qi and the others began to grow impatient, thinking Lin Chen's method might not work, and were about to offer a warning—

A sudden change occurred!

It wasn't the jade box containing the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill," nor the purple-gold jade box, but rather the seemingly ordinary cracks in the bluestone floor beneath Lin Chen. Suddenly, extremely faint, almost imperceptible pale blue light patterns, originating from the same source as the "Spiritual Essence" stone on the cave ceiling, began to illuminate! These light patterns, like a spiderweb, spread outwards from Lin Chen, especially towards the wooden shelf where the pills were placed!

Immediately afterward, above the previously empty and unprotected jade box containing the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill," the air slightly distorted, and a miniature phantom of an extremely complex phantom, entirely composed of pale blue light, flashed by! This phantom appeared quickly and disappeared even faster; if everyone hadn't been completely focused, it would have been almost imperceptible.

In the instant the illusory image appeared and vanished, Lin Chen, struck by a sudden inspiration, immersed himself entirely in the contemplation of "life" and "regeneration," visualizing his injuries healing under the vibrant life force. He then channeled this pure, selfless, and undaunted vitality, through the internal energy fluctuations resonating with the surrounding spiritual energy, gently, like a spring breeze caressing the water's surface, towards the pill.

"Buzz..."

A faint, almost ethereal sound, seemingly emanating from the depths of the soul, reverberated through the silent stone chamber. The "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill" trembled slightly, then automatically and slowly floated up from the jade box! Nine cloud-like patterns shimmered on its pale purple surface, and the medicinal fragrance instantly intensified several times over, becoming incredibly invigorating!

Even more miraculously, no protective barrier or restriction appeared around the pill. It simply floated silently in the air, as if waiting, or perhaps... scrutinizing.

Lin Chen's heart stirred. He slowly extended his left hand (his right hand still hanging), palm upward, his inner energy flowing, carrying a pure, vibrant resonance, and gestured "please" towards the pill. He didn't use any grasping or absorbing power; he simply waited quietly like a container.

The suspended "Nine-Turn Rejuvenating Pill" trembled slightly, seemingly hesitant. After a moment, it truly seemed to possess a spirit, slowly and lightly drifting towards Lin Chen's palm, finally settling steadily within it! The touch was warm and smooth, the medicinal power contained within, and a surge of vitality seeped into his palm through the meridians, invigorating him greatly, and even dispelling some of the remaining Yin poison within his body.

It worked! It actually worked! Without triggering any restrictions or encountering any attacks, this precious "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill" simply "voluntarily" fell into his hands!

Shadow Seven, Master Shi, and Doctor Chen were all dumbfounded, unable to believe it. They had never seen such a way of obtaining treasure; it completely exceeded their understanding of martial arts and formations.

"Young Master, what did you do…" Shadow Seven couldn't help but ask.

Holding the pill, Lin Chen was filled with shock and enlightenment. He vaguely guessed that the test of this "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill" might not be martial prowess or array formation skills, but rather an understanding of the "Dao," especially the harmony with the Dao of "vitality," "nature," and "non-contention." The Venerable Xuanzhen might have left this pill to save those truly destined and whose hearts resonated with his Dao. His own understanding of "Qi" through the *Qi Control Technique*, his resonance with the "Yin-Yang Diagram," and especially the pure protective intent brought by the "Heart-Soul Connection" combined with Daiyu's cool and ethereal spirit, had, by a twist of fate, perfectly aligned with the true meaning of this "test."

"Just luck. Perhaps my cultivation method is destined for this place." Lin Chen didn't explain further, carefully placing the pill into a specially made jade bottle in his robes. With the pill in hand, he felt reassured; with this pill, there was hope for restoring his strength.

However, the moment he put away the pills, another strange change occurred in the stone chamber!

"Boom!"

The entire stone chamber shook violently! The glowing stones on the walls flickered, and dust fell in clumps. The "Nine Palaces Starry Sky Restriction" light net on the purple-gold jade box suddenly shone brightly, nine-colored rays swirling wildly and emitting a sharp buzzing sound! At the same time, the pale blue light patterns on the floor in the center of the chamber suddenly became bright and dazzling, rapidly spreading and connecting towards the stone door, as if triggering a chain reaction!

"Not good! Taking the pill has triggered some kind of warning or self-destruct mechanism! This place is going to collapse! Run!" Ying Qi's expression changed drastically, and he shouted sharply.

Almost as soon as he finished speaking, the bronze door that had closed automatically creaked open with a sickeningly loud groan, slowly squeezing and deforming inward. Scorching heat and a dark red light emanated from the cracks! Behind the door came even clearer and more ferocious roars of ferocious beasts, and… heavy, rhythmic footsteps! Not just one bronze corpse puppet, but many!

“Go that way!” Master Shi pointed to the other side of the stone chamber, where there was a narrow, inconspicuous opening, previously hidden by a wooden frame, barely wide enough for one person to bend over. A faint breeze was now blowing out, carrying the scent of fresh air—it seemed to be a ventilation shaft or an emergency exit.

Without hesitation, Ying Qi rushed towards the opening, ducking down and slipping inside. Lin Chen followed closely behind, his movements somewhat slowed by the inconvenience of his right arm. Doctor Chen and Master Shi also quickly followed.

Just as Lin Chen's body was halfway into the hole, the bronze door behind him shattered with a deafening roar! A scorching wave of air, mixed with rubble, swept in! Several tall, ferocious bronze figures, wreathed in eerie green flames, roared as they charged into the stone chamber—more bronze corpse puppets! Behind them, from the crack, a gigantic claw covered in dark gold scales and bound by thick chains emerged again, slamming down with an aura of annihilation towards the center of the chamber!

"Quick!" Shadow Seven urged anxiously from inside.

Lin Chen used all his strength to struggle, tumbling into the hole. Almost simultaneously, a deafening crash, the roars of the corpse puppets, and the muffled howls of some terrifying being erupted behind him! The entire stone chamber, along with the passageway, began to completely collapse and crumble under that world-destroying force!

The cave was narrow, steep, and slippery, with loose rocks constantly falling. The group could only climb upwards using both hands and feet. Behind them, the deafening roar of collapse, the scorching heat, and the chilling, menacing aura clung relentlessly like a leech.

"Cough cough... This way! There's light!" Shadow Seven, leading the charge, suddenly shouted.

The group's spirits lifted, and they struggled upwards. After climbing about ten more zhang (approximately 33 meters), a small exit appeared ahead, revealing lush vegetation and sunlight! Fresh air rushed in, carrying the clean scent of rain-soaked earth.

Shadow Seven rushed out first, followed by Lin Chen, Doctor Chen, and Master Shi. When the last of them left the cave and looked back, they saw smoke and dust billowing from its depths, with loose rocks constantly tumbling down, quickly burying most of the entrance. The ground beneath their feet was still trembling slightly, and a muffled rumbling sound came from afar, as if something beneath the earth was struggling and wreaking havoc.

They were now in an unfamiliar, damp primeval forest, surrounded by towering ancient trees and thick moss, unsure of their exact location within Qixia Mountain. But at least they had escaped—escaped that terrifying underground tomb.

“This place is dangerous. Who knows if that ferocious beast will emerge? We must leave Qixia Mountain immediately!” Ying Qi said, catching her breath.

Lin Chen leaned against a large tree, his face pale. The wound on his right arm seemed to have reopened, sending shivers of pain through him. The desperate escape had been incredibly exhausting. But his heart was filled with the relief of surviving a close call, and… the warm jade bottle clutched tightly in his hand.

The Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill was in his hands. Although the process had been extremely dangerous, nearly costing him his life, it had ultimately been worth it.

“Leave here first, find a safe place. Young Master, you need to heal immediately,” Doctor Chen said, also panting. He looked quite disheveled, but his eyes remained calm.

Shadow Nine, unseen by anyone, had appeared on a nearby tree, like a ghost. He nodded slightly to Shadow Seven, indicating that the area was temporarily safe.

The group didn't linger. Led by Shadow Seven and Shadow Nine, they determined their direction (roughly towards Jinling City) and quickly left the dangerous area, heading out of the forest.

Along the way, no one spoke; only heavy breathing and hurried footsteps could be heard. Everyone was still shaken, the terrifying experience underground still fresh in their minds.

As Lin Chen hurried along, he felt the warmth emanating from the jade bottle in his arms, but his mind drifted to the image of the slender, waiting figure in the Xiaoxiang Pavilion.

"Yu'er, I've obtained the life-saving elixir. I've also... survived. Once I recover, I'll come back to find you."

He looked up at the gradually clearing sky peeking through the gaps in the forest, his gaze resolute.


Chapter 40: Taking Pills to Heal Injuries and Returning to Jinling

On the edge of Qixia Mountain, deep within a secluded mountain stream, the babbling water washed over smooth pebbles, producing a pleasant sound. Steep cliffs and dense vines on either side completely blocked the view. The bottom of the stream was relatively flat, covered with a thick layer of fallen leaves, soft and silent underfoot. This place was over twenty li from the site of the collapsed underground tomb, and its complex terrain made it difficult to be tracked, making it an ideal spot for a temporary rest and healing.

Shadow Seven and Shadow Nine set up makeshift warning traps around the perimeter and took turns on guard duty. Doctor Chen cleared a clean spot by the stream, spread out an oilcloth, and gestured for Lin Chen to sit down. Master Shi took out some dry rations and water and distributed them to everyone. After a night of desperate fleeing, everyone was exhausted, bearing scrapes and bruises, their clothes tattered, but their eyes held a sense of relief at surviving a close call.

Lin Chen leaned against the cold rock wall, the stinging pain in his right arm growing stronger, and a tightness in his chest—signs of disordered internal energy and damaged meridians. His ordeal underground, while yielding the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill," had also worsened his already flaring injuries. He took out the jade bottle containing the pill from his robes; it felt warm to the touch, as if it were gently pulsating with life.

"Young Master, there's no time to lose. While this place is still relatively safe, you must take the pill as soon as possible and circulate your internal energy to neutralize its effects." Doctor Chen's expression was solemn. "The 'Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill' possesses immense power, capable of bringing the dead back to life and cleansing the marrow and molting the body. Young Master, your injuries are still severe, and your meridians are fragile, so you need to be guided with extra care. I will be there to protect you, so I can intervene promptly if anything goes wrong. Lord Shadow Seven, Lord Shadow Nine, I entrust the outer perimeter to you."

Shadow Seven nodded: "Don't worry. This place is secluded; we take turns guarding it, not even a bird can fly in. Young Master, please focus on healing."

Lin Chen no longer hesitated. He sat cross-legged, facing upwards, and first, following the methods of the *Heart-Cleansing Mantra*, completely calmed his mind, dispelling fatigue and distracting thoughts. Once his mind was clear and his breathing became long and steady, he uncorked the jade bottle.

Instantly, an indescribable, wondrous medicinal fragrance, a blend of the pure dew of a hundred flowers, the purple aura of morning, and the essence of a ten-thousand-year-old ginseng king, filled the air. Just one whiff was enough to invigorate his spirit, and even the hidden pain within his body seemed to lessen. Inside the bottle, the pale purple pill with nine natural cloud-like patterns lay quietly, radiating a warm, precious light.

Lin Chen poured the pill into his palm, gazed at it for a moment, and then, without hesitation, put it into his mouth.

The pill melted instantly upon entering his mouth, not transforming into a scorching torrent as he had imagined, but rather like ice melting and snow thawing, turning into a warm, mellow, yet irresistibly refreshing liquid that flowed down his throat. At first, he felt little sensation, but the moment the liquid entered his stomach, it exploded!

"Boom!"

It was as if a warm sun had ignited deep within his body! An endless, pure, and exquisite life essence, like a bursting dam, erupted from his dantian, instantly surging to every limb, every internal organ, and even every inch of skin and every hair! This power was gentle yet vast, brimming with vibrant life and the wonders of creation, forming a stark contrast to the injuries, blood stasis, stagnant poison, and even the subtle, hidden wounds left from his previous cultivation and battles.

"Ugh..." Lin Chen groaned, his face instantly turning crimson. Steam rose from his head, and his pores opened, forcibly expelling wisps of foul-smelling black impurities mixed with dark red stagnant blood from his body by the surging medicinal power! This was a marrow cleansing and body transformation!

At the same time, his severely damaged meridians, like a dried-up riverbed, began to heal, widen, and become more resilient and strong at a visible speed under the cleansing and nourishing power of this boundless life force! The thin internal energy in his dantian, like rain after a long drought, frantically devoured the pure medicinal power, rapidly growing stronger and more solid, its color gradually shifting from pale gold to a deeper, more profound radiance imbued with a purplish hue.

However, the impact of this powerful medicinal force also brought immense pain. The throbbing pain from the forced widening and reshaping of his meridians, the tingling and itching deep within his bone marrow, the burning sensation as his internal organs were cleansed by the life force, and the intense dizziness as his soul was nourished by the medicinal power, feeling as if it were being torn apart and rebuilt… all these sensations intertwined, making him feel as if he were in purgatory. Lin Chen gritted his teeth, veins bulging on his forehead, sweat instantly soaking his clothes, but he maintained a sliver of clarity brought by the *Qi-Controlling Technique*, forcibly guiding this violent yet pure power, slowly circulating it according to the *Qi-Controlling Technique*, striving to use every ounce of medicinal power to repair his injuries and solidify his foundation.

"Guard your mind! Guide the medicinal power back to your dantian and spread to your limbs! Do not resist, go with the flow!" Doctor Chen's voice, like a morning bell, rang in Lin Chen's ears, carrying a strange soothing power. At the same time, several golden needles silently pierced several major acupoints around Lin Chen's body, helping to channel the excessively surging medicinal power and protect his heart and sea of ​​consciousness.

Time passed slowly. The extraordinary phenomena surrounding Lin Chen became increasingly apparent. A faint purple light flowed beneath his skin, and white mist condensed above his head into three ethereal lotus blossoms (the Three Flowers Gathering at the Crown). A rich, refreshing fragrance emanated from him, dispelling much of the foul stench he had previously expelled. The vegetation around the stream seemed to be nourished by this pure life force, visibly becoming more lush and vibrant. Several nearby wildflowers even bloomed with delicate, seasonal blossoms.

Shadow Seven, Shadow Nine, and Master Shi watched from afar, their eyes filled with astonishment. The efficacy of this "Nine-Turn Rejuvenating Pill" was indeed miraculous.

He sat there for a full six hours, from midday sun to sunset, and then to the rising moon.

When the last wisp of the overly potent medicinal power was guided by Lin Chen's unwavering will into his dantian for complete refinement and absorption, he slowly opened his eyes.

His eyes shone brightly, like the deep night sky or the restrained stars, and in his every glance, a profound, serene, and natural Daoist aura flowed. His complexion was rosy and healthy, without a trace of illness. His right arm, which had been hanging in a sling across his chest, was now moving freely, and he could even feel a greater power within it than before. His skin was as smooth as jade, with a faint, radiant glow. Every movement he made carried a deep and weighty aura, yet also possessed the unique agility and grace of the "Qi Control Technique."

Peak of the Eighth Rank! No, he's even halfway to the Seventh Rank! His inner energy is powerful and pure, several times stronger than before his injury! His meridians are wide and resilient, his blood and qi as vigorous as a furnace. More importantly, his soul is clear and refined, his perception greatly enhanced, and his control over his inner energy and power has reached a completely new and subtle level. The hidden injuries and mental depletion left by forcibly activating the "Thunderclap Fist" have long since healed, and even benefited from it, his soul strength surpassing its former glory. The stubborn, lingering poison from the cold palm strike within his body has been completely eradicated, leaving no trace.

A complete transformation, a new beginning!

"Congratulations, young master, your injuries have fully healed and your cultivation has greatly improved!" Doctor Chen withdrew his golden needles, a gratified smile on his face. "The Nine-Turn Rejuvenating Pill truly lives up to its name. Young master's foundation was already solid, and this experience of breaking through and rebuilding has benefited you immensely.
