Now, young master's cultivation has reached the peak of the eighth rank, just one step away from the seventh rank. Moreover, the strength of your foundation and the purity of your inner energy far surpass those of ordinary peak eighth-rank martial artists. Given time, the seventh rank is within reach."

Shadow Seven and Shadow Nine stepped forward, their eyes filled with admiration. Shadow Seven cupped his hands and said, "Young Master, you are blessed with great fortune. Not only have you been cured of your chronic illness, but you have also gained this opportunity, greatly increasing your strength. You will be much more confident in your future endeavors."

Lin Chen stood up, stretched his limbs, and a series of crisp popping sounds emanated from within his body, as if boundless power was waiting to be released. He solemnly bowed to Doctor Chen, Shadow Seven, and the others, saying, "My escape from this great calamity and recovery from my injuries are entirely due to your great assistance. Lin Chen will remember this forever."

"You're too kind, young master. It's our duty." Everyone quickly returned the greeting.

"We've lingered here long enough; we must return to Jinling as soon as possible." Lin Chen gazed towards Jinling City, a flicker of longing and sharpness in his eyes. "The matter of the armory must be reported to Her Highness the Princess. Something has likely happened at the Jia family residence as well. Moreover..." He touched the warm, smooth magnolia-shaped jade pendant in his robes, and the empty bottle containing the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill" (the medicinal effects had been absorbed, but the bottle remained), a strong longing for home welling up within him. Yu'er must be anxiously waiting.

"Young master is absolutely right." Ying Qi nodded. "We'll set off tonight, taking a shortcut. We should arrive outside Jinling City before noon tomorrow. We still need to find a safe way to enter the city to avoid being noticed by those with ulterior motives."

After a brief reorganization, extinguishing the campfire and erasing all traces of their stay, the group, led by Ying Qi, set off swiftly towards Jinling City under the moonlight. Lin Chen, fully recovered from his injuries and with greatly improved cultivation, wielded his Lightness Step with unprecedented speed, his figure darting through the moonlit forest like a ghost, easily keeping pace with Ying Qi and Ying Jiu, much to their astonishment.

They traveled in silence, focusing solely on their journey. At dawn, they reached the outskirts of Qixia Mountain. After a short rest and some dry rations, they disguised themselves as hunters or herbalists heading into the city to sell their mountain goods, blending into the sparse crowd of pedestrians and heading towards the west gate of Jinling.

The city gate was more heavily guarded than usual, with many more soldiers scanning those entering with sharp eyes. However, Lin Chen and his companions were well-disguised, and with Ying Qi's secret intervention (using a clandestine channel provided by the princess), they entered the city without incident.

Back in the city, Shadow Seven and Shadow Nine quietly disappeared to arrange contact with the princess. Doctor Chen and Master Shi also had their own destinations. Lin Chen changed back into a simple blue robe, concealing his aura, and like an ordinary scholar, hired an inconspicuous carriage and returned to the abandoned old mansion in the western city, secretly controlled by Jia Zheng. There, he changed into more presentable clothes, rested briefly, and made sure he left no trace of anything brought from Qixia Mountain (such as fallen leaves or soil) before leisurely walking towards Ningrong Street.

When he stepped into the west gate of the Jia mansion again, the sun was already setting. The gatekeeper, both surprised and delighted to see him return, hurriedly went inside to announce his arrival. Soon after, Li Gui, Jia Zheng's trusted attendant, rushed out to greet him, his face beaming with relieved joy.

"Young Master Lin! You're finally back! Master has been so worried these past two days, but dared not utter a sound… You, your complexion…" Li Gui looked at Lin Chen's rosy, healthy, and energetic appearance, almost unable to believe his eyes. A few days ago, when the young master left, although he could walk, his face was pale, and his right arm was in a sling; anyone could see that his serious injury hadn't healed. How could he have returned like a completely different person after a few days of "rest"? Not only were his injuries completely healed, but he seemed to have undergone a complete transformation, his aura becoming even more profound and ethereal, making him almost unbearable to look at.

"Thank you for your concern, Uncle Li Gui. This time, I found a beautiful and spiritual place, and also happened to find a good doctor; my injuries are no longer a problem." Lin Chen smiled slightly, his tone calm. "Is Uncle at home? I'll go and pay my respects to him and let him know I'm safe."

"Yes, yes! Master is in his study; he's been quite troubled these past two days. Young Master, please come with me!" Li Gui quickly led the way.

In the study of the east wing of Rongxi Hall, Jia Zheng paced anxiously, his brow furrowed. Lin Chen had been in seclusion for several days, supposedly recuperating from his injuries, and there had been no news of him. Although he knew his nephew was a prudent man and must have made arrangements, the thought of Lin Chen's serious injuries and his involvement with the dangerous forces of Prince Zhongshun and the Black Tiger Gang filled him with worry. What troubled him even more were the strange signs that had been creeping into the household these past two days. The east courtyard was becoming increasingly quiet, the Xue and Shi families seemed to be interacting more frequently with the household, and rumors were quietly circulating among the servants that Lin Chen had "disappeared" or "might have met with some misfortune"...

Just as he was lost in thought, there was a gentle knock on the study door, and Li Gui's voice, tinged with suppressed excitement, rang out: "Master, Young Master Lin has returned! He's requesting an audience outside!"

"What? Please come in!" Jia Zheng turned abruptly, his face lighting up with delight.

The door opened, and Lin Chen stepped in, bowing respectfully to Jia Zheng: "Nephew Lin Chen greets Uncle. Thank you for your concern, Uncle. I'm back."

Jia Zheng rushed forward, supporting Lin Chen, and looked him up and down, his eyes gleaming with astonishment: "Brother Chen, you...you're really healed? This complexion...this spirit...could it be that you've truly encountered a deity?" He clearly sensed that his nephew was not only completely healed, but his entire demeanor and aura were entirely different from before his injury—calm as a mountain, profound as the sea—even making this seasoned court official, accustomed to weathering storms, feel a faint sense of pressure.

“Thanks to your great fortune, Uncle, I had a truly extraordinary encounter on this trip. I was fortunate enough to find a miraculous medicine and have fully recovered. My cultivation has even improved slightly,” Lin Chen said briefly, without mentioning the “Nine-Turn Rejuvenating Pill” or the dangers of the Xuanzhen Martial Armory.

“Good! Good! Good!” Jia Zheng exclaimed three times, patting Lin Chen’s shoulder firmly (the pat felt solid and strong), his face full of relief. “It’s good that you’re recovered! It’s good that you’re recovered! You don’t know how anxious I was these past few days. It’s good that you’re back, it’s good that you’re back!” He pulled Lin Chen down to sit and carefully inquired about the “healing” process. Lin Chen was prepared; he only said that he went to a secluded mountain villa left by his parents, where the environment was tranquil, and he happened to meet a wandering hermit physician who bestowed upon him a miraculous elixir, thus recovering. The details were vague, but they made perfect sense.

Jia Zheng had no suspicions, only marveling at Lin Chen's good fortune. Then, his expression turned serious, and he said in a low voice, "Chen-ge'er, your return is just in time. These past few days, things have been a bit unsettled in the manor. It seems the news of your 'seclusion' hasn't escaped certain people. In the East Courtyard, the Xue and Shi families have been making some unusual moves. There are also some unpleasant rumors circulating among the servants. I'm worried that they see you seriously injured and think they can take advantage of the situation. Now that you've recovered and returned, it's a perfect opportunity to deter such villains!"

Lin Chen's gaze turned slightly cold: "Nephew understands. I'm sorry to trouble you, Uncle. How is Tan Chun doing?"

"Tan Chun is quite capable, managing the inner quarters impeccably, and suppressing much of the gossip. However, she's still young, and the pressure is considerable. With you back, she'll have someone to rely on," Jia Zheng sighed. "And… at Xiaoxiang Pavilion, Daiyu has been ill with worry since you left, and a few days ago she caught a cold and fell ill. Although she's not seriously ill, her health is declining. When you have time, go see her and put her mind at ease."

"Daiyu is ill?" Lin Chen's heart tightened. That bond of "mutual soul connection" seemed to stir with a clear, frail, and longing emotion. He immediately said, "Nephew will go see Sister Lin right away."

"Go, go. Comfort her well. You've just returned; rest well. We can discuss other matters tomorrow," Jia Zheng waved his hand understandingly.

Lin Chen excused himself and quickened his pace unconsciously, heading towards the Grand View Garden. The setting sun cast a long shadow of him.

The air inside Xiaoxiang Pavilion was filled with the fragrance of medicine.

Daiyu leaned against the soft couch by the window, covered with a brocade quilt, a book in her hand, her gaze fixed blankly on the darkening sky outside. She wore a moon-white nightgown over a light blue vest, which made her appear even more frail. Her face was pale, her lips bloodless; only her clear, autumn-water-like eyes remained, yet were filled with an inescapable melancholy and worry. Occasionally, she coughed softly, her brows furrowing in pain.

Zijuan was warming the medicine on a small stove. Seeing Daiyu lost in thought again, she sighed inwardly and softly advised, "Miss, the medicine is almost ready. Please drink it while it's hot. Young Master Lin is a lucky man; he will surely return safely. You must take care of yourself. Don't wait until he returns and sees you like this; he'll be heartbroken."

Daiyu snapped out of her reverie and softly hummed in agreement, but her spirits were low. How could she not be worried? The bond of their "mutual soul connection" had been stirring within her these past few days, sometimes a faint, comforting feeling, sometimes a sharp, painful warning, causing her sleepless nights and recurring nightmares. The storm the other day had left her standing by the window, lost in thought, and she had caught a cold; though not serious, it had made her look even more haggard.

Just as Zijuan poured the prepared medicine into a bowl and placed it before Daiyu, Xueyan's voice, trembling with both surprise and joy, suddenly rang out from outside the door:

"Miss! Miss! Young Master Lin is back! Young Master Lin has come to see you!"

"Clang!"

The scroll slipped from Daiyu's hands and fell to the ground. She jerked her head up, looking towards the doorway. Her pale face drained of all color in an instant, only to be replaced by an excited, unnatural flush. She struggled to sit up, but weakness and excitement made her dizzy.

The curtain was abruptly lifted. A tall, familiar figure, bathed in the cool air and the afterglow of the setting sun, strode in.

It was Lin Chen.

He stood in the doorway, his gaze immediately locking onto the thin, pale girl on the bed, who seemed fragile and easily broken. Their eyes met, and time seemed to stand still.

Daiyu gazed at him, mesmerized, at his healthy, rosy face, his calm, bright eyes, his upright, pine-like posture… He was back! He really was back! And, he seemed… completely healed? No longer the pale, weak figure he had left behind, but as if reborn, radiant and full of life, even more… breathtaking than before his injury.

Tears, without warning, surged forth. Not of sorrow, but of overwhelming joy, lingering fear, grievance, and the immense happiness of something lost and found. She opened her mouth, wanting to call out to him, but choked back sobs, unable to utter a sound, only letting the tears blur her vision and flow freely.

Lin Chen's heart ached as he saw Daiyu's pale, frail, and tear-streaked face, as if gripped by an invisible hand. He strode forward, knelt beside the bed, and gently took her cold, trembling hands in his.

"Yu'er, I'm back." He gazed at her, his voice low and gentle, filled with unmistakable tenderness and guilt. "I'm sorry for making you worry."

Feeling the real, warm warmth of his palms and hearing his familiar, comforting voice, Daiyu finally burst into tears, no longer holding back, like a river breaking its banks. She threw herself into Lin Chen's arms, tightly hugging his neck, burying her face in his shoulder, and sobbing uncontrollably, as if crying out all the worry, fear, longing, and pain of the past few days.

“Brother Lin… Waaah… You’re finally back… I was so scared… I was so scared you wouldn’t come back… Waaah…” She cried her heart out, her whole body trembling, her words incoherent.

Lin Chen felt a pang of pain and sorrow in his heart. He tightened his arms, holding her petite and slender body, which trembled from crying, tightly in his embrace. He gently stroked her back and whispered repeatedly in her ear, "It's my fault, it's my fault... I'm back, it's alright, Yu'er, it's alright... I'll never let you worry like this again..."

He could feel how shockingly thin the person in his arms was; he could feel the bones through her clothes. Her cries, weak and hoarse from illness, were filled with the immense emotional impact of being reunited after loss. The bond of their "soulmates" was now crystal clear, conveying her ecstasy, her grievances, her lingering fear, and her unconditional dependence and longing for him. At the same time, he, in turn, gently returned her joy, relief, and deep tenderness at his own recovery and safe return through this bond.

Zijuan and Xueyan had already turned away, quietly wiping away tears, their faces filled with genuine joy. They quietly withdrew, leaving the space to the two lovers, reunited after their ordeal and deeply in love.

After crying for an unknown amount of time, Daiyu's sobs gradually turned into low, choked sobs. She seemed to have exhausted all her strength, leaning softly against Lin Chen's chest, still clutching his clothes tightly, refusing to let go, as if letting go would cause him to disappear again.

Lin Chen looked down at the girl in his arms, her eyes red and swollen from crying, the tear stains still wet on her pale cheeks, his heart filled with an indescribable tenderness and sense of responsibility. He raised his hand and, with his fingertips, very gently, wiped away her tears, one by one.

"Yu'er, look, I'm all better. Better than before." He softened his voice, as if coaxing his most precious treasure, "Your jade pendant has always protected me." He pointed to the magnolia flower jade pendant he wore close to his chest.

Daiyu, still sobbing, lifted her tear-filled eyes and looked at him intently. It was true; his complexion was rosy, his eyes bright and clear, his breathing steady and long, and his right arm moved freely… He was truly healed, and it seemed… even better. The heavy weight that had been hanging over her heart finally lifted. A wave of exhaustion and relief washed over her, almost making her drift off to sleep, yet her heart was filled with reluctance to leave, wanting only to keep looking at him, to feel his presence.

“Brother Lin… are you really… alright? You’re not lying to me?” Her voice was hoarse, thick with a nasal tone, as she asked cautiously, as if still in a dream.

“Really, I’m not lying to you.” Lin Chen looked earnestly into her eyes and nodded solemnly. “I promise you, I will never be injured so badly again. I will be alright, and I will always be with you.”

Looking into his eyes, filled with genuine sincerity and tenderness, Daiyu finally believed this wasn't a dream. A faint, yet incredibly real, tearful smile slowly bloomed on her pale face, like a pear blossom opening after the rain, fragile and beautiful.

"Mmm," she softly responded, resting her head back on his strong shoulder, closing her eyes, savoring this hard-won peace and warmth. The worries and exhaustion of the past few days seemed to vanish completely in this moment, a deep drowsiness washing over her.

"Sleep, I'll stay here with you," Lin Chen said gently, adjusting his position to make her more comfortable.

Daiyu didn't speak again, only softly humming in agreement. Her breathing gradually became even and long, and she drifted off to sleep in his arms. Only her hands, still clutching his clothes, remained clenched.

Lin Chen held her quietly, looking at her still slightly furrowed brows and pale cheeks in her sleep, his heart filled with tenderness. He carefully channeled a wisp of warm, nourishing internal energy infused with the power of the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenating Pill," slowly guiding it into her body through their clasped hands, dispelling the lingering cold and nourishing her weakened heart and body.

Outside the window, dusk settled, and the lanterns were just beginning to glow. Inside the Xiaoxiang Pavilion, a tranquil and warm atmosphere prevailed. Only the occasional crackling of the candlelight and the steady, intertwined breathing of the two figures could be heard.

After some time, Zijuan quietly entered, carrying a bowl of warm medicine and a bowl of light porridge. Seeing the young lady sleeping soundly in Lin Gongzi's arms, her face radiating an unprecedented peace, she felt a mixture of emotion and joy. She gently placed the medicine and porridge on a low table beside her and mouthed to Lin Chen, "Young Master, the medicine and porridge."

Lin Chen nodded slightly, indicating he understood. He would wake Daiyu to take her medicine once she was soundly asleep.

He looked down at the peaceful sleeping face of the girl in his arms, his fingertips gently brushing the slightly damp strands of hair on her cheek, his gaze tender yet resolute.

"Yu'er, from this day forward, I will never let you shed tears because of me, never let you fall ill because of me. I will use all my strength to protect you for a lifetime of peace and happiness.

Those who have hurt us, those demons lurking in the shadows… once I have settled my affairs, that will be the time for our reckoning."


Chapter 41 Changes Occur in the Jia Family, Zhaoyang Sends a Message

Daiyu slept soundly in Lin Chen's arms, her breathing even, her brow relaxed—a rare peaceful sleep after days of worry. Lin Chen held her quietly until late at night, then carefully laid her back on the soft couch and covered her with a brocade quilt. She slept so deeply that she didn't even wake when he got up to leave, only unconsciously clutching a corner of his robe. She only released her grip when he gently placed the warm, smooth magnolia-shaped jade pendant beside her pillow, a faint smile seemingly lingering on her lips.

Lin Chen leaned down and gently kissed her smooth forehead, then straightened up and whispered to Zijuan, who was guarding the door, "Take good care of the young lady and make sure she takes her medicine on time. If anything happens, come to Tingzhu Pavilion immediately."

"Yes, young master, rest assured." Zijuan, seeing Lin Chen unharmed and even more refined than before, was both delighted and moved, and quickly agreed.

Leaving Xiaoxiang Pavilion, the night air was chilly. Lin Chen walked back to Tingzhu Pavilion, his steps steady, but his gaze had lost its previous gentleness, becoming calm and sharp, like a drawn sword, scanning the tranquil yet turbulent Jia Mansion in the night.

His injuries had healed, and his cultivation had greatly improved, but the trouble was far from over; in fact, it might have become even more intractable because of his safe return. Jia She and Prince Zhongshun would certainly not let the matter rest. The Black Tiger Gang had suffered heavy losses during their trip to Qixia Mountain, and might even have alerted some terrifying entity. This entity would likely hold him and Princess Zhaoyang accountable. Furthermore, the secret of the Xuanzhen Armory, the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill," was a treasure coveted by any force. He had to seize the initiative as soon as possible, accumulating enough power to clear away obstacles before the storm fully arrived.

Back at Tingzhu Pavilion, the courtyard was silent, but Lin Chen could keenly sense that the auras of the guards, both overt and covert, were more concentrated and vigilant than before. Clearly, Jia Zheng had strengthened his control over the place during his absence. He returned quietly to his meditation room without alerting anyone.

Everything in the quiet room was as before, except for a thin layer of dust. He brushed the dust off the futon, sat down cross-legged, and did not immediately begin to cultivate. Instead, he began to sort out the gains and losses of his trip to Qixia Mountain, as well as the matters that needed to be dealt with next.

The first priority is to report back to Princess Zhaoyang, detailing the situation at the armory and discussing subsequent countermeasures. The discovery of the Xuanzhen Armory, especially the existence of that terrifying and dangerous object, and the possible deeper secrets (the Purple Gold Jade Box), must be made known to the princess. Secondly, there's the matter of the Jia family internally. Jia She suffered a great loss, and seeing him "recover" and return, he's bound to make a move. Although Tan Chun can stabilize the inner quarters, she still needs to be present to support the family in the face of a possible joint attack from Jia She, the Xue family, and the Shi family. Thirdly, there's the consolidation and improvement of one's own strength. The effects of the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill" haven't been fully absorbed; although one's cultivation has reached the peak of the eighth rank, it needs to be stabilized, and mastery of the "Thunderclap Fist" needs to be deepened. Most importantly, an opportunity must be found to attempt to break through the seventh rank bottleneck! Once one breaks through to the seventh rank and masters the ability to control Qi, combat power will undergo a qualitative change, truly giving one the confidence to protect oneself and even counterattack in the ensuing chaos.

"It seems I need to contact the princess as soon as possible, and also see what Jia She is up to." Lin Chen's mind raced. Just as he was about to get up, his ears twitched slightly, and his gaze drifted towards the back window of the quiet room.

A faint, almost imperceptible sound of wind breaking came from outside the window. Immediately afterward, a plain white strip of paper, folded into a unique square shape with a dark dragon pattern printed on its edges, seemed to be carried by an invisible hand, slowly drifting in through the window crack and gently landing on the futon in front of him.

Is it Shadow Seven, or Shadow Nine? The princess's message has arrived, and so urgently; it seems the situation outside is more tense than anticipated.

Lin Chen unfolded the note. It bore Princess Zhaoyang's elegant yet resolute handwriting, but more concise than before, even carrying a hint of solemnity:

"The events at Qixia Mountain are known. The Black Tiger Gang has suffered heavy losses, and Zhongshun is furious. The Xue and Shi families have been making frequent unusual moves, suggesting they have backup plans. The Jia family may face a major upheaval soon. Jia She may use 'filial piety' and 'family law' as leverage to launch an attack, targeting Tanchun and you directly. The Jia matriarch's birthday in three days may be the starting point. I urge you to prepare early, observe the situation calmly, and if necessary, launch a swift counterattack without hesitation. The matter of Xuanzhen will be discussed later. Written by Lingxi."

Prince Zhongshun is furious! The Xue and Shi families are making moves! A major upheaval is imminent in the Jia family! The upcoming birthday celebration for Grandmother Jia in three days may be the catalyst!

The note, though short, is packed with information and conveys a sense of impending crisis. The princess has clearly obtained some crucial intelligence, possibly even knowing that Prince Zhongshun is behind the Black Tiger Gang and what happened to them at Qixia Mountain. Her deliberate emphasis on "no need to hesitate" authorizes him to act decisively, even with force, but it also suggests that the situation may have reached a point where swift action is necessary.

Was Jia She going to use "filial piety" and "family law" as a pretext for attacking them? Was his target still Tan Chun and himself? A cold glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. Yes, Jia She was the eldest son, inheriting the title, and naturally held an advantage in terms of "filial piety" and "propriety." If he used accusations of "disrespectful juniors," "unauthorized management," "colluding with outsiders," and "tarnishing the family reputation" to attack them, with the support of "relatives" like the Xue and Shi families, Jia Zheng and Tan Chun would indeed be put on the defensive. Especially Tan Chun, as an illegitimate daughter who managed the household affairs, was already easily criticized. Grandmother Jia's birthday celebration, with its many guests, was the perfect opportunity to launch an attack. Once the accusations were confirmed in front of everyone, Tan Chun's reforms could be completely ruined, and she might even be stripped of her management power. Lin Chen, as an "outsider" and the "culprit," would inevitably be implicated, facing anything from expulsion to...

"What a brilliant strategy of 'cutting off one's own supply lines' and 'using someone else to do one's dirty work'," Lin Chen sneered. This seemed to be the work of Jia She and that Prince Zhongshun—insidious yet effective. It appeared that seeing his "recovered" return, conventional assassination methods were ineffective, so they changed tactics, using "rules" and "power" to intimidate him.

"Since you want to play the 'rules,' then I'll play along." Lin Chen held the note close to the lamplight, watching it turn to ashes, his eyes deep and unfathomable. "Perfect, let me see just how many demons and monsters, humans and ghosts, are lurking in this Jia family."

Without further delay, he immediately began to act. First, he summoned a special whistle (not a sound, but a vibration of a specific frequency) left behind by Ying Qi for emergency communication and blew it from an inconspicuous crack in the brickwork of the quiet room. Moments later, a dark figure appeared silently in the corner of the room, as if blending into the wall—it was Ying Jiu.

“What are your orders, Young Master?” Ying Jiu asked in a low voice.

“In three days, it will be Grandmother Jia’s birthday. There may be unforeseen circumstances. I need you to closely monitor the actions of Jia She, the Xue family (especially Xue Pan), and the Shi family from the East Courtyard for the next few days, and listen to their communications with the outside world, especially with Prince Zhongshun’s side. Report any unusual activity to me immediately. At the same time, inform Ying Qi to strengthen the defenses of the princess’s strongholds in the city, especially those connected to the Jia family, to prevent them from resorting to desperate measures such as murder to silence witnesses or framing someone.” Lin Chen ordered in a deep voice.

“Yes.” Ying Jiu responded, his figure flickering and disappearing again, as if he had never appeared.

Then, Lin Chen left Tingzhu Pavilion and, under the cover of night, quietly went to Qiushuang Studio. Tan Chun was indeed still awake; the study was brightly lit, and she was checking a thick stack of account books with Shishu and Cuimo. Her face showed fatigue, but her eyes remained resolute. Upon seeing Lin Chen's late-night visit, she was first startled, then her expression brightened with delight.

"Brother Lin! You're back? Are you feeling much better?" Tan Chun put down the account book and came forward to greet him, looking him over with concern.

"I'm much better now, thank you for worrying, Third Sister." Lin Chen nodded, gesturing for her to dismiss the others. Shi Shu and Cui Mo understood and quietly withdrew, standing guard outside the door.

"Third Sister, I'll keep it brief." Lin Chen lowered his voice and briefly told Tan Chun the contents of the princess's message, as well as his own judgment. "In three days it will be the Old Madam's birthday. Jia She will most likely join forces with the Xue and Shi families to launch an attack, targeting you and me, especially your handling of household affairs. They will most likely accuse you of 'disrespecting elders,' 'unauthorized changes to ancestral rules,' 'improper personnel management,' or even 'colluding with outsiders and plotting rebellion.' Are you prepared?"

Upon hearing this, Tan Chun's expression instantly turned solemn, but there was no fear in her eyes; instead, a cold glint flashed within them: "As expected, your wicked intentions are still alive! Brother Lin, rest assured, since I took over, the accounts, personnel, and expenditures have all been clearly traceable and documented. My actions have all been aimed at eliminating long-standing abuses and saving expenses; I have no personal motives whatsoever, nor have I violated any ancestral rules. As for 'colluding with outsiders'... Hmph, if you want to condemn someone, you can always find a pretext! If they have evidence, let them present it! If they have no evidence, don't even think about slandering someone's innocence out of thin air!"

She paused, her tone becoming even more resolute: "Besides, more than half of the stewards and servants in the manor have now been replaced with reliable people, or those I promoted. Out of gratitude for the reforms, they might not be easily manipulated. As for the Old Madam… although her attitude is ambiguous, it concerns the Jia family's reputation and the stability of the inner quarters, so she might not let them run rampant. However…" A hint of worry flashed in her eyes, "If they use elders like Aunt Xue and the Old Madam Shi to exert pressure, or… if they publicly challenge us at the birthday banquet, it will be embarrassing in front of everyone. Especially for you, Brother Lin…"

"You don't need to worry about me," Lin Chen said calmly. "If they dare to implicate me, I'll handle it myself. But you need to be fully prepared. Make sure your account books, tallies, records of key personnel appointments and dismissals, and the causes, consequences, pros and cons of all the measures you've implemented are clearly organized. If necessary, you can present your case publicly. As for the old lady, you can find an opportunity to visit her privately before her birthday, explain the pros and cons, not to ask for her support, but at least to prevent her from being misled by one side. In addition, you can secretly contact your relatives and retainers who are on good terms with you or who are dissatisfied with Jia She, so that you can have someone to support you when the time comes."

“I understand.” Tan Chun nodded, her eyes gleaming with wisdom. “I will begin preparations tomorrow. The accounts are ready, and the personnel appointments and dismissals are also recorded. As for the old lady… I will find an excuse to report to her about the expenses I have recently saved and the several major cases of embezzlement that have been uncovered, so that she knows I am not acting recklessly. As for the clansmen and their retainers… Sister-in-law Zhu (Li Wan) has always been indifferent to worldly affairs, but she is fair and just, so she might speak up for me. There is also Lan Ge’er’s tutor, that old gentleman Jia Dairu. Although he is pedantic, he values ​​rules and regulations. If the evidence is conclusive, he might say a few words of justice.”

"Very good." Lin Chen looked at Tan Chun approvingly. This third young lady's resourcefulness and resilience were indeed far beyond ordinary people. "For the next three days, act as usual. Do not reveal any weaknesses, and do not argue with anyone. Observe the situation calmly and use stillness to control movement. I will be there at the crucial moment."

"Yes!" Tan Chun nodded emphatically. With Lin Chen's words, the last trace of unease in her heart dissipated. She knew that this Brother Lin was not only highly skilled in martial arts, but also extremely shrewd in strategy. With him by her side, she had a pillar of support.

Leaving Qiushuangzhai, Lin Chen didn't return to Tingzhuxuan, but instead detoured to the vicinity of Rongqing Hall. He didn't approach, only gazing at the courtyard, still brightly lit but seemingly shrouded in the somber atmosphere of old age. Grandmother Jia's thoughts were unfathomable, but with the birthday banquet approaching, her attitude as the birthday celebrant and the most senior member of the Jia family was crucial. Perhaps he should also find an opportunity to "pay his respects"?

As he pondered this, he suddenly saw a familiar figure sneak out from the side gate of Rongqing Hall, glancing left and right before hurrying towards the east courtyard. By the light of the lanterns under the eaves, Lin Chen clearly saw that it was Xing'er, Jia She's personal attendant! Why was he going to the east courtyard so late at night? To deliver a message? Or was he passing on some news?

Lin Chen's eyes narrowed, and he silently followed. His cultivation had greatly improved; his light and agile steps, combined with the "qi control" characteristic of the *Qi Control Technique*, made his movements almost imperceptible. He trailed Xing'er far behind, unnoticed.

Xing'er jogged to Jia She's study in the east courtyard, gently knocked on the door, whispered something, and then slipped inside. The study was brightly lit, and the faint sounds of conversation suggested that Jia She wasn't alone.

Lin Chen crouched in the shadows of the eaves behind the study, listening intently. His hearing and eyesight were now far superior to before; even through the window, he could make out the gist of the conversation.

"...Although the old lady didn't say it explicitly, judging from her tone, she was somewhat displeased with the Third Miss's recent actions. Especially today, when the Old Madam of the Shi family sent someone to deliver birthday gifts, mentioning that there had been 'significant changes' in the household recently, 'which is probably not a long-term solution for managing a household.' The old lady frowned upon hearing this." It was Xing'er's voice.

"Hmph, that old hag values ​​her reputation above all else and is terrified of gossip. The old matriarch's words hit her right where it hurts," Jia She's cold voice rang out. "However, that's not enough. What will the Xue family say? That brat Xue Pan can't afford to mess things up this time!"

Another, slightly slick and obsequious voice rang out (it seemed to be a courtier): "Don't worry, Master She, everything's been arranged with Master Xue. His newly acquainted 'friends from the martial arts world' have guaranteed that on the day of the birthday banquet, they'll find a pretext to humiliate that brat Lin Chen in public, preferably... to incur public wrath! Then we'll seize the opportunity to attack him, accusing him of ' associating with scoundrels, harboring ulterior motives, and corrupting the household's atmosphere,' and solidifying the accusation that the Third Miss 'misjudged people and let a wolf into the house'! With the support of the Xue and Shi families, even if the old lady wants to protect him, she won't be able to!"

"Good!" Jia She seemed to slam his hand on the table. "That's it! Remember, be clean, don't leave any evidence. Are the people that Xue Pan found reliable?"

"Absolutely reliable! They're all well-known 'tough guys' in the underworld, they never fail when they get paid. And... I heard one of them has some connections with the Prince Zhongshun's mansion, he's secretly supporting him..." The waiter lowered his voice.

"Watch your words!" Jia She whispered, but there was little blame in his tone, only a hint of smugness. "If this succeeds, not only will we remove a thorn in our side, but we'll also vent the Prince's anger. The Prince will naturally benefit from it. The Xue and Shi families will also get a share. As for the old lady... hmph, once the facts are laid out before her, even if she's unhappy, she'll have to accept it! In the end, the Jia family still belongs to me, Jia She!"

"Master She is wise!"

A low murmur of flattery and laughter echoed from the study.

On the eaves, Lin Chen's eyes were icy, but a chilling smile played on his lips. Just as he suspected. He'd colluded with Xue Pan, inviting "friends from the martial world" (likely remnants of the Black Tiger Gang, or other skilled fighters sent by Prince Zhongshun) to plot against him at the birthday banquet, and incidentally, to bring down Tan Chun. A well-thought-out scheme.

"Trying to embarrass me at the birthday banquet? To incur the wrath of everyone?" Lin Chen sneered inwardly. "Let's see who embarrasses whom, and who incurs the wrath of everyone."

He didn't alert them, quietly retreating back to Tingzhu Pavilion. Now that he knew their plan, he could calmly make his own arrangements, turn the tables on them, and even… launch a counterattack.

Back in his quiet room, he sat cross-legged and began to plan carefully. He had to attend the birthday banquet in three days, and he had to "perform well." Their "pretext" would be nothing more than creating conflict, framing him for theft, or causing him to misbehave while drunk. He needed to take precautions in advance and prepare evidence for a counterattack. He also needed to remind Tan Chun to pay close attention to the accounts and storerooms during the banquet, to prevent anyone from tampering with them and framing him.

“However, before that, there’s one more thing to do.” Lin Chen’s gaze fell on the deep night outside the window, a resolute glint in his eyes. “We need to keep that young master Xue and his ‘friends in the martial world’ in check for a few days.”

He took out the whistle he used to contact Ying Jiu again and blew it at a specific rhythm. A moment later, Ying Jiu’s figure reappeared.

"Young Master?"

"There's something I need you to do immediately," Lin Chen instructed in a low voice. "Find out where Xue Pan has been hanging out lately, and which so-called 'friends' he's been associating with. The key is whether they've been up to anything recently, especially regarding the Jia family's birthday banquet in three days. If you can get evidence of their conspiracy, or... find their hideout, that would be best. Remember, just investigate; don't alert them. If you have the chance, create some minor trouble for them, so they'll be 'too busy' before the banquet."

"Yes," Ying Jiu replied, disappearing again. For him, investigating the movements of a few spoiled brats and江湖混混 (jianghu hustlers) and creating some harmless "accidents" was not difficult.

Having arranged all this, Lin Chen finally breathed a sigh of relief. He sat cross-legged again and began to regulate his breathing. Although his injuries had healed and his cultivation had greatly improved, some of the medicinal power of the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill" remained dormant within his body, requiring slow refinement and absorption to transform it into his foundation. He circulated the *Qi Control Technique* and the *Heart Purification Technique*, guiding the vast and pure internal energy within his body, allowing it to flow slowly, constantly cleansing, widening, and refining his meridians and dantian, feeling the increasingly clear barrier leading to the seventh-grade bottleneck.

"Seventh grade..." Lin Chen murmured to himself. Once he broke through, his strength would undergo a dramatic transformation, allowing him to truly be fearless against the schemes and plots of people like Jia She and Prince Zhongshun, and to better protect those he wanted to protect. However, this bottleneck was exceptionally strong and not easily broken. Perhaps it required a thoroughly enjoyable battle, or a profound opportunity for enlightenment.

"No rush, let's get through the birthday banquet first." He suppressed his desire for a higher realm and focused his mind on cultivation.

Nothing was said that night.

The next day, Lin Chen first went to Jia Zheng to formally pay his respects, explaining that his injuries had healed and thanking his uncle for his concern. Jia Zheng, seeing that he was in good spirits and his breathing was steady, was overjoyed. He offered a few more words of encouragement and then let him go, not inquiring further about the details of his "healing," clearly showing his trust in him.

Afterwards, Lin Chen went to Rongqing Hall, claiming to be "returning from an outing to pay his respects to the matriarch," and requested an audience with Grandmother Jia. Grandmother Jia did not avoid him, but her attitude was somewhat distant than before, and her words were full of probing questions, asking him "Was everything alright while you were out?", "Did you encounter any difficulties?", and "What are your thoughts on recent events in the household?" etc. Lin Chen answered each question calmly, respectfully, and frankly, only saying that his recovery was going smoothly and that he was not very familiar with household affairs, only wishing Grandmother good health and harmony in the household. Grandmother Jia, seeing that he had answered flawlessly and could not find fault, had no choice but to give him a cup of tea and let him leave.

Emerging from Rongqing Hall, Lin Chen understood. Grandmother Jia did indeed harbor suspicions about him and Tan Chun, or at least disapproved of their "troublemaking," disrupting the facade of peace in the manor. At the birthday banquet, she probably wouldn't easily side with him. However, as long as he didn't openly favor Jia She, there was still room for maneuver.

For the next two days, the Jia manor appeared outwardly bustling and festive, preparing for the old lady's birthday. Servants swept the courtyards, hung lanterns and decorations, purchased birthday gifts, and prepared the banquet, working tirelessly. Each branch of the household was also putting in their best effort, preparing to display filial piety at the banquet, or perhaps seize the opportunity to socialize.

However, beneath this seemingly lively and peaceful surface, undercurrents were surging.

In the east courtyard, Jia She rarely left his residence, but the number of unfamiliar faces visiting his study had increased. Xue Pan was almost nowhere to be seen, reportedly "making friends and having fun outside." At the Shi family's residence, head maids would occasionally come over to chat with Lady Wang and Lady Xing.

Tan Chun, meanwhile, methodically handled the household affairs, secretly compiling and copying key account books, personnel records, and evidence from several verified but not yet publicized embezzlement cases, hiding them in a concealed place. She then found an opportunity to report several "good things" to Grandmother Jia privately—such as the amount of silver saved this month due to budget adjustments, and the loss recovered for the household by dismissing several embezzling stewards. She didn't boast of her achievements, only stating the facts. Grandmother Jia's expression softened slightly upon hearing this, but she didn't say much.

News also came from Ying Jiu's side. Xue Pan was indeed associating with a group of unidentified martial artists, frequently appearing at a gambling den/brothel called "Happy Forest" in the west of the city. The group consisted of about seven or eight people, led by a one-eyed man with a fierce aura, possessing at least a late-stage eighth-rank cultivation level. The others were mostly also at the eighth rank. They seemed to be plotting something, but when Ying Jiu approached, they were very wary, and Ying Jiu failed to uncover their specific plans. However, Ying Jiu "casually" added a small, colorless, odorless concoction specially made by Doctor Chen to their drinks—a substance that caused diarrhea for several days without any identifiable cause—and "accidentally" caused a misunderstanding among the gambling den's henchmen, resulting in chaos. It was estimated that the young master Xue and his "friends" would spend the next few days in bed or the outhouse, and whether they could attend the birthday banquet was uncertain.

Upon receiving this news, Lin Chen sneered inwardly. It seemed Jia She and Xue Pan's "brilliant plan" had already been half-defeated before it even began. However, he couldn't be careless; their opponents might have more than just this one trick up their sleeve.

Amidst this tense and delicate atmosphere, Grandmother Jia's birthday finally arrived.

On this day, the Jia mansion's main gates were wide open, and guests thronged the area. Almost every prominent official family, nobleman, imperial relative, and wealthy merchant in Jinling City sent representatives to offer their congratulations. Rongqing Hall, Rongxi Hall, and even several important halls and courtyards in the Grand View Garden were decorated with lanterns and colorful streamers, and banquets were laid out, filled with melodious music and laughter. Grandmother Jia, dressed in her brand-new imperial robes, sat regally in the main seat of Rongqing Hall, receiving the kowtows and birthday wishes from her children, grandchildren, and guests, her face beaming with a smile, like a kind and contented old matriarch.

Lin Chen had also changed into a brand-new royal blue brocade robe, which accentuated his tall and handsome figure, and his composed demeanor. He followed behind Jia Zheng, bowing and offering birthday wishes to Grandmother Jia with everyone else, his etiquette impeccable. Many guests noticed this outstanding and unfamiliar young man and inquired about him. They learned that he was Lin Chen, a member of a collateral branch of the West Courtyard, but highly regarded by Jia Zheng. Those with better connections had even heard rumors of him "defeating an eighth-rank official with one punch" and "gaining the princess's favor," and their gazes towards him became more inquisitive and eager to befriend him.

Jia She, Madam Xing, Aunt Xue, Madam Wang, Li Wan, Feng Jie (who was struggling with her illness), Tan Chun, Ying Chun, Xi Chun, Bao Yu, Dai Yu, Xiang Yun, Bao Chai, and other members of the Jia family, all dressed in their finest attire, took their seats according to seniority and social standing. Xue Pan, as expected, was absent; Aunt Xue simply claimed he had "caught a cold and was bedridden." From the Shi family, Xiang Yun's aunt had also arrived, chatting and laughing with Aunt Xue, Madam Wang, and others.

The birthday banquet proceeded lively and smoothly. After several rounds of drinks and dishes, everyone was in high spirits. Just when everyone thought the day would pass peacefully, and even Grandmother Jia showed signs of fatigue, preparing to rest—

Madam Xing, who had been silent and seated below Jia She, suddenly stood up, curtsied to Grandmother Jia, and spoke with a deliberately heavy and aggrieved tone:

"Grandmother, today is your joyous occasion, and I shouldn't trouble you with such trivial matters. However… there is something concerning the reputation of the Jia family and ancestral rules, something I feel like I have a bone stuck in my throat, and I must speak out. Since all our relatives and elders are here today, I would like to ask everyone to judge whether the rules of our Jia family still hold true!"

Here it comes! Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. He put down his wine cup, calmly looking at Madam Xing, then glancing at Jia She beside her, who was expressionless but with a hint of smugness in his eyes. The show is about to begin.


Chapter 42 A Shocking Turn of Events at the Birthday Banquet: The Xuanlong Decree is Issued

Upon hearing Madam Xing's words, the previously noisy and festive Rongqing Hall instantly fell silent. The music ceased, and laughter stopped. All eyes, filled with surprise and curiosity, fell upon the usually unassuming Madam She, then subconsciously swept over the expressionless Jia She beside her, the slightly changed expression of Tan Chun on the other side, and the still calm Lin Chen.

Grandmother Jia's smile slowly faded, her brow furrowing almost imperceptibly, but quickly returning to normal. She picked up her teacup, took a small sip, and said calmly, "Daughter-in-law She, today is a joyous occasion. Whatever it is, we can discuss it later. Let's not disturb the refined enjoyment of our distinguished guests."

This was already an offer of a way out. However, Madam Xing seemed determined today. Not only did she not back down, but she raised her voice, her tone trembling with tears, and said, "Grandma, please understand! I know today is a joyous occasion, and it's inappropriate. But this matter concerns the Jia family's century-old reputation, and even more so... the face of the late Master (Jia Daishan)! I really... I really can't bear it! A few days ago, the Third Miss took over the household affairs, which was a good intention, and I had no objections. But who knew that this Third Miss, being young and inexperienced, was actually... misled by some people with ulterior motives, and arbitrarily changed the rules set by our ancestors..."  "Ju, abusing his power, suppressing loyal servants, and even... even extending his reach to the expenses of our East Courtyard! And that's not all; recently, someone reported that the Third Miss's accounts for several transactions are unclear, and that she has... has close ties with that outsider Lin from the West Courtyard, which might be improper! I think, in a family like ours, reputation is paramount. If any unpleasant rumors spread, making outsiders think that our Jia family is in turmoil, or... or that we have some kind of shady business, wouldn't that ruin our ancestral legacy, and make the Master rest in peace even in the afterlife!"

As she spoke, she grew increasingly agitated, even wiping away tears. Coupled with her magnificent official robes, she truly resembled a "suffering wife." Her words were particularly sharp, directly targeting Tan Chun and Lin Chen, accusing them of a series of serious crimes including "unauthorized alteration of ancestral rules," "abuse of power," "suppression of dissidents," "unclear accounts," "collusion," and "tarnishing the family reputation." The final mention of the deceased Jia Daishan was especially insidious.

A low murmur of discussion erupted in the hall. Many guests' gazes swept back and forth between Tan Chun, Lin Chen, Jia Zheng, Jia She, and Madam Xing, filled with excited anticipation and amusement. Aunt Xue and Aunt Shi exchanged meaningful glances, but no one spoke up.

Tan Chun's face turned pale with anger, her delicate body trembling slightly, but she forced herself to remain calm. She rose and bowed to Grandmother Jia, her voice clear yet tinged with suppressed rage: "Grandmother, I dare not accept your words, Aunt. Since taking over the household affairs, all my actions have been meticulously documented and based on evidence. The so-called 'unauthorized alteration of ancestral rules' was merely a matter of following old practices, which led to increasing expenses and a deficit. To preserve the ancestral foundation, I had to make slight adjustments, increasing revenue and reducing expenditure. This is the proper way to manage a household; how can it be called 'unauthorized alteration'? As for 'abusing private punishments and suppressing loyal servants,' that's utterly absurd."  "Let's talk! Those who were punished were all found guilty of embezzling money from the household, or were unruly servants who abused their age and refused to be disciplined. All have confessions and signatures; the evidence is overwhelming! As for the unclear accounts and the claim of close ties with Brother Lin… these are utter fabrications and slander! Tan Chun's actions are upright and honest; her accounts are crystal clear and readily verifiable! Her relationship with Brother Lin stems from his rescue of her in a time of crisis, his instruction in martial arts, and his assistance in managing the household affairs. It is a brother-sister relationship, completely innocent. How could there be anything 'inappropriate'? I implore Your Majesty to judge!"

Her words were clear, logical, and well-founded. She pointed out that Lin Chen had "rescued her" and "instructed her in martial arts," implying a legitimate reason for their close relationship and subtly highlighting the difficulty and necessity of her internal affairs reorganization. Many guests who had initially come with the intention of watching the spectacle were somewhat convinced by her resolute words and frank expression.

Madam Xing, however, shrieked, "Inspect? How can we inspect? The account books were all done by your subordinates; who knows if they've been tampered with? Witnesses and evidence? Those servants are terrified of you; they'll just believe whatever you say. As for the brother-sister relationship? Hmph, a man from another family, constantly coming and going in the inner quarters, 'having close ties' with unmarried girls—what kind of talk is that? Old Madam, all the elders are here today; you must give our eldest son's family, and our deceased father-in-law, justice!"

She clung to the issues of "rules" and "reputation," even dragging in the deceased Jia Daishan, clearly intending to completely crush Tan Chun on moral and ethical grounds.

Grandmother Jia's face darkened considerably. She had never liked Tan Chun's overly forceful reforms, and harbored suspicions about Lin Chen, this "variable." Now, with Madam Xing's public attack, her words sharp and threatening the Jia family's reputation and ancestral rules, even if she wanted to smooth things over in front of so many guests, it would be difficult. Moreover, Jia She, the Xue family, and the Shi family were clearly prepared.

Her gaze slowly swept over the crowd below, finally settling on Jia She, who had remained silent: "She'er, is what your wife said true? You are the head of the family, inheriting the title; what do you have to say about this?"

Jia She then slowly put down his wine cup, stood up, bowed to Grandmother Jia, and then bowed to the surrounding guests. He sighed heavily, feigning seriousness, "Mother, dear friends and relatives, this is a misfortune for our family, such a shameful incident has occurred. Jia She… is ashamed! What Xing said, though somewhat hasty, is… largely correct. Third Sister is young and impetuous, eager to prove herself, and easily misled, which is understandable. However… the unclear accounts and collusion between insiders and outsiders are no small matter. The other day, a servant reported to me that a sum of three thousand taels for garden repairs handled by Third Sister…"  It was written down clearly, yet the actual materials and labor used were less than half! The remaining silver had vanished without a trace! Furthermore, the warehouse manager said that several precious antiques had mysteriously disappeared recently, and the last person to handle them was a servant from Lin Chen's side in the West Courtyard! I didn't believe it at first, but the witnesses and evidence were irrefutable! I originally wanted to investigate privately to avoid airing our family shame, but today, Xing Shi was so anxious that she revealed it in public… Alas, since that's the case, to protect the Jia family's reputation and to comfort my father's spirit in heaven, I can only… beg Mother, and all relatives and friends, to uphold justice!”

As he spoke, he pulled several copies of account books from his sleeve, along with a "confession" bearing his fingerprint, and a fragment of a jade tag clearly from the storeroom, presenting them with both hands. "This is the evidence! The embezzling steward has been apprehended by my son and is locked in the woodshed; he can be brought in for questioning at any time! The storeroom tag containing the missing antiques was found near the Listening Bamboo Pavilion in the West Courtyard! The evidence is irrefutable!"

A roar erupted in the hall! If Madam Xing's previous accusations were merely baseless, Jia She's pronouncements of "witnesses and evidence present" and "irrefutable evidence" were concrete proof of guilt! Three thousand taels of silver, precious antiques, embezzlement, theft, collusion… any one of these, if proven true, would be enough to ruin Tan Chun's reputation and condemn Lin Chen to eternal damnation! Moreover, Jia She had presented "evidence," which completely changed the nature of the case!

All eyes turned to Tan Chun and Lin Chen, filled with shock, disdain, schadenfreude, and disbelief.

Tan Chun was struck dumb; her face turned deathly pale, and her delicate body swayed precariously. She never imagined that Jia She and Madam Xing could be so vicious, not only fabricating accusations but also forging so-called "ironclad evidence"! She clearly remembered the accounts; there were absolutely no problems! She had never heard of any antiques missing from the storeroom! This was clearly a frame-up! But she was now utterly speechless; the other side had the "evidence," and she had no stronger counter-evidence!

Jia Zheng abruptly stood up, his face ashen, pointing at Jia She, trembling with rage: "You...you're slandering me! Chen-ge'er and Tan-chun are not like that! This evidence of yours is definitely fabricated!"

"Second brother, I know you love your niece and value this nephew," Jia She said, feigning heartbreak, "but facts are facts. Faced with evidence, how can you deny it? Are you going to disregard ancestral laws and the Jia family's reputation just to protect them?"

Aunt Xue then spoke up quietly, "Master Zheng, Master She, we're all family, why make things so awkward? In my opinion, there might be some misunderstanding. However, since Master She has presented evidence, to avoid suspicion and to clear the names of Third Miss and Young Master Lin, perhaps… Third Miss should hand over the steward's token, and it could be temporarily… managed by the First Madam? As for Young Master Lin, he's an outsider, and it's inappropriate for him to be involved in matters of the inner quarters' finances. Perhaps… he should be asked to leave the mansion first, and we can discuss it further after the matter is clarified? This way, we can maintain proper etiquette and avoid gossip."

These words seemed fair, but were actually malicious. Once Tan Chun handed over the token, the stewardship would fall into the hands of Madam Xing (Jia She), and it would be difficult to regain it. And "asking Lin Chen to leave the mansion" would be a direct expulsion, cutting off his support; from then on, whether he was right or wrong would be entirely up to them.

Aunt Shi chimed in, "Aunt Xue is right. Family scandals shouldn't be aired in public; let's calm things down first. Third Miss is still young; perhaps she's truly been deceived."

Everyone echoed her sentiments. The situation instantly took a sharp turn for the worse, extremely unfavorable for Tan Chun and Lin Chen. Grandmother Jia looked at the "evidence," then at the pale-faced Tan Chun and the furious Jia Zheng, and listened to the murmurs throughout the room. A struggle and weariness flashed in her eyes. Finally, seemingly having made up her mind, she slowly spoke: "Since that's the case..."

"Wait!"

Just as Grandmother Jia was about to make her decision, a clear, calm voice, yet strangely penetrating, suddenly rang out, reaching everyone's ears clearly.

Everyone turned to look, and saw Lin Chen, who had been sitting quietly at the table as if detached from the situation, slowly stand up.

His expression remained calm, his eyes clear, and even a faint, almost imperceptible smile played at the corners of his lips, as if the storm raging against him was merely a gentle breeze. He first bowed to Grandmother Jia, then turned, his gaze calmly sweeping over Jia She, Madam Xing, Aunt Xue, Aunt Shi, and others, finally settling on the few pages of so-called "evidence."

"What Master She just said is based on irrefutable evidence," Lin Chen said slowly, his voice not loud, yet carrying a power that involuntarily silenced everyone. "But I wonder, which steward in the manor is this 'witness'? Where is he in the woodshed right now? When and by whom was the ledger, the 'physical evidence,' checked? What is the list of the missing antiques? And what is the name of the last servant in the west courtyard who handled them? Where is he now?"

His questions were unhurried and clear, each one hitting the nail on the head.

Jia She was momentarily speechless, then flew into a rage: "Hmph! The witnesses and physical evidence are all there, how dare you try to deny it? The steward's name is Lai Fu, he's in charge of purchasing for the garden, and he's been temporarily detained out of fear of your retaliation. The ledger was obtained the day before yesterday, and the accountant can vouch for who handled it! The list of antiques is in my hands! As for that servant, he fled after hearing about the incident! Do you still intend to deny it?"

“Laifu?” Lin Chen raised an eyebrow slightly. “Is that the Laifu who was punished by Sister-in-law Lian two years ago for stealing and selling flowers and trees from the manor, and was sent to the estate, and then personally transferred back to the manor last year by Master She to manage the purchases for the abandoned garden in the back alley of the East Courtyard? How could someone in charge of an abandoned garden handle three thousand taels of money for repairing the main garden? This account book… was it checked the other day? As far as I know, after the Third Miss took over the household affairs, all the accounts were re-enrolled, in triplicate. One copy was kept in the public treasury, one was kept by the Third Miss, and one was sealed in the storeroom designated by the Old Madam. Which copy is the one Master She has? And which accountant checked it privately without the permission of the Third Miss or the Old Madam and ‘discovered’ the problem? As for that servant who fled to avoid punishment… what is his name? When did he enter the manor? What position does he hold in Tingzhu Pavilion? All the servants around me are personally appointed by Master Zheng, and there is a roster to check. I wonder which one it is?”

His words were a sharp rebuttal, not only pointing out Lai Fu's "criminal record" and questionable authority, but also indicating that the "evidence" in Jia She's hands was of dubious origin and procedurally irregular, and finally directly questioning the identity of the so-called "servant." Every sentence was reasonable and logically sound.

The people in the hall were stunned for a moment, then looked at Jia She with suspicion. Yes, the evidence was suspicious, the procedure was wrong, and the "witness" had a criminal record… If it was truly a frame-up, the method was far too crude.

Jia She's face flushed red and then paled. He hadn't expected Lin Chen to be so calm, much less that he knew so much about the household's personnel and accounting procedures! He had planned to use a sudden attack and fabricated "ironclad evidence" to quickly resolve the situation, but he hadn't expected the other party to so easily seize upon the flaws.

Seeing this, Madam Xing immediately shrieked, "Clever words! No matter how you try to deny it, the item was found near your Tingzhu Pavilion! That's irrefutable proof!"

"Oh? This jade fragment?" Lin Chen looked at the so-called "warehouse tag" in Jia She's hand, then smiled slightly. "Master She, may I borrow this item for a closer look?"

Jia She snorted coldly, handing the fragment to a servant beside him, who then presented it to Lin Chen. Lin Chen took it, examined it carefully, and then gently sniffed it. Then, in front of everyone, he suddenly brought the fragment close to the flame of a nearby candlestick.

"What are you doing?!" Jia She exclaimed in surprise.

The jade fragment, under the heat of the flame, quickly turned black at the edges, emitting a faint, pungent odor similar to burnt paint. A genuine warehouse tag jade tablet, made of special jade, is hard and heat-resistant, and would never be easily scorched by fire, nor would it emit such an odor.

“This jade fragment is porous and chars easily when exposed to fire, emitting a lacquer smell,” Lin Chen clearly stated, showing the charred fragment to everyone. “Obviously, someone used inferior jade, even stone powder, mixed with glue and lacquer to create a counterfeit, then deliberately aged it to resemble a warehouse label. Genuine warehouse labels are uniformly produced by the Imperial Household Department, using top-grade green jade, hard in texture, with unique patterns, and hidden markings. If Master She doesn't believe me, he can immediately retrieve an antique from the warehouse and compare its label. Or…” He turned to Jia Zheng, “Uncle, I remember His Majesty bestowed a pair of ‘Lotus Scroll Jade Pots’ two years ago. Their warehouse labels should have been specially made. Could we fetch them to examine and verify their authenticity?”

Jia Zheng, realizing the situation, felt reassured and immediately said to Li Gui, “Go! Go to the warehouse immediately and retrieve the labels for those jade pots! No, retrieve the labels for several other imperial gifts!”

“Yes!” Li Gui responded and hurried away.

Jia She and Madam Xing's faces instantly turned incredibly ugly. They were so focused on fabricating "evidence" that they overlooked the details, and they certainly hadn't expected Lin Chen to be so knowledgeable about the warehouse labels, spotting the flaw at a glance!

The atmosphere in the hall instantly reversed. Everyone's gaze towards Jia She and his wife was now filled with contempt and mockery. Fabricating evidence to frame their niece and junior—this method was utterly despicable!

Aunt Xue and Aunt Shi were also extremely embarrassed, lowering their heads to drink their tea, remaining silent.

Grandmother Jia's face was so dark it seemed to drip water. She never imagined that Jia She and Madam Xing would resort to such despicable means, publicly framing her at the birthday banquet and fabricating evidence! This was utterly disgraceful to the Jia family! Her gaze towards Jia She was filled with disappointment and rage.

Shortly afterward, Li Gui brought several genuine storeroom tags, all made of top-quality jade, warm and lustrous, with clear patterns and the Imperial Household Department's unique secret mark on the back. Compared to the charred "fragment," the authenticity was immediately apparent!

"She'er! You… what else do you have to say?!" Grandmother Jia slammed her hand on the table, her voice trembling with anger.

Jia She's face flushed and paled, sweat beading on his forehead, yet he stubbornly maintained his composure: "This...this must be due to the forger's exceptional skill. I...I was simply caught off guard and deceived by this villain..."

"Deceived?" Lin Chen gave him no further chance. His voice suddenly turned cold. He stepped forward, his gaze sharp as a knife, staring directly at Jia She. "Master She keeps saying he was deceived by a villain, but who exactly is this villain who forged evidence, framed his own nephew, and ruined the Jia family's reputation? Is it Lai Fu, who has a prior theft record but was recalled to the manor by Master She? Or...is there someone else instigating this?"

His words subtly pointed the finger at Jia She himself! A deathly silence fell over the hall; everyone held their breath.

Jia She felt an invisible pressure under Lin Chen's gaze, and his heart pounded with fear. He shouted, his voice trembling with rage, "Insolence! How dare you, a mere outsider, question me? Even if the evidence is false, your close relationship with Tan Chun is undeniable! And it is a fact that you, a man, have repeatedly interfered in the affairs of the inner quarters! Today, in front of all our relatives and friends, I want to ask you, what exactly are you trying to do? Do you really think the Jia family has no one to fend for you?"

Seeing his framing attempt fail, he reverted to his old tactics of "rules and etiquette" and "outsider interference," trying to confuse the issue.

Lin Chen suddenly smiled, a smile carrying a hint of mockery and an indescribable air of authority. He slowly took out a palm-sized black token from his robes—neither gold nor jade, with a five-clawed dragon engraved on the front and the ancient seal character "灵" (spirit) on the back—and held it high.

Under the lamplight, the token shimmered with a deep, eerie luster, emanating an aura of dignified nobility and unassailable power.

"The Xuanlong Token?!"

Several well-informed officials and nobles in the hall exclaimed in shock, their faces changing drastically, and they abruptly stood up!

"Seeing the token is like seeing the princess! It's Princess Zhaoyang's Xuanlong Token!"

"How could he have the Xuanlong Token?!"

Exclamations erupted one after another. Everyone was stunned by the sudden appearance of the token. The Xuanlong Token was the highest symbol of the princesses of the Great Chu royal family; seeing the token was like seeing the princess herself! The holder of this token could mobilize a portion of the royal resources, acting first and reporting later! This Lin Chen, a collateral descendant of the Jia family, actually possessed Princess Zhaoyang's Xuanlong Token! What did this mean? It meant that behind him stood the most favored princess of the day, Princess Zhaoyang! Perhaps even… the royal family!

Jia She, Madam Xing, Aunt Xue, Aunt Shi, and the others, the color drained from their faces instantly, their eyes filled with disbelief, shock, and fear. They had calculated everything, but they hadn't anticipated that Lin Chen had such a background! With the princess backing him, not only them, but even Prince Zhongshun would have to think twice!

Jia Zheng was also stunned. Looking at the Xuanlong Token, then at the calm Lin Chen, a storm raged within him. He had long known his nephew was extraordinary, but he hadn't expected such a deep connection with Princess Zhaoyang!

Grandmother Jia's pupils contracted, her hand holding the prayer beads trembling slightly. The Xuanlong Token… she finally understood where Lin Chen's confidence came from. With the princess's protection, he truly had no need to fear Jia She; in fact… the entire Jia family would have to treat him with deference!

Holding the Xuanlong Token, Lin Chen's gaze swept across the entire room, finally settling on the ashen-faced Jia She. His voice was calm, yet carried an undeniable authority: "Lord She, what are your intentions? I, Lin, am unworthy, but I have been appreciated by Princess Zhaoyang and entrusted with some tasks. Today's events, right and wrong, will be judged by public opinion. As for me and the Third Miss, we were acting on Her Highness's orders to secretly investigate certain illegal activities involving the court and the safety of the nation, and were thus forced to have some dealings. Unexpectedly, we have been falsely accused by some with ulterior motives of 'collusion with outsiders' and 'plotting rebellion'! I will certainly report this matter truthfully to Her Highness, and beseech Your Highness... to make a judgment!"

Acting on the Princess's orders? Investigating illegal activities? Involving the court and the nation?

These words were utterly shocking! They directly elevated Jia She and his associates' accusations to the level of "interfering with the princess's investigation" and "sabotaging important court affairs"! With such accusations pinned on them, not only Jia She, but the entire Jia family couldn't bear the consequences!

"No...impossible! You're talking nonsense!" Jia She was terrified, pointing at Lin Chen, stammering incoherently.

"Whether it's nonsense or not, Her Highness the Princess will have the judgment," Lin Chen said calmly, putting away the Xuanlong Token. "As for Master She, today he fabricated evidence, publicly framed someone, damaged the Jia family's reputation, and almost ruined Her Highness the Princess's important plans. How should this matter be resolved?"

He no longer addressed him as "Master She," but directly referred to "Jia She," the coldness in his tone sending a chill down everyone's spine.

Grandmother Jia finally realized that if she didn't intervene, the Jia family would be in grave danger! She took a deep breath, suppressing the turmoil in her heart, and shouted in a deep voice, "Enough! All of you, shut up!"

She stood up, her gaze sharp as lightning. She first glared fiercely at Jia She and Madam Xing, the chill in her eyes sending them plummeting into an icy abyss. Then, she turned to Lin Chen, her tone complex yet undeniably resolute: "Chen-ge'er, today's events are due to my lax management of the household, causing you to suffer injustice. I will thoroughly investigate this matter of forging evidence and framing a junior, and give you and San-ya-tou an explanation! As for the Xuanlong Token… it is Her Highness the Princess's trust; you must perform your duties diligently and not disappoint Her Highness's high expectations. Today's birthday banquet ends here. Li Wan, Feng-ya-tou, see off our distinguished guests. She'er, Zheng'er, and the rest of you, come with me to the back!"

She finished speaking in one breath, then, without looking at anyone, turned and walked towards the back hall with Yuanyang's support, her back appearing somewhat hunched.

A meticulously planned birthday banquet had begun, ultimately ending with Jia She and his wife's crushing defeat, Lin Chen revealing the Xuanlong Token to intimidate everyone, and Grandmother Jia forcibly cleaning up the mess. But everyone knew that today's events were far from over. The Jia family's fortunes were likely about to change.

The guests took their leave with varying expressions, but their gazes towards Lin Chen were no longer filled with disdain, but with deep awe and curiosity. This young master Lin, wielding the Xuanlong Token and favored by Princess Zhaoyang, would likely soon become a figure that no one in Jinling could ignore.

Jia She and Madam Xing, their faces ashen, followed Grandmother Jia to the back hall, utterly dejected. Aunt Xue and Aunt Shi also hurriedly took their leave, their faces grim. Jia Zheng glanced at Lin Chen with a complex expression and followed them.

Tan Chun walked to Lin Chen's side, her eyes filled with tears—tears of joy and lingering fear—and whispered, "Brother Lin, thank you..."

"It's alright," Lin Chen smiled gently at her, reassuring her. "Go back and rest first. Leave the rest to me and Uncle."

Tan Chun nodded and left, accompanied by Shi Shu and Cui Mo.

Lin Chen stood alone in the gradually emptying Rongqing Hall, looking at the scattered cups and plates and the flickering candlelight, his eyes deep and thoughtful.

The Xuanlong Token, while temporarily deterring the villains, also thrust him into the open, forcing him into a direct confrontation with Prince Zhongshun, Jia She, and others. The ensuing struggle would only intensify and become more dangerous.

However, he was fearless.

He tightened his grip on the magnolia-shaped jade pendant at his chest, then felt the warmth of the Xuanlong Token in his arms, a cold smile curving his lips.

Bring it on, I'll meet it head-on. Since there's no escaping it, then let's fight!

He turned and strode out of Rongqing Hall, heading towards Tingzhu Pavilion. The moonlight cast a long, imposing shadow of him, tall and resolute, like a mountain.

Meanwhile, from the back hall of Rongqing Hall, the faint sounds of suppressed anger from Grandmother Jia, Jia She's terrified pleas, and the crisp sound of shattering porcelain could be heard.

This night in the Jia household was destined to be sleepless for no one.


Chapter 43 The Princess's Secret Talk, Royal Secrets

The clamor and commotion of the birthday banquet receded like the tide, leaving behind only a scene of chaos and a deathly silence. The arguing and commotion in the back hall of Rongqing Hall lasted for about half an hour, finally ending with a suppressed, miserable groan from Jia She and a weary shout from Grandmother Jia: "Get out!" The couple from the East Courtyard were helped away, their faces ashen. It was said that Jia She had a clear handprint on his face, and Madam Xing was constantly weeping. Jia Zheng also returned to Rongxi Hall with a heavy expression, presumably unable to sleep tonight.

Inside Tingzhu Pavilion, candlelight blazed. Lin Chen had changed out of his lavish banquet attire, wearing only a simple blue cloth robe. He sat by the window in the quiet room, gazing at the deep night outside, unconsciously stroking the warm, smooth Xuanlong token in his hand. The token was cold, yet it seemed to still retain the scorching heat of the moment in Rongqing Hall, when everyone was watching and his word had been decisive.

Revealing the Xuanlong Token was a last resort. While the accusations against Jia She and his wife were crude, in that setting, presented as "witness testimony and evidence," and with the Xue and Shi families fueling the flames, if he hadn't decisively exposed the fabricated evidence and used the Xuanlong Token to intimidate everyone, Jia Zheng and Tan Chun alone would likely have suffered a severe loss—the management of the household would have been lost, and he himself might have been expelled. However, revealing the token also meant that his relationship with Princess Zhaoyang was completely public, placing him firmly on the opposite side of Prince Zhongshun and Jia She, leaving no room for maneuver. The future open and covert attacks would only increase and become more dangerous.

"But so what?" A cold glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. From the moment he decided to accept the princess's offer, to protect Daiyu, and to make a name for himself in this world of martial arts and romance, he was destined to be an enemy of these demons and monsters. He would simply meet them head-on.

What he was more concerned about now was Princess Zhaoyang's reaction. While revealing the Xuanlong Token at the birthday banquet had extricated him from the predicament, it had also essentially used the princess's influence, or rather, dragged her into the conflict, making her relationship with Prince Zhongshun and Jia She more public. Did the princess approve, be angry, or had other plans? He needed to communicate with her as soon as possible.

As if sensing his thoughts, a faint yet rhythmic sound of wind breaking through the night sky outside the window approached. Lin Chen's expression shifted, and he walked to the window.
