A pale purple figure, like a startled swan under the moonlight, gracefully swept across the wall of Tingzhu Pavilion, landing lightly in the courtyard with a few leaps—it was Princess Zhaoyang, Zhao Lingxi.

She was still dressed in her practical purple outfit, but now wore a matching cloak, the hood pulled low, obscuring most of her face. However, her unique aura, a blend of elegance and sharpness, remained undeniable. She had come alone, without Ying Qi or Ying Jiu.

"Your Highness," Lin Chen said, opening the door and bowing respectfully.

Zhao Lingxi nodded slightly and stepped inside. Beneath her hood, her clear, bright eyes gazed intently at Lin Chen, her gaze lingering on his face for a moment, as if scrutinizing or assessing him. Then, she sat down on the only chair in the quiet room, while Lin Chen stood beside her.

“Sit,” Zhao Lingxi said calmly, her voice unreadable.

Lin Chen sat down on the futon opposite her, waiting for her to speak.

"I am aware of the matter concerning the birthday banquet," Zhao Lingxi said slowly, her tone calm. "You handled it well. Exposing the false evidence and revealing the Xuanlong Token not only protected yourself and Tan Chun, but also deterred the villains, and brought the collusion between Jia She, the Xue family, and the Shi family into the open. However..." She paused, her gaze turning to Lin Chen, "Using my token to deal with the internal strife within the Jia family, isn't that... a bit of a waste?"

These words carried a subtle, yet palpable, warning. Lin Chen remained unmoved, stating frankly, "Your Highness is wise. Today's events, seemingly a dispute within the inner quarters, are in fact related to Your Highness's strategic plans. Jia She, the Xue family, and the Shi family are all henchmen of Prince Zhongshun. Their attack, launched under the guise of the birthday banquet, targets not only Tan Chun and myself, but also Master Zheng, aiming to completely control the Jia family and clear obstacles for Prince Zhongshun's plans in Jiangnan. My display of the Xuanlong Token was not only for self-protection, but also to demonstrate to everyone that the Jia family is not a place where they can act with impunity. Your Highness also has eyes and ears and influence in Jinling. This move may have been somewhat risky, but it can also be seen as a warning and counterattack against Prince Zhongshun. As for whether it was a waste of talent… the token was bestowed upon me by Your Highness; using it was to share Your Highness's burdens. How could there be a distinction between great and small?"

Zhao Lingxi listened quietly, her gaze lingering on Lin Chen's open and honest face. After a moment, she suddenly smiled softly, a smile like the thawing of an iceberg, instantly dispelling the heavy atmosphere and sense of distance in the room.

"Well said, 'If used, they will share Your Highness's burdens; what distinction is there between large and small?'" A hint of admiration flashed in her eyes. "You certainly know how to speak. Very well, although you may have used some influence in this matter, you handled it appropriately, and the result wasn't bad. After this incident, Jia She's prestige in the Jia family has been completely tarnished, and the old lady is utterly disappointed in him. For a short time, he shouldn't dare to openly cause trouble again. The Xue and Shi families will also be wary. This will certainly benefit my future arrangements."

Her tone softened, clearly not truly blaming him, but rather quite approving of Lin Chen's resourcefulness and courage.

"Thank you for your understanding, Your Highness." Lin Chen felt a slight sense of relief. It seemed the princess wasn't one to be bound by rigid rules, but rather valued the outcome and the bigger picture.

"Regarding Qixia Mountain, Ying Qi has already given a detailed report." Zhao Lingxi's tone shifted, her expression becoming serious again. "The Xuanzhen Armory, the Bronze Corpse Puppet, the Nine Palaces Star Luo Restriction, the Purple Gold Jade Box, and that… suspected suppressed evil spirit. Your journey was far more dangerous than expected, and the gains… were also unexpected." Her gaze swept meaningfully over Lin Chen, clearly already aware that he had obtained the "Nine Revolutions Rejuvenation Pill" and that his cultivation had greatly improved.

"It was all thanks to Your Highness's strategic planning, and the invaluable assistance of Ying Qi, Doctor Chen, Master Shi, and others, that Lin Chen was able to escape unscathed and gain some small rewards," Lin Chen said modestly, not taking credit for anything.

"What is your destiny is yours." Zhao Lingxi waved her hand. "The 'Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill' can help you recover and break through, which is a good thing. The stronger you are, the greater your help to me. However, the matter of the purple-gold jade box and the suspected evil object is no small matter. According to Master Shi's report, the 'Nine Palaces Star Luo Restriction' is an ancient and wondrous formation. What is hidden in that jade box is probably the true core secret of the Xuanzhen Martial Armory, and may even be related to its inheritance. As for that evil object... it can be guarded by several bronze corpse puppets with strength close to the seventh rank. Its strength before it was alive or before it was sealed is probably far beyond imagination. This matter is probably no longer a simple treasure hunt, but involves some ancient secrets, and may even... concern the fate of the nation."

Her tone was grave, clearly showing her extreme fear of the ferocious creature beneath the earth. "I have already ordered the relevant areas of Qixia Mountain to be secretly sealed off, and dispatched experts skilled in formations and ancient texts to investigate, striving to ascertain the origins and sealing status of that ferocious creature. Until the matter is clarified, no one is allowed to approach 'Hidden Dragon Abyss.' You must also keep this matter to yourself and never mention it to a third party, including Jia Zheng and Tan Chun."

"Yes, Lin Chen understands." Lin Chen nodded solemnly. He had personally experienced the terror of that ferocious creature and naturally knew its severity.

"As for Prince Zhongshun..." Zhao Lingxi's eyes flashed coldly, "The Black Tiger Gang suffered heavy losses at Qixia Mountain, and he must be furious. At the birthday banquet, you used my influence to publicly humiliate him (Jia She represented his interests). Knowing his temperament, he will never let this go. Next, he will probably use more overt and covert means to deal with you, to deal with Jia Zheng, and even... perhaps directly against me. You should be prepared."

"We'll meet force with force, and water with earth," Lin Chen said calmly. "However, I wonder what Your Highness's specific plans are? I'd like to hear the details."

Zhao Lingxi pondered for a moment, then slowly said, "After the birthday banquet incident, Jia She has temporarily lost power, Jia Zheng's influence in the household has increased, and Tan Chun has been able to solidify her position. This presents an opportunity for us to control the Jia family and acquire resources and information. You must assist Tan Chun in thoroughly sorting out the Jia family's internal affairs as soon as possible, especially the accounts, estates, shops, and other economic lifelines, ensuring they are in the hands of reliable people. At the same time, you must also be wary of whether Jia She, the Xue family, and the Shi family are still secretly colluding and exchanging information."

“Secondly, it concerns the ‘Map of Mountains and Rivers’.” She took a folded piece of silk from her sleeve and handed it to Lin Chen. “Based on the ‘Yin Map’ constellations you brought out and the fragment of the ‘Yang Map’ in my possession, combined with Master Shi’s exploration, this is a preliminary deduction of several possible locations where clues to the complete ‘Map of Mountains and Rivers’ or another fragment might exist. They are all in various prefectures in Jiangnan, some even near Jinling. You must keep a close watch secretly, and report to me immediately if you find any clues. Remember, this matter is top secret, and should not be used by anyone but a trusted confidant.”

Lin Chen took the silk and unfolded it. It contained the approximate locations and brief descriptions of several places. One of them was strikingly named: “Jinling, Jiming Temple, the underground palace of the ancient pagoda from the previous dynasty”? Jiming Temple? Wasn’t that one of the most popular temples in Jinling? Could the clues to Master Xuanzhen be hidden there?

"Finally, it's about yourself." Zhao Lingxi's gaze returned to Lin Chen, carrying a hint of scrutiny and expectation. "Your cultivation has now reached the peak of the eighth rank, with a solid foundation and the residual effects of the 'Nine Revolutions Rejuvenating Pill.' Reaching the seventh rank is just around the corner. The difference between the seventh and eighth ranks is like heaven and earth. Once you break through, you will truly have the capital to establish yourself in Jinling and even Jiangnan, and you will be able to share more of my responsibilities. I have half of the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique* here, the foundational chapter of my royal family's secret *Purple Cloud Divine Art*. Although it's not as profound as your cultivation method (she had already discerned Lin Chen's extraordinary skill), its explanations of 'Qi control,' 'condensation of Qi,' and 'divine intent' might be of some use to you. Take it and study it; it might help you break through your bottleneck faster."

As she spoke, she took out a small, yellowed ancient book from her bosom and handed it to Lin Chen.

A royal secret cultivation technique! Although it's only the basic level, its value, especially for Lin Chen who is currently attempting to break through to the seventh rank, is self-evident! This is undoubtedly the princess's further investment in and attempt to win him over.

"Thank you for your generous gift, Your Highness! Lin Chen will certainly not fail Your Highness's expectations!" Lin Chen solemnly accepted it. He now has a preliminary understanding of the "Qi Control" principle and needs higher-level theoretical guidance; this *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique* has arrived at just the right time.

“No need for thanks. Your strength is my strength.” Zhao Lingxi rose, walked to the window, and gazed at the deep night sky. Her voice carried a hint of etherealness yet unwavering resolve. “This world, seemingly peaceful, is actually turbulent beneath the surface. Prince Zhongshun is ambitious, colluding with powerful families to plot against us. The Northern Frontier, the Western Regions, and the Southern Barbarians are also restless. The court is rife with factional strife and accumulated corruption. My father… is getting old. Though I am a woman, I do not wish to see our ancestral legacy crumble and our country in turmoil. I need like-minded, capable, and courageous helpers. Lin Chen, are you willing to join me in cleansing this world of turbidity and restoring peace and tranquility?”

She turned around, her gaze intense, meeting Lin Chen's eyes directly. In those clear eyes burned a light beyond her years and gender—an ambition and idealism befitting a politician, dazzling and captivating, yet also carrying a heavy sense of responsibility and loneliness.

Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. He hadn't expected this princess from the deep palace to possess such ambition and insight. This was no longer merely a struggle for the throne; it concerned the ideals of all living beings. He slowly rose, meeting her gaze, and instead of answering immediately, asked a question.

"Your Highness, is it the position you seek? Or... the world?"

Zhao Lingxi paused slightly, then understood his meaning. A faint, yet arrogant, smile curved her lips: "That position, if it can bring peace to the world, I will take it. If not, I will find a more capable person to occupy it. What I want is a world of peace and prosperity, where the people live in harmony and happiness, where martial arts flourish, and where civilization endures. As for myself... where I sit is not important."

Lin Chen looked at her deeply, seeing sincerity in her eyes, and also the unwavering determination of someone in a position of power. He no longer hesitated, bowing deeply in a solemn salute.

"Your Highness possesses a heart for the world and lofty ambitions; Lin Chen admires you. I wish to follow in your footsteps and contribute my humble efforts. I will go through fire and water for you, without hesitation!"

This was not simply loyalty, but an affirmation and pursuit of a certain ideal and path. Perhaps, cooperating with such a knowledgeable, decisive, and principled royal heir was the best choice for him to realize his own value and protect his loved ones in this world of high martial arts and romance.

Zhao Lingxi's smile deepened, becoming more sincere. She stepped forward and gently patted Lin Chen's shoulder; the gesture seemed casual, yet it bridged the gap.

"Alright. From today onward, we need not be bound by formalities. In private, you may call me 'Lingxi.' And I will call you 'Lin Chen.' We... are allies, and also like-minded."

"Yes, Lingxi." Lin Chen readily complied, sensing that their relationship had taken another step forward, gaining a tacit understanding and trust that transcended mere exchange of interests.

"Alright, it's getting late, I should go back." Zhao Lingxi put her hood back on. "You should cultivate diligently and break through as soon as possible. For now, you'll be in charge of the Jia family. If there's any emergency, you can contact me through the specific method of the Xuanlong Token. Also..." She paused, her tone carrying a deeper meaning, "Be careful of the old lady of the Jia family. She seems to be on your side today, but in reality, she prioritizes the family's interests and balance. If necessary, she might sacrifice some people for the 'greater good,' including...you. Don't completely trust her."

"I understand." Lin Chen nodded. He had long understood the old lady's thoughts.

Zhao Lingxi said no more. With a flicker, she leaped out of the window, disappearing into the night. In a few leaps, she vanished, as if she had never been there.

Lin Chen stood by the window, gazing in the direction she had disappeared, motionless for a long time. In his hands, the half of the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique* and the silk scroll containing clues to the "Map of Mountains and Rivers" weighed heavily.

"Allies...fellow practitioners..." He murmured, his eyes gleaming.

This private conversation with Princess Zhaoyang had given him a clearer vision of his future path, and a heavier sense of responsibility and pressure. But equally, it had given him a more definite goal and stronger support.

"The most urgent task is to break through to the seventh rank!" Lin Chen clenched his fist, feeling the surging internal energy within him and the clear yet solid bottleneck barrier. With the guidance of the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*, coupled with the residual medicinal power of the "Nine Revolutions Rejuvenating Pill," he felt more confident about breaking through to the seventh rank.

He didn't delay any longer, immediately returning to his meditation cushion and sitting down. He opened the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique* and began to study it intently. The royal secret was indeed extraordinary. Its explanation of the essence of "qi control," the techniques for combining "divine will" and "internal energy," and the description of the "condensation of qi" process broadened his horizons. Many things that had been unclear when he was practicing the *Qi Control Technique* before suddenly became clear.

Before we knew it, the night had passed. Dawn was breaking in the east.

Lin Chen put down his book, his eyes shining brightly. He had gained much from a night of study. His understanding of the Dao of "Qi Control" had reached a new level. Within his dantian, the powerful internal energy, guided by the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*, seemed to become more fluid and refined, faintly showing the rudiments of "罡气" (罡气, a type of internal energy).

"It's time to attempt an attack." He slowly closed his eyes, his mind sinking into his dantian, beginning to mobilize all his internal energy, preparing for his first attempt to break through the seventh-grade barrier.

Meanwhile, at another end of Jinling City, deep within the luxurious villa where Prince Zhongshun was temporarily residing, in a secret room...

The atmosphere was oppressive, like the calm before a storm. Prince Zhongshun, a middle-aged man of about forty, with a dignified face, a short beard, and dressed in a bright yellow robe, stood with his hands behind his back by the window, facing away from the kneeling figures. Though he remained silent, his aura of authority, long accustomed to his position, seemed to lower the temperature in the secret room considerably.

Kneeling on the ground were none other than Jia She, Xue Pan's father Xue Qian (Xue Pan's brother), and Shi Ding, a powerful figure in the Shi family. All three were deathly pale, their foreheads drenched in cold sweat, and dared not utter a sound. Beside them stood an elderly man in black, his face somber and his aura sullen; he was one of Prince Zhongshun's trusted retainers.

"Useless!" After a long silence, Prince Zhongshun's icy voice rang out like ice shards hitting the ground. "A mere brat, a little girl, and a token of dubious authenticity, and you've turned your carefully laid scheme upside down? Jia She, you can be publicly humiliated even in your own home! The forged evidence is so crude that even a three-year-old could see through it! What use are you to me?!"

"Your Highness, please calm down! Your Highness, please calm down!" Jia She trembled with fear, kowtowing repeatedly. "It's...it's that boy's too cunning, and he's gained Princess Zhaoyang's favor...I...I never expected he would possess the Xuanlong Token..."

"Never expected? I told you to keep an eye on him, to investigate his background, and this is how you investigated?!" Prince Zhongshun whirled around, his eyes flashing with cold light. "And you, Xue Qian! I asked your son to do something, to find a few 'friends in the martial arts world,' and what happened? Before they even showed up, they were already dead! More trouble than they're worth!"

Xue Qian also repeatedly apologized, cursing his good-for-nothing son Xue Pan a thousand times in his heart.

Shi Ding remained silent, trembling with fear.

“Zhaoyang…good, very good!” Prince Zhongshun took a deep breath, suppressing his anger, a sinister glint in his eyes. “My good niece is determined to oppose me. Not only has she secretly investigated my background, but she has also extended her reach to Jiangnan, to the Jia family! And she has found such a capable…dog!”

He returned to his seat, his fingers lightly tapping the sandalwood armrest, producing a dull sound. “Jia She, listen to me carefully. The Jia family, you must keep them firmly under my control! Work hard on the old lady, make sure she sides with you. Jia Zheng, that girl, and that Lin Chen, must be eliminated as soon as possible! We cannot allow them to use Zhaoyang's influence to grow stronger in the Jia family!”

“Yes, yes! Your subject will certainly find a way!” Jia She quickly replied.

"As for you, Xue Qian," Prince Zhongshun looked at him, "your Xue family are imperial merchants, with wide connections. Keep a close eye on all major transactions involving grain, weapons, and medicinal herbs inside and outside Jinling City, especially those potentially related to Zhaoyang or that Lin Chen. Report to me immediately if you discover anything! Also, the connections with the independent cultivators in the East Sea must not be severed! 'Black Gold Iron' and 'Cold Star Sand' must be obtained as soon as possible!"

"Your Highness, rest assured, I will handle it!" Xue Qian quickly assured him.

"Shi Ding," Prince Zhongshun finally looked at the Shi family member, "your Shi family has some connections in the army and the Northern Frontier. Keep an eye on the movements in the Northern Frontier, especially... any news regarding the 'former dynasty's armory' or 'ancient cultivator ruins.' If you find anything, obtain it at all costs!"

"Yes, sir!" Shi Ding solemnly replied.

"Alright, all of you, get out of here! Remember, this is your last chance. If you mess this up again, hmph..." Prince Zhongshun sneered, his unspoken meaning sending chills down the spines of the three.

The three, feeling as if granted a pardon, scrambled out.

In the secret chamber, only Prince Zhongshun and the old man in black remained.

"Your Highness, that Lin Chen... seems no simpleton. To be so highly regarded by Zhaoyang, possess the Xuanlong Token, and have his strength increase at an alarming rate... the Qixia Mountain incident is likely related to him." The old man in black whispered.

"I know." Prince Zhongshun's eyes flashed with murderous intent. "This boy must not be allowed to live. You personally arrange for the 'Shadow' to be deployed to Jinling. Find an opportunity to strike decisively, leaving no future trouble! Remember, do it cleanly, don't let anyone find any fault, especially... don't implicate me!"

"Yes!" The old man in black's eyes flashed with a fierce light as he bowed and accepted the order.

“Zhaoyang…Lin Chen…Jia Mansion…Xuanzhen Armory…” Prince Zhongshun gazed at the brightening sky outside the window, a cold, determined smile curving his lips.

“This Jiangnan, this world, will ultimately belong to me. Anyone who stands in my way will only…die!”

The morning light pierced the clouds, bathing Jinling City in its glow. Beneath the calm surface of the new day, an even more turbulent killing intent and power struggle lurked.

Inside Tingzhu Pavilion, Lin Chen's aura surged, his pale golden inner energy now faintly tinged with purple, launching repeated assaults against the invisible barrier.

Meanwhile, in Xiaoxiang Pavilion, Daiyu had awakened, leaning against the window, clutching the warm magnolia jade pendant in her hand, gazing towards Tingzhu Pavilion, a faint sorrow and an unyielding longing in her eyes.


Chapter 44 Breakthrough to Seventh Rank, Undercurrents Rage

Listening Bamboo Pavilion, Quiet Room.

Lin Chen sat cross-legged on a futon, palms facing upwards, eyes closed, his breath deep and still. There was no wind in the room, yet his robes fluttered and rustled, his body enveloped in a misty aura of pale gold tinged with purple. This mist swirled ceaselessly, faintly carrying the sounds of wind and thunder—a manifestation of his surging inner energy, poised for a qualitative transformation.

Within his dantian, his powerful and pure inner energy, like boiling lava, circulated wildly under the dual guidance of the *Qi Control Technique* and the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*, following an unprecedented and more profoundly complex trajectory. With each cycle, his inner energy became more refined and agile. The seemingly impenetrable barrier between the eighth and seventh ranks of cultivation seemed to be slightly shaken by this continuous impact, emitting a subtle "crack" sound that only Lin Chen could perceive.

But this barrier was far more formidable than imagined. It wasn't merely a threshold for the total amount of internal energy, but a transformative requirement for the "quality" and "spirit" of that energy. One needed to refine their internal energy to a sufficiently pure and agile state, reaching the realm of "mind following energy" and "intention leading to energy," and even more importantly, to completely merge their martial arts intent with this transforming internal energy with a powerful spiritual will, only then could they break through and emerge as a butterfly from its cocoon.

Lin Chen was currently at this most crucial and perilous juncture. He immersed his entire being within his body, guiding the surging torrent of internal energy to repeatedly assault that barrier. Each impact was accompanied by the swelling pain in his meridians and a violent tremor in his spirit. But his will was as firm as iron, firmly stabilizing his sea of ​​consciousness with the *Qingxin Jue*, anchored by the cool and tranquil spiritual power of Daiyu transmitted through the bond of "heart and soul intertwined," remaining completely unmoved.

“Not enough… the ‘activity’ and ‘spirituality’ of my inner energy are still a hair’s breadth away… and my understanding of the essence of ‘Qi control’ seems to be separated by a thin veil…” Lin Chen clearly reflected his own state. After consuming the “Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill,” his inner energy surged, and his foundation became solid, but he still needed a catalyst to achieve precise control over this newfound power, especially to fully “control” it as if it were his own arm.

Involuntarily, he recalled his sparring session with Princess Zhao Lingxi at Qixia Mountain. Her majestic and dignified palm techniques, imbued with hidden authority, and the exquisite use of “momentum” in her every move, all surfaced in his mind. He also remembered the passage in the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*: “Qi is the servant of the heart. Only when the spirit and qi unite can one control it. The wonder of qi control lies in the spirit guiding the will, qi moving with intention, and transformations arising from the heart…”

Guided by divine will, guided by the flow of qi, and transformed by the heart… Lin Chen seemed to understand something. He no longer simply charged forward with his inner energy, but instead attempted to slowly and thoroughly merge his “divine will”—the martial will that combined the fearless ferocity of the Vajra Fist, the violent speed of the Thunderclap Fist, the penetrating shockwave of the Rock-Splitting Fist, and the unwavering belief in “protecting” Daiyu and his own path—with the surging inner energy.

He visualized himself as the thunder that tore through the sky, as the Vajra that toppled mountains, and as the rock that guarded the pure land in his heart. A powerful, unyielding, and unrepentant will, like a ignited spark, instantly ignited the inner energy throughout his body!

“Boom!”

It was as if something exploded in his mind! Under the guidance and fusion of this "divine will," the internal energy in his dantian underwent a profound transformation! No longer a simple torrent of energy, it carried Lin Chen's unique martial arts imprint, becoming more condensed, more agile, more… imbued with "life" and "will"! The pale golden internal energy was now completely imbued with a noble purple aura, with faint crackling electric sparks dancing within!

At this moment!

"Break!"

A silent roar erupted in Lin Chen's heart, transforming all his essence, energy, and spirit, along with the transformed, divinely fused purple-gold internal energy, into an indestructible spear of will, launching a final and most powerful strike against the already cracked barrier!

"Crack—Boom!"

It was as if porcelain had shattered completely, or as if spring thunder had exploded deep within the soul! That solid barrier, under the supreme strike that gathered all of Lin Chen's power and will, shattered and disintegrated! An even more vast, pure, and agile purple-gold inner energy, carrying his unique martial arts divine intent, surged out from behind the shattered barrier like a bursting dam, instantly sweeping over and filling every inch of the newly formed, wider, and more resilient dantian and meridians!

"Buzz..."

A continuous, melodious resonance, like the tinkling of metal and jade, resonated within his body—a sign of transformation. His blood and qi surged like a mighty river, his vitality as exuberant as a furnace. His five senses and six perceptions underwent another earth-shattering metamorphosis. Within a hundred feet, the rustling of grass, the chirping of insects, even the whispers of maids in the neighboring courtyard, were clearly audible. His vision allowed him to easily discern the finest veins on leaves several feet away. His spiritual power surged, becoming almost tangible, his control over himself reaching the pinnacle of "microscopic" precision. With a single thought, his inner energy could precisely reach any part of his body, without the slightest error!

The most significant change was in his perception and control of "Qi." At this moment, in his perception, the sparse primordial Qi that permeated the world was no longer an elusive wisp, but rather seemed to possess a blurred color and direction. With a slight thought, without consciously circulating the "Qi Control Technique," the purplish-gold inner energy within his dantian flowed spontaneously, generating a strange suction and affinity that slowly drew in the primordial Qi within a three-foot radius around him, channeling it through his pores and merging it into his body. Although the efficiency was still not high, the process was natural, like breathing! This signified that he had truly entered the realm of "Qi Control," becoming a seventh-rank martial artist capable of initially drawing upon and manipulating the primordial Qi of the world!

Early Stage Seventh Rank! A natural progression, a single, decisive achievement!

Lin Chen slowly opened his eyes. A flash of purple-gold divine light appeared in his pupils, then returned to a deep, calm stillness, but the aura of unwavering composure and absolute control was etched into his very bones. He gently clenched his fist, the air easily exploding in his palm with a crisp sonic boom. With another thought, a wisp of purple-gold, condensed, almost tangible energy emanated from his fingertips, three feet from his body, condensed yet not dissipating, subtly pulsating, bending and shifting with his will, as agile as his own limbs! This was precisely the hallmark ability of a seventh-rank martial artist—the external projection of inner energy, the condensation of qi into a physical form!

"This is the power of a seventh-grade..." Lin Chen felt the vast, flowing power within his body, brimming with immense confidence. Compared to his eighth-grade self, it wasn't just a leap in the quantity and quality of his internal energy, but a transcendence in his life level and understanding of the essence of power. Now, even without the explosive power of the "Thunderclap Fist" and the miraculous effects of the Soul-Nourishing Wood, he was confident he could defeat, or even kill, those two early-stage seventh-grade assassins head-on!

He stood up, stretched his limbs, and a series of crisp crackling sounds emanated from his body. He felt completely refreshed, as if he had inexhaustible strength. His right arm injury had long since healed, and now it was brimming with explosive power. He tried using the Lightness Step; his figure blurred, leaving a clear afterimage in his original position before his true body appeared in another corner of the quiet room, more than twice as fast as before! Moreover, he landed silently, leaving no trace, demonstrating a new level of control over his movement.

"The first move of the Thunderclap Fist, 'Thunderous Light Appears,' should now be able to unleash its true power. Perhaps... I could try to comprehend the second move?" Lin Chen pondered. His cultivation had broken through, his spiritual power had surged, and his understanding of the "thunder" intent had deepened—it was the perfect time to further refine this profound-level fist technique. However, now was not the time for secluded study.

He pushed open the door to his meditation room and stepped into the courtyard. It was already afternoon, and the sun was shining brightly. The guards in the courtyard were all startled to see him. They felt that this young master Lin's aura had drastically changed. Though he stood there, he seemed to blend into his surroundings, yet he also exuded a chilling sense of oppression that made people afraid to look him directly in the eye.

"Young Master, you've emerged from seclusion?" A squad leader of guards stepped forward and asked respectfully.

"Hmm." Lin Chen nodded slightly. "How have things been at home these past few days while I've been in seclusion?"

"Reporting to Young Master, the manor... has been relatively peaceful. However, in the East Courtyard, Master She and Madam seem to be staying indoors, and the servants are all very cautious. The Third Miss is still managing the household affairs, but... seems busier than usual. Also..." The guard captain lowered his voice, "Miss Lin of Xiaoxiang Pavilion... these past two days... has been lingering outside the courtyard for a while each day. She seems to want to come in, but is afraid of disturbing Young Master's cultivation. Each time, she only stands for a short while, asks if Young Master is alright, and then goes back. Judging from her complexion... she doesn't seem to be well."

Daiyu! Lin Chen's heart tightened. Yes, he had been in seclusion for two days, attempting to break through to the seventh rank. She must be extremely worried, yet dared not disturb him, only lingering outside... In the bond of "heart and soul intertwined," he could always feel her clear, anxious, and longing palpitations.

"I understand. You've worked hard, continue your duty." Lin Chen said, then left Tingzhu Pavilion without lingering. In a flash, he was out of the pavilion and headed quickly towards Xiaoxiang Pavilion. He didn't use any movement techniques, but his steps were steady and swift. Though seemingly slow, he arrived outside Xiaoxiang Pavilion in the blink of an eye.

The courtyard gate was ajar. Lin Chen was about to push it open when he heard Daiyu's low, coughing voice and Zijuan's words of comfort coming from inside.

"...Zijuan, do you think it's dangerous for Brother Lin to be in seclusion? He was so badly injured last time, and he's only recovered a few days ago... I've been feeling uneasy these past two days, like... like I can sense he's in a lot of pain, yet he's desperately trying to hold on to something..." Daiyu's voice was thick with emotion, clearly from crying again.

"Miss, please don't overthink it. Young Master Lin is highly skilled in martial arts and is blessed with good fortune; he'll definitely emerge from seclusion safely. You yourself haven't fully recovered, and you're standing in the wind every day waiting. If Young Master Lin knew, wouldn't he be even more heartbroken?" Zijuan's voice was full of helplessness.

His figure seemed like a dream, and she was afraid that in the blink of an eye, he would disappear again.

"Yu'er, I'm back." Lin Chen quickly stepped forward, knelt down in front of Daiyu, and gazed tenderly at her pale, thin, tear-streaked face, his heart breaking. He reached out, wanting to hold her slightly cool hand, but hesitated, afraid of being impolite, and only let it linger in mid-air.

Daiyu finally snapped out of her daze, tears streaming down her face like a broken string of pearls. But unlike last time, she didn't throw herself into his arms and weep uncontrollably. Instead, she gazed at him intently, as if trying to imprint his current state of peace and health, even more radiant than before, deep, deep into her heart. Her pale lips trembled, and after a long, choked sob, she finally managed to squeeze out a hoarse sentence, filled with endless fear and joy:

"You...you're alright...that's wonderful...really...wonderful..."

She reached out, her fingertips trembling, as if wanting to touch his face to confirm this wasn't a dream, but at the very last moment, she timidly withdrew, as if the person before her were fragile glass.

Lin Chen felt a sharp pain in his heart. Forgetting all etiquette, he grasped her cold, trembling hand tightly in his warm, broad palm. With his other hand, he gently wiped away her tears, one by one, with his fingertips.

“Silly Yu’er, I’m fine, really fine. Look, I’m perfectly fine, even better than before.” He softened his voice, as if coaxing his most precious treasure. “But you, why have you lost weight again? Have you not been taking your medicine properly, not resting enough? Didn’t Zijuan say you’ve been standing outside the courtyard these past few days? The wind is so strong, how can your body withstand it?”

His tone carried a reproachful concern, yet it gradually warmed Daiyu’s cold heart. Feeling the real, warm heat from his palm, seeing the undisguised tenderness and concern in his eyes, she finally believed that her Lin brother had truly returned safely, and he looked… even better than before. That calm, unwavering, and profound aura, those bright, deep eyes that seemed to see right through people, all revealed his different nature.

“I… I’m fine, just a little worried…” Daiyu lowered her head, her voice as soft as a mosquito’s buzz, her pale cheeks flushed with a sickly yet real blush from his touch and words.

Zijuan had already tactfully stepped aside, her eyes filled with tears of relief, and quietly picked up the handkerchief that had slipped from her hand.

"You said you were fine, but your hands are so cold, and your cough hasn't completely cleared up." Lin Chen frowned slightly. He could clearly sense that Daiyu's internal energy was weak, her heart and lungs showed signs of stagnation, her cold hadn't fully subsided, her worries had aggravated her illness, and coupled with her already weak constitution, her condition was not optimistic. Now that his cultivation had broken through, his perception had greatly increased, and he could even "see" the faint, sickly grayness shrouding her brows.

"Brother Lin's seclusion... was it successful? Is there anything wrong?" Daiyu raised her head, asking instead of answering, her eyes still filled with unwavering worry. She could sense the change in Lin Chen's aura, a deeper, more expansive feeling that reassured her, yet also gave her a vague feeling that he might have gone through unimaginable hardships.

"It went very smoothly." Lin Chen smiled, not wanting her to worry too much. "Not only did it go smoothly, but it also improved slightly. Look." With a slight thought, a wisp of purplish-gold energy, condensed as if it were solid, warm yet sharp, quietly emerged from his fingertips, swaying gently like a serpent. Reflecting the sunlight, it emitted a faint halo, but without any leaking pressure, showing meticulous control.

"This is..." Although Daiyu was not skilled in martial arts, her upbringing in a prestigious family gave her exceptional insight, and she immediately recognized it as the sign of internal energy being released and condensed into a physical form! This was something only a seventh-rank martial artist could achieve! "Brother Lin, you...you've broken through to the seventh rank?!" Her eyes blazed with immense joy, and her pale face instantly radiated an astonishing light, as if she were even happier than if she herself had received a tremendous benefit.

"Yes, a lucky breakthrough." Lin Chen dispersed his energy, nodded gently, and looked at her joyful expression, his heart filled with warmth. In this world, perhaps only she would so purely and unadulteratedly rejoice in his progress.

"That's wonderful! That's truly wonderful!" Daiyu cried tears of joy, tears streaming down her face again, but these were pure joy. "Brother Lin has reached the seventh rank of cultivation, so... so he'll be able to protect himself even better in the future. I... I can feel more at ease..." She stammered, tightly gripping Lin Chen's hand, as if trying to convey all her joy and relief to him.

"So, you need to get better quickly too, so I can relax, okay?" Lin Chen said, his tone gentle and unyielding. "Come, let me check your pulse."

He helped Daiyu sit down on the balustrade in the corridor, gently placing three fingers on her slender wrist. Daiyu's cheeks flushed slightly, but she did not resist, simply lowering her eyes and letting him do as he pleased.

Lin Chen slowly channeled a wisp of warm, peaceful, purplish-gold inner energy into Daiyu's body, exploring along her meridians. He now had meticulous control over his own power; this wisp of inner energy was as gentle as a spring breeze, used only for exploration and nourishment, and would never harm Daiyu's fragile meridians.

Where the inner energy passed, Daiyu felt a warm, comforting current flowing into her body along Lin Chen's fingers, quickly dispelling the chill and stagnation in her limbs. The tightness in her chest seemed to dissolve with this warmth, and her breathing became much smoother. She couldn't help but let out a soft "Mmm," her pale face flushing even more, as if tinged with a light blush.

Lin Chen's brows furrowed even more deeply. Daiyu's condition was worse than he had sensed. Not only had she caught a cold, causing stagnation in her heart and lungs, but long-term worry and anxiety had also depleted her vital energy, leading to a weakened heart meridian and signs of deficiency in her liver and kidneys. If it weren't for the bond of "heart and soul intertwined," which allowed him to occasionally replenish her with the pure life force fused with the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenation Pill," she would probably have already collapsed. Ordinary medicine could only treat the symptoms, not the root cause. If this continued, it might shorten her lifespan.

"Brother Lin, what's wrong? My illness... is it very troublesome?" Daiyu's heart tightened upon seeing his grave expression.

"It's nothing serious, just a lingering cold and added worries, so you need to take good care of yourself." Lin Chen withdrew his hand, suppressing his anxiety, and comforted him with a gentle smile, "I'll prescribe a medicine, take it on time. Most importantly, relax, don't overthink things, eat well, and rest well. With me here, I'll make sure you get better as soon as possible."

He had already made up his mind. Daiyu's illness stemmed from the dual depletion of her "spirit" and "vital energy," especially the excessive depletion of her mental energy. Ordinary medicine and rest would have limited effect. Perhaps, he could try using his special internal energy—which, after breaking through to the seventh rank and merging with martial arts intent and the bond of her "heart and soul"—to nourish her meridians and soothe her mind daily, supplemented by gentle medicine and dietary therapy tailored to her specific needs, gradually working towards a cure. Even… once his cultivation was more stable and his understanding of the "qi manipulation" was deeper, perhaps he could try guiding the sparse, life-giving primordial energy of heaven and earth to cleanse her marrow and improve her constitution. However, this required long-term planning and was extremely dangerous; it could not be attempted without absolute certainty.

"Yes, I'll listen to Brother Lin," Daiyu nodded gently, full of trust in him. With him by her side, even the greatest problems seemed insignificant.

"Young master, young lady, the medicine is ready." Zijuan brought over the warm medicine.

Lin Chen took the bowl, tested the temperature—it was just right. He scooped up a spoonful, gently blew on it, and held it to Daiyu's lips: "Here, drink the medicine first."

Daiyu's cheeks flushed even more, a little shy, but looking into Lin Chen's focused and gentle eyes, she obediently opened her mouth and swallowed the medicine. The medicine was bitter, but her heart trembled with sweetness.

Spoon by spoonful, Lin Chen patiently fed her the bowl of medicine, then took the warm water Zijuan handed her to rinse her mouth. Finally, as if by magic, he took out an oil paper package from his sleeve (actually the system space), inside which were several delicate pieces of rock sugar hawthorn cake with the fragrance of plum blossoms. "Eat some of this to get rid of the bitterness."

Watching him bustle about, so attentive and considerate, Daiyu's eyes shimmered with tears, her heart overflowing with immense happiness. She ate small bites of hawthorn cake, the sweetness spreading from her tongue to her heart.

After feeding her the medicine, Lin Chen chatted with Daiyu for a while, mostly telling her amusing, trivial stories to cheer her up. Seeing that the light sorrow between her brows had mostly faded and that she looked tired, Lin Chen helped her back to her room to rest. He then used his gentle inner energy to help her fall asleep peacefully. Only after watching her breathe evenly and drift into a deep sleep did Lin Chen carefully tuck her in and leave.

Stepping out of the Xiaoxiang Pavilion, the setting sun cast a long shadow of him. The gentleness on his face gradually faded, replaced by calm and contemplation.

Daiyu's illness must be cured as soon as possible. This requires top-tier medical skills, rare medicinal herbs, and perhaps even special cultivation techniques or a fortuitous encounter. It seems he must seek help from Princess Zhaoyang; perhaps the royal treasury holds the right remedy. Furthermore, he himself must intensify his cultivation, enhancing his control over "Qi" and "Spirit" to do more for her.

As for the threats within and outside the Jia family… Now that he has broken through to the seventh rank and possesses the Xuanlong Token, his alliance with Princess Zhaoyang should temporarily deter the villains. However, Prince Zhongshun will not let this go unpunished; even more dangerous assassinations and conspiracies are likely on the way. He needs to consolidate his cultivation level quickly, familiarize himself with the power of the seventh rank, and especially master the practical skills of "Qi control."

"By the way, how's Ying Jiu doing with the investigation into Xue Pan and those 'friends in the martial arts world'?" Lin Chen thought to himself, then returned to Tingzhu Pavilion and contacted Ying Jiu again in a special way.

A moment later, Ying Jiu's figure quietly appeared, and she whispered, "Young Master, Xue Pan and his accomplices have been hiding in the backyard of 'Happy Forest' to recuperate since the diarrhea incident that day, and haven't gone out. But last night, several uninvited guests with concealed auras and high skills sneaked into 'Happy Forest,' had a brief private conversation with Xue Pan and the others, and then left. Those people had a cold aura and were well-trained, unlike ordinary Jianghu outlaws, but rather seemed like... professional assassins or death squads. The leader among them is probably at least at the mid-stage of the seventh rank. After they left, Xue Pan and the others seemed to have received new instructions and have begun to prepare in secret. The target... seems to still be you, Young Master, but the specific plan has not yet been ascertained."

Professional assassins? Mid-stage of the seventh rank? A cold glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. It seems that Prince Zhongshun's side is already impatient and wants to use even more elite forces. Xue Pan and his gang of useless fools were indeed just a decoy in the open.

“Understood. Continue surveillance, but be extremely careful; the other side may have counter-tracking capabilities. Report to me immediately if there are any unusual movements,” Lin Chen instructed.

“Yes,” Ying Jiu replied, then added, “Also, Lord Ying Qi sent word that Her Highness the Princess requests your presence at the ‘Moon-Embracing Pavilion’ when it is convenient. It seems… there have been new discoveries, regarding clues to the ‘Map of Mountains and Rivers,’ and… the movements of Prince Zhongshun.”

New discoveries? Lin Chen’s heart stirred. “Understood, I will go tomorrow.”

Ying Jiu disappeared again.

Night deepened, and the Listening Bamboo Pavilion returned to silence. Lin Chen sat cross-legged in the quiet room, but did not immediately begin cultivation. Instead, he immersed his mind in the bond of “Heart-Soul Connection.” He could clearly sense that Daiyu on the other end was already asleep, her breathing steady, though still carrying a trace of frailty. He could feel her complete dependence and affection for him, which filled his heart with the responsibility and motivation to protect her.

"Yu'er, don't worry, I will definitely make you better and eliminate all threats so that you can live a peaceful and joyful life." Lin Chen silently vowed in his heart.

Immediately, he composed himself, consolidating his early seventh-grade realm while meticulously studying the profound techniques of "condensing qi," "transforming form," and "controlling qi with divine will" within the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*. Combining this with his own understanding of the "Thunderclap Fist" and "Vajra Fist," he attempted to integrate his qi control abilities more deeply into his fist and movement techniques, making the most thorough preparations for the impending fierce battle.

He knew that peaceful days were likely numbered. Prince Zhongshun's sword hung over his head. He himself needed to take the initiative, acquiring more resources, intelligence, and power to protect himself and his loved ones in this increasingly dangerous vortex.

Night enveloped Jinling. In every corner of the city, the web of conspiracies targeting Lin Chen was quietly tightening. A greater storm, surrounding the Xuanzhen Martial Arts Arsenal, royal power struggles, and family feuds, was slowly brewing and gathering in this seemingly peaceful night.


Chapter 45 The Princess's New Decree, Danger Looms at the Door

The next morning, as dawn broke.

Lin Chen finished his night of cultivation and meditation, and slowly opened his eyes. His eyes shone with a purple-gold divine light, and his aura became increasingly steady and refined; his early seventh-grade realm was now firmly established. Under the guidance of the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*, his understanding of the Dao of "Qi Control" had deepened further. He could now more freely draw upon and manipulate the sparse primordial energy within a few feet of his body. Although it was still insufficient to support prolonged Qi control flight or unleash powerful long-range attacks, the enhancements to his movement speed and technique variations, as well as his meticulous control over his own power, were now vastly different from before.

He changed into a neat blue outfit, over which he wore a worn dark blue cloak, concealing his aura to appear as an ordinary wandering swordsman. He carefully wore the magnolia-shaped jade pendant given to him by Daiyu, and also put away the Xuanlong Token and the silk scroll containing clues to the "Map of Mountains and Rivers." After giving a brief explanation, he quietly left Tingzhu Pavilion and headed towards the "Lanyue Pavilion" in the western part of the city.

Lanyue Pavilion remained serene, the elegant lakeside room filled with the aroma of tea. Princess Zhaoyang, Zhao Lingxi, had already arrived, still dressed in a light purple casual robe, without makeup, gazing at the morning mist on the lake. Her figure was graceful, her profile particularly beautiful in the morning light, yet shrouded in a faint, lingering solemnity.

"Lingxi," Lin Chen entered the elegant room, bowing in greeting. In private, the two had agreed to address each other by their given names.

Zhao Lingxi turned around, nodded slightly to him, gesturing for him to sit down, and personally poured him a cup of "Jade Dew Tea" that she had already brewed, pushing it towards him. "Sit. You look well; it seems your cultivation has completely stabilized."

"A slight improvement." Lin Chen didn't stand on ceremony, sitting down opposite her, taking a sip of the tea. The tea was warm and fragrant, dispelling the morning chill. "Ying Qi said you made a new discovery?"

“Hmm.” Zhao Lingxi put down her teacup, her expression solemn. “Two things. First, regarding the clues about the ‘Map of Mountains and Rivers’. Based on the ‘Yin Map’ you brought back and the fragment of the ‘Yang Map’ in my possession, plus Master Shi’s deductions over the past few days, we have now basically determined that the other key clue, or rather, the location that may hold the true core secrets of the ‘Map of Mountains and Rivers’, is located in the east of Jinling City, beneath the underground palace of the ‘Medicine Master Glazed Pagoda’ built by the previous dynasty at Jiming Temple.”

The underground palace of the Medicine Buddha Pagoda at Jiming Temple! It indeed matches the description on the silk scroll from yesterday. Lin Chen's heart stirred slightly: "Jiming Temple is a famous temple in Jinling, with flourishing incense offerings and strict security. It also has close ties with the royal family and powerful figures. The underground palace is a forbidden area, extremely difficult for ordinary people to enter. Investigating it will likely be difficult."

"Indeed," Zhao Lingxi nodded. "The current abbot of Jiming Temple, Master Liaokong, is deeply versed in Buddhism and has some connection with my father, the Emperor. He enjoys considerable prestige in the court and among the people. The temple also has martial monks protecting it, making its strength not to be underestimated. More importantly, Prince Zhongshun… seems to be paying attention to Jiming Temple as well. My men have discovered that recently, people suspected to be from Prince Zhongshun's residence have repeatedly entered and exited Jiming Temple under the guise of worshipping Buddha, and have been in close contact with a supervising monk. I suspect they may have also received some information, and perhaps… have already taken the lead and are eyeing the underground palace."

Prince Zhongshun has also set his sights on Jiming Temple? Lin Chen's eyes narrowed. This wasn't surprising; given Prince Zhongshun's determination to obtain the "Xuanzhen Armory," he wouldn't let any related clues slip by. It seemed this trip to Jiming Temple would likely lead to a direct confrontation with Prince Zhongshun's men.

“Therefore, I need you to go to Jiming Temple as soon as possible to investigate the underground palace, confirm whether the clues exist, and… to what extent Prince Zhongshun’s people have infiltrated.” Zhao Lingxi looked at Lin Chen, her gaze intense. “This matter should not be carried out openly; it must be done secretly. I will arrange for Shadow Seven and Shadow Nine to meet you outside and prepare a pass to Jiming Temple and a rough structural map of the underground palace for you. However, you must enter the core area of ​​the underground palace alone. With your current seventh-grade cultivation, as long as you don’t encounter any reclusive old monsters in the temple or the true masters ambushed by Prince Zhongshun, you should be able to protect yourself. Remember, the primary objective is to confirm the clues. If there is danger, prioritize your own safety and do not force it.”

“Understood.” Lin Chen replied in a deep voice. Investigating the Jiming Temple underground palace, though dangerous, is a crucial step in obtaining the complete clues to the “Map of Mountains and Rivers,” and he must go. Moreover, he also wanted to see exactly who Prince Zhongshun had sent.

"The second matter," Zhao Lingxi's tone grew even more serious, "is about Prince Zhongshun's next move. According to reliable intelligence, Prince Zhongshun is extremely furious about your display of the Xuanlong Token at your birthday banquet and your public defeat of Jia She. He has secretly dispatched several elite members of his 'Shadow Guard' to infiltrate Jinling. The leader, nicknamed 'Ghost Hand,' is at least at the mid-stage of the seventh rank, skilled in assassination, poison, and tracking. He is ruthless and infamous in the underworld. Their target is you. Moreover, Prince Zhongshun seems to be colluding with certain evil sects in Jiangnan, and may have even used some... unconventional methods. You must be extremely careful these days, paying attention to everything you do, from food and clothing to shelter and transportation, so as not to give anyone an opportunity to take advantage. I have also secretly increased the number of people protecting the Jia family, but even the best-laid plans can have flaws, and your own vigilance is of utmost importance."

"Shadow Guard? Ghost Hand? Mid-stage Rank 7?" A cold glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. They'd finally arrived, and a big fish at that. A mid-stage Rank 7, combined with poison for assassination, was indeed a formidable threat. However, he was now also a Rank 7, possessing the "Thunder Fist" and "Qi Control" abilities; he might not be without a fighting chance.

"We'll meet force with force, and water with earth," Lin Chen said calmly. "I'll be careful. However, passive defense is not a long-term solution. Lingxi, regarding Prince Zhongshun's plans in Jiangnan, especially his connections with the Xue and Shi families, as well as those Jianghu forces and evil sects, can we find more conclusive evidence, or… identify key points to strike?"

"A proactive attack?" A hint of admiration flashed in Zhao Lingxi's eyes, then she pondered, "Prince Zhongshun is shrewd and difficult to prove anything against. However, there are perhaps a few key points in his scheme. First, the Xue family controls the Grand Canal and the imperial merchant channels, which are crucial for transferring funds and supplies. Second, the Shi family's influence in the northern frontier may involve collusion with certain forces in the north. Third, there's the 'Xuanzhen Armory,' which he's determined to acquire and has invested heavily in. If we can severely damage him in this matter, it will disrupt his plans and perhaps even lure him out. The trip to Jiming Temple might be an opportunity. But remember, we mustn't be hasty and alert him."

"I understand," Lin Chen nodded. It seems that the Jiming Temple underground palace is not only related to the "Map of Mountains and Rivers," but may also become the first battlefield where he directly confronts Prince Zhongshun's forces.

"The journey to Jiming Temple is fraught with danger. This 'Pure Heart Jade Pendant' has the effect of calming the mind, warding off evil spirits, and providing early warning of danger. Keep it close to your body." Zhao Lingxi took off a jade pendant, entirely emerald green and carved with phoenix patterns, from her wrist and handed it to Lin Chen. "Also, take this bottle of 'Hundred Herbs Detoxifying Pill' and 'Golden Wound Relief Ointment' with you, just in case."

"Thank you." Lin Chen didn't refuse and solemnly accepted them. He knew that the princess truly regarded him as an important ally, which was why she was giving him so much support.

"There's no time to lose. Go back and prepare today. Tomorrow at midnight, I will have Ying Qi deliver the credentials and map to Tingzhu Pavilion. The day after tomorrow morning, there will be a Dharma assembly at Jiming Temple, with many worshippers. It will be a good opportunity to infiltrate and investigate." Zhao Lingxi stood up. "Take care."

"You too." Lin Chen stood up, cupped his hands in farewell.

After leaving Lan Yue Pavilion, Lin Chen didn't immediately return to the Jia Mansion. Instead, he detoured to Huichuntang, the largest pharmacy in Jinling City. Following the instructions of Doctor Chen and his own diagnosis of Daiyu's condition, he purchased a batch of high-quality, nourishing medicinal herbs, and also specially bought several rare ingredients with calming and soothing effects. Daiyu's illness required both internal and external treatment; medication and his own internal energy cultivation were needed simultaneously.

Back at Tingzhu Pavilion, it was already afternoon. He entrusted the herbs to a reliable servant, instructing him to prepare them according to the written prescription and method, and deliver them to Xiaoxiang Pavilion on time. Afterwards, he went to Xiaoxiang Pavilion again.

Daiyu had just taken her medicine and was resting on a soft couch by the window. Her face was still pale, but the lingering sorrow between her brows seemed to have dissipated somewhat. Upon seeing Lin Chen, her eyes immediately lit up, and she struggled to sit up.

"Don't move, lean back properly." Lin Chen stepped forward, pressed her shoulder, and sat down on the edge of the couch. He naturally took her wrist again and channeled a warm and peaceful purple-gold inner energy into her to soothe her meridians, dispel the cold, and nourish her heart.

This time, he was even more focused. His inner energy flowed not only with the immense vitality of the "Nine-Turn Rejuvenating Pill," but also with his newfound understanding of "qi" and "spirit" after breaking through to the seventh rank, especially the methods of "nourishing qi and returning to the origin" and "warming the spirit" from the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*. Wherever his inner energy passed, Daiyu felt a warmer, gentler current, as if carrying the warmth of sunshine, slowly permeating her limbs and bones. It not only dispelled the chill and stagnation in her body, but also seemed to quietly melt away the pent-up emotions, anxiety, and fear that had accumulated in her heart for many days. Her mind was indescribably peaceful and comfortable, and before she knew it, she leaned against the soft pillow and fell into a deep sleep, a faint, reassuring smile on her lips.

Lin Chen didn't stop, continuing to nourish her with his inner energy for another half an hour until her breathing was completely stable and her complexion regained a rosy hue. Only then did he slowly withdraw his inner energy. He noticed that the effect of this nourishment was much better than yesterday; Daiyu's heartbeat seemed stronger as well. This put his mind at ease.

"It seems that with my current internal energy quality, combined with the nurturing methods of the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*, and with long-term persistence, I should be able to gradually improve Yu'er's constitution and eradicate the root of her illness. However, this is not something that can be achieved overnight; it requires perseverance and cannot be interrupted." Lin Chen pondered, a sense of unease lingering. He was about to travel to Jiming Temple, a perilous journey with an uncertain return date. He feared that Daiyu's nurturing treatment would have to be interrupted during this time.

“I need to think of a way…” His gaze fell on the warm, smooth magnolia-shaped jade pendant on his chest, and a sudden thought struck him. This family heirloom jade pendant of Daiyu seemed to have a faint effect of calming the mind and gathering spiritual energy. Perhaps… he could try sealing a wisp of his pure inner energy and protective divine will within the pendant using a special method, allowing it to continuously and slowly release its nourishing power to protect Daiyu's mind while he was away? This idea was somewhat bold and required extremely high control over “qi” and spiritual power, but with his current seventh-grade cultivation and the wonders of the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*, perhaps he could give it a try.

However, this matter required preparation and could not be rushed, lest something go wrong. He decided to try it after returning from Jiming Temple.

Lin Chen sat in Xiaoxiang Pavilion for a while longer, until Daiyu was sound asleep, before giving some instructions to Zijuan and Xueyan and quietly leaving.

Back at Tingzhu Pavilion, he began preparing for his trip to Jiming Temple the next day. He checked his belongings, familiarized himself with the rough map and underground palace structure diagram of Jiming Temple (though rudimentary) sent by Ying Qi, and adjusted his own state. At the same time, he remained highly vigilant, wary of any possible attack by the "Shadow Guards" sent by Prince Zhongshun.

The night passed peacefully.

The next day, Lin Chen informed Jia Zheng under the pretext of "visiting a friend." Jia Zheng, knowing his current special status and the rationale behind his actions, didn't ask further questions, only reminding him to be careful.

At midnight, Ying Qi indeed arrived quietly, bringing a pass to Jiming Temple (a letter of recommendation for a retired official, bearing a special seal and forged from one of the most devout Buddhists), a relatively detailed sketch of the underground palace structure, and the coded signal and location for the outer rendezvous.

"Young Master, the situation within the temple is complex. Abbot Liaokong is unfathomable, and the other head monks are also quite powerful. The entrance to the underground palace is at the bottom of the Medicine Master's Glazed Pagoda, guarded by martial monks; ordinary people are not allowed to enter. With this letter of recommendation, you might be able to enter the pagoda to offer incense, but whether you can descend into the underground palace depends on the circumstances. Prince Zhongshun's men may also be mixed among the worshippers or monks, so please be careful," Ying Qi instructed in a low voice.

"Thank you," Lin Chen replied, noting it down.

After Ying Qi left, Lin Chen sat quietly for half an hour to adjust his state to its best before lying down fully clothed and closing his eyes to rest.

As dawn broke, Lin Chen rose. He changed into a slightly worn but still decent scholar's robe, donned a turban, and subtly disguised himself to conceal his striking appearance and demeanor, making him look like a somewhat down-on-his-luck but courteous scholar in his thirties. He carefully concealed his essentials (the Xuanlong Token, the Qingxin Jade Pendant, elixirs, some silver, and a letter of recommendation) on his person before quietly leaving the Jia residence.

Jiming Temple, located on Jilong Mountain east of Jinling City, is a grand temple built against the mountainside, with numerous halls and ancient trees reaching for the sky. It is the premier temple in Jinling. Today, the temple is holding a grand prayer ceremony for the birthday of the Medicine Buddha. Before dawn, the road leading to the temple gate is already bustling with people, men and women, young and old, scholars, farmers, merchants, and all sorts of people, including many high-ranking officials and nobles in fine clothes.

Lin Chen mingled in the crowd, unhurriedly ascending the stone steps. His aura was restrained, his gaze calmly sweeping over his surroundings. Sure enough, he spotted several individuals whose presence differed significantly from the ordinary worshippers. Some appeared to be ordinary merchants, yet their steps were steady and their eyes sharp; others posed as devout lay Buddhists, but secretly observed certain directions; still others wore monks' robes, yet their demeanor lacked the composure of monks, displaying instead a sharpness and vigilance. These people were likely sent by Prince Zhongshun, or were spies within the temple connected to him.

He remained impassive, following the flow of people into the mountain gate, passing through the Hall of Heavenly Kings and the Great Buddha Hall, heading straight for the temple's tallest and most prominent building—the Medicine Buddha Pagoda.

The Medicine Buddha Pagoda, thirteen stories high and constructed entirely of glazed bricks and tiles, shimmered and shone under the sunlight, exuding a solemn and sacred aura. In front of the pagoda lay a spacious plaza, where a ritual platform had been erected. Monks from the temple were chanting sutras and performing rituals; the sounds of bells and chimes filled the air, and incense smoke wafted through the air, creating a solemn and respectful atmosphere. Many pilgrims prostrated themselves and offered incense, praying for peace and well-being.

Lin Chen's goal was the entrance to the underground palace beneath the pagoda. He bypassed the platform and approached the bottom level. Monks guarded the bottom, and a wooden sign at the entrance read, "Underground Palace, Restricted to Unauthorized Personnel." Several pilgrims nearby peered curiously, but were all politely but firmly persuaded to leave by the monks.

Lin Chen observed for a moment and saw that there were four martial monks guarding the entrance. Their auras were deep and concentrated, indicating at least an eighth-rank cultivation level. The leader, a middle-aged monk, had bulging temples and bright, piercing eyes, suggesting he was likely at the peak of the eighth rank or even just entering the seventh. Forcing his way in was definitely out of the question.

He thought for a moment, took out the letter of recommendation given to him by Ying Qi, straightened his clothes, and put on an appropriate expression of respect and hope. He stepped forward and bowed to the middle-aged monk in the lead position: "Greetings, Master. I have been entrusted by someone to enter the underground palace to pray for the ancestors and offer eternal lamps. I hope you will grant me this favor." As he spoke, he presented the letter of recommendation with both hands.

The middle-aged monk accepted the letter of recommendation, carefully examining the seal and handwriting. He then glanced at Lin Chen a few times, a hint of doubt flashing in his eyes. However, the seal and signature on the letter were indeed the handwriting of a high-ranking protector of the temple (the retired old official), and could not be faked. He pondered for a moment and said, "Amitabha. The underground palace is a sacred place, not usually open to the public. However, since you have this intention, and it was recommended by Elder Chen… well, please follow me, but please remember that you must not make noise, move around at will, or touch any objects inside the underground palace. After making your offerings, you must leave immediately."

"Yes, I understand, thank you, Master." Lin Chen felt relieved and quickly agreed.

The middle-aged monk whispered a few words to a younger monk beside him, then said to Lin Chen, "Please, benefactor."

He led the way, with Lin Chen following closely behind. The other two monks also came up, one in front and one behind, clearly not entirely at ease. The four of them passed through a small door on one side of the tower's base and slowly descended a slightly dim stone staircase. Every few steps along the walls on either side of the staircase, an ever-burning lamp emitted a dim yellow light, and the air was filled with a faint scent of sandalwood and dust.

The further down the stairs they went, the cooler the air became, and the dimmer the light. After climbing about thirty or forty steps, a heavy ebony door, studded with copper nails, appeared ahead. There was a copper lock on the door, but it wasn't fully locked. The middle-aged monk took out a specially made key, inserted it into the keyhole, and turned it gently.

With a "click," the copper lock sprang open. The monk pushed open the door, and an even stronger aroma, a mixture of aged incense and some strange herbs, wafted towards him.

Behind the gate lay a massive, octagonal stone chamber. The chamber was three zhang high and several zhang in circumference, its walls inlaid with countless strange gemstones the size of pigeon eggs, emitting a soft white light (similar to the "Lingyun" stones in the Qixia Mountain grottoes, but with a weaker glow), illuminating the entire underground palace in a hazy glow. In the center of the chamber stood a seated statue of the Medicine Buddha, about ten zhang tall, carved entirely of white jade, its countenance solemn and lifelike. Before the statue were an incense table, prayer cushions, and thousands upon thousands of burning lamps, their tiny flames converging into a sea of ​​light, a truly magnificent sight. Along the walls were rows of tall wooden shelves reaching to the ceiling, neatly arranged with countless scrolls of scriptures, Buddha statues, ritual implements, and some dusty, ancient-looking boxes—clearly treasures offered or collected throughout the ages.

This was the core of the Jiming Temple underground palace, the place where the Medicine Buddha was enshrined and where the temple's precious heritage and relics were stored.

"Benefactor, please make your offering here. The offering is limited to the time it takes for one incense stick to burn. This humble monk will wait outside." The middle-aged martial monk said, pressing his palms together. He and two other martial monks retreated outside the door, but did not close it, clearly still keeping watch.

Lin Chen nodded, walked to the incense table, took three incense sticks, lit them under the ever-burning lamp, and respectfully inserted them into the incense burner. He then took out the silver notes he had prepared beforehand (as offerings for incense oil) and placed them in the donation box. After doing this, he did not leave immediately. Instead, he pretended to devoutly admire the Buddha statue and his surroundings, while his eyes quickly scanned the entire underground palace, especially the wooden shelves and boxes, secretly sensing the movements of the "Yang Map" fragment and the "Yin Map" jade slip in his possession.

However, to his disappointment, neither the map nor the jade slip showed any special reaction or resonance. This underground palace was vast, and the treasures numerous, but it seemed… there were no clues to the "Map of Mountains and Rivers"? Was his deduction wrong? Or were the clues hidden extremely deeply?

He walked slowly and impassively through the underground palace, his gaze carefully scanning every possible hidden mechanism or secret compartment. The stone walls were smooth, the Buddha statues solemn, the wooden shelves neatly arranged… everything seemed normal, yet it exuded an inexplicable, overly "orderly" feeling, as if it lacked… the weathered, patina of time? Especially some of the wooden shelves and boxes; though covered in dust, upon closer inspection, the wood didn't seem particularly ancient.

Suddenly, his gaze fell upon the base of the white jade seated statue of the Medicine Buddha. The base was also carved from white jade, shaped like a lotus platform, with intricate lotus and cloud patterns. But on the front of the base, in the center of the lotus petals, he vaguely saw what appeared to be an extremely tiny gap, slightly different in color from the surrounding area, its shape… like an irregular, keyhole-like indentation? Moreover, the lotus patterns around that indentation, upon closer inspection, seemed to form a strange, star-map-like pattern?

A chill ran through him. He subtly moved closer, pretending to bend down to adjust his robes, but actually intently examining the surface. Indeed! The indentation was extremely well-hidden, almost seamlessly integrated with the lotus pattern. Had he not possessed extraordinary eyesight and been trained in both "Yang" and "Yin" astrology, making him highly sensitive to star chart patterns, he would have been completely unable to spot it! Moreover, the star chart pattern, though only a few strokes long, subtly corresponded to a specific combination of stars in the "Yin" astrological chart he had copied!

The clue was here! This indentation was likely the mechanism that required a specific "key" or "token" to activate! And that star chart was the clue!

He suppressed his excitement and was about to examine the details of the indentation more closely to try and determine what kind of "key" he needed when—

"Eh? This benefactor, have you discovered something interesting?" A gentle voice with a strange magnetism suddenly rang out from not far behind Lin Chen.

Lin Chen's alarm bells rang, and he instantly turned around. He saw that, somehow, at the entrance to the underground palace, besides the three martial monks, there was another person. This person was about forty years old, wearing a worn gray monk's robe, tall and thin, with a gaunt face, narrow, slightly squinting eyes, and a warm smile on his face. He held a string of ebony prayer beads and was slowly walking towards him. His steps seemed slow, but he reached Lin Chen in a few steps, his gaze landing on Lin Chen with a half-smile, or more precisely, on the base of the Buddha statue that Lin Chen had just been staring at.

When did this person enter? Why didn't the three monks stop him? Lin Chen's heart sank. He hadn't even noticed this person's approach beforehand! Moreover, he sensed an extremely subtle yet chilling and dangerous aura emanating from this person! This was definitely a master, his cultivation probably... even above his own! At least a mid-stage seventh rank! Furthermore, judging from his attire, he seemed to be a monk from the temple, but his demeanor...

"Amitabha, this humble monk is Liao Chen, the steward of the Sutra Repository of this temple." The gray-robed monk clasped his hands in a respectful bow, his smile warm. "Seeing that you seem interested in the base of the Buddha statue, do you find the lotus carving exquisite?"

Liao Chen? Steward of the Sutra Repository? Lin Chen's vigilance intensified, but his face remained impassive as he returned the bow, saying, "So it's Master Liao Chen. I merely noticed that the lotus pattern is slightly different from the others, and out of curiosity, I took a closer look. I had no other intentions. Please forgive me, Master."

"Hehe, it's alright. The lotus pattern on this base was carved by craftsmen of the previous dynasty; it is indeed unique." Liao Chen smiled, but his gaze remained fixed on the base, a barely perceptible glint of greed and inquiry flashing in his eyes. "Since you were recommended by Elder Chen, you must also be a connoisseur of antiquities. I wonder if you have any knowledge of this relic of the 'Xuanzhen' lineage from the previous dynasty?"

Xuanzhen! He directly mentioned "Xuanzhen"! Lin Chen's heart trembled, but his face showed a perfectly appropriate look of bewilderment: "Xuanzhen? Master, you mean..."

"Oh, nothing, just a casual question." Liao Chen seemed to realize his slip of the tongue, chuckled, but his gaze deepened as he looked at Lin Chen, as if trying to see through him. "You have finished your offerings, benefactor. This place is cold and damp; it is not advisable to stay long. Please return."

This was a clear signal to leave. Moreover, Lin Chen could sense that the three martial monks who had been guarding the entrance were now subtly closing in, their gazes fixed on him. This Liao Chen was likely Prince Zhongshun's mole at Jiming Temple, or perhaps even a member of the "Shadow Guard" in disguise! He realized that his attention to the base mechanism had aroused suspicion!

This place was no place to linger! Lin Chen's mind raced, and he immediately cupped his hands and said, "Yes, I will take my leave now. Thank you, Master, thank you to all the masters." With that, he turned and walked towards the entrance of the underground palace, his steps unhurried, as if he were truly just an ordinary pilgrim.

Liao Chen still wore a smile, watching him leave until Lin Chen's figure disappeared on the stone steps. Only then did the smile slowly fade, and a sinister glint flashed in his eyes.

"This person... is not simple. The way he looked at that pedestal just now... was definitely not ordinary curiosity." Liao Chen muttered to himself, then said to the leading middle-aged monk, "Keep a close eye on him. If he makes any unusual moves after leaving the temple, or contacts anyone suspicious... you know what to do."

"Yes, Uncle-Master." The middle-aged monk respectfully replied, a fierce glint flashing in his eyes.

Liao Chen ignored him, turned and walked to the Medicine Buddha pedestal, squatted down, and carefully examined the indentation in the center of the lotus pattern. He stretched out his finger and gently stroked it, his eyes gleaming with excitement and greed.
