“After searching for so long… has the clue to the ‘key’ finally appeared? Could that person… be the one carrying the ‘key’? Or… did he just notice something amiss?” Liao Chen muttered to himself, then sneered, “Regardless, better to kill the innocent than let the guilty go free. It’s time to make a move on ‘Ghost Hand’…”

Inside the underground palace, the ever-burning lamp burned silently. The murderous intent and greed hidden beneath the compassionate Buddha statue had already subtly permeated the air.

Lin Chen strode out of the Medicine Master's Glazed Pagoda, blending into the crowd in the square. He could feel several unfriendly gazes, silently locking onto him from within the pagoda and among the crowd. Liao Chen, as expected, didn't intend to let him go.

“This place is not safe to linger; I need to leave the Jiming Temple area as soon as possible.” Lin Chen thought to himself, quickening his pace and heading down the mountain. At the same time, he secretly communicated with the “Pure Heart Jade Pendant” in his robes. The pendant emitted a faint coolness, a warning of the hidden malice around him.

However, just as he reached the vicinity of the mountain gate and was about to blend into the flow of people coming down the mountain, a strange thing happened!

Not far ahead, a ragged little beggar, who had been squatting on the ground playing with pebbles, suddenly cried out in pain, clutching his stomach and rolling on the ground, foaming at the mouth, his face instantly turning ashen!

"Ah! Someone's dead!"

"Is it the plague? Run!"

The surrounding crowd erupted into chaos, panicked and fleeing in all directions, completely blocking the already crowded mountain gate! Amidst the confusion, several figures, previously hidden in the crowd, moved like ghosts, using the crowd as cover to silently and rapidly approach Lin Chen! Their hands gleamed with cold light—poisoned short blades and hidden weapons! One figure, exuding the coldest and most ruthless aura, pounced from the shadow of a large tree to the side, his five fingers forming claws, the tips black and carrying a foul stench, aimed straight for Lin Chen's vital point in the back! His speed was astonishing, revealing him to be a mid-stage seventh-rank cultivator!

A ghostly hand! The Shadow Guard's assassination attempt had been launched brazenly in broad daylight, amidst the throngs of worshippers at the gates of Jiming Temple!


Chapter 46 Bloodbath at the Mountain Gate, Liao Chen Appears

Before the mountain gate, chaos erupted as people and horses stumbled, screams and cries shaking the heavens. The little beggar's "sudden death" and the sudden chaos in the crowd were clearly premeditated, designed to create panic, disrupt the situation, and create the perfect opportunity for the assassination! The assassin lurking in the chaos, like a shark smelling blood, instantly bared his ferocious fangs!

Facing Lin Chen, three assassins disguised as worshippers, using the shoving of the crowd as cover, had closed in to within a few feet of him. Their poisoned short blades and hidden weapons, like the spitting tongues of venomous snakes, sealed off his space to dodge left and right. The angles were cunning and ruthless, aimed directly at his throat, heart, and groin! All three were at the eighth rank of cultivation, and their perfect coordination clearly indicated that they were well-trained assassins!

From the side, the most sinister and ruthless figure—Ghost Hand—had already pounced! He was thin, dressed in ordinary gray shorts, and his face was so plain he'd be easily lost in a crowd. Only his hands stood out: large knuckles, an unnatural bluish-black complexion, and sharp, black fingertips—clearly indicating he had mastered some kind of vicious claw technique, coated with potent poison! His pounce was swift as a ghost, bringing with it a nauseating, foul stench. His five fingers formed a claw, aimed straight at Lin Chen's back. Even before the claw's attack arrived, the chilling killing intent and poisonous aura made him feel as if he had fallen into an ice cave, his blood and qi freezing!

A pincer attack from both sides, the killing trap was set! An ordinary early-stage seventh-rank martial artist, caught off guard by this sudden attack and perfect coordination, would likely be thrown into disarray, either dead or seriously injured!

However, Lin Chen was no longer the Lin Chen who had just recovered from severe injuries at Tingzhu Pavilion and could only fight for his life with unorthodox methods! He was now a seventh-rank martial artist with a solid foundation, a nascent divine will, and had truly entered the realm of "Qi Control"! Faced with this sudden and deadly trap, though shocked, he remained completely calm. The "Clear Heart Technique" activated on its own, instantly dispelling the mental erosion and blood stagnation caused by the poisonous gas, leaving his mind as clear as an ice mirror.

With the aid of the "Qi Control Technique," his light and agile footwork displayed astonishing explosive power and nimbleness within a limited space! His body twisted unpredictably, like a willow catkin in the wind, narrowly avoiding the most lethal attacks from the three short blades in front of him. Only one blade grazed his left sleeve, drawing a trail of blood. Simultaneously, his left palm struck out with lightning speed, the wind whistling, carrying a force that was both powerful and yielding—the "Push" technique, a fusion of the Vajra Fist intent and the wonders of Qi Control. It wasn't a direct confrontation, but rather a use of the opponent's force, slightly deflecting the poisoned short blade of one assassin on his right, causing it to collide with another assassin's weapon with a crisp "clang," sparks flying!

His main focus and strength were concentrated on the most dangerous "ghost hand" behind him! Just as the ghost hand's dark, poisonous claws were about to touch his back, Lin Chen suddenly turned around, his eyes flashing with purple-gold divine light, and his right fist shot out from under his ribs like a dragon emerging from the abyss!

There was no dazzling lightning, no deafening thunder. This punch was silent and completely restrained. Yet, wherever the fist passed, the air seemed to be instantly sucked out and compressed, forming a tiny, heart-stopping collapse! A thin, condensed, almost tangible layer of purplish-gold qi covered the fist. This qi circulated, faintly forming a miniature, constantly rotating, pale golden Vajra phantom, within which wisps of lightning danced! This was a punch that he had initially fused with the fist intent of the "Vajra Fist," the thunderous power of the "Thunderbolt Fist," and the "purplish-gold qi" and "qi control" he had condensed after breaking through to the seventh rank! Although he hadn't used the complete moves of the "Thunderbolt Fist," it already contained its essence, its power far exceeding the full-force attack of an ordinary early-stage seventh-rank martial artist!

Fist and claw clashed!

"Bang!"

A muffled, deafening explosion! There was no earth-shattering roar, but at the center of the collision, a visible, distorted, pale golden shockwave erupted! Where the shockwave passed, the blue bricks on the ground shattered with a "crack," and rubble was flung outwards as if by an invisible hand! Several unfortunate worshippers nearby were struck by the shockwave, instantly struck as if by a heavy blow, spitting blood as they flew backwards, causing even greater chaos.

The indifference and cruelty on Ghost Hand's face were instantly replaced by unbelievable horror! He felt an overwhelming, unstoppable, and incomparably powerful fist force, containing a violent penetrating force, surge forth from his opponent's fist like a volcanic eruption, instantly tearing apart the venomous energy on his claws and slamming into the meridians of his arm! The fist force was not only powerful but also carried a strange vibration and heat, as if tiny bolts of lightning were exploding within his meridians, frantically destroying and burning! His signature "Mysterious Netherworld Ghost Claw," capable of piercing through metal and stone, was like ice meeting boiling water, rapidly melting away before this supremely yang and powerful fist force imbued with thunderous intent!

"Crack!"

A crisp cracking sound rang out! Ghost Hand screamed in agony as his entire right arm twisted at an eerie angle, his five black fingers torn open, blood spurting out a mixture of blood and bluish-black venom! He flew backward like a kite with a broken string, crashing into a stone pillar behind the mountain gate with a dull thud, cracking the pillar! He staggered to the ground, his face deathly pale, his right arm hanging limply, half-crippled, and a trickle of black blood spilling from his mouth, clearly indicating that his internal organs had also suffered severe shock and burns!

One punch! Just one punch! The mid-stage seventh-rank "Ghost Hand," an elite assassin among the Shadow Guards, actually suffered a severe blow from Lin Chen's punch, which fused the essence of multiple martial arts techniques!

Lin Chen, too, swayed slightly, sliding back half a step. His chest heaved slightly, but the surge of qi and blood was instantly suppressed by his powerful inner energy. The purple-gold light in his eyes intensified, his fighting spirit surging! So this was the power of a seventh-rank! This was the power of mastering and integrating one's own martial arts! Ghost Hand was strong, but his martial arts style was insidious and unpredictable, taking a shortcut. In terms of foundation and the purity and refinement of his martial arts intent, he was far inferior to this newcomer who had experienced life and death, possessed a solid foundation, and had fused the medicinal power of the "Nine Revolutions Rejuvenating Pill" and the profound mysteries of the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*!

Everything happened too fast. From the assassination attempt to Ghost Hand's severe injury and retreat, it was all over in the blink of an eye! The three eighth-rank assassins who had been attacking head-on had only just regained their footing and adjusted their offensive. When they saw Ghost Hand being knocked back and severely injured by Lin Chen's punch, their eyes showed horror. But they were well-trained and knew there was no way out. One of them shouted, and the three of them charged forward again, disregarding their own lives. Blades, poisoned darts, and hidden weapons rained down on Lin Chen like a storm, trying to buy Ghost Hand a chance to catch his breath or to hold Lin Chen back while waiting for other possible ambushes.

"You're courting death!" A cold glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes, his killing intent palpable. Since his opponent intended to kill him, he had no reason to be polite! He moved again, this time combining his light and agile steps with the subtleties of "Qi control" to the extreme. He seemed to transform into a pale golden phantom weaving through the flashing blades, appearing in front and then suddenly behind. The three eighth-rank assassins, though fighting desperately, couldn't even touch the hem of his clothes.

"Thunderclap Fist—Lightning Appears!"

Lin Chen spotted an opening, abruptly halting his movement, and unleashed another powerful right punch! This time, he made no attempt to conceal his attack; the purplish-gold aura on his fist instantly transformed into silver-white, with wisps of electricity becoming clearly visible. The air was violently rubbed together, producing short, ear-piercing crackling sounds!

"Boom!"

A fist-shaped burst of energy, crackling with silver-white electricity, shot out from his body, swift as lightning, and struck the chest of an assassin directly in front of him with pinpoint accuracy! The assassin only had time to raise his short blade to his chest before the punch arrived! The short blade snapped with a "crack," and the punch struck his chest without any resistance!

"Pfft!"

The assassin's chest caved in instantly, a bowl-sized hole exploding in his back, charred black and reeking of burnt meat. He flew backward like a tattered sack, knocking over his two companions. The three rolled in a heap, one dead instantly, two severely injured, and rendered incapacitated.

Lin Chen pressed his advantage, his figure blurring as he appeared before the heavily wounded Ghost Hand. His eyes showed no mercy. He raised his left palm, purple-gold energy surging, ready to finish off this most dangerous enemy with a single blow, while simultaneously searching his soul to extract information.

"Amitabha! Benefactor, please have mercy!"

Just as Lin Chen's palm strike was about to land, a deep, peaceful Buddhist chant, like a morning bell, suddenly rang out before the mountain gate. The voice wasn't loud, but it clearly drowned out all the chaos and screams, reaching everyone's ears directly. It carried a strange, calming power, causing the fleeing and crying pilgrims to instinctively slow their movements.

Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat, his palm strike paused slightly, and he looked up.

He saw the grey-robed monk, Liao Chen, whom he had seen in the underground palace, slowly walking out, holding ebony prayer beads. His face still wore that warm smile, but now, against the backdrop of the mess and blood, the smile appeared somewhat eerie. Behind him followed the leading middle-aged martial monk and several other monks with imposing auras, subtly sealing the mountain gate's exit.

"Master Liaochen?" Lin Chen withdrew his palm and took two steps back, creating distance. He looked at Liaochen with icy eyes. "What does this mean, Master? These people committed murder in broad daylight in the sacred Buddhist land. As a steward of the temple, instead of capturing the culprits, you are trying to obstruct my efforts to eliminate evil?"

"Amitabha." Liao Chen clasped his hands together, his gaze sweeping over the dead and wounded assassins and the severely injured, blood-spitting Ghost Hand on the ground. A barely perceptible shadow of gloom flashed in his eyes, but his smile remained unchanged. "You misunderstand, benefactor. This is a sacred Buddhist sanctuary; how can killing be tolerated? Whether the perpetrator was the one who committed the crime or acted in self-defense, once you step into the temple, you should cease hostilities. As for who is right and who is wrong, the authorities will investigate. You are highly skilled in martial arts and have already subdued him. Why add to the bloodshed? Why not hand him over to the authorities, or... hand him over to this temple for safekeeping, and then decide his fate after the truth is ascertained?"

Hand him over to the authorities? Or put him under the temple's custody? Lin Chen sneered inwardly. This Liao Chen was indeed in cahoots with Ghost Hand! Handing him over to the authorities, given Prince Zhongshun's influence in Jinling, he'd probably be released the moment he was sent in. Putting him under the temple's custody? That would be like throwing a meat bun to a dog—gone without a trace! Liao Chen clearly wanted to protect Ghost Hand while simultaneously "inviting" him into the temple for a "detailed discussion." Whether he survived or was punished would be entirely up to them.

"Master, you are mistaken," Lin Chen said firmly, neither humble nor arrogant. "Such a thug has brazenly committed violence within the Buddhist community, injuring innocent pilgrims. His crimes are heinous. We martial artists, seeing injustice, should naturally draw our swords. Since you have mentioned the authorities, then so be it. I request that you bear witness and join me in turning this villain over to the authorities, so that this sacred Buddhist site may be restored to its former tranquility!"

His words resonated deeply, and standing on the moral high ground of "eliminating evil" and "maintaining the purity of the Buddhist community," he immediately garnered the support of the surrounding pilgrims who had calmed down somewhat.

"Yes! This young master is right! He should be handed over to the authorities!"

"These damned thieves, how dare they commit violence before the Bodhisattva!"

"Master, you must do us justice!"

Liao Chen's smile froze slightly, a flash of anger crossing his eyes. He hadn't expected Lin Chen to be so difficult to deal with, not only for his high martial arts skills but also for his sharp tongue, actually turning the tables on him. Of course, he couldn't really hand over Ghost Hand to the authorities; that would be tantamount to exposing Prince Zhongshun's mole at Jiming Temple to Princess Zhaoyang and the court. But if he forcibly stopped him, his image as a "high monk" would collapse under the watchful eyes of everyone, and Jiming Temple's reputation would also be damaged.

Just as Liao Chen was caught in a dilemma, secretly signaling to the monks behind him, preparing to forcibly intervene and, under the pretext of "suppressing the rioters and protecting the temple," "invite" Lin Chen and Ghost Hand inside—

"Clatter, clatter, clatter..."

A series of unhurried yet exceptionally clear hoofbeats came from the official road at the foot of the mountain, rapidly approaching. Immediately afterwards, a group of about twenty soldiers, gleaming in armor, long swords at their waists, and exuding a sharp aura, led by a stern-faced middle-aged official in a blue robe, parted the chaotic crowd and arrived at the mountain gate. The soldiers immediately dispersed, controlling the situation, surrounding the dead and wounded assassins and Ghost Hand, and separating Lin Chen and Liao Chen from the others.

"Who dares to cause such a disturbance and fight here? Disturbing this sacred Buddhist site?" The middle-aged official's gaze swept across the scene like lightning, his brow furrowing especially when he saw the ghostly hand and the corpse on the ground. His gaze finally settled on Lin Chen and Liao Chen.

Liao Chen's expression changed again upon seeing the group of soldiers and officials. He recognized the official; he was one of the constables of the Jinling Prefecture, surnamed Tie, known as "Iron Face." He was upright and incorruptible, never bowing to any powerful figure, and was one of those secretly cultivated by Princess Zhaoyang. How could he have arrived so quickly? Could it be… a pre-arranged plan?

Lin Chen's heart also skipped a beat. He looked at Constable Tie, then glanced at Liao Chen's grim expression, and immediately understood. It seemed Princess Zhaoyang had been prepared. Perhaps as soon as he left the Jia Mansion, a scout had reported him, and maybe Shadow Seven and Shadow Nine were nearby. Seeing the incident at Jiming Temple, they immediately notified Constable Tie to come and "maintain order." The princess was indeed thorough in her planning.

"Officer Tie has arrived just in time!" Lin Chen spoke first, pointing to the ghostly hand and the dead and wounded assassins on the ground. "I am Lin Chen. Today, I came to Jiming Temple to offer incense, but I was attacked in public by these people. Fortunately, I have some martial arts skills, which allowed me to protect myself and even wound the ringleader. This villain is highly skilled and ruthless; he is probably no ordinary bandit. Master Liaochen, out of compassion, wanted to persuade me to stop and hand the matter over to the temple or the government. I believe that such villains should be apprehended and brought to justice by upright officials like Officer Tie, to serve as a warning to others and to restore peace to Jiming Temple!"

In a few words, he explained the events, highlighting the ruthlessness and suspicious nature of Ghost Hand and his men. He also misinterpreted Liao Chen's "obstruction" as "mediation," giving the other side a way out while simultaneously passing the buck to Constable Tie.

Constable Tie's sharp gaze swept over Lin Chen, then over the dying Ghost Hand lying on the ground, his eyes filled with venom, and over the uniform clothing and weapons worn by the dead and wounded assassins (though unmarked, their style was consistent). He already understood the situation. He nodded slightly to Lin Chen, then turned to Liao Chen, cupping his hands in a respectful gesture, and said, "Master Liao Chen, I am ordered to maintain order in Jinling. This serious case of public violence occurred at the gate of Jiming Temple. I must bring all those involved back to the yamen for thorough interrogation. I hope you will forgive any disturbance to the Buddhist community."

Liao Chen's facial muscles twitched a few times, and he forced a smile, saying, "Amitabha, it is the Iron Constable's duty, how could this humble monk dare to obstruct it? However... this Buddhist sanctuary is a place of purity, and the sight of bloodshed is ultimately ominous. I hope the Iron Constable will uphold justice and not let anyone go unpunished." He knew that with the Iron Constable present, it was impossible for him to keep Ghost Hand or Lin Chen alive. He could only watch helplessly as the Iron Constable ordered his men to tie up the severely injured Ghost Hand and the two still-breathing assassins, examined the scene, and recorded the testimonies of several eyewitness pilgrims (Lin Chen secretly gestured to a few who had previously spoken up in agreement).

"Young Master Lin, you also need to come with me to the yamen to make a statement and explain the details of what happened," the Iron Constable said to Lin Chen, his tone official, but his eyes carried a subtle, almost imperceptible hint.

“That’s only right.” Lin Chen nodded in agreement. He knew it was procedure, and also a form of protection. Once inside the government office, on Iron Constable’s territory, it wouldn’t be so easy for Prince Zhongshun and Liao Chen to make a move. Moreover, he could conveniently use Iron Constable to secretly pass on the clues discovered in the Jiming Temple underground palace to Princess Zhaoyang.

“Master, farewell.” Iron Constable bowed to Liao Chen, then, with his soldiers and the prisoner, turned and descended the mountain with Lin Chen.

Liao Chen stood at the mountain gate, watching their departing figures. The smile on his face vanished completely, replaced by a sinister killing intent. He slowly turned the ebony prayer beads in his hand, his eyes flashing with cold light.

“Lin Chen… Princess Zhaoyang… Head Constable Tie… Good, very good.” He muttered to himself, his voice as cold as a venomous snake. “It seems he’s determined to oppose the Prince. The secret of the Medicine Buddha’s base… must not fall into your hands. That piece of trash Ghost Hand couldn’t even handle a mere early-stage seventh-rank cultivator, and he even got himself killed… But, perfect, we can use the government to point the finger at Princess Zhaoyang, accusing her of raising assassins and plotting something sinister… Hehe.”

He turned and whispered to the middle-aged martial monk beside him, “Go, report today’s events in detail to the Prince via carrier pigeon. Also, report the anomaly of the base in the underground palace. Please instruct the Prince whether… we should act ahead of schedule and open the underground palace?”

“Yes, Uncle-Master.” The middle-aged martial monk responded and withdrew.

Liao Chen looked down the mountain again, a cruel smile playing on his lips.

"Lin Chen, you think you're safe once you're inside the government office? The Prince's 'Shadow Guards' have more than just 'Ghost Hand.' We... have a long road ahead."

A mountain breeze carried a faint scent of blood. The morning bell of Jiming Temple still rang out melodiously, but it seemed shrouded in an ominous shadow.

Down the mountain, on the official road leading to the Jinling government office, Lin Chen and the head constable rode side by side.

"Young Master Lin, Her Highness the Princess is aware of the matter at Jiming Temple. She instructed that you stay at the government office briefly, just to make a show of it. Someone will escort you through a secret passage to a safe place shortly. Her Highness has important matters to discuss with you," the head constable said in a low voice.

"Thank you, Head Constable," Lin Chen nodded, but his heart was far from at ease. He knew that the bloody battle at the gates of Jiming Temple was only the beginning. A much larger storm was brewing unseen. And the strange indentation and star map on the Medicine Buddha's pedestal were like an alluring riddle, deeply imprinted in his mind.

The key… the token… what could it be?


Chapter 47 The Princess's New Plan: A Nighttime Sneak Peek at the Rooster's Crow

In a secluded side room in the back hall of the Jinling Prefectural Government Office,

the head constable dismissed his attendants and personally poured tea for Lin Chen before speaking in a low voice, "Young Master Lin, today's events were extremely dangerous. If Her Highness the Princess hadn't made prior arrangements, ordering me to secretly monitor the movements of Jiming Temple, and if we hadn't received the urgent report from the Shadow Guards, the consequences would have been unimaginable. That monk Liaochen has been operating in Jiming Temple for many years; many of the temple's warrior monks obey his commands, and there must have been accomplices outside the temple providing backup. It is extremely fortunate that you escaped unscathed today."

"Thank you for your timely assistance, Officer Tie." Lin Chen bowed in thanks, inwardly admiring Princess Zhaoyang's meticulous arrangements. "As for Liao Chen, what is his background? Judging from his martial arts style, it's insidious and unpredictable, unlike those of orthodox Buddhism, more like... a member of an evil sect?"

"Young Master has a keen eye." The head constable said solemnly, "This Liao Chen stayed at Jiming Temple twenty years ago. Because of his mastery of Buddhist scriptures and some martial arts skills, he gradually gained favor and rose to the position of steward of the Sutra Repository. But his true identity is a remnant of the former Jiangnan underworld sect, the 'Netherworld Cult'! After the Netherworld Cult was destroyed, its remnants scattered and went into hiding. This Liao Chen, somehow, climbed the social ladder to Prince Zhongshun's favor and, with his secret support, infiltrated Jiming Temple, firstly to monitor the temple's movements, and secondly... probably to search for some relics from the previous dynasty related to the 'Xuanzhen Armory' for Prince Zhongshun. Today, when you discovered the anomaly in the underground palace, he must have become suspicious, and may even have realized that you are carrying clues. Young Master, you must be extra wary of this person in the future."

Remnants of the Netherworld Cult? A spy for Prince Zhongshun? Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. No wonder Liao Chen's cold aura was so out of place with Buddhism. This man had been lurking in Jiming Temple for years, knew the underground palace inside and out, and had the support of Prince Zhongshun; he was indeed a big problem.

"What does Constable Tie intend to do with 'Ghost Hand' and the others?" Lin Chen asked again.

"'Ghost Hand' is a core member of Prince Zhongshun's 'Shadow Guard,' and knows many secrets. But he's stubborn and suffering from a rare poison (a backlash from his own poison cultivation), and could die at any moment. It will be difficult to get him to talk," Constable Tie frowned. "As for the assassins, they're all death squads; we won't get anything out of them. However, with the evidence of their public assassination attempt at the mountain gate today, which injured innocent people, plus the testimonies of the young master and several pilgrims, we can at least confirm their crimes of 'plotting against meritorious officials' and 'disturbing the tranquility of Buddhism.' Temporarily detaining them in the government jail will make Prince Zhongshun hesitant to openly release them. This will also buy Her Highness some time."

He paused, then took out a small, neither-gold-nor-iron token from his robes and handed it to Lin Chen: "Her Highness the Princess has ordered that you leave immediately through this secret passage and proceed to the 'Ciyun Nunnery's' back mountain villa in the west of the city. Her Highness is waiting there to discuss important matters. This is your pass. The entrance to the secret passage is beneath the bed in this room."

Lin Chen took the token; it was icy cold to the touch. The front was engraved with the character "玄" (Xuan), and the back with cloud patterns. Without further delay, he thanked the head constable again and, following his instructions, moved the bed. Sure enough, he found a movable stone slab. Lifting it revealed a deep, downward-facing stone staircase, barely wide enough for one person to pass through.

"Take care, young master," the head constable said, bowing behind him.

Lin Chen nodded, slipped into the secret passage, and the stone slabs silently closed behind him. The passage was pitch black, but his eyesight was now exceptional, and he possessed spiritual perception, so it wasn't a problem. He quickened his pace, proceeding down the winding stone steps. After about the time it takes for an incense stick to burn, light appeared ahead—an exit, with the sound of flowing water coming from outside.

Outside the exit was a hidden tributary of an underground river, with a small, covered boat moored on the bank. The boat was empty, but the oars were complete. Lin Chen untied the rope, jumped onto the boat, and drifted downstream. The river was swift, and the boat moved quite fast. After traveling through the dark underground waterway for about half an hour, the path suddenly opened up ahead; they had left Jinling City and arrived at a desolate river bend in the western suburbs. On the bank, Ying Qi had been waiting for some time.

"Young Master, please follow me." Ying Qi said little, leading Lin Chen ashore and through a dense bamboo forest. Before them appeared a secluded and elegant mountain villa nestled among ancient trees and vines. This was the villa behind the Ciyun Nunnery.

Inside the study of the villa, Princess Zhaoyang, Zhao Lingxi, stood by the window, holding an ancient book in her hand, her brows slightly furrowed, seemingly deep in thought. Hearing footsteps, she turned around and, seeing Lin Chen unharmed, a barely perceptible hint of relief flashed in her eyes.

"Lingxi," Lin Chen greeted her with a bow.

"Sit." Zhao Lingxi put down her book, gesturing for him to sit, and personally poured him a cup of tea. "Ying Qi has already given a general report on the matter at Jiming Temple. It's good that you're alright. Liao Chen indeed couldn't hold back and revealed his true colors. The capture of 'Ghost Hand' is a blow to Prince Zhongshun. However, this has alerted them, and Liao Chen and Prince Zhongshun will likely accelerate their actions."

"I did make some discoveries in the underground palace." Lin Chen described in detail the strange indentation and star map pattern on the white jade base of the Medicine Buddha, then took out the fragment of the "Yang Map" and the jade slip of the "Yin Map." "The star map around the indentation subtly matches the positions of several auxiliary stars near the seventh star of the Big Dipper, 'Yao Guang,' in the 'Yin Map' that I copied. The indentation's irregular shape seems to require a specific, oddly shaped 'key' to open. I suspect that 'key' might be another fragment of the 'Map of Mountains and Rivers,' or... a related token."

Zhao Lingxi took the tattered scroll and jade slip, carefully examining them against Lin Chen's description. Her eyes gleamed with excitement: "Not bad! This concave shape... seems to correspond to the incomplete patterns on the edge of this 'Yang Map' in my hand! It seems that the underground palace of Jiming Temple is indeed the key! That 'key' is most likely the missing core part of the 'Map of Mountains and Rivers,' or a 'secret key' that can open the true entrance to the armory!"

She rose and paced around the study, pondering, "Liao Chen has been lurking in Jiming Temple for years, undoubtedly searching for this item as well. Prince Zhongshun is determined to obtain it. Today's events have shown them that you discovered the secret of the foundation; they will likely spare no effort to seize the 'key' first, or… destroy the clues. We must act quickly and get ahead of them."

"How should we act? The underground palace is guarded by martial monks, and Liao Chen is a seventh-rank expert. There are likely other remnants of the Netherworld Cult or Prince Zhongshun's spies within the temple. A direct assault would be unlikely to succeed and would easily alert the authorities and the outside world," Lin Chen said.

“A direct assault won’t work.” Zhao Lingxi stopped, a sharp glint in her eyes. “We can… feign an attack while secretly advancing.”

“I’d like to hear the details.”

“What Prince Zhongshun and Liaochen are most worried about right now is that we'll get the ‘key’ first, or reveal the secrets of the underground palace to the public,” Zhao Lingxi analyzed. “They will definitely strengthen the defenses of the underground palace, and might even try to forcefully open the base beforehand. We'll give them that opportunity.”

“Does the princess mean… a feigned retreat? To lure the snake out of its hole?”

“That’s right.” Zhao Lingxi nodded. “I will have Iron Constable make a big deal out of the capture of ‘Ghost Hand,’ spreading rumors that we’ve extracted important clues from him, pointing to the Jiming Temple underground palace containing the treasure of the former dynasty’s rebels, and that the court will soon send an imperial envoy, together with the Jinling government, to thoroughly investigate the Jiming Temple underground palace! Liaochen and Prince Zhongshun will definitely be restless upon hearing this. They will either immediately move or destroy the ‘key,’ or… they will take the risk of forcibly opening the base and taking the ‘key’ before our ‘investigation’.”

“Then we can secretly monitor them and act when the opportunity arises. If they take the ‘key,’ we’ll ambush them halfway, unaware of their danger. If they try to move or destroy it, we can also use the opportunity to determine the location of the ‘key,’ or even… stage a fake ‘investigation’ to force them into confusion and reveal their weaknesses.” Lin Chen immediately understood the princess’s intention.

“Exactly.” Zhao Lingxi gave Lin Chen an approving look. “However, this matter requires careful planning. Liao Chen is cunning and treacherous, and the temple is his territory. We shouldn’t have too many people, nor should we get too close, lest we alert him. We need people skilled in concealment, with exceptional lightness skills, and strong enough to handle unexpected situations, to infiltrate the temple and closely monitor the underground palace and Liao Chen’s movements.”

Her gaze fell on Lin Chen: "This task is best left to you. You are now a seventh-grade cultivator, possess excellent lightness skills, have entered the underground palace before and are familiar with the terrain, and are a key person Liao Chen is keeping a close eye on. If you infiltrate, he might not even suspect you. I will have Shadow Seven and Shadow Nine provide support from the outside, and arrange several experts skilled in mechanisms and poisons to prepare escape routes and rendezvous points outside the temple. You only need to lie in wait and monitor, confirming the whereabouts of the 'key' or Liao Chen's movements; there's no need to act rashly. If anything changes, immediately send a signal, and we will act immediately."

Infiltrate Jiming Temple to closely monitor Liao Chen and the underground palace? This mission is indeed dangerous. Liao Chen is a master of at least the mid-seventh grade, and there are many martial monks and possible remnants of the Netherworld Cult within the temple. But as the princess said, his strength has greatly improved, and he is familiar with the terrain, making him the best candidate. Moreover, he is also eager to find out what the "key" is and what its connection is to the "Map of Mountains and Rivers."

"Lin Chen accepts the order." He agreed without hesitation.

“This matter should be handled sooner rather than later. Prince Zhongshun will likely respond soon.” Zhao Lingxi took out a set of night clothes, a more detailed map of Jiming Temple, several specially made pills and talismans for concealing one's presence and providing early warning, and a delicate copper tube capable of emitting special fireworks signals from a drawer in her desk. She handed these to Lin Chen. “These are for you. You should rest and recuperate here for half a day. Tonight at midnight, sneak into the back mountain of Jiming Temple through the secret passage. Ying Qi will take you to the designated location. Remember, safety first. If things become unbearable, retreat immediately. Do not act recklessly.”

“Yes.” Lin Chen took the items and carefully put them away.

“Furthermore…” Zhao Lingxi paused, her tone softening slightly, “You don’t need to worry about Miss Daiyu. I’ve already arranged for Doctor Chen to enter the Jia residence under the guise of ‘taking the princess’s pulse’ to treat Miss Daiyu, and he will leave behind a prescription and medicine. Doctor Chen’s medical skills are superb; with him looking after her, Miss Daiyu’s condition should stabilize. You just need to focus on handling this matter.”

Lin Chen felt a warmth in his heart. He hadn’t expected the princess to have considered even this, showing such concern for Daiyu. He bowed again solemnly, “Thank you, Lingxi.”

“Go, and prepare well.” Zhao Lingxi waved her hand.

Lin Chen left the study and, under Ying Qi’s arrangement, went to a quiet room in the detached courtyard to meditate and familiarize himself with the newly acquired items, especially the pills for concealing one’s aura and the warning talismans. At the same time, he repeatedly mentally rehearsed the terrain of Jiming Temple, the structure of the underground palace, and the various situations he might encounter and the corresponding countermeasures.

His strength had greatly increased, and with the princess's numerous assistance, he was quite confident about this mission. However, Liao Chen and Prince Zhongshun were not easy opponents; he needed to be extremely vigilant.

Time passed quietly, and night fell.

Midnight was approaching, and all was quiet. Lin Chen changed into his night clothes, took a pill to conceal his presence, hid the warning talisman close to his body, and tied the signal copper tube to the inside of his arm. With everything prepared, he pushed open the door to the quiet room.

Ying Qi, also dressed in black, was already waiting outside. "Young Master, please follow me."

The two moved with lightning speed, like wisps of smoke blending into the night, silently leaving the courtyard and disappearing into the dense forest behind the mountain. Ying Qi was extremely familiar with the terrain, leading Lin Chen swiftly along the rugged mountain path, avoiding any potential patrolling monks or sentries.

About half an hour later, the two arrived at the foot of a steep cliff behind Jiming Temple. The cliff face was covered in vines, and a very narrow, completely vine-covered crevice was faintly visible.

"Enter through this crack and climb upwards for about twenty zhang (approximately 33 meters). You will reach an abandoned monk's quarters behind the Medicine Master's Glazed Pagoda, near the ventilation shaft of the underground palace. It has been uninhabited for a long time and is relatively safe. You can lie in ambush there and observe the movements in the direction of the underground palace entrance and Liaochen's residence. This is a map and the location of the sentries." Ying Qi handed Lin Chen a more detailed sketch and pointed to a spot on the cliff face. "I will lie in ambush on that cliff, and Ying Jiu will be on the other side. If anything happens, use this firework as a signal, and we will respond immediately. If you need to send a message, you can use this 'Sound Transmission Shell,' infuse it with your inner energy, and both of us within a three-mile radius will be able to hear a brief message."

Lin Chen accepted a palm-sized, conch-shaped white seashell and nodded. "Thank you for your trouble."

"Take care, young master." Ying Qi bowed, then vanished into the nearby woods in a flash.

Lin Chen took a deep breath and looked up at the steep cliff and the crevice. He circulated his Qi Control Technique, his purple-gold inner energy flowing through his body. Light as a feather, he used both hands and feet, gripping the protrusions and vines of the rock face, silently climbing upwards like a nimble ape. Twenty zhang was nothing more than a few leaps for him now.

Soon, he reached the top of the crevice, parted the dense vines, and indeed saw a half-collapsed, dilapidated monk's quarters, buried under weeds. The quarters were hidden in the shadows behind the Medicine Master's Pagoda. Through the broken windows, one could see the area where the entrance to the underground palace was located several dozen zhang ahead, as well as the secluded Zen temple where Liao Chen resided not far away.

He slipped into the monks' quarters, finding a corner where he could observe the outside world without being detected. He held his breath, concealing his presence, as if becoming one with the darkness. His gaze, sharp as lightning, pierced through the cracks in the window, sweeping towards the entrance to the underground palace and the Liaochen Zen Temple.

At the entrance to the underground palace, lights blazed, and the number of guarding monks was several times greater than during the day, each one vigilant, swords drawn, as if facing a formidable enemy. Clearly, Liaochen had heightened its defenses.

The Liaochen Zen Temple, however, was utterly silent. Only the dim light from the study window illuminated a figure bent over a desk, writing—it was Liaochen himself.

Lin Chen patiently lay in wait, silently observing. Time ticked by, and the night grew deeper. Within Jiming Temple, apart from the watchman's clapper and the occasional bark of a dog, all was quiet. But beneath this tranquility, Lin Chen could sense a tense, oppressive atmosphere, like a storm brewing, permeating the temple.

Suddenly, the door to the study in Liaochen Zen Temple opened. Liaochen emerged, followed by the middle-aged martial monk who was the leader. The two exchanged a few words in hushed tones; Liaochen seemed to hand something to the middle-aged monk and gave him some instructions. The middle-aged monk bowed in acceptance and hurried away, heading straight for the entrance to the underground palace.

"Are they about to make their move?" Lin Chen's heart tightened, and he focused intently.

The middle-aged monk arrived at the entrance of the underground palace, whispered some instructions to the guarding monks, and then, leading four of the most skilled monks, opened the palace gate and filed inside! After about the time it takes to brew a cup of tea, the five hurriedly emerged, carrying what appeared to be a square wooden box, about a foot in size, tightly wrapped in black cloth! The middle-aged monk, carrying the box, looked tense and quickly returned to the Chenchan Temple.

A wooden box! What's inside? Could it be… a "key"? Or something else retrieved from the underground palace?

Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. He stared intently at the middle-aged monk as he placed the wooden box into the study of Liaochen Temple. Then, the lights in the study abruptly went out!

Immediately afterward, the back door of Liaochen Temple opened silently. A slender, agile figure dressed in night clothes darted out like a ghost, the black-cloth-wrapped wooden box tucked under his arm. Determining its direction, it sped towards the abandoned monks' quarters area behind the mountain where it was hiding!

It was Liao Chen! He really was going to move with the "key"! And the route he chose was this secluded mountain path! That old fox, so cunning!

Lin Chen held his breath, completely concealing himself in the darkness, his gaze like a hawk's, locking onto the rapidly approaching shadow. His right hand had already subtly grasped the signal tube, while his left hand slowly condensed a wisp of purplish-gold, extremely refined and sharp energy.

Should he signal now to notify Shadow Seven and Shadow Nine to intercept? Or… follow him and see where Liao Chen was taking the "key"? Perhaps, he could follow the trail to Prince Zhongshun's secret stronghold outside the city?

In a flash, Lin Chen made his decision. He quietly rose, like a cat in the night, silently slipping out of the monks' quarters, carefully trailing Liao Chen several dozen feet behind, and stealthily making his way deeper into the back mountain.


Chapter 48 Night Battle in the Back Mountain, The Mystery of the Key

The night was as dark as ink, the mountains and forests deep and secluded. The rugged path behind Jiming Temple was long overgrown with weeds and vines, yet Liao Chen seemed to know the place intimately. His figure moved swiftly among the jagged rocks and ancient, gnarled trees, as fast as a ghost, yet remarkably light, making almost no sound. He carried a wooden box wrapped in black cloth under his arm, as carefully as if protecting his life, occasionally glancing back with sharp, eagle-like eyes, clearly highly vigilant.

Lin Chen pushed his *Qi Control Technique* and Lightness Step to their limits, concealing his aura to almost nothing. His figure seemed to blend into the night wind and the shadows of the trees, hovering about thirty feet behind Liao Chen. Thanks to the extraordinary eyesight and perception of a seventh-rank martial artist, and the cover of the mountain terrain, he barely escaped detection. But he could sense that Liao Chen's speed was increasing, as if he were eager to reach a destination, and also more vigilant, occasionally making feints or suddenly stopping to listen, clearly testing whether he was being followed.

"This old fox is indeed troublesome," Lin Chen thought to himself, becoming even more cautious. He knew that Liao Chen's cultivation was at least at the mid-stage of the seventh rank, with rich combat experience and mastery of the Netherworld Sect's insidious techniques. Even if he wasn't afraid, a direct confrontation wouldn't guarantee victory. Moreover, Liao Chen likely held a crucial "key." If he couldn't strike decisively, forcing Liao Chen to destroy or take the key, or attracting his accomplices, all his efforts would be in vain.

He followed Liao Chen while observing his surroundings. This was deep within the back mountains of Jiming Temple, a place rarely visited by humans, with towering ancient trees, jagged rocks, and the occasional hooting of owls adding to the eeriness. Liao Chen seemed to be heading towards a dangerous place called "Soul-Severing Cliff" in the back mountains. Rumor had it that the area was treacherous, teeming with poisonous miasma and ferocious beasts, and contained bottomless cliffs—an ideal place to dispose of a corpse and cover his tracks.

"He wants to go to Broken Soul Cliff? Is there a rendezvous arranged by Prince Zhongshun there? Or... a hidden hiding place?" Lin Chen's mind raced. Broken Soul Cliff had complex terrain; if there was an ambush, he would be in danger alone. But giving up now was unacceptable. The key was right in front of him; seizing it would be crucial to solving the mystery of the "Map of Mountains and Rivers," and even thwarting Prince Zhongshun's scheme.

He touched the signal tube on the inside of his arm, hesitated for a moment, and ultimately didn't activate it. Shadow Seven and Shadow Nine were on the perimeter; it would take time for them to arrive. Besides, activating the signal would inevitably alert Chen. If Chen, in desperation, destroyed the key or fled to a more dangerous place, it would be worse. He decided to continue tracking, figuring out Chen's destination and the person he was meeting, before making his move. If an opportunity arose, he would try to ambush him and seize the key.

After tracking for about the time it takes for an incense stick to burn, the terrain ahead suddenly became treacherous, revealing a bottomless, mist-shrouded cliff—the "Soul-Severing Cliff." The cliff's edge was lined with jagged rocks, and a few withered trees stood like ghostly shadows in the night wind. Liao Chen stopped on a relatively flat boulder at the cliff's edge, looking around as if waiting for something.

Lin Chen, hiding behind a huge boulder several dozen feet away, held his breath, his gaze fixed on Liao Chen, while simultaneously sensing his surroundings. Apart from the sound of the night wind and insects, there seemed to be nothing unusual. Could it be that Liao Chen had only come here to hide the key, without any backup?

Just then, Liao Chen suddenly turned around, facing the general direction where Lin Chen was hiding. A strange, mocking smile appeared on his face as he said loudly, "Amitabha, benefactor, you've followed this humble monk all the way; you must be tired. Now that you're here, why not show yourself? This dark and windy night, at the edge of the Broken Soul Cliff, is a perfect place for discussion."

He'd been discovered! Lin Chen's heart skipped a beat. Was Liao Chen's perception far beyond his expectations? Or had he been acting all along, deliberately leading him here? Without further hesitation, he slowly emerged from behind the rocks, his night clothes fluttering slightly in the wind, his gaze calmly meeting Liao Chen's.

"Master Liao Chen, your perception is truly keen. I was merely taking a night stroll and happened to pass by; I didn't mean to disturb your peaceful cultivation," Lin Chen said calmly, while secretly gathering his inner energy, a faint purple-gold aura emanating from his body.

"Hehe, why deceive yourself, benefactor?" Liao Chen placed the wooden box wrapped in black cloth at his feet, leisurely patting non-existent dust off his monk's robe. "From the underground palace onwards, benefactor has paid special attention to the Medicine Buddha's pedestal. Today, the mountain gate 'coincidentally' appeared, ruining this humble monk's plans. Now, you've followed me all the way here... Benefactor, let's not beat around the bush, you've also come for this 'key,' haven't you?" He pointed to the wooden box at his feet.

"A key? What key? I don't understand what you're saying, Master." Lin Chen denied it outright, but his vigilance only grew stronger. Liao Chen's composure suggested he had something to rely on.

"Don't understand?" Liao Chen's smile turned cold, a sharp glint flashing in his eyes. "This humble monk has been lurking in Jiming Temple for twenty years, painstakingly searching, finally obtaining a clue to this 'Mysterious Key.' You, a mere brat, dare to covet it? Princess Zhaoyang sent you, didn't she? Hmph, that little brat thinks she can rival the Prince just because she has some of the Emperor's favor? Wishful thinking! Today, this humble monk will deliver you from this world, and also extinguish Princess Zhaoyang's hopes!"

Before the words were even finished, Liao Chen's figure suddenly shot up! His movement was devoid of any trace of the monk's benevolence; a dense, chilling aura, like the cold winds of the underworld, erupted from his body. His figure transformed into a gray shadow, so fast it was almost beyond visual perception. His five fingers formed claws, the tips gleaming black, carrying a pungent, fishy smell and a ghostly shriek, as he slashed towards Lin Chen's face! It was his signature move—the Netherworld Ghost Claw! And he unleashed his full power from the start, the claw wind covering an area of ​​several feet, chilling to the bone, and accompanied by a strange mental fluctuation, attempting to shake Lin Chen's mind!

"Well done!" Lin Chen was prepared. Facing this fierce and strange claw attack, he advanced instead of retreating! He used the *Cleansing Heart Technique* to stabilize his consciousness and dispel the eerie mental attack. His feet moved with light, agile steps, his body sliding like a fish, narrowly avoiding the strongest point of the claw's attack. Simultaneously, his right fist condensed a purple-gold aura, and on the fist's edge, a pale golden Vajra phantom and leaping silver-white lightning reappeared. He delivered a simple, powerful punch to the side opening of the Dust Claw's attack! This was his "Shock" technique, a fusion of the Vajra Fist, Thunderclap Fist intent, and the wonders of Qi control!

"Bang!"

Fist and claw collided again! A shockwave exploded, sending debris flying! This time, Liao Chen was clearly on guard; the Yin energy on his claws intensified, clashing fiercely with Lin Chen's Purple-Gold Thunder Fist! Lin Chen felt a chilling, vicious, and highly corrosive energy seep into his arm's meridians, causing a slight numbness. Liao Chen also staggered, taking a half-step back; the dark light on his claws dimmed slightly, clearly indicating that Lin Chen's Yang-infused fist energy, infused with thunder intent, had a restraining effect on his Yin-poisonous technique.

"Good lad! Your cultivation has improved so much in just a few days! And you've even fused with thunder fist intent?" A hint of surprise flashed in Liao Chen's eyes, then transformed into an even stronger killing intent. "You must not be allowed to live!"

He let out a sharp shout and moved again. This time, he didn't fight head-on, but instead brought the eerie and cunning of the Netherworld Claws to its extreme. His figure moved around Lin Chen like a ghost, his claw shadows overlapping, making it difficult to distinguish between reality and illusion. Each claw was filled with chilling poison and soul-capturing demonic sounds, attacking Lin Chen's vital points from all directions. He also occasionally flicked out a few fine, colorless, and odorless "Netherworld Poison Needles," or sprinkled out "Bone-Rotting Poison Powder" with a sweet and fishy smell. He was insidious and ruthless, using every means possible.

Lin Chen focused intently, combining his light and agile footwork with the subtleties of "Qi control," maneuvering and dodging within the limited space, leaving no room for error. His fists moved with a mix of hardness and softness, shock and defense, effortlessly employing the ferocity of the Vajra Fist, the penetrating power of the Rock-Splitting Fist, and the explosive force of the Thunderclap Fist, along with the techniques of deflecting and neutralizing force from the "Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique." A purple-gold Qi shield protected his body, repelling and dispersing most of the poison needles and powder. Occasionally, seizing an opportunity, he would unleash a powerful punch infused with thunderous intent, forcing Liao Chen to temporarily retreat.

The two fought with lightning speed, their movements blurry and swift on the small clearing beside the Broken Soul Cliff. The clash of their energies was deafening, sending fragments of rock and wood flying everywhere. Liao Chen, with his profound cultivation and seasoned experience, employed insidious and unpredictable techniques, further enhanced by his use of poison, making him impossible to defend against. Lin Chen, on the other hand, possessed a solid foundation, pure martial arts intent, and powerful, refined inner energy. His lightning-based fist technique countered the poison, and although his practical experience was slightly inferior, he maintained his composure using the *Cleansing Heart Mantra*, and his mastery of qi enhanced his control over his movements, resulting in a stalemate. For a short time, it was difficult to determine a winner.

However, Lin Chen's heart grew heavy. Liao Chen was clearly not using his full strength; he seemed to be testing, or rather, waiting for something. Moreover, the wooden box was not far from Liao Chen's feet, yet he seemed unconcerned about being caught in the crossfire. This was illogical.

Could it be… the wooden box is fake? A diversionary tactic? A thought flashed through Lin Chen's mind, and he spared a sliver of attention from the battle to glance at the wooden box. It was wrapped in black cloth, completely concealing any abnormalities. However, his keen spiritual senses faintly detected that the aura emanating from the wooden box seemed… too “ordinary”? Completely different from the “spiritual essence” of the underground palace.

Just as doubt arose in his heart, a cold glint flashed in Liao Chen's eyes. Suddenly, his attack changed, abandoning the hit-and-run tactic and lunging forward, both claws striking out with a fierce momentum, aiming directly for Lin Chen's center, as if intending to perish together!

Lin Chen dared not be careless. He unleashed both fists, his purple-gold aura blazing, preparing to meet the attack head-on.

However, Liao Chen's seemingly desperate attack abruptly changed direction midway! His body twisted unpredictably, as if boneless, narrowly avoiding Lin Chen's fists. Simultaneously, his left claw feinted, while his right claw, at an incredible angle, flashed towards Lin Chen's waist…the signal copper tube!

His true target was the signal copper tube! He wanted to prevent Lin Chen from summoning reinforcements!

Lin Chen's heart tightened. He retreated rapidly, simultaneously slashing downwards with his left palm to parry Liao Chen's right claw. But Liao Chen's attack was premeditated and incredibly fast. Although Lin Chen avoided a fatal blow, the signal copper tube's strap was severed with a "sizzle" by the sharp energy emanating from Liao Chen's fingertips! The copper tube flew out of his hand, landing in the vast mist below the Broken Soul Cliff!

"Haha! Kid, without signal, let's see how you call for help now!" Liao Chen laughed wildly after landing a successful attack, his offensive becoming even fiercer. He no longer held back, unleashing the full power of his Netherworld Ghost Claw. Claw shadows filled the sky, a howling wind arose, and streams of black energy surged from his body, coiling towards Lin Chen like venomous pythons. Clearly, he had used some kind of trump card, an evil technique!

Lin Chen's heart sank upon losing the signal tube, but he remained calm. He took a deep breath, focusing all his attention on the battle, activating the "Clear Heart Technique" to its fullest extent, his mind clear and focused. Facing Liao Chen's relentless onslaught, he pushed his agile footwork and "Qi control" to the limit, weaving and dodging through claw shadows and black mist, like a small boat in a raging storm, seemingly in grave danger, yet never swallowed up.

"This can't drag on! I must finish this quickly!" Lin Chen knew that Liao Chen's delaying tactics meant he had a backup plan. Moreover, this was Liao Chen's chosen battlefield, likely harboring an ambush. He had to seize the key as soon as possible (or verify its authenticity) and then escape.

A flash of purple-gold light appeared in his eyes, and he decided to take a gamble. Just as Liao Chen's claw reached for his shoulder, and the other for his throat, and black energy was about to wrap around his legs, Lin Chen neither dodged nor evaded, but instead deliberately faced Liao Chen's ghostly claws with his shoulder!

"Pfft!"

Liao Chen's ghostly claws dug deep into Lin Chen's left shoulder! The jet-black nails pierced the flesh, and a surge of chilling poison rushed in! Lin Chen groaned, his face paling, but a resolute and mad glint flashed in his eyes! Using the force of this claw, he slammed forward, and the purple-gold energy on his right fist instantly transformed into a dazzling silver-white, a violent lightning intent soaring into the sky, the air violently rubbed together, emitting ear-piercing shrieks and crackling explosions!

"Thunderclap Fist—Lightning Appears! Open!"

This time, it was the complete, unreserved first move of the "Thunderclap Fist," infused with all his essence, energy, spirit, and the true meaning of thunder! The fist, like real thunder tearing through the night sky, carried a destructive ferocity and majesty, crashing down on Liao Chen's chest! The speed and power of the punch far surpassed any previous strike!

Liao Chen never expected Lin Chen to retaliate in such a near-suicidal manner! His claw had landed firmly, and he thought victory was assured. Just as he was about to unleash his poison technique to finish off his opponent, the terrifying thunder fist was already upon his chest! He tried to dodge, but it was too late. He tried to parry with his claw, but his arm was restrained by Lin Chen's forward momentum!

"No!!!"

Liao Chen's eyes finally revealed utter horror. He roared, barely managing to cross his left arm across his chest while simultaneously activating all his Yin Sha Qi to protect himself.

"Boom—!!!"

A deafening thunderclap erupted at the edge of the Broken Soul Cliff! Blinding silver-white lightning instantly engulfed the two figures locked in combat! The violent shockwave ripped away and shattered rocks and trees within several feet! Gravel tumbled down the cliff edge, plunging into the bottomless abyss.

The lightning dissipated.

Liao Chen staggered back more than ten steps, barely managing to steady himself at the edge of the cliff. His left sleeve was torn to shreds, his arm charred black, a bloody mess, and a fist-sized indentation in his chest, smoke billowing from it. Large mouthfuls of black blood spilled from his mouth, his face was deathly pale, and his breath was extremely weak. He had clearly suffered severe internal injuries and burns from the lightning strike. He stared intently at Lin Chen, his eyes filled with resentment, fear, and disbelief.

Lin Chen was also in bad shape. His left shoulder was pierced by the ghostly claw, five black holes oozing cold, poisonous energy that was frantically corroding his meridians and flesh, leaving half his body numb. His face was pale, blood trickling from his mouth, and his internal energy had been severely depleted by the punch. Yet he remained standing, tiny arcs of electricity still flickering on his right fist, his gaze coldly fixed on Liao Chen.

“Cough cough… Good… Good thunderbolt fist… What a ruthless brat…” Liao Chen coughed up blood, his voice hoarse, “But… you think you’ve won? Look at your left shoulder! You’ve been poisoned by this monk’s ‘Netherworld Bone-Corroding Poison.’ Without my unique antidote, within three days, your whole body will fester and turn into a pool of pus and blood! Haha… cough cough…”

Lin Chen glanced down at the wound on his left shoulder. The dark flesh was visibly necrotizing and emitting a foul stench, a chilling, numbing sensation spreading rapidly. His brow furrowed slightly, but his expression remained calm. "Poison? Indeed troublesome. However, Master seems to have forgotten that since I dared to take your claw head-on, I naturally…was prepared."

He extended his undamaged right hand, taking out the emerald-green "Pure Heart Jade Pendant" that Zhao Lingxi had given him, and placed it near the wound on his left shoulder. Simultaneously, he secretly activated a special technique from the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique*—"Guiding Qi Back to its Origin, Expelling Evil and Poison"—infusing a wisp of pure, gentle, and vibrant purple-gold internal energy into the jade pendant.

"Buzz..."

The Qingxin Jade Pendant suddenly emitted a soft, emerald-green light. A cool, tranquil aura, imbued with powerful vitality, surged from the pendant, flowing into the wound on Lin Chen's left shoulder, guided by the light. Under the illumination of the emerald-green light and the cleansing effect of the vital energy, the black, rotting flesh actually stopped spreading and dying. It even began to slowly expel black, poisonous blood, and granulation tissue faintly twitched at the edges of the wound, beginning to heal slowly! Although it couldn't immediately remove all the toxins, it had clearly been effectively contained!

"This...this is...the royal 'Qingxin Warm Jade'? How could it be in your hands?!" Liao Chen's pupils constricted, and he exclaimed in shock, his eyes filled with jealousy and resentment. This kind of treasure, capable of expelling poison, healing wounds, and calming the mind, was a royal secret, something even Prince Zhongshun might not possess. Princess Zhaoyang had actually bestowed it upon this brat?!

"Master, it seems your poison isn't so potent after all." Lin Chen put away the jade pendant (its radiance subdued), feeling the coolness and tingling sensation (rebirth) on his left shoulder, his heart slightly at ease. The princess's gift was indeed extraordinary.

"You…!" Liao Chen coughed up another mouthful of blood in anger, his injuries seemingly worsening. He swept his gaze venomously over Lin Chen, then looked at the black cloth-covered wooden box on the ground, a flicker of struggle in his eyes before turning into resolute determination.

"Boy, you ruined the prince's grand plan and injured me so badly, this is an irreconcilable enmity! But you think you've obtained the 'key'? Haha… dream on!" Liao Chen suddenly burst into maniacal laughter, violently stomping on the black cloth-covered wooden box on the ground!

"Not good!" Lin Chen's expression changed. Although he suspected the wooden box was fake, what if it was real? Wouldn't it be a pity if it were destroyed? He moved swiftly, trying to stop him.

However, Liao Chen's kick wasn't to crush the wooden box, but... a kick! He kicked the wooden box violently, sending it flying towards the bottomless, misty abyss below the Broken Soul Cliff!

"The real 'key' has already been taken by the Prince's men! This is just bait to lure you in! You can search slowly right here on this cliff! Haha... uh!"

Liao Chen's maniacal laughter abruptly stopped. Because in the instant he kicked the wooden box away, his mind momentarily distracted, and his injuries flared up, Lin Chen's figure appeared before him like a ghost. A cold, powerful hand, wrapped in purple-gold energy, gripped his throat like an iron clamp!

"Speak! Where is the real key? What other arrangements has Prince Zhongshun made in Jinling?!" Lin Chen's gaze was icy cold, his voice carrying a chilling killing intent. With a slight tightening of his fingers, Liao Chen immediately struggled to breathe, his face flushing red.

"Cough cough...you...don't even think about it..." A mad glint flashed in Liao Chen's eyes. He bit his tongue sharply, seemingly about to use some kind of self-harm or communication technique.

But Lin Chen was faster! His right hand swiftly struck several major acupoints on Liao Chen's chest, sealing his internal energy flow. Simultaneously, a wisp of internal energy imbued with lightning surged into his body, shattering the secret technique Liao Chen had just formed. Liao Chen groaned, the light in his eyes quickly dimming. He slumped down, barely clinging to life.

"Won't talk? I have plenty of ways to make you talk." Lin Chen said coldly, intending to try and force an explanation using a "Soul Searching and Heart Questioning" technique recorded in the *Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique* (a rather rudimentary technique, and extremely harmful to the recipient).

Just then, another unexpected event occurred!

"Whoosh—Crack!"

A sharp, rhythmic whistle shot up from the mountains opposite Broken Soul Cliff, exploding in the night sky into a bizarre, skull-like green firework! The firework's light illuminated half the sky, reflecting the eerie smile of relief and resentment on Chen's pale face.

It was a signal! Prince Zhongshun's men were nearby! And they had seen the battle and the firework! They were coming soon!

Lin Chen's expression changed. That old fox, Liao Chen, really had a backup plan! His kicking of the wooden box and deliberately provoking him was probably just to buy time, waiting for his accomplices to see the signal and arrive! This skull firework was both a cry for help and likely… a signal to silence him! If Prince Zhongshun's men arrived, to maintain secrecy, they might also silence Liao Chen!

He couldn't stay any longer! He had to leave immediately! Lin Chen made a decisive decision, abandoning his interrogation. He glanced at the dying Liao Chen, then looked at the bottomless abyss of the Broken Soul Cliff that had swallowed the wooden box. A flicker of resentment flashed in his eyes, but it quickly turned into resolve.

Without further hesitation, he gathered his last breath, used his light-footed steps, and turned to flee in the opposite direction from the signal firework—towards the more complex terrain of "Wild Man's Gully," deeper in the mountains behind Jiming Temple! Before leaving, he didn't forget to pull the seemingly ordinary ebony prayer beads from Liao Chen's waist—this old monk's personal item might be useful.

Shortly after his figure disappeared into the forest, several powerful and agile figures, like owls, darted down from the opposite mountain ridge of Broken Soul Cliff and landed beside Liao Chen. The leader was none other than the old man in black who had previously spoken secretly with Prince Zhongshun! His icy gaze swept over the heavily injured and dying Liao Chen, then at the traces of the battle on the ground and the abyss below, his face so dark it could drip water.

“Useless!” The old man in black kicked Liao Chen, who groaned and passed out completely. “Couldn’t even handle a mere early-stage seventh-rank brat, and you even lost the ‘key’ (a fake key)! What use are you to the Prince!”

“Elder, that brat…” a man in black whispered beside him.

"Chase him! He's been poisoned by the Netherworld Bone-Corroding Poison and fought a fierce battle; he can't have gone far! Follow the bloodstains and the scent, pursue him! Dead or alive, find him at all costs, and... that string of prayer beads!" The black-clad elder's eyes flashed with murderous intent. "Also, send a signal to seal off all exits from the back mountain of Jiming Temple! Not a single bird should be able to fly out!"

"Yes!"

Several dark figures immediately gave chase in the direction Lin Chen had disappeared. The black-clad elder stood still, gazing at the bottomless Broken Soul Cliff, then at the unconscious Liao Chen, a cruel glint in his eyes.

"Liao Chen, you know too much. The Prince's order is that if things cannot be done, then... send you to see Buddha." He slowly raised his palm, a ball of pitch-black energy, radiating a dense aura of death, gathering in his palm, about to strike down on Liao Chen's crown.

However, just as his palm was about to fall, another unexpected event occurred!

Liao Chen, who had been unconscious, suddenly opened his eyes, two eerie, ghostly green lights bursting forth from them! He abruptly opened his mouth, and a thin, almost invisible, emerald-green light shot out like lightning towards the black-robed elder's brow! The distance was too close, the speed too fast; the black-robed elder had no time to react!

"Pfft!"

A pale green light pierced the black-clad elder's brow! The elder shuddered violently, his eyes widening in disbelief and terror. His raised hand froze in mid-air, his aura rapidly waning, his skin visibly turning ashen and withered, as if all life had been drained from him in an instant! He opened his mouth, as if to speak, but no sound came out. He fell straight backward, lifeless!

The pale green light, after piercing the elder's brow, strangely returned and disappeared back into Chen's mouth. An unnatural, eerie blush appeared on Chen's deathly pale face. He struggled to sit up, watching the elder on the ground rapidly turn into a desiccated corpse, a flicker of pleasure and madness in his eyes.

"Trying to silence me? Heh heh... Your Highness, you didn't expect this, did you? My 'Netherworld Soul Planting Technique' has already planted a 'soul seed' in the person you sent to monitor me! Using the essence and soul of a mid-stage seventh-rank elder of yours will perfectly aid my healing, and perhaps even... elevate me to a higher level!"

He sat cross-legged, his hands forming a strange hand seal. A dense, nauseating emerald light surged around him, frantically devouring and refining the remaining essence and soul energy within the black-clad elder's corpse. His aura began to slowly recover and rise at an eerie speed! And his appearance, illuminated by the emerald light, appeared increasingly ferocious and sinister, inhuman.

At the edge of the Broken Soul Cliff, the emerald light was eerie, and death permeated the air. Liao Chen's madness and the pursuit by Prince Zhongshun's men were like two invisible nets, silently enveloping Lin Chen, who had fled into "Savage Gully."


Chapter 49 A Narrow Escape: The Secret of the Rosary Beads

"Wild Man's Gully" is no exaggeration. This place is deep within the Qixia Mountains, the most primitive and treacherous section of the canyon. The cliffs on both sides are as if cut by a knife and axe, even monkeys would find it difficult to climb. Ancient trees reach for the sky at the bottom of the valley, vines grow like pythons, and fallen leaves have accumulated for countless years, reaching up to the knees. The rotting branches and leaves emit a pungent, musty smell, mixed with the miasma from unknown poisonous insects, forming a pale purple mist that never dissipates. It is eerie and terrifying even during the day, rarely visited by humans, let alone in the dead of night.

Lin Chen, barely clinging to his last breath, fled desperately through the slippery, muddy, and thorn-covered valley floor. The wound on his left shoulder had long since gone numb, but the chilling and vicious "Netherworld Bone-Corroding Poison" clung to him like a leech, relentlessly eroding his meridians and blood. Wherever it passed, flesh darkened and necrotized, waves of numbness and excruciating pain washing over him. Even more troublesome was the immense depletion of his internal energy. The fierce battle with Liao Chen, especially the final, full-force "Thunderbolt Fist," had nearly drained him of most of his energy. Now, his dantian was empty, his meridians throbbed with pain, and each leap and breath aggravated his injuries, causing his vision to blur.

Behind him, the sounds of clothes rustling and branches scraping against each other were clearly audible, along with faint shouts and barks (perhaps from tracking beasts). There was more than one group of pursuers, at least five or six in total. Judging from their auras, at least two were early-stage seventh-rank experts, and the rest were all peak eighth-rank masters! Moreover, they were clearly skilled in tracking and encirclement techniques, fanning out from the cliffs on either side and behind, constantly compressing his space.

"Cough cough..." Lin Chen felt a sweet taste in his throat, forcibly suppressing a mouthful of blood. He staggered, almost tripping over a thick, horizontal vine. He grabbed onto a giant tree that would take several people to encircle, breathing heavily. Cold sweat mixed with the black blood seeping from his shoulder, soaking his clothes. The ebony prayer beads he had taken from Liao Chen were now emitting an increasingly clear, warm, and even strangely fluctuating aura, pressed against his chest, as if responding to something.

Was it a contingency plan left by Liao Chen? Or did the prayer beads themselves hold a hidden secret? Lin Chen had no time to ponder this, but he instinctively felt that these prayer beads might be the key to his escape. He tried to send a wisp of his inner energy into the prayer beads.

"Buzz..."

The prayer beads trembled slightly, and the warmth suddenly intensified! Then, fragmented, distorted images, filled with an aura of gloom, greed, bloodshed, and madness, surged into Lin Chen's mind like a tidal wave! These were fragments of Liao Chen's memories! Fragments of his soul, a remnant of the divine imprint passively triggered when Liao Chen was near death or under control, from this "natal artifact" he had worn close to his body for years, refined with his own blood and Netherworld evil arts!

The scene includes a hidden corner of the underground palace of Jiming Temple, a whispered conversation between Chen and Prince Zhongshun's secret envoy, scenes of various poisonous and evil arts being practiced, and even more... fragments about the true whereabouts of the "key" and the deeper secrets of the "Map of Mountains and Rivers"!

"The key...there's more than one...yin and yang complement each other...the 'Heart of the Medicine Buddha' at Jiming Temple is yin...the 'Eye of the Night Wanderer' at the City God Temple is yang...only when yin and yang are united can the true map be revealed...the true map points to...the Jinling Dragon Vein...the Imperial Mausoleum Palace..." The fragmented information, mixed with Liao Chen's obsession and madness, assaulted Lin Chen's mind.

More than one key! Yin and yang complement each other! The "Heart of the Medicine Buddha" at Jiming Temple is the yin key, and the "Eye of the Night Wanderer" at the City God Temple is the yang key? Only when yin and yang are united can the true "Map of Mountains and Rivers" be revealed? And the true map points to the Jinling Dragon Vein, the Imperial Mausoleum Palace?! This amount of information is overwhelming! Prince Zhongshun's painstaking planning is not only for the treasures of the Xuanzhen Armory, but also for the Jinling Dragon Vein, and even...something within the Imperial Mausoleum Palace? This is simply an outrageous ambition!

Lin Chen's heart trembled violently, and he almost lost his composure. But he immediately activated the *Heart-Cleansing Mantra*, forcibly stabilizing his mind and temporarily suppressing and sealing away the chaotic information. Now was not the time to delve into the details; escaping was paramount! Moreover, these prayer beads seemed to be more than just a memory vessel…

He endured the discomfort in his soul and the excruciating pain in his body, continuing to channel his inner energy into the prayer beads. This time, he no longer attempted to read memories, but instead tried to activate the power that the prayer beads might contain.

As expected! With the infusion of internal energy, the seemingly natural wood grain on the surface of the ebony prayer beads slowly glowed with an extremely dim, almost imperceptible, dark green light! A strange, contradictory aura, carrying both chilling and vibrant energy, emanated from the prayer beads, flowing slowly down Lin Chen's arm towards the wound on his left shoulder.

"Hiss..."

With a sickeningly faint sound, the black, necrotic wound on Lin Chen's left shoulder, emitting a foul stench, reacted violently to the dark green light, writhing and contracting as if hot oil had been poured onto snow! The necrotic, blackened flesh at the wound's edges was gradually "burned" and "purified" by the light, dissipating into wisps of black smoke to reveal the fresh, new flesh beneath! The "Netherworld Bone-Corroding Poison," deeply ingrained in his bones and meridians, was like a subject encountering a king before this dark green light, which was of the same origin but even more refined and eerie—it was swiftly suppressed, dispelled, and even…reversely absorbed and refined by the light!

Liao Chen's natal magic weapon could actually suppress and absorb his own "Netherworld Bone-Corroding Poison"! This was probably something Liao Chen himself hadn't anticipated, or perhaps a deliberate contingency plan (to prevent himself from being poisoned or controlling others).
