Lin Chen, by sheer chance, had found the key to the antidote in a desperate situation!

Although the dark green light carried a strong, sinister aura, absorbing and refining it caused Lin Chen to feel waves of chilling discomfort emanating from his very soul, as if he were touching something forbidden. But at this moment, survival was paramount, and he couldn't afford to worry about anything else. He gritted his teeth and persevered, guiding the dark green light surging from the prayer beads to cleanse his body of the deadly poison. At the same time, he circulated the cool energy from the "Purple Cloud Qi Control Technique" and the "Heart-Soul Connection" bond to protect his heart and sea of ​​consciousness, resisting the erosion of the sinister energy.

The effect was remarkable! In just a dozen breaths, most of the blackness from the wound on his left shoulder faded, the excruciating pain and numbness greatly reduced, and new granulation tissue grew and healed at a visible speed. The rampant poison within his body was also suppressed and nearly half eliminated, and his internal energy flowed much more smoothly again. Although still weak and severely injured, he had at least temporarily escaped the danger of dying from the poison and regained some mobility.

"There he is! Chase him!"

"He's injured, he can't have gone far! Surround him!"

The sounds of pursuers were now almost upon him! Several dark figures swooped down from the cliffs on either side like giant birds, while more footsteps and barking dogs rapidly approached from behind! His pursuers had clearly discovered his location and were preparing to encircle and annihilate him!

A cold glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. He knew he could no longer escape. His opponents outnumbered him, were strong, and were adept at encirclement. His injuries hadn't healed, and his internal energy was depleted. Continuing to run would only lead to his death by attrition. He had to take advantage of the moment when his poison had subsided and he still had the strength to fight, to launch a preemptive strike, disrupt their formation, and perhaps find a glimmer of hope!

He abruptly stopped, leaning against a massive, moss-covered rock. His left hand (due to his injured right arm) gripped the ebony prayer beads tightly, channeling his newly recovered, meager internal energy, along with the strange, dark green light emanating from the beads, into his meridians, preparing for a desperate last stand. Simultaneously, his right hand slowly rose, a faint yet concentrated wisp of purplish-gold energy emanating from his fingertips, flickering with faint lightning.

"Whoosh! Whoosh! Whoosh!"

Three poisoned crossbow bolts, arranged in a triangular formation, shot out from the dense forest on the left, their angles treacherous, aiming directly at Lin Chen's upper, middle, and lower body! Immediately afterward, two dark figures pounced from the right, their blades flashing like snow, whistling through the air, slashing towards his neck and abdomen! Directly in front, a seventh-rank early-stage expert, wielding a pair of Emei Daggers with the most composed aura, leaped out like a cheetah, the tips of the daggers gleaming with a chilling blue light, aiming straight for Lin Chen's heart! Above, two more figures, wielding poisoned hooks, descended from mid-air, sealing off any chance for him to leap upwards!

A trap set in heaven and earth, a deadly scheme!

However, just as all the attacks were about to strike, Lin Chen moved!

He neither dodged nor met the attacks head-on. Instead, he leaned back sharply, pressing himself against the rock behind him, and simultaneously, with his left hand gripping the ebony prayer beads, he pressed them hard against the ground! Using a special technique, he frantically channeled the dark green light from the prayer beads into the thick layer of humus beneath his feet!

"Netherworld—Rotten Ground!"

A low, hoarse voice, seemingly not his own, squeezed from Lin Chen's throat. This was a rudimentary, evil technique he had hastily grasped from fragments of Liao Chen's memories—a technique that used "Netherworld Qi" to pollute the earth's energy and create traps! Though crude and extremely energy-consuming, it might be surprisingly effective at this moment!

"Boom!"

The ground within a three-zhang radius of Lin Chen's feet suddenly trembled! A thick, dark green mist, carrying a pungent stench and strong corrosive properties, erupted from the humus layer like a fountain, instantly spreading and completely enveloping Lin Chen and the surrounding space! This mist not only obscured vision but also corroded flesh, eroded internal energy, and disrupted perception! It was a variant of the "Netherworld Bone-Corroding Poison"!

"Ah! What the hell?!"

"Watch out! It's poisonous!"

"My eyes!"

The leading eighth-rank assassins were caught off guard and instantly engulfed by the dark green poisonous mist, letting out shrill screams. Their protective auras rapidly dissolved under the corrosive influence of the mist, their skin and flesh burned with excruciating pain, and their vision blurred, losing all sense of direction. Even the leading seventh-rank expert's expression changed, and he hurriedly held his breath and retreated, swinging his Emei dagger in an attempt to dispel the poisonous mist.

Lin Chen, however, desired this moment of chaos and delay! The instant the poisonous mist erupted, he pushed his Lightness Step and the subtleties of "Qi Control" to their limits, his figure moving like a ghost, skimming the ground, passing between two eighth-rank assassins whose movements were slowed by the poisonous mist! Simultaneously, his right index and middle fingers joined together, gathering the last wisp of pure, lightning-infused purple-gold qi, transforming it into two needle-like sword energies, which flashed towards the temples of the two assassins!

"Pfft! Pfft!"

Two soft sounds, like ripe watermelons being punctured. The two eighth-rank assassins didn't even have time to scream before their foreheads were pierced, their brains splattering. Without a sound, they collapsed limply to the ground, lifeless.

Having landed a successful blow, Lin Chen didn't hesitate. Taking advantage of the poisonous fog and the momentary shock of his attackers, he darted forward again, lunging towards another eighth-rank assassin on his right, who was frantically swinging his longsword because his vision was clouded by the poisonous fog. His left hand, ebony prayer beads, flashed with a ghostly light once more. A thin, hair-like, yet incredibly swift, dark green energy shot out from the beads, striking the assassin's wrist holding the sword!

"Swoosh!" The assassin's wrist instantly turned black and festered, his longsword clattering to the ground. Lin Chen was already before him, a simple yet powerful hand chop striking his neck! With a crack, his neck bone snapped, the assassin's eyes widened, and he fell to the ground in despair.

In the blink of an eye, three men were dead! Poisonous mist, surprise attack, precise strike—Lin Chen used his remaining strength and the unorthodox methods left behind by Liao Chen to their fullest extent, catching his pursuers completely off guard!

"Damn it! Die!"

The leading seventh-rank expert finally reacted, both shocked and enraged. He hadn't expected Lin Chen to possess such fighting power and unpredictable methods despite being severely injured and poisoned! He roared, no longer hesitant to engage in the poisonous fog, his twin daggers transforming into two eerie blue lightning bolts, piercing through the thinning poisonous fog with a piercing shriek, aiming straight for Lin Chen's back! This attack, fueled by rage, was incredibly powerful, clearly intended to kill Lin Chen instantly!

Lin Chen had just killed his third opponent; his old strength was exhausted, and new strength had not yet been generated. His back was wide open, and he was about to be pierced through by the twin spikes!

On the verge of death! A ruthless glint flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. Instead of dodging, he channeled all the remaining internal energy within him, along with the last trace of dark green light absorbed from the ebony prayer beads, into his right fist! He whirled around, facing the rapidly enlarging, dark blue spikes, and unleashed a punch!

This punch lacked dazzling lightning and any flamboyant flourishes. It contained only a fierce, resolute, and unstoppable martial will, compressed to its extreme—a violent and complex power mixed with his own purple-gold qi, residual lightning intent, and that eerie dark green light!

"Let's perish together!"

"Boom—!!!"

Fists and daggers clashed again! More violently and brutally than any previous collision! The violent shockwave completely dispersed the remaining poisonous mist, revealing a scene of carnage and corpses. Lin Chen felt as if he had been struck by a heavy hammer; his chest throbbed with pain, blood gushing from his mouth. He was sent flying backward like a kite with a broken string, crashing heavily into the giant rock behind him, cracking the rock before finally sliding to the ground. His vision went completely black, his ears were ringing, his internal organs felt displaced, and countless bones in his body were broken. He had completely lost the ability to move, with only a faint consciousness remaining.

The seventh-rank expert also grunted, staggering back several steps. The pair of Emei daggers, forged from fine steel, in his hands were bent and deformed by Lin Chen's desperate punch! A strange, violent, sinister force, imbued with the heat of thunder, pierced through the daggers into the meridians of his arm, wreaking havoc. His entire right arm instantly became numb and tingling, his internal energy became disordered, and he felt a sweet taste in his throat, almost spitting out blood. He looked at Lin Chen, who lay motionless on the ground, seemingly covered in blood, his eyes filled with shock and lingering fear. This kid is simply a madman!

"Elder! Are you alright?" The other two eighth-rank assassins, who had narrowly escaped the poisonous fog and were only slightly injured, quickly stepped forward to help him.

"Cough cough... I'm fine." The seventh-rank expert (elder) suppressed his injuries, waved his hand, and looked at the barely breathing Lin Chen with a sinister gaze. "This brat is at his last gasp. Go, finish him off! Take his head, and... that string of prayer beads in his hand, bring them back to the Prince!"

"Yes!" The two eighth-rank assassins' eyes flashed with cruelty as they drew their swords and approached Lin Chen.

Lin Chen's consciousness was blurred; he could only watch helplessly as the two figures, filled with chilling killing intent, drew closer. He didn't even have the strength to move a finger. The ebony prayer beads in his arms had lost their luster and become cold. Was he... really going to die here? Yu'er... Princess... I'm sorry...

Just as the two assassins' blades were about to fall, and Lin Chen closed his eyes in despair, awaiting death—

"Whoosh! Whoosh!"

Two sharp, short, almost inaudible sounds of air being pierced rang out without warning! The sounds came from the upper side, from the steep, moss-covered cliff face!

The two eighth-rank assassins and the seventh-rank elder, all seasoned veterans, paled upon hearing the sound and instinctively dodged to the sides! However, the two streaks of black light were faster than imagined, their angles incredibly tricky and unpredictable, as if they had calculated every possible way they could evade!

"Pfft!"

"Ugh!"

Two screams rang out almost simultaneously! One of the eighth-rank assassins, who had been raising his blade to strike Lin Chen, had a small, translucent hole in his throat, blood gushing out. His eyes were filled with disbelief and horror as he clutched his throat, making hoarse sounds, and slowly collapsed. The other assassin had also been pierced through the chest by a streak of black light, dying instantly without a sound!

The seventh-rank elder, though reacting swiftly and avoiding vital points, still suffered a deep, bleeding wound on his left shoulder blade, grazed by a streak of dark light. A chilling, vicious force, penetrating to the bone, relentlessly eroded the wound! He groaned, his face instantly turning deathly pale, and he looked up at the cliff face in shock and rage: "Who is it?! Hiding and attacking from the shadows!"

On the cliff face, all was silent, save for the rustling of the vines in the night wind. It was as if the two deadly blows had been nothing but an illusion.

But the next moment, a black figure, deeper and more elusive than the night itself, like a weightless ghost, slowly "floated" down from the shadows of the cliff face. The newcomer was also dressed in black, his face veiled with a black cloth, revealing only a pair of cold, merciless eyes, like an eternally frozen pool. In his hand, he toyed with two slender, jet-black, gleaming, hiltless daggers, their tips still stained with traces of blood. His landing was as light as a falling leaf, without making a sound, not even disturbing the surrounding dust.

Shadow Seven! It's Shadow Seven! He's finally arrived! And with his first move, it was a fatal strike! Clearly, he had been lying in wait, patiently anticipating the perfect opportunity.

"It's you... the 'Shadow' beside Princess Zhaoyang?" The seventh-rank elder clearly recognized Shadow Seven's identity, his eyes filled with even greater apprehension. Clutching his bleeding wound, he slowly retreated. Both were seventh-rank elders, but he was injured first, and facing the "Shadow," renowned for his stealthy assassinations, his chances of victory were slim.

Ying Qi didn't answer, not even glancing at the elder. His cold gaze swept over the barely alive Lin Chen on the ground, confirming he was still alive, a barely perceptible flicker appearing in his eyes. Then, he flashed to Lin Chen's side, crouching down to quickly examine his injuries, his brow furrowing almost imperceptibly. Lin Chen's injuries were more severe than he had anticipated.

He swiftly retrieved several medicine bottles from his robes, indiscriminately stuffing the various colored powders and pills inside into Lin Chen's mouth. He then channeled a pure, gentle yet powerfully vital energy into Lin Chen's heart meridian, helping him absorb the medicinal effects. Having done this, he slowly rose and turned to the seventh-rank elder, who appeared to be facing a formidable enemy.

"He... I'll protect him." Shadow Seven's voice was hoarse and dry, like two rusty iron pieces rubbing against each other, devoid of any emotional fluctuation, yet carrying an undeniable, chilling killing intent. "You... can leave. Or... die."

The seventh-rank elder's facial muscles twitched violently, his eyes flashing with anger and resentment, but even more so with fear. He knew that even in his prime, he might not be able to gain the upper hand against Shadow Seven, let alone now, severely injured. Moreover, now that the other party had shown himself, Shadow Nine and the other reinforcements on the perimeter would likely arrive soon. Staying any longer meant certain death.

"Good! Good 'Shadow'! I'll remember this favor! We'll meet again!" He uttered these harsh words, not daring to linger for a moment, his figure retreating rapidly, disappearing into the depths of the dense forest in a few leaps, not even bothering to care about his companion's corpse.

Ying Qi didn't pursue, but stood quietly in place until the elder's aura completely disappeared. Only then did he slowly turn around and look at Lin Chen again.

Under the combined effects of the internal energy Ying Qi infused and the potent medicine, Lin Chen finally caught his breath. He opened his eyes with difficulty, looked at Ying Qi's blurry figure, his lips moved as if he wanted to say something, but no sound came out.

"Don't speak, concentrate and regulate your breathing," Ying Qi whispered, bending down to carefully hoist Lin Chen onto his back. Lin Chen's body felt almost weightless, a testament to the severity of his injuries and the depletion of his vital energy.

"Young Master, hold on. I'll take you back to the villa. Doctor Chen and the Princess are waiting for you." Ying Qi's voice remained calm, but his movements were exceptionally gentle and steady. He determined the direction, then spread his body, carrying Lin Chen, and sped towards the villa behind Ciyun Nunnery, where Princess Zhaoyang resided, outside Yeren Gully. His movements were even faster and more stable than when he arrived, as if he were carrying not a person, but a feather.

The night remained deep. In the Savage Gully, only a few gradually cooling corpses remained, along with the lingering, faint stench of blood and poisonous mist, bearing witness to the brief but brutal battle that had just taken place.

At the edge of the Broken Soul Cliff, Liao Chen, having devoured the black-robed elder's entire essence and soul, had his eerie green light recede. Although his aura was still weak, the eerie blush on his face had faded considerably, and his eyes had regained their clarity. However, the madness and resentment deep within his eyes had deepened even further.

He slowly rose, glanced at the black-robed elder's shriveled and gruesome corpse, then looked in the direction Lin Chen and Ying Qi had departed, a cold, venomous smile curling at the corner of his lips.

"Lin Chen... Princess Zhaoyang... Hehe, you've got good luck this time. However, you only know one side of the 'key's' secret. The real 'Night Wanderer's Eye' isn't in the City God Temple... but in a place you'd never expect. Once I've fully recovered my divine powers and obtained the 'Night Wanderer's Eye,' I'll settle this score with you properly!"

His figure flickered, transforming into a faint gray shadow, and he silently vanished into the night, disappearing into the mountains and forests in a direction completely opposite to Jiming Temple and Ciyun Nunnery.


Chapter 50 Healing at the Secluded Courtyard, Night Wanderer's Eye

Inside the secluded courtyard behind Ciyun Nunnery, the air was thick with the aroma of medicine.

Lin Chen lay on the soft bed, his face still as pale as paper, but the lingering deathly pallor between his brows had largely dissipated. The wound on his left shoulder had been cleaned, sutured, and treated with a specially made, fragrant "Jade Skin and Bone-Regenerating Ointment" by Doctor Chen, and carefully bandaged with a clean cloth. All the other wounds, bruises, and fractures on his body had also been properly treated. He was now fast asleep, his breathing still weak, but steady and long, no longer showing any signs of stopping.

Beside the bed, Princess Zhao Lingxi, dressed in an elegant moon-white robe and without makeup, sat quietly, gently wiping the fine beads of sweat from Lin Chen's forehead with a warm, damp towel. Her movements were focused and natural, as if performing an ordinary task, but the slight furrow in her brow and the hidden worry in her eyes betrayed her turbulent emotions.

Doctor Chen, tidying his medicine chest nearby, said in a low voice, "Your Highness, rest assured, Young Master Lin's pulse has stabilized. Although his internal energy is weak, his foundation is intact, and the remaining poison in his body has been largely removed by the power of that strange prayer bead. However, his injuries are too severe, with excessive blood loss, internal organ turmoil, and damaged meridians. Furthermore, he forcibly activated a secret technique, depleting his vital energy. He will likely need several months of quiet recuperation, supplemented with the finest medicinal herbs, before he can fully recover. And… in the short term, he absolutely must not engage in any combat, otherwise, he may suffer from deep-seated injuries that are difficult to heal, affecting his future martial arts progress."

"Thank you, Doctor Chen." Zhao Lingxi put down the towel, looking at Lin Chen's still-furrowed brows in his sleep, and sighed softly. "If it weren't for my sake, he wouldn't have been in such a dangerous situation, almost losing his life. In any case, we must use the best medicine to ensure his recovery. Just let us know if you need anything."

"This old man will do his best," Doctor Chen replied, then hesitated for a moment, "However… the lingering, cold, and evil aura within the young master's body, though temporarily suppressed, seems to originate from the same source as the ebony prayer beads, and is quite stubborn. It's likely some kind of hidden danger left behind by that old scoundrel Liao Chen. This old man can only gradually dissolve it with gentle medicine. To eradicate it completely, perhaps the young master's own cultivation will need to recover, and he can slowly refine it using a supremely yang cultivation method or a purifying and righteous qi."

“I understand.” Zhao Lingxi nodded, her gaze falling on the ebony prayer beads beside Lin Chen’s pillow, their dull color still faintly radiating a chilling aura. These beads were brought back by Ying Qi, who said Lin Chen had clutched them tightly before he fell unconscious. She had already examined them; they did indeed contain sealed portions of Liao Chen’s memories and soul imprint, and also contained a pure yet malevolent “Netherworld Power.” It was remarkably effective in healing Lin Chen and expelling poison, but like a leech, it left a hidden danger.

“Liao Chen… a remnant of the Netherworld Cult, a henchman of Prince Zhongshun…” A cold glint flashed in Zhao Lingxi’s eyes. “Ying Qi, have you found Liao Chen’s whereabouts?”

Ying Qi, who had been standing guard by the door like a shadow, whispered, "Your Highness, I tracked him to the Broken Soul Cliff and found only a dried corpse drained of its life essence. Upon identification, it was found to be one of the elders of the 'Shadow Guard' under Prince Zhongshun. Liao Chen has vanished without a trace. Strong traces of ghostly energy and spatial fluctuations remained at the scene, suggesting that he may have used some kind of blood escape technique or had accomplices who helped him escape from the Jiming Temple area. I have ordered the relevant key roads to be sealed off and have increased manpower to search for his whereabouts in Jinling and the surrounding area, but... this cunning fiend has probably already fled far away, or perhaps changed his appearance and infiltrated the shadows."

"He escaped?" Zhao Lingxi frowned even more deeply. Liao Chen knew too many secrets, especially those concerning the "key" and the "Map of Mountains and Rivers." His escape would have endless consequences. Moreover, the fact that he managed to escape the encirclement and attack of Ying Qi and the Shadow Guard elders, even managing to kill one of them, meant his strength and cunning were probably more terrifying than anticipated. More importantly, the secret of the "key"...

"Before Lin Chen lost consciousness, did he leave any words? Or convey any information through the prayer beads?" Zhao Lingxi asked Ying Qi.

Ying Qi shook his head: "Before the young master lost consciousness, he only uttered a few vague words: 'key...yin and yang...City God Temple...' before falling unconscious. I suspect it might be related to the memories sealed within those prayer beads."

"Yin and yang? City God Temple?" Zhao Lingxi pondered. She picked up the ebony prayer beads again, gathering a wisp of pure royal purple energy at her fingertips and slowly infusing it. This time, she didn't attempt to forcefully decipher or read the memories (to avoid damaging or triggering a counterattack), but instead used a gentle, guiding method to try and resonate with the lingering aura of Lin Chen within the prayer beads, as well as those fragmented memories.

A moment later, the prayer beads warmed slightly, and clearer yet more fragmented images, accompanied by fragments of Liao Chen's resentful and greedy thoughts, flooded into Zhao Lingxi's mind. She saw the indentation and star map on the Medicine Buddha's base in the underground palace of Jiming Temple, fragments of Liao Chen's secret conversation with the black-robed elder, and more information about the "key"—more than one! The Yin Key, "Medicine Buddha's Heart," was in Jiming Temple, and the Yang Key, "Night Wanderer's Eye," was in... the City God Temple? No, that's not right. The images suddenly distorted, and Liao Chen's maniacal smile appeared: "Fake! All fake! 'Night Wanderer's Eye' isn't in the City God Temple at all! It's... it's..." The images abruptly stopped, as if forcibly erased or covered up by a powerful force.

But at the last moment, Zhao Lingxi still caught a fleeting glimpse of a scene—it seemed to be the interior of a temple that was bustling with incense offerings yet exuded an eerie and sinister aura? The appearance of the statue was indistinct, but in its hand, it seemed to be holding a strange jade stone shaped like an eyeball, emitting a faint, eerie light? And on one corner of the temple's plaque, the words "Night Tour" were faintly visible.

"The Night Wanderer's Temple? Or... a side hall dedicated to the 'Night Wanderer'?" Zhao Lingxi withdrew her finger, her expression grave. Liao Chen's memories had clearly been tampered with, or perhaps because he practiced evil arts, his memories were inherently chaotic. The true location of the "Night Wanderer's Eye" seemed to be concealed by some kind of restriction or hint, leaving only the potentially misleading information of "City God Temple." But that last blurry image might be the true clue.

“The City God Temple…the Night Wanderer Temple…” Zhao Lingxi pondered. The City God Temple in Jinling was a place of great religious significance, a sacred site for the official worship of a deity, and heavily guarded. If the “Night Wanderer’s Eye” was truly there, Prince Zhongshun and Liao Chen would likely have already succeeded, or at least been closely monitoring him. As for the “Night Wanderer’s Temple” in that blurry image…within Jinling City, there didn’t seem to be any well-known temples dedicated solely to the “Night Wanderer.” Perhaps some remote villages outside the city, or the ancestral halls or family temples of certain ancient families, might have side halls or shrines dedicated to the “Night Wanderer” (a minor deity in folk legend who patrols the night). That range was vast, practically like finding a needle in a haystack.

"It seems the matter of the key is more complicated than we imagined. Liao Chen deliberately left the clue of the 'City God Temple,' perhaps to lure us there and trap us, or to cause a conflict with Prince Zhongshun's men. The real 'Night Wanderer' is probably hiding in a more secretive and unexpected place." Zhao Lingxi said to Ying Qi, "Pass down the order to secretly investigate all temples, shrines, and historical sites in and around Jinling that may be related to the 'Night Wanderer,' especially those that seem inconspicuous, have little incense offerings, or are connected to certain ancient families or the remnants of previous dynasties. Remember, do not alert them."

"Yes," Shadow Seven replied.

"Furthermore," Zhao Lingxi glanced at Lin Chen, who was still unconscious on the bed, a complex look flashing in her eyes, "Lin Chen's serious injuries are all because of me. Until he recovers, all actions targeting Prince Zhongshun and 'The Key' should be suspended. Everything should prioritize his safety and recovery. We should also send more people to secretly protect the Jia family, especially Miss Daiyu in Xiaoxiang Pavilion. Liaochen and Prince Zhongshun may target those around him as a form of blackmail or revenge."

"Understood, Your Highness." Ying Qi nodded, hesitated for a moment, and then said, "Your Highness, news came from Constable Tie that 'Ghost Hand' died in prison... from poisoning. His death was gruesome, his body was covered in sores. The coroner's examination revealed that he was poisoned by an extremely rare mixed poison that had penetrated deep into his bone marrow before he entered prison, and there is no antidote. There were no valuable clues among his belongings. Prince Zhongshun has already put pressure on the government through certain channels, demanding that the case be closed as soon as possible and that 'Ghost Hand's' body be taken back for burial. Constable Tie withstood the pressure and temporarily detained the body on the grounds that 'there are many suspicious points and a thorough investigation is needed,' but I'm afraid... it won't last long."

"Dead?" Zhao Lingxi sneered. "Clean enough. Prince Zhongshun is indeed ruthless, leaving no survivors. Tell Constable Tie not to resist, just give him the body. However, before returning the body, have Doctor Chen... examine it 'carefully' again, especially... the teeth, hair roots, fingernails, and other places where tiny objects might be hidden. I suspect that 'Ghost Hand' might still be carrying some information that he didn't have time to pass on."

"Yes." A knowing glint flashed in Ying Qi's eyes as she quietly withdrew to relay the orders.

The room fell silent again, save for Lin Chen's steady breathing and the occasional crackling of the charcoal fire. Doctor Chen had already packed up his medicine kit, bowed, and left to prepare the next dose of medicine.

Zhao Lingxi sat alone on the edge of the bed, quietly watching Lin Chen's pale sleeping face. The flickering candlelight cast dappled shadows on his face. This young man, in just a few months, had grown from an insignificant, even bullied, collateral descendant of the Jia family to what he was now. His martial arts, strategy, and temperament were all superior. Moreover, at crucial moments, for her, for this turbulent Chu, he had stood up for her, risking his life and nearly dying several times.

"Lin Chen… how many secrets are you still hiding?" Zhao Lingxi murmured to herself, her fingertips unconsciously tracing the string of ebony prayer beads. "That strange power to expel poison and heal wounds, that composure far beyond his years, that ability to seem to have a special connection to the secrets of the previous dynasty and ancient martial arts techniques… and also, that deep-seated, life-or-death protectiveness towards Daiyu, that willingness to sacrifice everything…"

She recalled that day at Qixia Mountain, when Lin Chen unhesitatingly shielded Daiyu from a fatal blow; she remembered the attack on Tingzhu Pavilion, where he was critically wounded and on the verge of death, yet clutching tightly the soul-nourishing wood pendant he intended to give to Daiyu; and she recalled the meticulous care and silent devotion Lin Chen had shown Daiyu, which she had secretly discovered. That emotion, pure and ardent, untainted by any self-interest or calculation, seemed so precious in this cold and treacherous vortex of power struggles, yet also... so heartbreaking.

“Perhaps, this is what sets you apart from others.” Zhao Lingxi sighed softly, a faint melancholy, unnoticed even by herself, flashing in her eyes. “With love and protection, one can forge ahead without hesitation, even unto death. But this world, this royal family, this realm… haven’t they been far too cruel to you, to her?”

She stood up, walked to the window, and opened it. The night breeze, carrying the coolness of the mountains and the fragrance of grass and trees, swept in, dispelling the medicinal smell in the room and clearing her chaotic thoughts slightly.

In the distance, the evening bell of Ciyun Temple drifted softly, echoing through the silent valley. Further afield, in Jinling City, the myriad lights still shone brightly, yet who knew how many undercurrents and lurking dangers lay hidden beneath the surface?

"No matter what, Lin Chen, since you've chosen to stand beside me, I, Zhao Lingxi, will never let you down." She gazed at the deep night sky, her eyes rekindling with a resolute and sharp light. "This empire, this chaotic situation, we... will break it together!"

Just then, a faint groan came from the bed.

Zhao Lingxi turned abruptly, only to see Lin Chen's eyelashes flutter slightly before he slowly opened his eyes. His eyes, though still showing the weakness and exhaustion of his injuries, had regained their clarity, like two deep pools reflecting the flickering candlelight, calmly looking at her.

"Lingxi..." Lin Chen's voice was hoarse and dry, almost inaudible.

"Don't move, and don't speak." Zhao Lingxi quickly returned to the bedside, pressing down on his attempt to sit up, a genuine, relieved smile on her face. "It's good you're awake. How are you feeling? Is anything still bothering you?"

She turned and poured a glass of warm water, carefully helping Lin Chen sit up and bringing the glass to his lips. Lin Chen took a few small sips from her hand; the warm water soothed his parched throat and invigorated him slightly.

"I...I'm fine." He leaned against the soft pillows Zhao Lingxi had placed there, his gaze sweeping across the familiar quiet room, then landing on his tightly bandaged left shoulder. Finally, he looked at the string of ebony prayer beads beside the pillow, a complex emotion flashing in his eyes. "Liao Chen...escaped?"

"Yes." Zhao Lingxi nodded, not hiding anything. "He killed an elder of the Shadow Guard and used a secret technique to escape, his whereabouts unknown. However, this string of prayer beads you brought back is very valuable. We obtained some important information about the 'key' from it."

She recounted Ying Qi's report, along with the misleading information she had gleaned from the prayer beads regarding the "Yin-Yang Key," the "City God Temple," and the final, blurry image of the "Night Wanderer Temple," to Lin Chen.

Lin Chen listened quietly, his brow slightly furrowed, clearly processing the information. After a moment, he said in a low voice, "Liao Chen is cunning. He deliberately left the clue about the 'City God Temple,' he must have a scheme. The real 'Night Wanderer' is probably even more deeply hidden. Moreover… I suspect he may have also left some kind of backup plan through these prayer beads, or… a means of surveillance." He looked at the prayer beads, his eyes filled with vigilance.

“I had the same concern.” Zhao Lingxi picked up the prayer beads and examined them carefully. “These prayer beads are made of a special material and have been refined for many years with the evil power of Liaochen’s blood, making them a truly sinister artifact. When you were unconscious, they spontaneously released nether energy to expel the poison, but also left a sinister aura within your body. Doctor Chen said it needs to be slowly refined using pure yang techniques or purifying the mind and body. I originally wanted to destroy them, but I was afraid of alerting them or damaging any other clues they might contain. I will keep them for now, sealing them with a royal secret method before studying them further.”

“Thank you for your trouble.” Lin Chen nodded, without objection. These prayer beads were indeed sinister; carrying them around was unwise.

"Your most important task right now is to recover," Zhao Lingxi said solemnly, looking at his pale face. "Everything else can wait until you've healed. I've already ordered increased security here, and Doctor Chen will stay to treat you. Don't worry about the Jia family or Miss Daiyu; I've made arrangements. Just focus on resting."

Hearing "Miss Daiyu," a flicker of undisguised worry and urgency flashed in Lin Chen's eyes. "Is Yu'er... alright? Does she know about my injury? Is it causing her concern?"

Seeing his reaction, Zhao Lingxi sighed inwardly, but put on a reassuring smile: "Don't worry, Miss Daiyu doesn't know you're seriously injured. I only had Doctor Chen enter the manor under the pretext of 'taking the princess's pulse,' and to treat and recuperate her. I told him you were entrusted by me to go out on an important errand and would be back in a few days. Although she's worried, Doctor Chen is highly skilled and has prescribed the right medicine for her, and also left some calming medicine. Her complexion has improved a lot these past few days, but she still worries about you. Once your injuries have stabilized a bit, you can write a letter saying you're alright, and I'll have someone secretly send it to her so she can be at ease."

Upon learning that Daiyu was safe and well cared for, Lin Chen's tense nerves finally relaxed, and gratitude appeared in his eyes: "Thank you... Lingxi, I've troubled you again this time."

"There's no need for thanks between us." Zhao Lingxi waved her hand, tucking the fallen blanket around him. "You risked your life for me, for Great Chu, almost losing it yourself. What I did was simply my duty. Alright, you've just woken up; you shouldn't overexert yourself. Sleep a little longer; the medicine Dr. Chen prepared will be ready soon."

Lin Chen indeed felt waves of weakness and exhaustion wash over him. No longer able to force himself, he nodded gently and closed his eyes again. However, before his consciousness sank into darkness, the blurry image of the "Night Wanderer's Temple" and Liao Chen's final, crazed, and eerie smile remained indelible in his mind, like a brand.

The key… the Night Wanderer's Eyes… Prince Zhongshun… Liao Chen…

And then there was the deep-seated longing and protective thought he held for Daiyu.

All of this was a tangled mess, a chaotic jumble. He knew he had to recover quickly. Only with enough power could he protect those he cared about, unravel the mystery, and... carve a path to survival in this vortex.

Hearing Lin Chen's breathing become even and deep again, Zhao Lingxi gently blew out the bedside candle, leaving only a corner lamp casting a soft glow. She sat quietly by the bed for a moment, confirming Lin Chen was sound asleep, before rising, picking up the ebony prayer beads, and silently leaving the quiet room.

Outside, the night air was cool, the moonlight like water.

Zhao Lingxi stood on the veranda, gazing at the seemingly ordinary yet strangely alluring prayer beads in her hand, her eyes deep and unfathomable.

"Night Wanderer's Eye... Yin-Yang Key... Jinling Dragon Vein... Imperial Mausoleum Palace..." she murmured, repeating these keywords, her mind racing through various possibilities.

Suddenly, as if remembering something, she took out another small, exquisite jade pendant from her bosom—a pure purple-gold piece carved with phoenix patterns. It was the very same "Purple Phoenix Heart-Protecting Pendant" she had given to Lin Chen, which he had then secretly used to seal his inner energy and divine sense before returning it to her to secretly protect Daiyu. The pendant was now emitting a faint, warm purple-gold halo, within which seemed to emanate a very subtle trace of Lin Chen's aura, slowly flowing and resonating.

"Yin and Yang complement each other… Dragon and Phoenix bring auspiciousness…" Zhao Lingxi's eyes suddenly lit up. A bold, almost inconceivable thought flashed through her mind like lightning!

"Could it be... that the so-called 'Yin-Yang Key' doesn't simply refer to two items, but rather to two... complementary yet distinct 'powers' or 'qualities'? Does the 'Medicine Buddha Heart' represent ultimate Yin, purification, and vitality? Does the 'Night Wanderer's Eyes' represent ultimate Yang, insight, and dispelling illusion? And could Lin Chen's 'Purple Phoenix Pendant,' which integrates his own inner energy, thunderous intent, and guardian spirit, and Daiyu's family heirloom 'Magnolia Pendant,' with its calming and soul-stabilizing effects... also subtly align with some mystery of 'Yin and Yang,' or 'Dragon and Phoenix'? Perhaps... they even share some unknown connection with that 'key'?"

This idea was too astonishing, too ethereal. But Zhao Lingxi's intuition told her that this might not be unfounded. Lin Chen and Daiyu, these two seemingly ordinary young people, were both shrouded in a mysterious mist. Did their encounter, their mutual understanding, and even their "soulmate" bond also play an unrevealed role in this grand game?

“It seems we need to re-examine many things.” Zhao Lingxi put away the ebony prayer beads and the purple phoenix pendant, gazing towards Jinling City. Her eyes flickered with a mixture of solemnity and an indescribable anticipation.

“Lin Chen, Daiyu… perhaps you are the true variables in this game… capable of breaking the deadlock.”

The night deepened. The bells of Ciyun Nunnery tolled once more, it was midnight.

And in a distant, hidden corner of Jinling City, shrouded in darkness…

Inside a dilapidated shrine, shrouded in gloom and eeriness, stood a strangely shaped deity, neither human nor beast, holding a jade eye-shaped statue.

Liao Chen, his face still pale, but his breathing much more stable, even exuding a more sinister and profound aura, sat cross-legged on a futon before the statue. In his hand, he held a string of ebony prayer beads, almost identical to the one he had given Lin Chen, only its color was deeper and its malevolent aura more pronounced.

His eyes were closed, chanting incantations, a faint, eerie green light emanating from him. Suddenly, without warning, one of the beads in his hand cracked with a tiny fissure, then completely dimmed, turning into a small clump of ashes.

Liao Chen abruptly opened his eyes, his gaze blazing with a green light, staring intently at the ashes, his face instantly turning incredibly grim, even showing a hint of fear.

"The connection... is broken?! That brat... actually discovered the hidden trick in the prayer beads? Or... is there a master of soul sealing by Princess Zhaoyang's side?!" He gritted his teeth, his voice hoarse like an owl's, "Lin Chen... you ruined my grand plan, destroyed my magical artifact, this enmity is irreconcilable! And Zhaoyang... do you think you can win by obtaining half-true, half-false clues? Heh heh... the real game has only just begun!"

He slowly rose, walked to the strange statue, and reached out to gently stroke the eye-shaped jade stone in the statue's hand. The jade stone was icy to the touch, and a faint, eerie light seemed to flow within it, vaguely reflecting Chen's hideous and twisted face.

"The Night Wanderer's Eye...it's almost here...almost there...when this old monk's divine power is fully restored, when I obtain the true 'Yang Key,' unlock the 'True Map,' and find that thing in the Dragon Vein Imperial Mausoleum...at that time, not only Prince Zhongshun, but the entire Great Chu Empire will tremble at my feet! Lin Chen, Zhaoyang, you...just wait! Hahaha...cough cough..."

The maniacal laughter echoed in the gloomy, dilapidated temple, lingering for a long time. Outside the temple, the night wind howled, as if countless wronged souls were weeping.

And in the statue's hand, the "Night Wanderer's Eye" flashed a faint, eerie light, as if...it had truly blinked.
